name: 3/4 sit-up
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Focus on lifting with your abs, not momentum, and keep your lower back pressed into the floor.
coaching_cues: Curl up slow, chin off chest.
Press lower back down flat.
Stop at 45, squeeze those abs.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn right through your upper abs and core. If your neck is straining or your hip flexors are taking over, reset your form and go slower. Stay with that burn and keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your feet lift off the ground, your hip flexors have taken over.

name: 45° side bend
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended straight down by your sides.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, slowly bend your torso to one side, lowering your hand towards your knee.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your core tight and only bend sideways — no twisting or leaning forward.
coaching_cues: Tight core, bend straight sideways.
Reach your hand toward your knee.
Control the return back to center.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a stretch and burn along the side of your waist right now. If you feel it in your back, you're probably twisting or bending forward. Stay focused on that side burn and keep working through it.
coach_guide.warning: Any forward lean or twisting motion takes the work away from your obliques.

name: air bike
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands placed behind your head.
Lift your legs off the ground and bend your knees at a 90-degree angle.
Bring your right elbow towards your left knee while simultaneously straightening your right leg.
Return to the starting position and repeat the movement on the opposite side, bringing your left elbow towards your right knee while straightening your left leg.
Continue alternating sides in a pedaling motion for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your lower back pressed to the floor and really drive those elbows to knees.
coaching_cues: Lower back stays glued down.
Drive elbow to opposite knee hard.
Keep that pedaling rhythm smooth.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in your abs and a stretch through your hip flexors. If your lower back starts arching up, slow down and focus on pressing it to the floor. Keep that core fire burning and push through every rep.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your lower back lifts off the floor, you've lost core control.

name: all fours squad stretch
instructions: Start on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and your knees directly under your hips.
Extend one leg straight back, keeping your knee bent and your foot flexed.
Slowly lower your hips towards the ground, feeling a stretch in your quads.
Hold this position for 20-30 seconds.
Switch legs and repeat the stretch on the other side.

coach_guide.intro: Sink your hips back and down to feel a deep stretch through the front of your thigh.
coaching_cues: Keep hips square to the ground.
Sink back slow and controlled.
Breathe deep into the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a long stretch running down the front of your thigh from hip to knee. If you feel pinching in your knee, ease off the depth slightly. Let that quad release and keep breathing.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing this stretch means you miss the muscle entirely.

name: alternate heel touchers
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Extend your arms straight out to the sides, parallel to the ground.
Engaging your abs, lift your shoulders off the ground and reach your right hand towards your right heel.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the left side, reaching your left hand towards your left heel.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your lower back glued to the floor and reach by crunching your obliques, not swinging your arms.
coaching_cues: Shoulders stay lifted throughout.
Reach with your side, not arm.
Alternate in a smooth rhythm.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques burning along your sides with each reach. If your neck is doing the work, relax your head back more. Lock into that side burn and keep those touches coming.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your shoulders drop between reps kills the tension completely.

name: alternate lateral pulldown
instructions: Sit on the cable machine with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lean back slightly and pull the handles towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull with your elbows, not your hands, and think about driving them down toward your hips.
coaching_cues: Lean back slightly, chest up.
Drive one elbow down hard.
Squeeze your lat at the bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel each side of your back light up individually as you pull. If your biceps are burning out first, focus more on the elbow drive. Keep chasing that lat squeeze and do not quit.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with both arms equally defeats the purpose of alternating.

name: ankle circles
instructions: Sit on the ground with your legs extended in front of you.
Lift one leg off the ground and rotate your ankle in a circular motion.
Perform the desired number of circles in one direction, then switch to the other direction.
Repeat with the other leg.

coach_guide.intro: Make big controlled circles to fully warm up the ankle joint through its complete range.
coaching_cues: Draw the biggest circles possible.
Keep your leg completely still.
Reverse direction halfway through.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel gentle movement and warmth building around your entire ankle joint. If you feel any sharp clicking or pain, make your circles smaller. Keep those ankles moving nice and smooth.
coach_guide.warning: Tiny rushed circles do nothing for your mobility.

name: archer pull up
instructions: Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back.
As you pull yourself up, bend one arm and bring your elbow towards your side, while keeping the other arm straight.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar and your bent arm is fully flexed.
Lower yourself back down with control, straightening the bent arm and repeating the movement on the other side.
Alternate sides with each repetition.

coach_guide.intro: Think about pulling yourself toward the bent arm side while keeping the straight arm locked out.
coaching_cues: Pull toward your bent elbow.
Keep straight arm fully locked.
Control the descent completely.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense contraction in the lat on your pulling side. If your shoulders are riding up to your ears, reset and pull your shoulder blades down first. This is elite level so embrace the challenge.
coach_guide.warning: Bending both arms turns this into a regular pull up.

name: archer push up
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Extend one arm straight out to the side, parallel to the ground.
Lower your body by bending your elbows, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Push back up to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, extending the opposite arm out to the side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Shift your weight toward your bent arm side and keep that extended arm straight throughout.
coaching_cues: Shift weight to working side.
Keep extended arm straight out.
Lower chest toward bent hand.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working hard on the bent arm side with a deep stretch across the straight arm side. If your hips are sagging, tighten your core and reset. You have got this, keep grinding.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips drop puts dangerous stress on your lower back.

name: arm slingers hanging bent knee legs
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your arms fully extended and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Engage your core and lift your knees towards your chest, bringing them as close to your elbows as possible.
Slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use your abs to drive your knees up, not momentum from swinging your body.
coaching_cues: Stay still at the shoulders.
Drive knees toward elbows.
Lower slow, no swinging.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs burning intensely as you lift those knees. If you are swinging back and forth, pause and reset your body position. Control that swing and keep those reps clean.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging turns this into a momentum exercise with zero ab work.

name: arm slingers hanging straight legs
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your arms fully extended and your legs straight down.
Engage your core and lift your legs up in front of you until they are parallel to the ground.
Hold for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your legs locked straight and use pure core strength to raise them parallel to the ground.
coaching_cues: Lock legs completely straight.
Lift with your lower abs.
Fight the swing on lowering.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core, especially lower abs, working overtime on this one. If your legs are bending, lower them less high until you build strength. This is tough so stay mentally locked in.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your knees makes this dramatically easier and less effective.

name: arms apart circular toe touch (male)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms extended to the sides.
Keeping your legs straight, bend forward at the waist and reach down towards your toes with your right hand.
As you reach down, simultaneously lift your left leg straight up behind you, maintaining balance.
Return to the starting position and repeat the movement with your left hand reaching towards your toes and your right leg lifting up behind you.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your legs straight throughout and reach opposite hand to opposite foot with control.
coaching_cues: Arms wide, legs stay straight.
Touch toe then rotate through.
Keep your balance centered.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a stretch through your hamstrings and a squeeze in your glutes on the standing leg. If you are losing balance constantly, slow down the movement. Keep that body moving with control.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your knees removes the hamstring stretch completely.

name: arms overhead full sit-up (male)
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Extend your arms overhead, keeping them straight.
Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is upright.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your arms reaching overhead throughout to make your abs work harder against the extra lever.
coaching_cues: Arms stay by your ears.
Roll up one vertebra at a time.
Control the descent back down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire abdominal wall burning from top to bottom. If your arms are swinging forward for momentum, keep them locked overhead. Push through that burn, you are building serious core strength.
coach_guide.warning: Throwing your arms forward to help you up is cheating your abs.

name: assisted chest dip (kneeling)
instructions: Adjust the machine to your desired height and secure your knees on the pad.
Grasp the handles with your palms facing down and your arms fully extended.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lean your torso forward to target your chest more than your triceps during this dip.
coaching_cues: Lean forward, elbows back.
Lower until arms hit parallel.
Press up through your chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and squeeze across your chest with each rep. If your shoulders are pinching at the bottom, do not go quite as deep. Keep pressing through and build that chest.
coach_guide.warning: Staying upright shifts all the work to your triceps instead.

name: assisted hanging knee raise
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your arms fully extended and your palms facing away from you.
Engage your core muscles and lift your knees towards your chest, bending at the hips and knees.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your abs.
Slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive your knees up using your abs, not by swinging or using momentum from your hips.
coaching_cues: Initiate with your lower abs.
Bring knees to chest height.
Pause and squeeze at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs working hard to lift those knees up. If you are swinging around, reset and start each rep from a dead hang. Stay controlled and own every single rep.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum from swinging means your abs are not doing the work.

name: assisted hanging knee raise with throw down
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your arms fully extended and your palms facing away from you.
Engage your core and lift your knees towards your chest, keeping your legs together.
Once your knees are at chest level, explosively throw your legs down towards the ground, extending them fully.
Allow your legs to swing back up and repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The throw down is controlled power, not just dropping your legs recklessly.
coaching_cues: Knees up with ab control.
Throw down with power.
Reset fully before next rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an explosive contraction through your entire core on the throw down. If your lower back is arching at the bottom, reduce the power slightly. Keep that explosive energy going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Uncontrolled throw downs put serious stress on your lower back.

name: assisted lying calves stretch
instructions: Lie on your back with your legs extended.
Bend one knee and place your foot flat on the ground.
Using your hands or a towel, gently pull your toes towards your body, feeling a stretch in your calf.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Release the stretch and repeat on the other leg.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your leg straight and gently pull your toes toward you to feel the calf stretch.
coaching_cues: Keep stretching leg straight.
Pull toes toward your shin.
Breathe and hold steady.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch running from your heel up through your calf muscle. If you feel any sharp pain behind your knee, back off the intensity slightly. Relax into it and let that calf release.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your knee releases the calf and stretches your hamstring instead.

name: assisted lying glutes stretch
instructions: Lie on your back with your legs extended.
Bend your right knee and place your right ankle on your left thigh, just above the knee.
Grasp your left thigh with both hands and gently pull it towards your chest.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Release and repeat on the other side.

coach_guide.intro: Relax into this stretch and let gravity do the work for you.
coaching_cues: Pull gently, do not force it.
Keep your head and shoulders down.
Breathe deep into the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch right in the middle of your glute on the bent leg side. If you feel it in your lower back, ease off the pull and relax your spine. Stay with it and let that tension melt away.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too hard too fast will cause your body to tense up and resist the stretch.

name: assisted lying gluteus and piriformis stretch
instructions: Lie on your back with your legs extended.
Bend your right knee and place your right ankle on your left thigh, just above the knee.
Grasp your left thigh with both hands and gently pull it towards your chest.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Release the stretch and repeat on the other side.

coach_guide.intro: This targets deep into your hip, so breathe and sink into it slowly.
coaching_cues: Draw that knee toward opposite shoulder.
Keep your tailbone grounded down.
Relax your neck and jaw completely.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your hip and possibly into your outer glute where the piriformis lives. If you feel pinching in the front of your hip, back off slightly. Breathe through it and let your body open up.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing the stretch will cause your hips to rotate and you will lose the target.

name: assisted lying leg raise with lateral throw down
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your arms by your sides.
Place your hands under your glutes for support.
Engage your abs and lift your legs off the ground, keeping them straight.
While keeping your legs together, lower them to one side until they are a few inches above the ground.
Pause for a moment, then lift your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat the movement to the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Control the throw down by bracing your core before your legs drop.
coaching_cues: Brace hard before the throw.
Resist the drop with your abs.
Keep lower back pressed to floor.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core working overtime to control your legs as they fall to each side. If your lower back arches off the ground, the weight is winning. Fight for control and keep those abs burning.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your lower back pops up, you have lost core engagement.

name: assisted lying leg raise with throw down
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your arms by your sides.
Place your hands under your glutes for support.
Engage your core and lift your legs off the ground, keeping them straight.
Raise your legs until they are perpendicular to the ground.
Lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Simultaneously, throw your legs down towards the ground, keeping them straight.
Raise your legs back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Your job is to resist the throw down, not just catch your legs.
coaching_cues: Press your lower back down.
Control the descent with your abs.
Stop legs before they hit ground.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn through your lower abs as you fight gravity on every rep. If you feel strain in your lower back, reset your position and brace harder. Keep fighting that resistance and stay strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your legs drop without control puts all the stress on your lower back.

name: assisted motion russian twist
instructions: Sit on the ground with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Hold the medicine ball with both hands in front of your chest.
Lean back slightly, engaging your abs and keeping your back straight.
Slowly twist your torso to the right, bringing the medicine ball towards the right side of your body.
Pause for a moment, then twist your torso to the left, bringing the medicine ball towards the left side of your body.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate from your ribcage, not just your arms swinging side to side.
coaching_cues: Twist from your ribs, not arms.
Keep the ball close to chest.
Control the rotation both directions.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques working hard on each side as you rotate through. If you only feel your arms or shoulders, slow down and initiate from your core. Lock in that twist and keep the burn going.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the ball with momentum takes all the work away from your abs.

name: assisted parallel close grip pull-up
instructions: Adjust the machine to your desired weight and height.
Place your hands on the parallel bars with a close grip, palms facing each other.
Hang from the bars with your arms fully extended and your feet off the ground.
Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bars, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bars.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive your elbows down toward your hips to engage your lats fully.
coaching_cues: Pull elbows down to your hips.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at top.
Lower with control, full stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats working hard along the sides of your back with each rep. If you feel it mostly in your biceps, focus on the elbow drive. Keep squeezing those lats and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Using only arm strength will burn out your biceps before your back gets any work.

name: assisted prone hamstring
instructions: Lie face down on a mat or bench with your legs fully extended.
Have a partner or use a resistance band to secure your ankles.
Engage your hamstrings and lift your legs towards your glutes, keeping your knees straight.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Squeeze your hamstrings to lift, do not just kick your feet up.
coaching_cues: Curl heels toward your glutes.
Keep your hips pressed into floor.
Squeeze hard at the top position.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction through the back of your thighs from just below your glutes to behind your knees. If you feel cramping, ease up slightly but keep tension. Stay with that hamstring squeeze and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your hips off the surface shifts the work away from your hamstrings.

name: assisted prone lying quads stretch
instructions: Lie face down on the ground with your legs extended.
Bend your left knee and reach back with your left hand to grab your left foot or ankle.
Gently pull your left foot towards your glutes, feeling a stretch in your left quad.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then release.
Repeat with your right leg.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your hips flat on the ground to get a true quad stretch.
coaching_cues: Press your hip into the floor.
Pull heel gently toward your glute.
Keep both knees close together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch running down the front of your thigh from hip to knee. If you feel knee discomfort, ease off the pull slightly. Breathe into that stretch and let your quad release.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hip rise up takes all the stretch out of your quad.

name: assisted prone rectus femoris stretch
instructions: Lie face down on the ground with your legs straight.
Bend your right knee and reach back with your right hand to grab your right foot or ankle.
Gently pull your right foot or ankle towards your glutes, feeling a stretch in the front of your right thigh.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Release and repeat on the other side.

coach_guide.intro: Press your hip down firmly to target the rectus femoris across your hip.
coaching_cues: Drive your hip bone into floor.
Pull heel toward glute gently.
Keep your core lightly engaged.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this stretch high on your thigh near the hip crease where the rectus femoris crosses. If you only feel it mid-thigh, press your hip down more. Hold steady and let that deep stretch work.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back releases tension from the target muscle.

name: assisted pull-up
instructions: Adjust the machine to your desired weight and height settings.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Hang with your arms fully extended and your feet off the ground.
Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the handles, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the handles.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Focus on pulling your elbows down toward your sides, not just hanging there.
coaching_cues: Drive your elbows down and back.
Chest up, shoulders down throughout.
Control the descent, don't drop.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this working the sides of your back, right under your armpits. If it's all in your arms, focus more on squeezing those shoulder blades. Keep going with that back engagement.
coach_guide.warning: Don't let your shoulders roll forward or you'll lose all the back work.

name: assisted seated pectoralis major stretch with stability ball
instructions: Sit on a stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a stability ball with both hands and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Slowly lower the stability ball towards your chest, feeling a stretch in your pectoral muscles.
Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let the ball pull your arms back while you sit tall and proud.
coaching_cues: Keep your chest lifted high.
Squeeze shoulder blades together gently.
Breathe into your chest opening.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a stretch across your chest and the front of your shoulders as the ball draws your arms back. If you feel shoulder pinching, bring your arms slightly lower. Enjoy that chest opening and keep breathing.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your upper back completely defeats the purpose of this stretch.

name: assisted side lying adductor stretch
instructions: Lie on your side with your legs straight and stacked on top of each other.
Bend your bottom leg slightly for stability.
Place your top foot on a stable surface, such as a bench or step.
Keeping your top leg straight, slowly lower it towards the ground, feeling a stretch in your inner thigh.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.

coach_guide.intro: Let gravity pull your top leg down while you stay completely relaxed.
coaching_cues: Keep your hips stacked vertically.
Let the leg drop with gravity.
Breathe and release inner thigh.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a stretch along your inner thigh from groin toward your knee. If you feel any knee discomfort, bend your leg slightly. Relax into it and let your adductors lengthen naturally.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling your hips forward or backward changes the stretch angle completely.

name: assisted sit-up
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench or have someone hold your feet down.
Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use your abs to curl up, not momentum from swinging your arms.
coaching_cues: Curl your spine up one vertebra.
Keep your chin off your chest.
Lower with control, do not drop.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs working through the entire movement, especially the upper portion near your ribs. If you feel neck strain, lighten the hand pressure behind your head. Keep curling strong and finish every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling on your neck to get up puts dangerous strain on your spine.

name: assisted standing chin-up
instructions: Adjust the machine to your desired assistance level.
Stand on the foot platform and grip the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your chest up and shoulders back, engage your core, and slightly bend your knees.
Pull your body up by flexing your elbows and driving your elbows down towards your sides.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lead with your chest to the bar, not just pulling with your arms.
coaching_cues: Drive your chest up to bar.
Squeeze shoulder blades back and down.
Lower with full control each rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats and biceps working together as you pull yourself up. If you only feel your arms, focus on driving elbows back. Keep pulling strong and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Shrugging your shoulders up steals work from your lats.

name: assisted standing pull-up
instructions: Adjust the machine to your desired weight and height settings.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your lats and biceps, and pull yourself up towards the handles.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your back muscles.
Slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Think about pulling the bar down to your chest, not just lifting yourself.
coaching_cues: Pull elbows down toward ribs.
Lead with your chest up high.
Control the lower, full stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your back muscles doing most of the work with your biceps assisting. If your forearms burn out first, check your grip pressure. Keep driving with your back and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Kicking your legs or swinging takes all the work away from your back.

name: assisted standing triceps extension (with towel)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a towel with both hands behind your head.
Keep your elbows close to your ears and your upper arms stationary.
Slowly extend your forearms upward, squeezing your triceps at the top.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the towel back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows locked in place and only move from the elbow down.
coaching_cues: Lock elbows tight by your ears.
Extend fully, squeeze at the top.
Lower slow, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the back of your arms working hard from elbow to shoulder. If you feel shoulder strain, bring your elbows slightly forward. Keep squeezing those triceps and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbows flare out shifts stress to your shoulders.

name: assisted triceps dip (kneeling)
instructions: Adjust the machine to your desired weight and height.
Kneel down on the pad facing the machine, with your hands gripping the handles.
Lower your body by bending your elbows, keeping your back straight and close to the machine.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows tight to your body and lower with control to feel it in those triceps.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay glued to your sides.
Lower until arms hit ninety degrees.
Press up strong, squeeze at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in the back of your upper arms right now. If your shoulders are doing the work, focus on keeping those elbows back. That tricep burn means you are doing it right, keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your elbows flare out wide, your shoulders take over and triceps get nothing.

name: assisted wide-grip chest dip (kneeling)
instructions: Adjust the machine to your desired height and secure your knees on the pad.
Grasp the handles with a wide grip and keep your elbows slightly bent.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Push yourself back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lean your torso slightly forward and keep those elbows wide to target your chest.
coaching_cues: Lean forward, chest leads the way.
Elbows point out to the sides.
Squeeze your chest as you press.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your chest, especially toward the outer edges. If your shoulders or triceps are burning more, lean forward a bit more. Your chest is working hard, keep that wide position and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Staying too upright turns this into a tricep exercise and your chest misses out.

name: astride jumps (male)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and lower your body into a squat position.
Jump explosively upwards, extending your legs and arms.
While in the air, spread your legs apart and bring your arms out to the sides.
Land softly with your feet shoulder-width apart, bending your knees to absorb the impact.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode up from your squat and land soft with bent knees to protect your joints.
coaching_cues: Sink low, then explode up.
Arms drive high for momentum.
Land soft, knees stay bent.
coach_guide.feel: Your heart rate should be climbing and your thighs should be burning. If your knees hurt on landing, focus on bending them more. You are building power and endurance, keep those explosive jumps coming.
coach_guide.warning: Landing with straight locked knees puts dangerous stress on your joints.

name: back and forth step
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step forward with your right foot, bending your knee and lowering your body into a lunge position.
Push off with your right foot and step back to the starting position.
Repeat the movement with your left foot, alternating legs with each step.
Continue stepping back and forth, maintaining a steady pace.
Repeat for the desired duration or number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stay light on your feet and keep your chest up as you step forward and back.
coaching_cues: Quick feet, light on the ground.
Chest stays tall and proud.
Push back strong to starting position.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart pumping and your legs warming up with each step. If you feel off balance, shorten your step slightly. This is building your cardio engine, keep that rhythm going.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your front knee cave inward puts stress on your knee joint.

name: back extension on exercise ball
instructions: Place the stability ball on the ground and lie face down on top of it, with your hips resting on the ball and your feet against a wall or other stable surface.
Position your hands behind your head or crossed over your chest.
Engage your core and slowly lift your upper body off the ball, extending your back until your body forms a straight line from your head to your heels.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Squeeze your glutes and lift with your lower back, not momentum, to build spine strength.
coaching_cues: Glutes squeeze tight before lifting.
Lift slow, control the movement.
Hold at the top for one second.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire lower back working along with your glutes. If you feel it in your neck, you are lifting too high. Your spine is getting stronger with every rep, keep that controlled movement going.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum to swing up takes the work away from your back muscles.

name: back lever
instructions: Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back.
Bend your knees and tuck them towards your chest.
Slowly lift your legs up, keeping them straight, until your body is parallel to the ground.
Hold this position for a few seconds, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your shoulder blades down hard and keep your entire body tight as one unit.
coaching_cues: Shoulders down, chest stays open.
Core locked in super tight.
Body moves as one solid piece.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire upper back and lats working intensely along with your core. If your lower back is straining, keep your core tighter. This is serious strength work, you are building something amazing.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your shoulders creep up toward your ears puts dangerous stress on the joint.

name: back pec stretch
instructions: Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms straight out in front of you, parallel to the ground.
Cross your arms in front of your body, with your right arm over your left arm.
Interlock your fingers and rotate your palms away from your body.
Slowly raise your arms up and away from your body, feeling a stretch in your back and chest.
Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, then release.
Repeat on the opposite side.

coach_guide.intro: Cross your arms and round your upper back to feel a deep stretch through your lats.
coaching_cues: Arms cross, reach for opposite shoulders.
Round your upper back forward.
Breathe deep into the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a gentle stretch spreading across your upper back and into your lats. If you feel sharp pain, ease off the stretch slightly. Let that tension release with each breath, you are doing great.
coach_guide.warning: Holding your breath prevents your muscles from fully releasing and relaxing.

name: backward jump
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees slightly and jump backwards, pushing off with both feet.
Land softly on the balls of your feet, bending your knees to absorb the impact.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Look back quickly before jumping and land softly with your knees bent to absorb impact.
coaching_cues: Quick glance back before you jump.
Drive arms back for power.
Land soft on balls of feet.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads firing hard and your heart rate climbing. If your knees feel stressed, focus on bending deeper when you land. You are building explosive power, keep those jumps strong.
coach_guide.warning: Jumping blind without checking behind you risks collision or injury.

name: balance board
instructions: Place the balance board on a flat surface.
Step onto the balance board with one foot, ensuring it is centered.
Slowly shift your weight onto the foot on the balance board, keeping your core engaged.
Maintain your balance and stability as you hold the position for a desired amount of time.
Repeat the exercise with the other foot.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your core tight and find a focal point ahead to help you balance steadily.
coaching_cues: Eyes fixed on one spot ahead.
Core stays engaged and tight.
Small adjustments, stay in control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads and all those small stabilizer muscles working to keep you balanced. If you are wobbling too much, relax your grip and trust your legs. Every second on that board makes you stronger, keep finding your center.
coach_guide.warning: Looking down at your feet disrupts your balance and makes stabilizing harder.

name: band alternating biceps curl
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the band with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and slowly curl one arm up towards your shoulder, squeezing your biceps at the top.
Lower the arm back down to the starting position and repeat with the other arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows pinned to your ribs and squeeze hard at the top of each curl.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay glued to ribs.
Squeeze bicep hard at the top.
Control the band on the way down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong pump building in your biceps with each rep. If your shoulders are swinging, go lighter on the tension. That burn means growth, keep alternating and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your body to curl steals the work from your biceps.

name: band alternating v-up
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs straight and your arms extended overhead, holding the band.
Engage your abs and lift your legs and upper body off the ground simultaneously, reaching your hands towards your toes.
As you lower your legs and upper body back down, switch the position of your legs, crossing one over the other.
Repeat the movement, alternating the position of your legs with each repetition.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press your lower back into the floor and reach high to really fire up those abs.
coaching_cues: Lower back stays pressed down.
Reach hands toward your toes.
Control the lowering, do not drop.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core burning, especially your lower abs. If your lower back lifts off the floor, do not go as low with your legs. Your abs are on fire and getting stronger, push through it.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back off the floor strains your spine and cheats your abs.

name: band assisted pull-up
instructions: Attach the band to a pull-up bar or sturdy anchor point.
Step onto the band and grip the bar with your palms facing away from you, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Hang with your arms fully extended, keeping your core engaged and your shoulders down and back.
Pull your body up towards the bar by squeezing your shoulder blades together and driving your elbows down towards your hips.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar, then slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive your elbows down toward your hips and pull your chest to the bar with control.
coaching_cues: Elbows drive down toward hips.
Chest reaches up to the bar.
Lower with control, no dropping.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats doing the heavy lifting, not just your arms. If your biceps are burning first, focus more on driving those elbows down. You are building serious back strength, keep pulling.
coach_guide.warning: Kipping or swinging takes the muscle-building tension out of the movement.

name: band assisted wheel rollerout
instructions: Kneel on the floor and hold the handles of the band with both hands, palms facing down.
Place the band on the ground in front of you and position your hands shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and slowly roll the wheel forward, extending your body as far as you can while maintaining control.
Pause for a moment at the furthest point, then slowly roll the wheel back towards your knees to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Brace your core hard before rolling out and only go as far as you can control.
coaching_cues: Core braced tight before you move.
Roll out slow and controlled.
Pull back with your abs working.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core working hard, especially your lower abs. If your lower back starts to dip, do not roll out as far. Your abs are being challenged like never before, keep that core tight and roll on.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling out too far and letting your hips sag puts serious strain on your lower back.

name: band bench press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the band handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms fully, pushing the bands away from your chest.
Slowly lower the bands back down to your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press straight up and squeeze your chest hard at the top of every rep.
coaching_cues: Press straight up, not forward.
Squeeze chest hard at the top.
Lower with control, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working through the full range of motion. If your shoulders are taking over, bring your grip in slightly narrower. Your chest is filling with blood and getting stronger, keep pressing.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbows out at ninety degrees puts unnecessary stress on your shoulders.

name: band bent-over hip extension
instructions: Attach the band to a sturdy anchor point at ankle height.
Stand facing away from the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step back to create tension in the band, keeping your knees slightly bent.
Hinge at the hips and lean forward, maintaining a neutral spine.
Extend your right leg straight back, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Lower your right leg back down and repeat with the left leg.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Squeeze your glute before you extend and keep your back flat throughout the movement.
coaching_cues: Squeeze glute, then extend back.
Keep your back flat and stable.
Hold at the top for one count.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glute doing all the work on the leg that is extending. If you feel it in your lower back, reset your hinge position. Your glutes are firing perfectly, keep that squeeze going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back takes the work out of your glutes and strains your spine.

name: band bicycle crunch
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your hands behind your head and your knees bent.
Lift your feet off the ground and bring your right knee towards your chest while simultaneously twisting your torso to bring your left elbow towards your right knee.
Straighten your right leg while bringing your left knee towards your chest and twisting your torso to bring your right elbow towards your left knee.
Continue alternating the twisting motion, as if you are pedaling a bicycle, while keeping your core engaged throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Focus on the twist, not the speed, and keep your lower back pressed into the floor.
coaching_cues: Elbow reaches past the opposite knee.
Lower back stays glued to floor.
Slow pedal, do not rush it.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn across your entire midsection, especially on the sides when you twist. If your neck is straining, look at one spot on the ceiling and let your abs do the work. That burn means it is working, keep pedaling.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your lower back arches off the floor, you have lost core tension.

name: band close-grip pulldown
instructions: Attach the band to a high anchor point, such as a pull-up bar or sturdy beam.
Stand facing the anchor point and grab the band with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Step back to create tension in the band, keeping your feet hip-width apart.
Engage your core and keep your back straight as you pull the band down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the band back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive your elbows down and back like you are putting them in your back pockets.
coaching_cues: Chest stays proud, shoulders down.
Squeeze your lats at the bottom.
Control the band on the way up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats working hard, that wide muscle right under your armpits and down your sides. If you feel it mostly in your arms, focus on pulling with your elbows instead. Your back is firing, keep that squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: If your shoulders creep up toward your ears, you are using traps instead of lats.

name: band close-grip push-up
instructions: Place a band around your upper arms, just above the elbows.
Assume a push-up position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Bend your elbows and lower your chest towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows tight to your ribs and focus on squeezing at the top.
coaching_cues: Elbows brush against your sides.
Body stays in one straight line.
Push through your palms, squeeze triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in the back of your upper arms, right behind where your biceps are. If you feel it in your chest or shoulders, bring those elbows in tighter. That tricep burn is exactly what we want, keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your elbows flare out wide, your chest takes over the movement.

name: band concentration curl
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your legs spread apart and your feet flat on the ground.
Hold one end of the band in your hand and step on the other end with your foot on the same side.
Lean forward slightly and rest your elbow on the inside of your thigh, just above the knee.
With your palm facing up, slowly curl your hand towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your hand back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Pin your elbow firmly against your inner thigh and let only your forearm move.
coaching_cues: Elbow stays locked on thigh.
Squeeze hard at the top.
Lower slow, fight the band.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your bicep peak burning right in the middle of your upper arm. If your shoulder is moving or your elbow lifts off your thigh, reset your position. Isolate that bicep and keep squeezing.
coach_guide.warning: If your elbow drifts away from your thigh, you are using momentum instead of muscle.

name: band fixed back close grip pulldown
instructions: Attach the band to a fixed point above you, such as a pull-up bar or sturdy beam.
Sit down on a bench or chair facing the band, with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent.
Grasp the band with a close grip, palms facing towards you.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Pull the band down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, feeling the contraction in your lats.
Slowly release the band back to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your torso completely still and let your lats do all the pulling.
coaching_cues: Pull elbows down toward hips.
Squeeze shoulder blades together.
Hold the squeeze for one count.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats firing hard down the sides of your back with each rep. If your biceps are burning more than your back, think about pulling with your elbows. Your lats are working, keep that back engaged.
coach_guide.warning: If you lean back and rock your torso, you lose the lat focus entirely.

name: band fixed back underhand pulldown
instructions: Attach the band to a sturdy anchor point above your head.
Stand facing the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the band with an underhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step back to create tension in the band, keeping your arms fully extended.
Engage your core and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Pull the band down towards your chest, leading with your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, feeling the contraction in your lats.
Slowly release the tension in the band and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Think about bending the bar toward your chest while keeping your elbows forward.
coaching_cues: Pull elbows down and forward.
Chest stays lifted and proud.
Squeeze lats hard at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction through your lats and a solid pump in your biceps as a bonus. If you only feel your arms, focus on driving those elbows down first. Great back work, keep pulling strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbows drift behind you shifts the work away from your lats.

name: band front lateral raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the band in front of your thighs with your palms facing down.
Keep your arms straight and lift the band up in front of you until your arms are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the band back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lead with your pinkies slightly higher and keep your shoulders away from your ears.
coaching_cues: Arms stay straight but not locked.
Lift to shoulder height only.
Lower with control, no dropping.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the front and sides of your shoulders burning equally right now. If your traps are taking over and your neck feels tight, drop the resistance. That shoulder burn is perfect, keep lifting.
coach_guide.warning: Raising your arms above shoulder height shifts tension to your traps.

name: band front raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the band in front of your thighs with your palms facing down.
Keep your arms straight and slowly raise them forward until they are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your arms straight and think about pushing the band away as you lift.
coaching_cues: Shoulders stay down and back.
Stop when arms reach shoulder height.
Control it on the way down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the front of your shoulders working hard with each rep you complete. If your lower back is arching or your traps are burning, brace your core tighter. Front delts are on fire, keep that form clean.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the band up with momentum takes the tension off your shoulders.

name: band hip lift
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place a resistance band just above your knees.
Engage your glutes and core muscles.
Press your heels into the ground and lift your hips off the floor, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Push your knees out against the band and squeeze your glutes like you are cracking a walnut.
coaching_cues: Drive through your heels hard.
Push knees out against band.
Squeeze glutes at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes burning deep in your backside, not your lower back or hamstrings. If your lower back is doing the work, tuck your pelvis under more at the top. Those glutes are firing, hold that squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: If your lower back arches excessively at the top, you have gone too high.

name: band horizontal pallof press
instructions: Attach the band to a sturdy anchor point at waist height.
Stand perpendicular to the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the band handle with both hands and step away from the anchor point to create tension in the band.
Bring your hands to your chest, keeping your elbows bent and close to your body.
Engage your core and maintain a stable stance.
Extend your arms straight out in front of you, pushing the band away from your body.
Hold the extended position for a few seconds, focusing on maintaining tension in your core.
Slowly bring your hands back to your chest, resisting the pull of the band.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Brace your core like someone is about to push you and do not let the band rotate you.
coaching_cues: Press straight out from chest.
Do not let hips or shoulders rotate.
Pull back slow with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core working hard to resist the rotation, especially your obliques. If you feel it in your arms or shoulders, you are pushing too fast. Your core is resisting perfectly, stay strong.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your hips or shoulders twist toward the anchor, your core has lost.

name: band jack knife sit-up
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs straight and your arms extended overhead, holding the band.
Engage your abs and lift your legs and upper body simultaneously, bringing your hands towards your feet.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs and upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Think about touching your hands to your toes by folding at the waist like a jackknife.
coaching_cues: Legs and arms meet in middle.
Lower back stays on the floor.
Lower both with control together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire abdominal wall burning from top to bottom right now. If your lower back lifts off the ground, do not go as low on the descent. That full ab burn is exactly right, keep folding.
coach_guide.warning: If your lower back arches off the floor, you have lost core control.

name: band kneeling one arm pulldown
instructions: Attach the band to a sturdy anchor point above your head.
Kneel down and hold the band with one hand, palm facing down.
Extend your arm fully overhead, keeping your elbow slightly bent.
Engage your lat muscles and pull the band down towards your side, bringing your elbow towards your ribcage.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Drive your elbow down toward your hip and feel your lat squeeze at the bottom.
coaching_cues: Elbow drives down to hip.
Squeeze your lat at bottom.
Control the band going up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel one side of your back working intensely with each rep you perform. If your bicep is doing all the work, think elbow first, not hand. One lat at a time, keep that squeeze going.
coach_guide.warning: If you rotate your torso to pull harder, you lose the single arm focus.

name: band kneeling twisting crunch
instructions: Attach the band to a sturdy anchor point at waist height.
Kneel down facing away from the anchor point and hold the band with both hands, keeping your elbows bent and close to your sides.
Engage your abs and slowly twist your torso to one side, bringing your hands towards your opposite hip.
Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Think about driving your elbow toward the opposite hip while keeping your hips square.
coaching_cues: Twist from ribcage, not hips.
Elbow drives toward opposite hip.
Control the return, no snapping.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques burning hard on the sides of your waist with each twist. If your hips are rotating with you, anchor them down and twist only your upper body. Those obliques are on fire, keep twisting.
coach_guide.warning: If your hips rotate with your torso, your obliques are not doing the work.

name: band lying hip internal rotation
instructions: Lie on your back with your legs straight and a resistance band looped around your feet.
Bend your knees and bring them towards your chest, keeping your feet together.
Slowly rotate your knees outwards, away from each other, while keeping your feet together.
Pause for a moment at the end of the rotation, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep the movement small and controlled, focusing on the rotation happening at your hip joint.
coaching_cues: Feet stay together throughout.
Knees open slowly outward.
Control the return, squeeze glutes.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the deep muscles on the outside of your hips working with each rotation. If you feel it in your knees or lower back, make the movement smaller. Those deep hip muscles are activating, keep rotating.
coach_guide.warning: Moving too fast or too big takes the focus away from the hip rotators.

name: band lying straight leg raise
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs straight and your feet together.
Place the band around the arches of your feet and hold the ends of the band with your hands.
Engaging your abs, lift both legs off the ground until they are perpendicular to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press your lower back into the floor and lift your legs using only your abs.
coaching_cues: Lower back stays glued down.
Legs stay straight throughout.
Lower slow, stop before back arches.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs burning intensely below your belly button right now. If your lower back pops off the floor, do not lower your legs as far. That low ab burn is exactly what we want, keep lifting.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your lower back arches off the floor, your abs have stopped working.

name: band one arm overhead biceps curl
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place one end of the band under your foot.
Hold the other end of the band with your arm fully extended overhead, palm facing forward.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, curl your forearm towards your shoulder, squeezing your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your forearm back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your upper arm locked in place and only move from the elbow down.
coaching_cues: Upper arm stays completely still.
Squeeze hard at the bottom.
Control the stretch overhead.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense squeeze right in the peak of your bicep. If your shoulder is doing the work, anchor that elbow tighter. That burn means growth, keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your elbow drifts forward, you lose all the tension on the bicep.

name: band one arm single leg split squat
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and place a resistance band around your ankles.
Extend one leg forward and rest the top of your foot on a bench or step behind you.
Hold onto a support with one hand for balance.
Bend your standing leg and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your knee in line with your toes.
Push through your heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

coach_guide.intro: Drive through your front heel and keep your torso upright throughout the movement.
coaching_cues: Front heel stays planted down.
Chest proud, core braced tight.
Knee tracks over your toes.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your front quad burning deeply and your glute working hard to stabilize. If your knee caves inward, push it out over your pinky toe. Stay strong through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning forward shifts the load off your quads and onto your lower back.

name: band one arm standing low row
instructions: Attach the band to a stable anchor point at waist height.
Stand facing the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the band with one hand, palm facing inward, and step back to create tension in the band.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Pull the band towards your waist, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the band back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your elbow back like starting a lawnmower and squeeze your shoulder blade in.
coaching_cues: Elbow drives straight back.
Squeeze shoulder blade to spine.
Slow release, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your mid-back right along your spine. If it is all in your arm, focus on the shoulder blade squeeze first. Keep that back firing strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating your torso turns this into a twist instead of a row.

name: band one arm twisting chest press
instructions: Attach the band to a sturdy anchor point at chest height.
Stand with your side facing the anchor point and grab the band with one hand.
Step away from the anchor point to create tension in the band.
Position your feet shoulder-width apart and slightly bend your knees.
Bring your hand holding the band across your body, towards the opposite shoulder.
While maintaining tension in the band, push your hand forward and away from your body, extending your arm.
Slowly return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch sides and repeat the exercise with the other hand.

coach_guide.intro: Punch across your body while rotating through your core for maximum chest engagement.
coaching_cues: Rotate from your hips up.
Punch and squeeze at finish.
Control the return slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest stretching on the way back and squeezing hard as you press. If your shoulder is taking over, keep that elbow slightly lower. Power through each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your arm drift too high puts dangerous stress on your shoulder joint.

name: band one arm twisting seated row
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold the band with one hand and extend your arm fully in front of you.
Keeping your back straight, pull the band towards your body by bending your elbow and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
At the same time, twist your torso towards the side of the pulling arm.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly release the tension in the band and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Twist as you pull and squeeze your shoulder blade back to hit your upper back fully.
coaching_cues: Pull elbow back and rotate.
Shoulder blade squeezes to spine.
Stay tall, do not round forward.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats and mid-back working together with each twist. If you only feel your arm, slow down and focus on the squeeze. Great work, finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back takes tension away from the target muscles completely.

name: band pull through
instructions: Attach a resistance band to a sturdy anchor point at ground level.
Stand facing away from the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step forward to create tension in the band, keeping your knees slightly bent.
Hinge at the hips and push your glutes back, maintaining a slight bend in your knees.
Lower your torso until it is parallel to the ground, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings.
Drive your hips forward and squeeze your glutes to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge at your hips and squeeze your glutes hard to drive your hips forward.
coaching_cues: Push hips back, feel hamstrings.
Squeeze glutes to stand tall.
Keep spine neutral throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings at the bottom and a powerful glute squeeze at the top. If your lower back is working, hinge deeper at the hips. Drive those hips and keep going.
coach_guide.warning: Using your lower back instead of your glutes defeats the entire purpose.

name: band push sit-up
instructions: Attach the band securely to a stable anchor point.
Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Hold the band with both hands and extend your arms straight up towards the ceiling.
Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Push the band forward as you sit up to keep constant tension on your abs.
coaching_cues: Arms punch forward on rise.
Exhale and crunch hard up.
Lower with control, stay tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core burning especially the upper abs as you push forward. If your hip flexors are cramping, focus on the crunch not the sit. Stay with it, you are getting stronger.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with your arms instead of pushing makes your abs work less.

name: band reverse fly
instructions: Attach the band to a stationary object at chest height.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the band with both hands in front of you.
Keep your arms straight and lift them out to the sides until they are parallel to the ground.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lead with your elbows and squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top.
coaching_cues: Slight bend in elbows only.
Squeeze shoulder blades together.
Control back to start position.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn in your rear delts and between your shoulder blades. If your traps are taking over, lower your shoulders away from your ears. Keep squeezing through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your elbows too much turns this into a rowing movement.

name: band reverse wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground.
Hold the band with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and wrap it around your fingers.
Rest your forearms on your thighs, with your wrists hanging off the edge.
Slowly curl your wrists upward, squeezing your forearms.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your wrists back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your forearms flat on your thighs and only move at the wrist.
coaching_cues: Lift knuckles toward ceiling.
Squeeze forearms at the top.
Lower slow, full range down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn on top of your forearms near your elbows. If your grip is failing first, loosen up slightly and focus on the wrist movement. Keep those forearms burning.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your forearms off your thighs takes tension away from the target.

name: band seated hip internal rotation
instructions: Sit on a chair or bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Place a resistance band around your thighs, just above your knees.
Keep your knees bent at a 90-degree angle and your feet shoulder-width apart.
Engage your glutes and slowly rotate your knees outward, pushing against the resistance of the band.
Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your knees still and rotate only from the hip socket.
coaching_cues: Feet move in, knees stay.
Squeeze glutes throughout movement.
Control the rotation both ways.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your glutes and around your hip joint. If you feel it in your knees, keep them more stable and move slower. This is building real hip strength.
coach_guide.warning: Moving your knees instead of your feet means your hips are not doing the work.

name: band seated twist
instructions: Sit on the ground with your legs extended in front of you and your back straight.
Wrap the band around your waist and hold the ends with both hands.
Engage your abs and slowly twist your torso to one side, keeping your back straight and your feet on the ground.
Pause for a moment at the end of the twist, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat the twist to the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate from your ribcage while keeping your hips completely still.
coaching_cues: Twist slow, lead with chest.
Keep hips locked facing forward.
Exhale as you rotate harder.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques working hard on each side as you twist. If your hips are moving, anchor your legs down harder. Stay controlled and keep that core burning.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating from your hips instead of your core makes this ineffective.

name: band shoulder press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place the band under your feet.
Hold the band with your palms facing forward and raise your hands to shoulder height, elbows bent.
Press the band overhead, fully extending your arms.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the band back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press straight up and fully extend your arms without letting your elbows flare out.
coaching_cues: Press up in straight line.
Lock out arms at top.
Lower slow to shoulder height.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders burning especially the front and side delts. If your lower back is arching, tighten your core and keep ribs down. Push through that shoulder burn.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back turns this into an incline press and strains your spine.

name: band shrug
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place the band under your feet, holding the ends with your hands.
Keep your arms straight and relaxed, and let the band hang in front of your thighs.
Engage your traps by shrugging your shoulders upward, lifting the band as high as possible.
Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Shrug straight up toward your ears and squeeze at the top.
coaching_cues: Shoulders go straight up.
Squeeze and hold one second.
Lower slow, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your traps burning from your neck down toward your shoulder blades. If you feel it in your arms, relax them and just lift with your shoulders. Keep building those traps.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling your shoulders forward or back puts unnecessary stress on the joint.

name: band side triceps extension
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the band with both hands, palms facing down.
Extend your arms straight out to the sides, keeping them parallel to the ground.
Slowly bend your elbows and bring your hands towards your shoulders, keeping your upper arms still.
Pause for a moment, then slowly extend your arms back out to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your upper arms still at your sides and only extend from the elbow.
coaching_cues: Elbows pinned at your sides.
Extend arms straight out fully.
Squeeze triceps hard at end.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in the back of your arms near your elbows. If your shoulders are moving, lock those elbows tighter to your body. Finish strong through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbows drift makes your shoulders take over the movement.

name: band single leg calf raise
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and place the band around the ball of your left foot.
Hold onto a stable object for balance if needed.
Slowly raise your left heel off the ground, lifting your body weight onto the ball of your foot.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your left heel back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the right leg.

coach_guide.intro: Drive through the ball of your foot and squeeze your calf hard at the top.
coaching_cues: Rise high onto your toes.
Squeeze and pause at top.
Lower slow, stretch at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your calf muscle burning from your heel up to your knee. If you are losing balance, hold something steady and focus on the squeeze. Keep those calves working strong.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing through reps instead of controlling them limits your calf development.

name: band single leg reverse calf raise
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and place the band around the ball of your foot.
Hold onto a stable object for balance.
Slowly raise your heel off the ground, lifting your body weight onto the ball of your foot.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg.

coach_guide.intro: Balance is key here, so squeeze your standing glute and focus on a slow, controlled lift.
coaching_cues: Press through the ball of your foot.
Squeeze at the top for two seconds.
Lower with control, do not drop.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your calf muscle, right below the back of your knee. If you feel wobbly or your ankle is rolling, hold onto something sturdier. Stay focused and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the lowering phase will rob you of half the benefit.

name: band single leg split squat
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and place a resistance band around your ankles.
Take a big step forward with your right foot and a smaller step back with your left foot.
Bend your knees and lower your body until your right thigh is parallel to the ground, keeping your left knee slightly above the ground.
Push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your torso upright and drive through your front heel to target the quads.
coaching_cues: Front knee tracks over your toes.
Drop your back knee straight down.
Push up through your front heel.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in the front of your thigh and a stretch in your back hip. If your front knee caves inward, reset your stance. Push through that burn, you are building strength.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning forward shifts the work away from your quads and into your lower back.

name: band squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, with the band placed just above your knees.
Keeping your chest up and core engaged, push your hips back and bend your knees to lower into a squat position.
Make sure your knees are tracking over your toes and your weight is in your heels.
