Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Push your knees out against the band throughout the entire movement to fire up your glutes.
coaching_cues: Knees press out against the band.
Sit back like into a chair.
Drive up through your heels.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong burn on the sides and back of your hips. If the burn is only in your thighs, push your knees out harder. Keep that tension and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your knees collapse inward, your glutes stop working.

name: band squat row
instructions: Attach the band to a sturdy anchor point at waist height.
Stand facing the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the band handles with your palms facing each other and your arms extended in front of you.
Bend your knees and lower into a squat position, keeping your back straight and chest lifted.
From the squat position, pull the band handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sync your squat and row so you pull as you stand, creating one powerful movement.
coaching_cues: Squat down with arms extended forward.
Row and stand at the same time.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and upper back working together as a team. If your lower back is straining, check your hip hinge. You are building total body strength, keep it up.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with your arms before you stand turns this into an arm exercise.

name: band standing crunch
instructions: Attach the band to a sturdy anchor point at waist height.
Stand facing away from the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the band with both hands and bring it up to your chest, keeping your elbows bent and close to your body.
Engage your abs and slowly crunch forward, bringing your chest towards your knees.
Pause for a moment at the top of the crunch, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This is all about your abs pulling your ribs down, not bending at the hips.
coaching_cues: Exhale and crunch your ribs down.
Keep your hips completely still.
Squeeze your abs at the bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep contraction right through your midsection, like your abs are shortening. If you feel it in your hip flexors, you are bending at the waist instead of crunching. Stay locked in and feel that squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Bending forward at the hips takes your abs out of the equation.

name: band standing rear delt row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place the band under your feet.
Hold the band handles with your palms facing each other and your arms extended in front of you.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Pull the band towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lead with your elbows out wide and squeeze your rear delts at the top.
coaching_cues: Pull elbows out and back wide.
Squeeze shoulder blades together hard.
Control the band back slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in the back of your shoulders and between your shoulder blades. If you feel it mostly in your biceps, widen your elbow angle. Light up those rear delts and keep pulling.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with your elbows tucked in shifts all the work to your biceps.

name: band standing twisting crunch
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place the band around your upper back, crossing it in front of your chest.
Hold the ends of the band with your hands, keeping your elbows bent and close to your sides.
Engage your abs and twist your torso to one side, bringing your elbow towards the opposite knee.
Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate from your ribcage, not your hips, and keep your lower body completely still.
coaching_cues: Twist from your ribs, not hips.
Elbow drives toward opposite knee.
Control back to center slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong squeeze on the side of your abs with each twist. If your hips are rotating, you are losing the core benefit. Stay tight and twist with purpose.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating your hips instead of your ribs makes this a hip exercise.

name: band step-up
instructions: Place a band around your thighs, just above your knees.
Stand facing a step or platform with your feet hip-width apart.
Step up onto the platform with your right foot, pushing through your heel.
Extend your left leg behind you, keeping it straight.
Lower your left foot back down to the ground.
Repeat with your left foot stepping up onto the platform.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press through your heel on the step and keep your knee pushing out against the band.
coaching_cues: Drive up through your heel.
Keep your knee pressing outward.
Stand tall at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glute on the stepping leg fire hard as you rise. If you feel it mostly in your quad, lean your chest slightly forward. Every step up is building a stronger foundation.
coach_guide.warning: Pushing off your back foot steals the work from your glute.

name: band stiff leg deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place the band around your ankles.
Hold the band with both hands in front of your thighs, palms facing your body.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, hinge at the hips and slowly lower your upper body towards the ground.
As you lower, push your hips back and allow your knees to bend slightly.
Lower the band towards the ground, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then engage your glutes and hamstrings to lift your upper body back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your back flat and hinge from your hips to load your hamstrings and glutes.
coaching_cues: Push your hips back behind you.
Keep your back flat, not rounded.
Feel the stretch in your hamstrings.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch down the back of your thighs and tension in your glutes. If your lower back rounds or aches, you have gone too far. Respect the stretch and power back up.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back shifts dangerous stress onto your spine.

name: band straight back stiff leg deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place the band around your upper legs.
Hold the band with both hands in front of your thighs, palms facing your body.
Keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent, hinge at the hips and lower the band towards the ground.
Feel the stretch in your hamstrings as you lower the band.
Engage your glutes and hamstrings to lift your body back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Maintain a completely straight back as you hinge, feeling the load through your entire posterior chain.
coaching_cues: Hinge hips back with straight spine.
Slight knee bend, not a squat.
Squeeze glutes to stand tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel tension from your hamstrings up through your glutes and lower back as one connected unit. If your back starts to round, reduce your range. Keep that spine proud and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Any rounding in your spine means you have hinged too deep for today.

name: band straight leg deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place the band around your feet.
Hold the band with both hands, palms facing your body, and keep your arms straight.
Engage your core and maintain a slight bend in your knees.
Slowly hinge forward at your hips, keeping your back straight and chest lifted.
Lower the band towards the ground while keeping your legs straight.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then squeeze your glutes and hamstrings to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge from your hips while keeping your spine long and core braced.
coaching_cues: Push your hips back like closing a door.
Keep the band tension constant throughout.
Squeeze your glutes to stand tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings on the way down and your lower back working to stay neutral. If you feel rounding in your spine, reset your posture. Stay long and strong.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your back rounds, you have shifted the load away from where it belongs.

name: band twisting overhead press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place the band under your feet.
Hold the band handles at shoulder height with your palms facing forward.
Engage your core and press the band overhead, fully extending your arms.
As you press, twist your torso to one side, keeping your hips stable.
Pause for a moment at the top, then return to the starting position.
Repeat the press and twist on the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate through your core as you press, keeping your base stable and strong.
coaching_cues: Twist from your waist, not your feet.
Press and rotate as one smooth motion.
Lock out your arm at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders working hard at the top and your obliques firing during the twist. If your lower back arches, brace harder. Own that rotation.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your feet spin takes the work away from your core.

name: band two legs calf raise - (band under both legs) v. 2
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place a resistance band under both feet.
Hold the ends of the band with your hands for stability.
Raise your heels off the ground as high as possible, using your calves.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rise as high as possible on your toes and pause at the top for maximum contraction.
coaching_cues: Drive through the balls of your feet.
Pause and squeeze hard at the top.
Lower slowly with full control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in both calves, especially at the peak. If you feel it in your ankles or lose balance, slow down. Push through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing through reps without a pause means your calves never fully contract.

name: band underhand pulldown
instructions: Attach the band to a high anchor point, such as a pull-up bar or sturdy beam.
Stand facing the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the band with an underhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms fully overhead, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Engage your lats and pull the band down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull with your elbows driving down and back, squeezing your lats at the bottom.
coaching_cues: Chest up, shoulders down and back.
Elbows drive toward your hips.
Squeeze your lats at the bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats working hard under your armpits and between your shoulder blades. If your biceps burn out first, focus on the elbow path. Let your back do the work.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with your hands instead of your elbows turns this into an arm exercise.

name: band v-up
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs straight and your arms extended overhead, holding the band.
Engaging your abs, lift your legs and upper body off the ground simultaneously, reaching your hands towards your toes.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs and upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lift your legs and torso together, meeting in the middle with control.
coaching_cues: Reach your hands toward your toes.
Keep your legs straight and strong.
Lower with control, do not crash down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire abdominal wall working intensely, especially at the top. If your lower back lifts off painfully, reduce your range. Meet in the middle and own it.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum to swing up means your abs are not doing the work.

name: band vertical pallof press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and wrap the band around a sturdy object at chest height.
Hold the band with both hands and step away from the anchor point, creating tension in the band.
Position yourself perpendicular to the anchor point, with your side facing the band.
Extend your arms straight out in front of you, keeping your hands at chest height.
Engage your core and press the band away from your chest, fully extending your arms.
Hold the position for a few seconds, then slowly bring the band back towards your chest.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Press the band straight out and resist the rotation pulling you sideways.
coaching_cues: Brace your core like taking a punch.
Press straight out from your chest.
Do not let your hips or shoulders twist.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your deep core and obliques fighting to keep you square. If your body rotates toward the anchor, step closer to reduce tension. Stay locked in.
coach_guide.warning: Allowing any rotation means the band is winning and your core is not.

name: band wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground.
Hold the band with both hands, palms facing up, and rest your forearms on your thighs.
Slowly curl your wrists upward, squeezing your forearms.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your wrists back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Isolate your wrists completely by keeping your forearms glued to your thighs.
coaching_cues: Curl only at the wrist joint.
Squeeze hard at the top position.
Lower slowly to feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a burning pump in your inner forearms near your wrists. If your elbows lift or arms move, reset your position. Stay strict for maximum results.
coach_guide.warning: Moving your forearms off your thighs brings other muscles into play.

name: band y-raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the band in front of your thighs with your palms facing inwards.
Keep your arms straight and lift them up and out to the sides, forming a 'Y' shape with your body.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lead with your thumbs and keep your arms at a forty-five degree angle from your body.
coaching_cues: Thumbs point to the ceiling.
Lift into a wide Y shape.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn on the back and top of your shoulders. If you feel it in your traps or neck, lower the tension. Build those rear delts strong.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting straight to the sides turns this into a lateral raise and misses the target.

name: barbell alternate biceps curl
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Keep your upper arms stationary and exhale as you curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the barbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the barbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale as you slowly begin to lower the barbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, alternating arms.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your upper arms locked at your sides and curl one arm at a time with full control.
coaching_cues: Pin your elbows to your ribs.
Squeeze your bicep hard at the top.
Lower slowly and switch arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in the peak of your bicep on every rep. If your elbows drift forward, the weight is too heavy. Keep it strict and feel the pump.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your body or letting elbows move forward steals tension from your biceps.

name: barbell bench front squat
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell resting on your upper chest, just below your collarbone.
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, keeping your elbows up and your upper arms parallel to the ground.
Lower your body down into a squat position by bending at the knees and hips, keeping your back straight and your chest up.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the squat, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows high and chest proud as you sit down between your heels.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay up, chest stays tall.
Sit down between your legs.
Drive through your whole foot to stand.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads burning intensely, especially in the bottom position. If you tip forward or your elbows drop, the weight may be too heavy. Stay upright and strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbows drop pulls your chest forward and loads your lower back.

name: barbell bench press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press from your chest, not your shoulders, and keep those elbows at a forty-five degree angle.
coaching_cues: Lower to your chest, not your neck.
Drive through your chest muscles.
Keep those elbows at forty-five degrees.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your chest muscles, especially the middle part. If it's all in your shoulders, bring your elbows in closer to your body. Power through with that chest burn.
coach_guide.warning: Flare your elbows out wide and you're setting up for shoulder problems.

name: barbell bench squat
instructions: Set up a barbell on a squat rack at chest height.
Stand facing away from the rack, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and step back, ensuring your feet are still shoulder-width apart.
Lower your body down into a squat, keeping your knees in line with your toes.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit back into your hips and drive through your heels to protect your knees.
coaching_cues: Chest up, core braced tight.
Sit back like finding a chair.
Drive through heels to stand.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn across the front of your thighs and into your glutes. If your lower back is doing the work, reset your posture and brace harder. Stay strong and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your heels lift off the floor, you have lost control of the movement.

name: barbell bent arm pullover
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet on the floor.
Hold a barbell with a shoulder-width grip and extend your arms straight above your chest.
Lower the barbell behind your head while keeping your arms slightly bent.
Pause for a moment, then raise the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows bent and drive the stretch through your lats, not your shoulders.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay bent throughout.
Stretch deep behind your head.
Pull with your lats, not arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a powerful stretch and contraction along the sides of your back under your armpits. If your shoulders are aching or triceps burning, lighten the weight. Feel those lats working and keep pulling.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your lower back arches off the bench, you have gone too far.

name: barbell bent over row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Bend forward at the hips while keeping your back straight and chest up.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Pull the barbell towards your lower chest by retracting your shoulder blades and squeezing your back muscles.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the bar to your lower ribs by driving your elbows back behind you.
coaching_cues: Elbows drive back behind your ribs.
Squeeze shoulder blades together hard.
Bar touches your lower chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this across your entire upper back, especially between your shoulder blades. If it's all in your arms, focus on that elbow drive. Power through with that back squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Keep that chest up or you'll lose your back position and power.

name: barbell biceps curl (with arm blaster)
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Place your upper arms against the arm blaster, keeping your elbows close to your torso.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the barbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let the arm blaster lock you in and focus purely on squeezing your biceps.
coaching_cues: Upper arms pressed into blaster.
Squeeze hard at the top.
Lower slow, stay controlled.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn right in the belly of your biceps with nowhere to hide. If your shoulders are creeping forward or back is swaying, check your setup. This is pure bicep work so embrace the burn.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your elbows drift forward off the blaster, you are cheating the rep.

name: barbell clean and press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell on the floor in front of you.
Bend your knees and hinge at the hips to lower down and grip the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Drive through your heels and extend your hips and knees to lift the barbell off the floor, keeping it close to your body.
As the barbell reaches your thighs, explosively extend your hips, shrug your shoulders, and pull the barbell up towards your chest.
As the barbell reaches chest height, quickly drop under it and catch it at shoulder level, with your elbows pointing forward and your palms facing up.
From the catch position, press the barbell overhead by extending your arms and pushing the barbell straight up.
Lower the barbell back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This is one explosive movement so drive hard from the floor and catch with confidence.
coaching_cues: Explode up through your hips.
Catch the bar at your shoulders.
Press straight overhead to finish.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads and glutes fire on the pull, then shoulders engage on the press. If you are muscling it with your arms, focus on that hip drive. This is power so move with purpose.
coach_guide.warning: The moment the bar drifts away from your body, you have lost your leverage.

name: barbell clean-grip front squat
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell resting on your upper chest, with your elbows pointing forward.
Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, as if you are sitting back into a chair.
Keep your chest up and your back straight as you lower down, making sure your knees do not go past your toes.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep those elbows high throughout to stay upright and protect your lower back.
coaching_cues: Elbows up, chest proud.
Sink hips between your heels.
Drive up, squeeze glutes hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads working together with a big stretch at the bottom. If your wrists are screaming or you are tipping forward, work on that front rack mobility. Stay tall and power through.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your elbows drop, your chest will collapse forward.

name: barbell close-grip bench press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with a close grip, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Narrow grip means elbows stay tight to your body — this hits triceps hard.
coaching_cues: Elbows close, not flared out.
Touch your chest, full range.
Drive up with triceps power.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel serious tension on the back of your arms, not your chest. If your chest is doing the work, bring those elbows in tighter. Lock onto that tricep burn and power through.
coach_guide.warning: Wide elbows turn this into chest press and miss the triceps completely.

name: barbell curl
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing forward.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and exhale as you curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the bar until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale as you slowly begin to lower the bar back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl with your biceps only — keep those elbows locked at your sides.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay glued to your sides.
Squeeze hard at the top.
Control the weight on the way down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this burning right in the peak of your biceps. If your shoulders or back are taking over, lighten the weight and focus on form. Keep that bicep fire burning through every curl.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your elbows start swinging forward, you have lost bicep isolation.

name: barbell deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell on the ground in front of you.
Bend your knees and hinge at the hips to lower your torso and grip the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and chest lifted as you drive through your heels to lift the barbell off the ground, extending your hips and knees.
As you stand up straight, squeeze your glutes and keep your core engaged.
Lower the barbell back down to the ground by bending at the hips and knees, keeping your back straight.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive through your heels and squeeze your glutes hard at the top.
coaching_cues: Chest up, bar stays close to body.
Drive heels down, hips forward fast.
Squeeze glutes tight at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your glutes and the back of your legs. If your lower back is doing all the work, reset your hip hinge. Lock into that glute burn and keep driving those hips.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your back rounds, you've lost proper form.

name: barbell decline bench press
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Focus on your lower chest and keep those elbows tucked in tight.
coaching_cues: Press from your lower chest.
Keep elbows close to your body.
Control the weight down slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this working your lower chest muscles hard. The decline angle targets that lower portion specifically. If you feel it more in your shoulders, focus on pressing from your chest. Keep going with that lower chest pump.
coach_guide.warning: Let your elbows flare out and you'll miss the lower chest completely.

name: barbell decline bent arm pullover
instructions: Lie down on a decline bench with your head lower than your hips and your feet secured.
Hold a barbell with a pronated grip (palms facing away from you) and extend your arms straight above your chest.
Lower the barbell behind your head in a controlled manner, keeping your arms slightly bent.
Pause for a moment, then raise the barbell back to the starting position by contracting your lats.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the decline angle to get a deeper lat stretch and keep your core tight.
coaching_cues: Arms bent, elbows soft.
Reach far behind your head.
Pull through your lats powerfully.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an incredible stretch along your entire lat from armpit to lower back. If your shoulders are taking over, reduce the range slightly. Embrace that deep stretch and pull strong.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your ribs flare up, you have lost core control.

name: barbell decline close grip to skull press
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your head lower than your feet and hold a barbell with a close grip.
Lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms to press the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows pinned in place and let your triceps do all the work.
coaching_cues: Elbows point to the ceiling.
Lower slow toward your forehead.
Lock out and squeeze triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn on the back of your arms from elbow to shoulder. If your elbows are flaring out or shoulders are straining, tighten your form. Those triceps are working so finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your elbows flare outward, you shift stress to your shoulders.

name: barbell decline pullover
instructions: Lie down on a decline bench with your head lower than your hips and your feet secured.
Hold the barbell with a pronated grip (palms facing away from you) and your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms above your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Lower the barbell in an arc motion behind your head, feeling a stretch in your chest and shoulders.
Pause for a moment, then return the barbell to the starting position by reversing the motion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull with your chest and keep your core engaged to control the movement.
coaching_cues: Slight bend in your elbows.
Stretch deep, feel your chest.
Pull the bar back over chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch across your entire chest and into your serratus. If your shoulders feel jammed or lower back is arching, reduce the range. Open up that chest and own the stretch.
coach_guide.warning: The moment you straighten your arms completely, your shoulders take over.

name: barbell decline wide-grip press
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Grasp the barbell with a wide grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lower the barbell to your chest, keeping your elbows out to the sides.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive your back into the bench and push through your chest, not your shoulders.
coaching_cues: Wide grip, elbows flared out.
Lower to your lower chest.
Press up, squeeze chest hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your outer chest fibers working hard with a big stretch at the bottom. If your front shoulders are burning, tuck your elbows slightly. Fill that chest with blood and push strong.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your butt lifts off the bench, you have lost your decline advantage.

name: barbell decline wide-grip pullover
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your head lower than your hips and your feet secured.
Hold a barbell with a wide grip and extend your arms straight above your chest.
Lower the barbell behind your head in a controlled manner, keeping your arms straight.
Pause for a moment, then raise the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the wide grip to emphasize your lats and control the weight throughout.
coaching_cues: Arms straight, grip wide.
Lower slow behind your head.
Pull back using your lats.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a massive stretch through your lats and even into your chest. If your elbows are bending too much or shoulders are straining, reset your form. That wide stretch is the goal so stay controlled.
coach_guide.warning: The moment you bend your elbows excessively, your triceps take over the movement.

name: barbell drag curl
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Let the barbell hang at arm's length in front of your thighs.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, curl the barbell up towards your chest by contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drag the bar up your body, keeping your elbows behind you the entire time.
coaching_cues: Drag the bar along your torso.
Elbows drive straight back, not out.
Squeeze hard at the top, lower slow.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense squeeze in your biceps, especially at the peak. If your front shoulders are burning, your elbows drifted forward. Keep that bar close and feel the biceps work.
coach_guide.warning: If the bar floats away from your body, you have turned this into a regular curl.

name: barbell floor calf raise
instructions: Place a barbell on the floor in front of you.
Stand with the balls of your feet on the edge of the barbell, with your heels hanging off.
Hold onto a stable object for balance if needed.
Raise your heels as high as possible, using your calves to lift your body.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press through the balls of your feet and squeeze at the top for a full contraction.
coaching_cues: Rise up slow, pause at the top.
Control the drop, stretch at bottom.
Keep your knees straight, not locked.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your calves and a strong stretch at the bottom. If your ankles wobble, slow down and stabilize. Push through and feel those calves fire.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom robs your calves of the stretch and increases injury risk.

name: barbell front chest squat
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Hold the barbell in front of your chest with your hands shoulder-width apart, elbows pointing forward.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your body down into a squat position, pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows high and chest proud as you sit back into the squat.
coaching_cues: Elbows up, chest stays tall throughout.
Sit back and down between your heels.
Drive up through your whole foot.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads working hard, with tension in your upper back holding the bar. If your lower back rounds, you are going too deep. Stay tight and power up.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your elbows drop, the bar rolls forward and your position is lost.

name: barbell front raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip.
Keep your arms straight and lift the barbell forward and upward until it reaches shoulder level.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your arms straight and lift with control — no swinging or body momentum.
coaching_cues: Arms stay straight, shoulders stay down.
Lift slow and controlled to shoulder height.
Feel it in the front delts only.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this burning right in the front of your shoulders. If your lower back is arching or you feel it in your traps, lighten the weight. Keep that front delt fire burning through every rep.
coach_guide.warning: The moment you start swinging or using your back, you have lost the target.

name: barbell front raise and pullover
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Keep your arms straight and raise the barbell in front of you until it reaches shoulder height.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Next, lower the barbell behind your head, keeping your arms straight.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then raise the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Control the bar throughout the entire arc and keep your core braced tight.
coaching_cues: Raise with straight arms to shoulder height.
Pull over with a slight elbow bend.
Keep your ribcage down, core tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest stretch during the pullover and your front shoulders work on the raise. If your lower back arches off the ground, you have lost core control. Stay locked in.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back turns this into a shoulder strain waiting to happen.

name: barbell front squat
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Hold the barbell in front of your shoulders, resting it on your collarbone and shoulders.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your body down into a squat position, pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows high and torso upright as you descend into the squat.
coaching_cues: Elbows up, pointing straight ahead always.
Drop hips between heels, stay tall.
Drive up through your full foot.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads and glutes burning with strong upper back engagement. If your wrists hurt, work on your rack position mobility. Stay upright and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your elbows will pitch you forward and put your lower back at risk.

name: barbell full squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you begin to lower your body down.
Bend at the knees and hips, pushing your hips back and down as if sitting into a chair.
Lower yourself until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Keep your knees in line with your toes and your weight in your heels.
Drive through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit back into your hips first, then drive through your heels to stand.
coaching_cues: Hips back first, knees follow down.
Drive through heels, squeeze glutes up.
Chest proud, core stays tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this burning in your glutes and quads right now. If your knees are caving in or you feel it all in your back, widen your stance. Power through with that deep glute fire.
coach_guide.warning: Never let your knees cave inward during the movement.

name: barbell full squat (back pov)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you begin to lower your body down.
Bend at the knees and hips, pushing your hips back and down as if sitting into a chair.
Lower your body until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Keep your knees in line with your toes and your weight in your heels.
Drive through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Brace your core hard and push your hips back before bending your knees.
coaching_cues: Big breath, brace core before descending.
Hips back first, then knees bend.
Spread the floor apart with your feet.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads loaded evenly with your core staying tight. If your heels lift, work on ankle mobility or widen your stance. Own every inch of the movement.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your knees cave inward puts serious stress on your joints.

name: barbell full squat (side pov)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you begin to lower your body down.
Bend at the knees and hips, pushing your hips back and down as if sitting into a chair.
Lower your body until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Keep your knees in line with your toes and your weight in your heels.
Drive through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit back into your hips while keeping your chest up and spine neutral.
coaching_cues: Chest up, eyes forward, spine neutral.
Hips drop below parallel if mobile.
Drive up like you are pushing floor away.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel glutes fire hard out of the hole and quads burn throughout. If your lower back rounds at the bottom, stop higher until mobility improves. Strong and controlled wins.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back at the bottom is a fast track to injury.

name: barbell full zercher squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Hold the barbell in the crooks of your elbows, with your hands gripping the barbell for stability.
Engage your core and keep your chest lifted as you lower your hips back and down into a squat position.
Keep your knees in line with your toes and your weight in your heels.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top of the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hug the bar tight in your elbows and keep your torso as upright as possible.
coaching_cues: Squeeze the bar tight in elbow crooks.
Stay tall, do not let chest drop.
Push knees out as you stand up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes, quads, and core all working together intensely. If your biceps cramp, squeeze less with your arms and more with your back. Embrace the challenge.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your upper back round will dump the bar forward out of your arms.

name: barbell glute bridge
instructions: Start by lying flat on your back on the ground with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Place a barbell across your hips, holding it securely with both hands.
Engage your glutes and core muscles, then lift your hips off the ground until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your glutes.
Slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive through your heels and squeeze your glutes hard at the top of every rep.
coaching_cues: Push heels down, lift hips high.
Squeeze glutes hard at the top.
Lower with control, do not drop.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a powerful glute contraction at lockout with minimal hamstring involvement. If your hamstrings cramp, bring your feet closer to your hips. Make those glutes do the work.
coach_guide.warning: Hyperextending your lower back at the top shifts work away from your glutes.

name: barbell glute bridge two legs on bench (male)
instructions: Start by sitting on the edge of a bench with your upper back resting against it and your feet flat on the ground, hip-width apart.
Place a barbell across your hips, holding it securely with both hands.
Engage your glutes and core muscles, then press through your heels to lift your hips off the bench, creating a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your glutes.
Slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press through your heels on the bench and drive your hips to full extension.
coaching_cues: Upper back anchored, feet firm on bench.
Drive hips up until body is straight.
Squeeze glutes two seconds at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes burning intensely at the top with hamstrings assisting. If you feel it mostly in your lower back, reset your position and tuck your chin. Lock in that glute squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Pushing through your toes instead of heels will shift the work to your quads.

name: barbell good morning
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell resting on your upper back.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, hinge forward at the hips, pushing your buttocks back as if you were trying to touch the wall behind you with your glutes.
Lower your torso until it is parallel to the ground, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings.
Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position by squeezing your glutes and pushing your hips forward.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit your hips back like you're reaching for something behind you.
coaching_cues: Hips back, chest stays proud.
Feel hamstring stretch, then drive up.
Squeeze glutes to stand tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your hamstrings and glutes as you hinge forward. If your back is doing the work, really push those hips back more. Stay with that posterior chain burn.
coach_guide.warning: Never round your back during the hinge movement.

name: barbell guillotine bench press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lower the barbell slowly towards your neck, keeping your elbows pointed outwards.
Pause for a moment when the barbell is just above your neck.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows wide and lower the bar toward your neck, not your chest.
coaching_cues: Bar path aims toward your throat.
Elbows flare out wide, stay controlled.
Press up in a slight arc.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and burn across your upper chest near your collarbone. If you feel strain in your shoulders, the bar is going too low. Own that upper chest stretch and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Never bounce the bar or lose control near your neck.

name: barbell hack squat
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes slightly turned out.
Hold the barbell behind your legs, resting it on your upper thighs.
Lower your body by bending at the knees and hips, keeping your back straight and your chest up.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Push through your heels and keep your torso as upright as possible.
coaching_cues: Chest stays tall, core braced tight.
Heels drive hard into the floor.
Hips and knees rise together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this lighting up your quads and glutes, especially at the bottom. If your lower back rounds or takes over, reduce the weight. Stay tall and squeeze those glutes at the top.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your back rounds forward, you lose the lift.

name: barbell high bar squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Place the barbell on your upper back, resting it on your traps.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you begin to squat down, pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Lower yourself until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Drive through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit between your hips while keeping your chest proud and core braced.
coaching_cues: Break at hips and knees together.
Knees track over your toes always.
Drive up through your whole foot.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads and glutes working hard through the full range. If your heels lift or knees cave in, check your stance width. Stay solid and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your knees collapse inward puts your joints at risk.

name: barbell incline bench press
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Lie down on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Target your upper chest by pressing up and slightly forward from your collarbone.
coaching_cues: Press from your upper chest.
Drive up and slightly forward.
Keep those elbows at forty-five degrees.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this right under your collarbone in your upper chest. The incline angle is what targets that area. If it's all in your shoulders, lower the weight and focus on chest engagement. Keep pushing with that upper chest burn.
coach_guide.warning: Press straight up instead of slightly forward and you're working shoulders, not chest.

name: barbell incline close grip bench press
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Lie down on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the barbell with a close grip, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows tight to your sides to maximize tricep activation.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay glued to your ribs.
Lower bar toward your lower chest.
Lock out strong at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn through the back of your arms, especially near lockout. If your shoulders take over, bring your grip in slightly. Squeeze hard at the top and keep grinding.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbows shifts the work away from your triceps.

name: barbell incline reverse-grip press
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Lie back on the bench and grasp the barbell with a reverse grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it towards your upper chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Grip the bar with palms facing you and keep elbows tucked throughout.
coaching_cues: Palms face your head, grip firm.
Elbows travel close to your sides.
Press straight up, squeeze triceps hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps and upper chest working together on every rep. If your wrists feel strained, adjust your grip width slightly. Keep that tricep burn going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting the bar drift too far forward strains your wrists.

name: barbell incline row
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Lie face down on the bench with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the barbell towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your elbows back and squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top.
coaching_cues: Chest stays pressed into the pad.
Drive elbows back toward your hips.
Squeeze shoulder blades for one second.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles pinching together at the top of each rep. If you feel it mostly in your arms, focus on leading with your elbows. Build that back thickness one squeeze at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your chest off the pad turns this into a momentum swing.

name: barbell incline shoulder raise
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with your back against the pad and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell up to shoulder height, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Slowly raise the barbell overhead, extending your arms fully.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Push the bar forward and up by protracting your shoulder blades at the top.
coaching_cues: Arms stay straight throughout the movement.
Reach forward like punching the ceiling.
Control the bar back down slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the muscles along the side of your ribcage working as you push forward. If you feel it in your front shoulders only, focus on the reach. Punch up and feel those serratus fire.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your elbows turns this into a press instead of a raise.

name: barbell jefferson squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, resting it on the front of your body, just below your waist.
Step your left foot forward and your right foot back, keeping your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Push through your heels to stand back up to the starting position.
Repeat the movement, alternating your forward and back foot with each repetition.

coach_guide.intro: Straddle the bar and keep your torso upright as you descend.
coaching_cues: One foot forward, one foot back.
Drop straight down between your legs.
Push evenly through both feet up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and inner thighs working hard in this unique stance. If one leg dominates, adjust your foot position for balance. Embrace the awkwardness and build that strength.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting your torso during the lift puts your spine at risk.

name: barbell jm bench press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lower the barbell to your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in close to your body.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lower the bar toward your chin with elbows pointing forward, not out.
coaching_cues: Elbows point toward the ceiling first.
Bar lowers toward your chin area.
Extend hard and lock triceps out.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense stretch and contraction in your triceps throughout each rep. If your shoulders ache, you are going too low. Keep tension on those triceps and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping the bar too low turns this into a skull crusher.

name: barbell jump squat
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Once you reach the bottom of the squat, explode upwards by jumping off the ground.
As you jump, extend your hips, knees, and ankles, pushing through your toes.
Land softly back into the squat position and immediately repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode up from the bottom and land softly with bent knees.
coaching_cues: Squat down then explode straight up.
Land soft with knees slightly bent.
Reset your balance before repeating.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads and glutes powering each jump while your core stays tight. If your knees hurt on landing, focus on softer landings with more knee bend. Stay explosive and keep that power coming.
coach_guide.warning: Landing with stiff legs sends shock straight to your joints.

name: barbell lateral lunge
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
Take a big step to the side with your right foot, keeping your left foot planted.
Bend your right knee and lower your body down into a lunge position, keeping your left leg straight.
Push off with your right foot and return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, stepping with your left foot.

coach_guide.intro: Push your hips back and sit deep into the working leg while keeping your chest tall.
coaching_cues: Step wide and sink hips back.
Chest stays lifted, core tight.
Drive through the heel to stand.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and burn in the glute and inner thigh of your working leg. If your knee caves inward or your back rounds, reset your stance. Own that lateral power.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your knee drift past your toes puts unnecessary stress on the joint.

name: barbell low bar squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell resting on your upper back.
Keeping your chest up and core engaged, slowly lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bar sits lower on your traps so you can hinge more and load your glutes.
coaching_cues: Hips break first, then knees bend.
Knees track over your toes always.
Drive the floor away to stand.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and hamstrings working hard through the entire range. If it is all in your lower back, focus on bracing harder. You have got this, stay tight.
coach_guide.warning: Losing upper back tightness will cause the bar to roll up your neck.

name: barbell lunge
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and a barbell resting on your upper back.
Take a step forward with your right foot, keeping your torso upright.
Lower your body by bending your right knee until your thigh is parallel to the ground.
Push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat with your left leg, alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Step out confident and drive back up through your front heel.
coaching_cues: Big step out, torso stays tall.
Drop straight down, knee tracks toe.
Drive front heel, power back up.
coach_guide.feel: Your front glute should be on fire right now, with some burn in that back leg too. If you feel wobbly, take a bigger step out next time. Keep pushing through that front heel burn.
coach_guide.warning: Don't let your front knee drift past your toes.

name: barbell lying back of the head tricep extension
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows locked in place and let only your forearms move the weight.
coaching_cues: Elbows point to the ceiling fixed.
Lower the bar behind your head.
Squeeze triceps hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense stretch and contraction in the back of your upper arms. If your shoulders are doing the work, pin those elbows tighter. Finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbows turns this into a shoulder exercise.

name: barbell lying close-grip press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with a close grip, hands shoulder-width apart, palms facing towards your feet.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Slowly lower the barbell towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest, then push it back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Tuck your elbows close to your ribs to maximize tricep activation.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay glued to your sides.
Lower the bar to your chest.
Press straight up, lock out hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps burning through the entire press. If your shoulders take over, bring your grip slightly narrower. Keep pushing through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing the bar off your chest robs your triceps of the work.

name: barbell lying close-grip triceps extension
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Grasp the barbell with a close grip, hands shoulder-width apart, palms facing up.
Extend your arms fully, lifting the barbell above your chest.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lock your upper arms vertical and move only at the elbow joint.
coaching_cues: Upper arms stay perfectly still.
Lower with control to forehead level.
Extend fully and squeeze hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the long head of your triceps stretching and contracting deeply. If your elbows flare out wide, reset your grip. That burn means growth.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping the weight too fast puts your elbow joints at risk.

name: barbell lying extension
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Control the descent to your forehead and explode back to lockout.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay fixed, pointing up.
Lower slowly toward your forehead.
Press up and squeeze at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong stretch in your triceps at the bottom and a hard squeeze at the top. If you feel elbow pain, lighten the load. Keep that control.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the lowering phase makes this exercise ineffective and risky.

name: barbell lying lifting (on hip)
instructions: Lie flat on your back on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent.
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip and position it on your hips.
Engaging your glutes, lift your hips off the bench until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Squeeze your glutes at the top and avoid pushing through your lower back.
coaching_cues: Drive through your heels hard.
Squeeze glutes fully at top.
Lower with control, repeat smoothly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes doing all the lifting with hamstrings assisting. If your lower back cramps, you are hyperextending too far. Find that perfect hip height.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back at the top shifts tension away from glutes.

name: barbell lying preacher curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with your chest against the pad and your arms extended over the edge, holding a barbell with an underhand grip.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the bar until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your upper arms glued to the pad and curl with pure bicep strength.
coaching_cues: Chest stays pressed into pad.
Curl up slow, squeeze at top.
Lower with a three count tempo.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working from stretch to peak contraction. If your shoulders lift off the pad, the weight is too heavy. Feel every inch of the curl.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your body to lift the weight defeats the entire purpose.

name: barbell lying triceps extension
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Think about moving only your forearms while your upper arms stay locked vertical.
coaching_cues: Elbows fixed, pointing at ceiling.
Lower bar toward your forehead slowly.
Extend arms fully, squeeze triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch at the bottom and a powerful contraction at lockout. If your elbows drift backward, you are losing tension. Stay strict and strong.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum instead of control limits your tricep development.

name: barbell lying triceps extension skull crusher
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment when the barbell is just above your forehead, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The skull crusher name reminds you to control the bar path carefully.
coaching_cues: Keep elbows shoulder width apart.
Lower to just above your forehead.
Press back up in a smooth arc.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps loaded throughout with a great stretch at the bottom. If you feel strain in your elbows, adjust your grip width. Crush this set.
coach_guide.warning: Letting the bar drift toward your face is dangerous and breaks form.

name: barbell narrow stance squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes pointing slightly outward.
Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you slowly lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your torso upright and drive through your heels to maximize glute engagement.
coaching_cues: Chest up, hips back and down.
Knees track over your toes throughout.
Squeeze glutes hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your glutes and some tension in your quads. If your lower back is straining, focus on bracing your core harder. Own every rep and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your heels lift or knees cave inward, you have lost proper form.

name: barbell one arm bent over row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a barbell with one hand using an overhand grip.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your head in a neutral position.
Pull the barbell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Lower the barbell back down to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Row with your back, not your arm, by initiating with your shoulder blade.
coaching_cues: Pull elbow straight back toward hip.
Squeeze shoulder blade in at top.
Keep your back flat throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction between your shoulder blade and spine. If your bicep burns more than your back, focus on the elbow drive. Keep pulling with purpose.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your torso rotates or rounds, you have lost upper back tension.

name: barbell one arm floor press
instructions: Lie flat on your back on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Hold the barbell with one hand, palm facing up, and extend your arm straight up over your chest.
Slowly lower the barbell towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Control the weight and keep your elbow tucked to isolate the tricep.
coaching_cues: Lower until arm touches the floor.
Press straight up over chest.
Keep your elbow close to body.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong burn in the back of your upper arm. If your shoulder takes over, tuck your elbow tighter. Push through and lock it out.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your elbow flares wide, you shift stress to your shoulder.

name: barbell one arm side deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell in one hand with an overhand grip.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Bend at the hips and lower the barbell towards the outside of your leg, keeping your arm straight and your chest up.
Lower the barbell as far as you can while maintaining good form.
Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: This is a hip hinge, so push your hips back while keeping the bar close.
coaching_cues: Push hips back, not down.
Keep the bar brushing your leg.
Drive hips forward to stand tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your hamstring and glute on the working side. If your lower back aches, reset your hip hinge. Power through those hips.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your back rounds or you lean sideways, reset your position.

name: barbell one arm snatch
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outwards.
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and lower your hips into a squat position, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Explosively extend your hips, knees, and ankles, driving the barbell upwards.
As the barbell reaches chest level, pull it upwards with your arm, keeping it close to your body.
Rotate your elbow under the barbell and extend your arm fully overhead, locking out your elbow.
Lower the barbell back down to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: This is an explosive full body movement, so drive hard through your legs first.
coaching_cues: Explode up through your legs.
Pull the bar close to body.
Punch your arm straight overhead.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your legs, hips, and shoulder working as one powerful chain. If the bar swings away from you, keep it closer. Commit to the explosion.
coach_guide.warning: The moment you pull with your arm before your hips extend, you lose power.

name: barbell one leg squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell across your upper back.
Lift one foot off the ground and extend it forward, keeping it parallel to the ground.
Bend your standing leg and lower your body down as if sitting back into a chair, keeping your chest up and your back straight.
Lower yourself until your thigh is parallel to the ground, then push through your heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Balance and control matter most, so pick a spot and stay focused.
coaching_cues: Sink hips back and down slowly.
Keep your chest lifted throughout.
Drive through heel to stand up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in your quad and glute on the standing leg. If you wobble, slow down and brace harder. Show that single leg who is boss.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your knee collapses inward or you rush, you risk losing balance.

name: barbell overhead squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Hold the barbell with a wide grip, positioning it overhead with your arms fully extended.
Engage your core and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your chest up and knees tracking over your toes.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep the bar stacked over your midfoot and your chest proud throughout.
coaching_cues: Arms locked, bar stays overhead.
Sit deep between your hips.
Drive up while pressing bar high.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads and core working hard to stabilize. If the bar drifts forward, push it back and sit taller. Stay strong under that weight.
coach_guide.warning: The moment the bar moves forward of your feet, you will lose the lift.

name: barbell palms down wrist curl over a bench
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your forearms resting on your thighs, palms facing down.
Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Lower the barbell towards the ground by flexing your wrists, keeping your forearms stationary.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly raise the barbell back up by extending your wrists.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Forearms stay glued to your legs — this is pure wrist movement only.
coaching_cues: Forearms locked, wrists do the work.
Slow descent, controlled lift back up.
Feel it burn along your forearms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this burning along the top of your forearms, right where they meet your wrists. Keep those forearms completely still and let your wrists do all the work. Embrace that forearm burn.
coach_guide.warning: Moving your forearms turns this into an arm exercise instead.

name: barbell palms up wrist curl over a bench
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Rest your forearms on the bench, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Keeping your forearms stationary, exhale and curl your wrists upwards as far as possible.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Forearms stay planted — curl those wrists up as high as possible.
coaching_cues: Forearms locked on the bench.
Curl wrists up, squeeze at the top.
Control the negative, power the positive.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this burning deep in your forearms, right along the underside. If you are not getting the burn, make sure your wrists are doing all the work. Keep grinding through that forearm fire.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your forearms off the bench kills the isolation.
