name: barbell pendlay row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your chest up.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Pull the barbell towards your upper abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Every rep starts from a dead stop, so reset and explode each time.
coaching_cues: Bar touches floor each rep.
Pull explosively to lower chest.
Squeeze shoulder blades together hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire upper back firing with each explosive pull. If you feel it mostly in your arms, focus on driving elbows back. Attack every rep from the floor.
coach_guide.warning: The moment you bounce the bar or use momentum, you cheat your back.

name: barbell pin presses
instructions: Set up a barbell on a power rack at chest height.
Stand facing the barbell and position yourself underneath it, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grip the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest, with your arms fully extended.
Lower the barbell down towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in close to your body.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Start from a dead stop on the pins to build raw pressing strength.
coaching_cues: Set up tight under the bar.
Drive the bar up explosively.
Lock out fully at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps and chest working hard from the sticking point. If your shoulders strain, check your elbow position. Press with power and control.
coach_guide.warning: The moment you lose tightness at the bottom, you lose your power base.

name: barbell preacher curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with your upper arms resting on the pad and your chest against the support.
Grasp the barbell with an underhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the barbell up towards your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your upper arms glued to the pad and curl with controlled intent.
coaching_cues: Upper arms stay on pad always.
Squeeze biceps hard at the top.
Lower slowly, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense bicep contraction at the top and a deep stretch at the bottom. If your shoulders creep forward, press your chest into the pad. Make every curl count.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your elbows lift off the pad, you lose bicep isolation.

name: barbell press sit-up
instructions: Lie flat on your back on a mat with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, resting it on your chest.
Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press the bar straight up as you sit, keeping your core tight throughout the movement.
coaching_cues: Press up as you crunch forward.
Keep the bar path vertical and steady.
Control the descent, no flopping back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs burning as they work to lift your torso while stabilizing the weight. If your lower back is straining, reduce the weight. Stay focused and power through each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting the bar drift forward puts dangerous stress on your shoulders and spine.

name: barbell prone incline curl
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Lie face down on the bench with your chest and stomach resting against it.
Hold a barbell with an underhand grip, shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms fully, allowing the barbell to hang down towards the floor.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the barbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let your arms hang straight down and curl without swinging or lifting your chest off the bench.
coaching_cues: Chest stays glued to the bench.
Squeeze hard at the top of each curl.
Lower slow, full arm extension every rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in your biceps with zero momentum stealing the work. If your shoulders are creeping up, relax them down. This strict form builds real strength.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your chest off the bench means you are using momentum instead of your biceps.

name: barbell pullover
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet on the floor.
Hold a barbell with a shoulder-width grip and extend your arms straight above your chest.
Keeping your arms straight, lower the barbell behind your head in a controlled manner until you feel a stretch in your lats.
Pause for a moment, then raise the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep a slight bend in your elbows and focus on stretching your lats as you lower the bar behind your head.
coaching_cues: Arms stay long with soft elbows.
Stretch deep, feel your lats lengthen.
Pull with your back, not your arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your lats and the sides of your chest at the bottom. If you feel shoulder pain, reduce range. Own that stretch and squeeze back up.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your elbows too much turns this into a tricep exercise and loses the lat focus.

name: barbell pullover to press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet on the ground.
Hold the barbell with a pronated grip (palms facing away from you) and extend your arms straight above your chest.
Keeping your arms straight, lower the barbell behind your head in an arc-like motion until you feel a stretch in your lats.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the motion and press the barbell back to the starting position above your chest.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Control the pullover portion then smoothly transition into a press, keeping your core braced the whole time.
coaching_cues: Pullover with straight arms, full stretch.
Bring bar to chest, then press up.
Brace your core through both movements.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats stretch on the pullover and your chest engage on the press. If your lower back arches off the bench, tighten your abs more. Great combo movement, stay controlled.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the transition between pullover and press kills your muscle connection and risks injury.

name: barbell rack pull
instructions: Set up a barbell on a rack at knee height.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outwards.
Bend at the hips and knees to lower yourself down and grip the barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and lift the barbell by extending your hips and knees, pulling your shoulders back and squeezing your glutes at the top.
Lower the barbell back down to the starting position by bending at the hips and knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive through your heels and squeeze your glutes hard at the top while keeping your back flat.
coaching_cues: Push the floor away with your heels.
Hips and shoulders rise together, stay flat.
Squeeze glutes at lockout, stand tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and hamstrings powering the lift with your back staying neutral. If your lower back rounds, the bar is too low. Lock out strong at the top every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back under heavy load is a fast track to serious injury.

name: barbell rear delt raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Raise the barbell out to the sides, keeping your arms straight, until they are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lead with your elbows pointing back and keep your torso angle fixed as you raise the bar.
coaching_cues: Elbows drive back, not out to sides.
Hold the top for a one count.
Torso stays still, no swinging up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn in the back of your shoulders near your shoulder blades. If your traps take over, go lighter and focus on rear delts. Keep grinding through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum by swinging your torso takes all tension off your rear delts.

name: barbell rear delt row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Pull the barbell towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the bar toward your upper chest with your elbows flaring out wide to target the rear delts.
coaching_cues: Elbows flare wide, pull to upper chest.
Squeeze shoulder blades back at top.
Keep your chest up, back stays flat.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your rear delts and upper back muscles working hard. If you feel it mostly in your biceps, widen your grip and flare elbows more. Strong back, proud chest, keep pulling.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with narrow elbows shifts the work to your lats and biceps instead of rear delts.

name: barbell rear lunge
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and a barbell resting on your upper back.
Take a step backward with your right foot, landing on the ball of your foot.
Bend both knees to lower your body until your left thigh is parallel to the ground.
Push through your left heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat with the other leg.

coach_guide.intro: Step back with control and keep your front knee tracking over your toes as you lower straight down.
coaching_cues: Step back far enough for proper depth.
Drop straight down, not forward.
Drive through front heel to stand up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your front glute and quad doing most of the work. If your knee caves inward, push it out over your pinky toe. Stay balanced and power up from that front leg.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your front knee shoot past your toes puts excessive stress on your knee joint.

name: barbell rear lunge v. 2
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell across your upper back.
Take a step backward with your right foot, landing on the ball of your foot.
Bend both knees to lower your body until your left thigh is parallel to the ground.
Push through your left heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat with the other leg.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your torso upright and step back far enough to create two ninety degree angles with your legs.
coaching_cues: Chest stays tall throughout the lunge.
Back knee drops straight toward floor.
Push through front foot to return up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads on the front leg burning with each rep. If you feel unstable, narrow your stance width slightly. Stay upright and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning your torso forward shifts the load to your lower back and reduces glute activation.

name: barbell revers wrist curl v. 2
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent.
Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Keeping your forearms stationary, exhale and curl your wrists upward as far as possible.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let your wrists hang fully and curl the bar up using only your forearm muscles.
coaching_cues: Forearms stay flat on your thighs.
Curl wrists up, squeeze at the top.
Lower with control, full range of motion.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a burning pump in the top of your forearms near your elbows. If you feel wrist pain, reduce the weight immediately. Those forearms will be on fire, embrace it.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your forearms off your thighs means you are cheating with momentum.

name: barbell reverse close-grip bench press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with a reverse grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Slowly lower the barbell down towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment when the barbell is just above your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows tucked tight and lower the bar to your lower chest.
coaching_cues: Grip reversed, elbows pinned to sides.
Lower to the bottom of your chest.
Press straight up, squeeze triceps hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel intense tension in the back of your upper arms. If your wrists or shoulders ache, lighten the weight and reset your grip. Stay locked in and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbows out shifts the load away from your triceps.

name: barbell reverse curl
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Keep your upper arms stationary and exhale as you curl the barbell upward, contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the barbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the barbell is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale as you slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lock your upper arms in place and let your forearms do all the work.
coaching_cues: Palms down, wrists stay straight throughout.
Upper arms glued to your sides.
Squeeze at the top, lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your outer forearms and biceps. If your shoulders are swinging, you are using momentum instead of muscle. Keep it strict and feel the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the bar up means the weight is too heavy.

name: barbell reverse grip bent over row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up, until your torso is almost parallel to the floor.
Pull the barbell towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive your elbows back and squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward, chest proud and back flat.
Pull the bar into your belly button.
Squeeze your back hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats and mid-back working hard on every rep. If your lower back is straining, raise your torso slightly. Own that squeeze and keep pulling.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts your spine at risk.

name: barbell reverse grip decline bench press
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Grasp the barbell with a reverse grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Control the bar path and keep your elbows from flaring wide.
coaching_cues: Reverse grip, thumbs pointing outward.
Lower slow to your lower chest line.
Drive up strong, lock out at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper chest and front shoulders engaged. If your wrists feel strained, adjust your grip width slightly. Push through and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting the bar drift toward your face puts your shoulders in danger.

name: barbell reverse grip incline bench press
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Lie down on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the barbell with a reverse grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Focus on the upper chest by pressing in a straight line above your collarbone.
coaching_cues: Palms face you, grip just outside shoulders.
Lower to your upper chest slowly.
Press up and squeeze chest at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper pecs burning near your collarbone. If tension shifts to your front delts only, widen your grip slightly. Stay focused and power through.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing the bar off your chest steals the muscle-building tension.

name: barbell reverse grip incline bench row
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench facing the backrest with your chest against it.
Grab the barbell with a reverse grip (palms facing down) and hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and core engaged.
Pull the barbell towards your upper abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pin your chest to the pad and row with your back, not your arms.
coaching_cues: Chest stays pressed into the bench.
Pull elbows back toward your hips.
Squeeze shoulder blades together, then lower.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats and lower traps firing on each pull. If your biceps burn out first, focus on leading with your elbows. Lock in and build that back.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your chest off the pad means you are using momentum.

name: barbell reverse grip skullcrusher
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Hold the barbell with a reverse grip, palms facing towards your face, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping your elbows in and your wrists straight.
Slowly lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows pointed at the ceiling and only bend at the elbow joint.
coaching_cues: Reverse grip, palms facing your face.
Lower the bar toward your forehead slowly.
Extend arms fully, squeeze triceps hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and burn in your triceps. If your elbows drift backward, reset them over your shoulders. Stay tight and crush this set.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbows flare wide shifts stress to your shoulders.

name: barbell reverse preacher curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with your chest against the pad and your arms extended straight down, holding a barbell with an overhand grip.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the barbell upward while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the barbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the barbell is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pin your upper arms to the pad and curl with strict control.
coaching_cues: Palms down, arms locked on the pad.
Curl up slow, squeeze your biceps hard.
Lower with a three-count, full stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the outer part of your biceps and forearms working. If your shoulders roll forward, you are going too heavy. Embrace the burn and keep grinding.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your elbows off the pad removes tension from the biceps.

name: barbell reverse wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Slowly curl your wrists upward, bringing the barbell towards your body.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Isolate your forearm extensors by moving only at the wrist.
coaching_cues: Forearms flat on thighs, wrists hang free.
Curl your knuckles up toward the ceiling.
Lower slow, feel the stretch at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the top of your forearms burning intensely. If nothing happens, slow down and focus on the squeeze. Small movements build serious grip strength.
coach_guide.warning: Moving your forearms off your thighs brings other muscles into play.

name: barbell rollerout
instructions: Kneel on the floor and hold a barbell with both hands, shoulder-width apart.
Roll the barbell forward, extending your arms and keeping your core engaged.
Continue rolling forward until your body is fully extended and your arms are overhead.
Pause for a moment, then slowly roll the barbell back towards your knees, maintaining control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Brace your core tight and roll out only as far as you can control.
coaching_cues: Core tight, hips stay in line.
Roll forward slow, arms fully extended.
Pull back using your abs, not arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core shaking, especially your lower abs. If your lower back dips, shorten your range until you are stronger. Stay controlled and build that core.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag puts dangerous pressure on your lower back.

name: barbell rollerout from bench
instructions: Start by kneeling on the floor with a barbell placed on a bench in front of you.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keeping your core engaged and your back straight, slowly roll the barbell forward, extending your arms in front of you.
Continue rolling the barbell forward until your body is fully extended and your arms are overhead.
Pause for a moment at the fully extended position, then slowly roll the barbell back towards your body, returning to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Brace your core like you are about to take a punch and roll out only as far as you can control.
coaching_cues: Squeeze your abs, tuck your hips under.
Roll out slow, arms stay straight.
Pull back with your core, not arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn through your entire midsection, especially the lower abs. If your lower back starts aching, you have gone too far out. Stay tight and own every inch of the movement.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your lower back arches and hips sag, you have lost all core tension.

name: barbell romanian deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing forward.
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Bend at the hips, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Lower the barbell towards the ground, keeping it close to your body.
Feel the stretch in your hamstrings as you lower the barbell.
Once you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, push your hips forward and stand up straight.
Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement.
Lower the barbell back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Push your hips back like you're trying to close a car door with your butt.
coaching_cues: Hips back, bar slides down legs.
Feel the hamstring stretch, then snap up.
Squeeze glutes hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and burn running down the back of your legs into your glutes. If it's all in your back, push those hips back more. Ride that hamstring burn all the way up.
coach_guide.warning: Keep the bar glued to your legs throughout the entire movement.

name: barbell seated behind head military press
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and bring it down to shoulder level, behind your head.
Press the barbell upward until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Only go as low as your shoulder mobility safely allows and keep your core braced tight.
coaching_cues: Elbows point out at forty-five degrees.
Lower to ear level, no deeper.
Press straight up, squeeze at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders working hard, especially the rear and side delts. If you feel pinching or sharp pain in the joint, stop immediately and switch to front presses. Protect those shoulders and push strong.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping the bar too low behind your head puts your shoulder joint at serious risk.

name: barbell seated bradford rocky press
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell to shoulder height, keeping your elbows slightly bent and pointing forward.
Press the barbell overhead, fully extending your arms.
Lower the barbell back to shoulder height and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep the bar moving in a smooth arc from front to back without locking out overhead.
coaching_cues: Front to back, just clear your head.
Keep constant tension, no lockout.
Control the rhythm, stay smooth.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a continuous burn across all three heads of your shoulders with no rest at top or bottom. If your traps are burning more than your delts, you are shrugging too much. Keep those shoulders on fire.
coach_guide.warning: Locking out overhead gives your muscles a break and defeats the purpose of this movement.

name: barbell seated calf raise
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor and a barbell resting on your thighs.
Place the balls of your feet on a raised platform, such as a block or step.
Position the barbell across your thighs and hold it securely with your hands.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, lift your heels off the ground by extending your ankles.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Push through the balls of your feet and squeeze hard at the top of every single rep.
coaching_cues: Drive up high, squeeze two seconds.
Lower your heels below the platform.
Control down, explode up each rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn deep in your calf muscles, especially near the lower portion. If you feel it mostly in your ankles or shins, reposition your feet on the platform. Chase that deep calf pump.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom robs your calves of the stretch and time under tension they need.

name: barbell seated calf raise
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor and a barbell resting on your thighs.
Place the balls of your feet on a raised platform, such as a block or step.
Lower your heels as far as possible, feeling a stretch in your calves.
Raise your heels as high as possible, contracting your calves.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Push through the balls of your feet and squeeze hard at the top of every single rep.
coaching_cues: Drive up high, squeeze two seconds.
Lower your heels below the platform.
Control down, explode up each rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn deep in your calf muscles, especially near the lower portion. If you feel it mostly in your ankles or shins, reposition your feet on the platform. Chase that deep calf pump.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom robs your calves of the stretch and time under tension they need.

name: barbell seated close grip behind neck triceps extension
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold the barbell with a close grip behind your neck, palms facing forward.
Keep your elbows close to your head and slowly lower the barbell towards the back of your head.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lock your elbows in place pointing straight up and only move from the elbow down.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay locked, pointing ceiling.
Lower slow behind your head.
Squeeze triceps hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and burn in the long head of your triceps near your armpit. If your shoulders are doing the work or elbows are flaring, go lighter. Lock in and feel those triceps scream.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your elbows start drifting forward or out, your triceps lose all the tension.

name: barbell seated close-grip concentration curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Rest your upper arms against your inner thighs, just above your knees, and let the barbell hang down in front of you.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pin your upper arms against your thighs and curl with zero body movement.
coaching_cues: Upper arms glued to inner thighs.
Curl up slow, squeeze at top.
Lower with control, full stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps doing all the work with a strong peak contraction at the top. If your back is rounding or you are rocking, the weight is too heavy. Stay locked in and build that peak.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your elbows off your thighs shifts the work away from your biceps immediately.

name: barbell seated good morning
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and a barbell resting on your upper back.
Keep your back straight and your chest up.
Slowly hinge forward at the hips, lowering your torso towards the ground.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Push your hips back like closing a car door and keep your spine completely neutral.
coaching_cues: Hips push back, chest stays proud.
Feel the stretch in your hamstrings.
Drive hips forward, squeeze glutes.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings and glutes loading up as you hinge forward. If your lower back is rounding or taking over, reduce the range. Sit back and feel those glutes fire.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back under load puts your spine in a very dangerous position.

name: barbell seated overhead press
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and bring it to shoulder level, with your elbows bent and palms facing forward.
Press the barbell overhead by extending your arms fully.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stack the bar directly over your shoulders and keep your core braced throughout.
coaching_cues: Press straight up, bar over shoulders.
Core tight, ribs stay down.
Lower with control to chin level.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your front and side delts burning as you press, with your core working to stabilize. If your lower back is arching, brace harder or use less weight. Stay stacked and press with power.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back to push the weight up takes tension off your shoulders.

name: barbell seated overhead triceps extension
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart, and raise it overhead.
Lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms close to your head.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms to raise the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lock your elbows in place pointing straight up and only move from the elbow down.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay locked, pointing ceiling.
Lower slow until forearms touch biceps.
Squeeze triceps hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your triceps at the bottom and a strong squeeze at lockout. If your elbows are flaring out or shoulders are aching, lighten the load. Keep those elbows tight and feel the burn.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your elbows start flaring out to the sides, your shoulders take over the movement.

name: barbell seated twist
instructions: Sit on a flat bench with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent.
Hold a barbell with both hands in front of your chest, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Engage your core and slowly twist your torso to one side, keeping your back straight.
Pause for a moment at the end of the twist, then slowly rotate back to the starting position.
Repeat the twist to the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate from your core, not your arms or shoulders.
coaching_cues: Hips stay locked and facing forward.
Twist controlled, no momentum swinging.
Squeeze obliques at each end point.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel deep burn through your obliques and waist. If lower back aches, reduce range. Keep that rotation tight.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the bar with arms means core is not working.

name: barbell shrug
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell in front of you with an overhand grip.
Keep your arms straight and your back straight throughout the exercise.
Lift your shoulders up towards your ears as high as possible, squeezing your traps at the top.
Hold for a moment, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lift your shoulders straight up toward your ears, no rolling or rotating.
coaching_cues: Shoulders go straight up to ears.
Squeeze your traps at the top.
No rolling, just up and down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this right on top of your shoulders and into your neck area. If you're not feeling much, you might need more weight. Keep going with that trap squeeze at the top.
coach_guide.warning: Never roll your shoulders in circles, just straight up and down.

name: barbell side bent v. 2
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with both hands, palms facing down.
Keep your back straight and core engaged throughout the exercise.
Slowly bend your torso to the right side, lowering the barbell towards your right knee.
Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Repeat the movement on the left side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bend directly to the side, targeting your obliques.
coaching_cues: Slide bar straight down your leg.
Keep chest up and facing forward.
Control the return, squeeze at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a stretch and burn along your side waist. If back feels strained, go lighter. Own that side bend.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning forward or back means you lost the oblique target.

name: barbell side split squat
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Hold a barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your body down into a squat position, bending at the knees and hips.
As you lower, push your knees out to the sides and keep your weight on your heels.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drop into a wide lateral lunge, driving through lead leg.
coaching_cues: Push hips back as you bend.
Keep trailing leg straight and grounded.
Drive up through your working heel.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel quads and inner thigh burning hard. If knees cave, widen stance slightly. Push through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Knee collapsing inward means you need to sit back more.

name: barbell side split squat v. 2
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outwards.
Hold a barbell across your upper back, resting it on your shoulders.
Take a big step to the side with your right foot, keeping your left foot planted.
Bend your right knee and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your chest up and your back straight.
Push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, stepping out with your left foot.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Same lateral squat pattern with a slight variation in depth.
coaching_cues: Sit deep into your working hip.
Chest stays tall throughout movement.
Explode up, control the descent down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel deep quad and adductor work. If balance is off, slow it down. Stay strong in that stance.
coach_guide.warning: Rising onto toes means weight shifted too far forward.

name: barbell single leg deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip.
Shift your weight onto your left foot and lift your right foot slightly off the ground.
Hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your right leg extended behind you for balance.
Lower the barbell towards the ground, keeping it close to your body and your left leg slightly bent.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then engage your glutes and hamstrings to lift your torso back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge on one leg while keeping hips square and level.
coaching_cues: Push back leg straight behind you.
Hips stay level, do not rotate.
Drive through standing heel to rise.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel glutes and hamstrings firing intensely. If lower back takes over, reduce range. Balance and power together.
coach_guide.warning: Hip rotating open means you lost glute engagement.

name: barbell single leg split squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
Take a large step forward with one leg, keeping your torso upright.
Lower your body by bending your front knee and hip, while keeping your back leg straight.
Continue lowering until your front thigh is parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

coach_guide.intro: Drop into a deep split squat, front leg does the work.
coaching_cues: Lower until back knee nearly touches.
Front knee tracks over your toes.
Drive up through your front heel.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel quads burning on front leg. If knee pain occurs, check your stance width. Finish every rep strong.
coach_guide.warning: Torso leaning too far forward means quads are not loaded.

name: barbell sitted alternate leg raise
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and hold a barbell across your thighs.
Keeping your legs straight, lift one leg up as high as possible while keeping the other leg on the ground.
Lower the raised leg back down and repeat with the other leg.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep core braced while alternating leg raises under control.
coaching_cues: Lower back stays pressed down firmly.
Raise one leg, lower with control.
Keep barbell steady across your chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel lower abs working hard each rep. If back arches, reduce leg range. Keep that core tight.
coach_guide.warning: Back arching off the surface means abs are not engaged.

name: barbell sitted alternate leg raise (female)
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and hold a barbell across your thighs.
Place your hands on the sides of the bench for support.
Keeping your legs straight, lift one leg up as high as possible while keeping it parallel to the ground.
Lower the leg back down and repeat with the other leg.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Same alternating leg raise with focus on controlled movement.
coaching_cues: Press lower back flat throughout.
Alternate legs with steady rhythm.
Barbell stays still, core does work.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel lower abs firing on every rep. If hip flexors dominate, bend knees slightly. You have got this.
coach_guide.warning: Rocking the barbell means momentum is taking over.

name: barbell skier
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Simultaneously lift the barbell up towards your shoulders while jumping slightly off the ground.
As you reach the top of the movement, quickly reverse the motion and lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive elbows up and forward like youre skiing uphill.
coaching_cues: Elbows lead the movement, not hands.
Keep slight bend in your elbows.
Control down, explode on the raise.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel front and side delts burning. If traps take over, lighten the weight. Keep those shoulders working.
coach_guide.warning: Shrugging shoulders up means traps are stealing the work.

name: barbell speed squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your hips back and down, as if sitting into a chair.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Move fast but controlled, powering up from the bottom.
coaching_cues: Drop quick, explode up with power.
Keep chest tall and core braced.
Drive through heels, squeeze glutes top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel glutes and quads firing with each explosive rep. If form breaks, slow the tempo. Bring that power every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Heels rising off the floor means you are leaning forward.

name: barbell split squat v. 2
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
Take a large step forward with your right foot, keeping your torso upright.
Lower your body by bending your knees and hips until your right thigh is parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat with your left leg forward for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drop straight down through rear knee, front quad does the work.
coaching_cues: Front knee tracks over your toes.
Drop rear knee straight to floor.
Drive up through your front heel.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel deep burn in front quad and glute. If knee caves inward, reset your stance. Own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning forward shifts weight off your quads.

name: barbell squat (on knees)
instructions: Start by kneeling on the ground with your knees hip-width apart and your toes pointing forward.
Place a barbell across your shoulders, gripping it with an overhand grip and your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and keep your chest lifted as you slowly lower your body down by bending your knees, keeping your back straight.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stay tall and squeeze quads hard at the top.
coaching_cues: Sit hips back while staying upright.
Drive knees forward over your toes.
Squeeze quads hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel intense quad engagement throughout. If lower back takes over, check your brace. Keep that tension constant.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts your spine at risk.

name: barbell squat jump step rear lunge
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back.
Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Explode upwards, jumping off the ground as high as you can.
Land softly on your feet and immediately step back with one leg into a reverse lunge.
Lower your body down until your front thigh is parallel to the ground, keeping your back straight.
Push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat the jump and lunge sequence on the other leg.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explosive jump, controlled lunge, stay athletic throughout.
coaching_cues: Explode up through your heels fast.
Land soft then step into lunge.
Keep chest proud through every transition.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel quads burning with heart rate climbing. If you lose balance, slow the transitions down. Push through the challenge.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the lunge destroys your form and balance.

name: barbell standing ab rollerout
instructions: Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the barbell with both hands in front of your thighs.
Engage your core and slowly roll the barbell down towards the ground, keeping your back straight and your arms extended.
Continue rolling the barbell forward until your body is fully extended and your hands are directly above your head.
Pause for a moment, then slowly roll the barbell back towards your thighs, maintaining control and keeping your core engaged.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Brace your core and roll out with control.
coaching_cues: Squeeze abs before you roll out.
Keep arms straight, hips following bar.
Pull back using your core only.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel deep ab tension throughout the roll. If lower back arches, shorten your range. Build that core strength.
coach_guide.warning: Letting hips sag puts dangerous stress on spine.

name: barbell standing back wrist curl
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip.
Rest the barbell on the back of your hands with your palms facing down and your fingers pointing towards your body.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl your wrists upwards as far as possible.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Isolate the wrist extensors with slow controlled curls.
coaching_cues: Keep forearms still, only wrists move.
Curl knuckles up toward the ceiling.
Lower slowly for three full seconds.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel burn on top of your forearms. If grip fails first, go lighter. Strengthen those often neglected muscles.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum takes tension off your forearms completely.

name: barbell standing bradford press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the barbell in front of your shoulders with an overhand grip.
Press the barbell overhead, fully extending your arms.
Lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Alternate front and back press to hit all delt heads.
coaching_cues: Press up and clear your head.
Lower behind then press up again.
Keep core tight throughout each rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel shoulders working from every angle. If neck strains, check your bar path. Build those boulder shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping bar too low behind strains your shoulders.

name: barbell standing calf raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place a barbell across your upper back.
Raise your heels off the ground as high as possible, using only your toes.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rise up high on toes and squeeze calves hard.
coaching_cues: Push through balls of your feet.
Pause and squeeze hard at top.
Lower heels slow below the platform.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel calves burning from stretch to squeeze. If ankles wobble, narrow your stance slightly. Chase that deep calf pump.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom steals your calf gains.

name: barbell standing close grip curl
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, hands close together.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale as you curl the weights while contracting your biceps. Continue to raise the bar until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale as you slowly begin to bring the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Close grip shifts focus to your outer bicep head.
coaching_cues: Keep elbows pinned at your sides.
Curl with pinkies driving up high.
Control the weight on the way down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel outer biceps working hard each rep. If elbows drift forward, go lighter. Build those peaked biceps.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the bar means your biceps are not working.

name: barbell standing close grip military press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly closer than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell to shoulder height, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Press the barbell overhead, extending your arms fully.
Lower the barbell back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Close grip hits front delts hard, stay strict.
coaching_cues: Hands inside shoulder width on bar.
Press straight up, head pushes through.
Lock out strong then lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel front delts and triceps firing together. If lower back arches, tighten your core more. Press with power.
coach_guide.warning: Excessive back arch turns this into an injury.

name: barbell standing concentration curl
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell in one hand, palm facing up.
Rest your opposite hand on your thigh for support.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the weight up towards your shoulder.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Lean forward and isolate one bicep completely.
coaching_cues: Brace free hand on your thigh.
Curl slow keeping upper arm vertical.
Squeeze hard at top then lower slow.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep bicep contraction and stretch. If you swing, lighten the load immediately. Focus builds the muscle.
coach_guide.warning: Using body momentum defeats the isolation purpose completely.

name: barbell standing front raise over head
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Slowly raise the barbell in front of you, keeping your arms straight and your palms facing down.
Continue lifting until the barbell is slightly above shoulder level.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Control the arc, keep your core braced throughout.
coaching_cues: Arms straight, lift from the shoulders.
Pause briefly when bar is overhead.
Lower with control, do not swing.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this across your front delts and upper chest. If lower back aches, tighten your core. Strong shoulders are built here.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back means the weight is too heavy.

name: barbell standing leg calf raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place a barbell across your upper back.
Raise your heels off the ground as high as possible, using your calves.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Full range of motion, squeeze hard at the top.
coaching_cues: Rise up onto your toes slowly.
Pause and squeeze at the peak.
Lower heels below the platform edge.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in both calves equally. If one side dominates, check your foot position. Push through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom means you lost the stretch.

name: barbell standing overhead triceps extension
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip.
Raise the barbell overhead, fully extending your arms.
Keeping your upper arms close to your head, slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then raise the barbell back to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep elbows locked in place, only forearms move.
coaching_cues: Elbows point straight up, stay still.
Lower the bar behind your head slowly.
Squeeze triceps hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel intense tension in your triceps. If elbows flare out, go lighter. Lock out strong every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Elbows drifting forward means triceps lose tension.

name: barbell standing reverse grip curl
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps, bringing the barbell as close to your shoulders as possible.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Overhand grip targets forearms and outer biceps.
coaching_cues: Knuckles face up throughout the curl.
Keep elbows pinned to your sides.
Squeeze at top, lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your forearms and biceps working together. If wrists hurt, loosen your grip slightly. Strong grip, strong arms.
coach_guide.warning: Wrists bending back means grip is too tight.

name: barbell standing rocking leg calf raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell across your upper back.
Raise your heels off the ground as high as possible, balancing on the balls of your feet.
Slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rock forward onto toes, maximize calf contraction.
coaching_cues: Shift weight forward onto your toes.
Rise high and squeeze your calves.
Rock back and repeat the motion.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a continuous pump in your calves. If balance wobbles, use a lighter weight. Keep that rhythm going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Flat feet through the movement means no calf activation.

name: barbell standing twist
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell in front of your chest with both hands, palms facing down.
Engage your core and keep your back straight throughout the exercise.
Slowly twist your torso to the right, pivoting on your feet and hips, while keeping your lower body stable.
Pause for a moment at the end of the twist, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat the twist to the left side.
Continue alternating twists for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate from your core, not your hips.
coaching_cues: Feet stay planted, hips face forward.
Twist using your obliques only.
Control the rotation in both directions.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques working on each twist. If your hips spin, slow it down. Carve that waistline with control.
coach_guide.warning: Hips rotating with you means core is not engaged.

name: barbell standing wide grip biceps curl
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, hands wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and your elbows close to your torso.
Exhale and curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Wide grip shifts focus to your outer biceps.
coaching_cues: Grip wider than shoulder width apart.
Keep elbows stationary at your sides.
Squeeze biceps hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the outer head of your biceps firing. If shoulders take over, check your grip width. Build that peak.
coach_guide.warning: Elbows swinging forward means you are using momentum.

name: barbell standing wide military press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Lift the barbell to shoulder height, keeping your elbows slightly in front of the bar.
Press the barbell overhead, extending your arms fully.
Lower the barbell back to shoulder height and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Wide grip emphasizes outer delts and upper chest.
coaching_cues: Grip wide, press straight overhead.
Keep core tight, ribs down.
Lower to chin level with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders and upper chest engaged. If neck tenses up, relax your traps. Press strong and steady.
coach_guide.warning: Lower back arching means core has disengaged.

name: barbell standing wide-grip curl
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands wider than shoulder-width apart.
Let the barbell hang at arm's length in front of your thighs, with your palms facing away from your body.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the barbell upward by contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the barbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the barbell is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Wide grip targets the outer bicep head.
coaching_cues: Hands wider than shoulder width apart.
Curl up without moving your elbows.
Lower slowly, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel tension on the outside of your biceps. If inner elbows ache, narrow grip slightly. Squeeze every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning back means the weight is too heavy.

name: barbell step-up
instructions: Stand in front of a bench or step with a barbell resting on your upper back.
Place one foot on the bench or step, ensuring your entire foot is in contact with the surface.
Push through your heel and step up onto the bench or step, fully extending your hip and knee.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Repeat with the opposite leg.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive through your heel, keep chest tall.
coaching_cues: Plant full foot on the box.
Push through heel, stand up tall.
Lower with control, switch legs.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads firing hard. If knees cave inward, use lighter weight. Power up every step.
coach_guide.warning: Pushing off back foot means glutes are not working.

name: barbell stiff leg good morning
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps.
Keeping your back straight, hinge forward at the hips, pushing your glutes back.
Lower your torso until it is parallel to the ground, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings.
Engage your glutes and hamstrings to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge deep at the hips, feel the stretch through your glutes.
coaching_cues: Push hips back, chest stays proud.
Soft knee bend, shins stay vertical.
Squeeze glutes hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in glutes and hamstrings at the bottom. The burn builds as you stand. Own that hinge pattern.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back means the weight is too heavy.

name: barbell straight leg deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing forward.
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Bend at your hips and lower the barbell towards the ground, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Lower the barbell until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.
Engage your hamstrings and glutes to lift the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep the bar close and let your hamstrings do the work.
coaching_cues: Bar drags along your legs throughout.
Push hips back until hamstrings stretch.
Drive through heels to stand tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong stretch down the back of your thighs. Hamstrings should be screaming by the last reps. Embrace that tension.
coach_guide.warning: Bending knees too much shifts work away from hamstrings.

name: barbell sumo deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing outwards.
Place a barbell on the ground in front of you, centered between your feet.
Bend your knees and lower your hips, keeping your back straight and chest up, to grip the barbell with an overhand grip.
Engage your core and drive through your heels to lift the barbell off the ground, extending your hips and knees simultaneously.
As you lift, keep your chest up and back straight, and push your hips forward to fully engage your glutes.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Wide stance, knees track over toes, power from the hips.
coaching_cues: Toes point out, knees follow toes.
Drive knees out as you descend.
Squeeze glutes explosively at lockout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your inner thighs and glutes working hard. Hips do the heavy lifting here. Stay powerful and controlled.
coach_guide.warning: Knees caving inward puts your joints at risk.

name: barbell thruster
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell at shoulder height with an overhand grip.
Lower into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
As you reach the bottom of the squat, explosively drive through your heels to stand up, simultaneously pressing the barbell overhead.
Lower the barbell back to shoulder height as you lower back into the squat position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Smooth transition from squat to press, use leg drive.
coaching_cues: Squat deep, elbows stay high forward.
Explode up, transfer power overhead.
Lock out arms with core tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your whole body working as one unit. Legs power the press, shoulders finish. Keep that rhythm flowing.
coach_guide.warning: Pressing before full hip extension wastes your leg power.

name: barbell upright row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Let the barbell hang in front of your thighs, arms fully extended.
Keeping your back straight and core engaged, exhale and lift the barbell straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lead with your elbows, keep the bar close to your body.
coaching_cues: Elbows drive up and out wide.
Bar travels close to your torso.
Control the weight back down slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your side delts and upper traps firing. Shoulders should burn near the top. Keep pulling strong.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum means your delts are not doing the work.

name: barbell upright row v. 2
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Let the barbell hang in front of your thighs, arms fully extended.
Keeping your back straight, exhale and lift the barbell straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Focus on pulling high, squeezing at the top position.
coaching_cues: Initiate by lifting elbows first always.
Pull bar to upper chest height.
Pause briefly then lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel tension across your shoulders and upper back. The squeeze at top is where growth happens. Stay focused up there.
coach_guide.warning: Yanking the weight steals tension from target muscles.

name: barbell upright row v. 3
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Let the barbell hang in front of your thighs, arms fully extended.
Keeping your core engaged and back straight, exhale as you lift the barbell straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale as you slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Controlled tempo throughout, maximize time under tension.
coaching_cues: Elbows always stay above your wrists.
Raise bar to chin level maximum.
Lower slowly for three full seconds.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a constant burn in your delts. No rest at bottom keeps muscles working. Push through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Raising the bar too high causes shoulder impingement.

name: barbell wide bench press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with a wide grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows slightly flared out.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest, then push it back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Wide grip emphasizes outer chest, control the bar path.
coaching_cues: Grip wide, lower bar to chest.
Press up in slight arc inward.
Squeeze chest hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your outer pecs stretching deep at bottom. Chest should feel full and pumped. Build that wide chest.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring elbows too wide puts shoulders in danger.

name: barbell wide reverse grip bench press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the barbell with a wide reverse grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Lower the barbell slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in and your wrists straight.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest, then push it back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Reverse grip shifts focus to upper chest, stay controlled.
coaching_cues: Palms face you, grip shoulder width.
Lower bar to lower chest area.
Press up, squeeze upper chest hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper pecs and front delts engaged. This angle hits chest differently. Enjoy that unique pump.
coach_guide.warning: Weak grip on reverse press risks dropping the bar.

name: barbell wide squat
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Hold the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps or rear delts.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your body down into a squat, pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Wide stance targets inner thighs and builds quad strength.
coaching_cues: Feet wide, toes point outward slightly.
Sit back and down between legs.
Drive through heels, squeeze quads up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel quads and inner thighs working together. Adductors get extra attention with this stance. Stay low and strong.
coach_guide.warning: Heels lifting means you are shifting too far forward.

name: barbell wide-grip upright row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell with an overhand grip, hands wider than shoulder-width apart.
Let the barbell hang in front of your thighs, arms fully extended.
Keeping your back straight, exhale and lift the barbell straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lead with elbows wide, keep the bar close to body.
coaching_cues: Elbows flare out wide to sides.
Pull bar up to chest height only.
Control the weight back down slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this across your outer shoulders and upper traps. If wrists hurt, loosen your grip slightly. Nice wide pulls.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too high puts unnecessary stress on shoulders.

name: barbell wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your forearms resting on your thighs, holding a barbell with an underhand grip.
Allow the barbell to roll down to your fingertips, keeping your wrists straight.
Slowly curl the barbell up towards your forearms by flexing your wrists.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Isolate your forearms with wrists hanging off the bench.
coaching_cues: Let the bar roll to fingertips.
Curl wrists up and squeeze tight.
Keep forearms pressed against the bench.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in your inner forearms near the elbow. This pump builds serious grip strength. Embrace the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Moving your forearms off the bench steals the tension.

name: barbell wrist curl v. 2
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent.
Hold a barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Slowly curl your wrists upward, bringing the barbell towards your forearms.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Wrists curl under for the inner forearm squeeze.
coaching_cues: Palms face down throughout the movement.
Lift only with your wrist muscles.
Pause at the top for one second.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this on top of your forearms near your wrists. Lighter weight works better here. Keep those reps clean.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum means the weight is too heavy.

name: barbell zercher squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Hold the barbell in the crooks of your elbows, with your hands gripping the bar for stability.
Engage your core and keep your chest lifted as you lower your hips back and down into a squat position.
Keep your knees in line with your toes and your weight in your heels.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the squat, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold the bar in your elbow crooks and sit deep.
coaching_cues: Bar locked tight in elbow creases.
Push knees out over your toes.
Drive up through your whole foot.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your glutes and core working overtime. Your upper back will burn too. This is total body strength.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your upper back will make you drop forward.

name: basic toe touch (male)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms by your sides.
Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Reach down towards your toes with your hands, keeping your legs as straight as possible.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge at hips and reach long toward your toes.
coaching_cues: Soft bend in your knees allowed.
Reach fingers toward your toes slowly.
Squeeze glutes as you stand back up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a stretch down your hamstrings and activation in glutes standing up. Keep breathing through each rep. Simple but effective.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back takes away the stretch.

name: battling ropes
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Hold one end of the rope in each hand, with your palms facing each other.
Raise your arms to shoulder height, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Begin making alternating waves with the ropes by rapidly raising and lowering each arm.
Continue for the desired duration or number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Create powerful waves with alternating arm slams.
coaching_cues: Grip tight and stay in quarter squat.
Alternate arms with explosive power.
Keep the waves running to the end.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders burning and heart rate climbing fast. This is cardio and strength combined. Keep those waves alive.
coach_guide.warning: Standing too tall reduces power and shoulder engagement.

name: bear crawl
instructions: Start on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and your knees directly under your hips.
Lift your knees slightly off the ground, keeping your back flat and your core engaged.
Move your right hand and left foot forward simultaneously, followed by your left hand and right foot.
Continue crawling forward, alternating your hand and foot movements.
Maintain a steady pace and keep your core tight throughout the exercise.
Continue for the desired distance or time.

coach_guide.intro: Stay low with opposite hand and foot moving together.
coaching_cues: Hips stay low near the ground.
Opposite hand and foot move together.
Keep your core tight and engaged.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your whole body working especially shoulders and core. Your heart rate will spike quickly. Stay low and keep moving.
coach_guide.warning: Hips rising high turns this into an easy walk.

name: behind head chest stretch
instructions: Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Interlace your fingers behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Slowly squeeze your shoulder blades together and push your chest forward.
Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
Release the stretch and repeat as desired.

coach_guide.intro: Open up your chest by clasping hands behind your head.
coaching_cues: Fingers interlocked behind your head.
Pull elbows back and squeeze shoulder blades.
Breathe deep and hold the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a nice opening across your chest and front shoulders. Hold steady and let tension release. Great for posture reset.
coach_guide.warning: Pushing your head forward reduces the chest stretch.

name: bench dip (knees bent)
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with your hands gripping the edge next to your hips.
