Slide your butt off the bench and straighten your legs in front of you, keeping your heels on the ground.
Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stay close to the bench and let your triceps do all the lifting work.
coaching_cues: Back stays near the bench.
Lower with control, elbows back.
Push through your hands to rise.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this burning on the back of your arms, not your shoulders. If your shoulders hurt, you're dipping too far down. Feel that tricep fire and keep going with it.
coach_guide.warning: Drifting away from the bench puts dangerous stress on your shoulders.

name: bench dip on floor
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with your hands gripping the edge, fingers pointing forward.
Slide your butt off the bench, supporting your weight with your hands.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hands on floor behind you, dip straight down.
coaching_cues: Fingers point toward your heels.
Bend elbows straight back to ninety.
Press through palms and squeeze triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this burning in the back of your arms. Keep your hips close to your hands. Strong lockout at the top.
coach_guide.warning: Elbows flaring out shifts work away from triceps.

name: bench hip extension
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back against the bench and your feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands on the bench for support.
Engage your glutes and hamstrings, then lift your hips off the bench until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive your heel to the ceiling and squeeze glutes.
coaching_cues: Keep your hips square to floor.
Lift leg using only your glutes.
Pause at top and squeeze hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your glute cheek working. No swinging needed just controlled lifts. Build that glute strength.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back means you lifted too high.

name: bench pull-ups
instructions: Position yourself under a bar or a sturdy horizontal surface that is at chest height.
Grab the bar or surface with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Hang with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Pull your chest towards the bar or surface by squeezing your shoulder blades together and bending your elbows.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar or surface.
Lower yourself back down to the starting position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your chest to the bench, squeeze those lats hard.
coaching_cues: Drive elbows down toward your hips.
Chest touches the bench each rep.
Control the lower, no dropping down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep squeeze in your upper back and lats. If arms tire first, focus on elbow drive. Keep pulling strong.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with arms only means lats are not working.

name: bent knee lying twist (male)
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Extend your arms out to the sides, perpendicular to your body.
Keeping your knees together, slowly lower them to one side, aiming to touch the ground with your knees.
Pause for a moment, then engage your core and slowly lift your knees back to the starting position.
Repeat the movement to the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate from the hips while keeping shoulders planted down.
coaching_cues: Knees stay glued together throughout.
Lower legs slowly to each side.
Shoulders stay flat on the ground.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this in your glutes and obliques as you control the rotation. Keep the movement smooth and controlled. Great mobility work.
coach_guide.warning: Shoulders lifting off means you are rotating too far.

name: biceps leg concentration curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your legs spread apart and your feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand and place your elbow on the inside of your thigh, just above the knee.
With your palm facing up, curl the dumbbell towards your shoulder while keeping your upper arm stationary.
Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Use your leg as resistance and squeeze the bicep hard.
coaching_cues: Press arm firmly against your thigh.
Curl slow and squeeze at top.
Control the negative, no dropping down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in your bicep peak. If shoulder takes over, reposition your elbow. Squeeze every single rep.
coach_guide.warning: Elbow moving forward means you lost bicep isolation.

name: biceps narrow pull-ups
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your palms facing towards you and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and pull yourself up towards the bar, focusing on using your biceps to lift your body.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Narrow grip shifts the work to your biceps.
coaching_cues: Hands close together, palms facing you.
Pull chin over the bar each rep.
Squeeze biceps hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps burning more than your back. If forearms give out first, reset your grip. Keep squeezing at the top.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your body means you need to slow down.

name: biceps pull-up
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and pull yourself up by bending your elbows, bringing your chest towards the bar.
Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Palms toward you, focus on bicep squeeze at top.
coaching_cues: Pull elbows straight down to ribs.
Chin clears the bar every rep.
Lower with control, full arm extension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel biceps working hard through the entire pull. Back will help but biceps should burn. Strong work up there.
coach_guide.warning: Kipping or swinging steals the bicep tension away.

name: body-up
instructions: Start by placing your hands on a raised surface, such as a bench or parallel bars, with your palms facing down and fingers pointing forward.
Extend your legs out in front of you, keeping your heels on the ground and your body straight.
Lower your body by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides, until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then push through your palms to straighten your arms and lift your body back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press through your hands and squeeze triceps to lift.
coaching_cues: Keep elbows tight to your body.
Press up until arms fully extend.
Lower slow, keep core engaged tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel triceps burning as you press your body up. If shoulders ache, adjust hand position slightly. Push through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Elbows flaring out shifts work away from triceps.

name: bodyweight drop jump squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Jump up explosively, extending your hips, knees, and ankles.
While in mid-air, quickly bring your feet together.
Land softly on the balls of your feet and immediately drop back into a squat position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode up from the squat and land soft.
coaching_cues: Drop into squat, then explode up.
Land soft with knees slightly bent.
Reset quickly and go again fast.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel glutes and quads firing explosively on each jump. Heart rate will climb fast. Embrace the power.
coach_guide.warning: Landing with straight legs is hard on your knees.

name: bodyweight incline side plank
instructions: Start by lying on your side with your legs extended and stacked on top of each other.
Place your forearm on the ground directly below your shoulder, with your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle.
Engage your core and lift your hips off the ground, creating a straight line from your head to your feet.
Hold this position for the desired amount of time.
Lower your hips back down to the ground and repeat on the other side.

coach_guide.intro: Stack your body on the incline and brace your core.
coaching_cues: Hips stay high, no sagging down.
Top arm reaches straight to ceiling.
Squeeze obliques to hold the position.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques and core working to stabilize. The incline makes it easier to build strength. Hold strong and breathe.
coach_guide.warning: Hips dropping means your core stopped working.

name: bodyweight kneeling triceps extension
instructions: Kneel down on the ground with your knees hip-width apart.
Place your hands on the ground in front of you, shoulder-width apart, fingers pointing forward.
Extend your legs straight behind you, balancing on your toes and hands, forming a straight line from head to heels.
Bend your elbows and lower your upper body towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your hands to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lower your body by bending elbows, then press back up.
coaching_cues: Elbows point straight back behind you.
Lower forehead toward the ground slowly.
Press up and squeeze triceps hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel triceps burning through the full range of motion. Keep hips steady throughout the movement. Finish every rep strong.
coach_guide.warning: Hips sagging means core is not engaged enough.

name: bodyweight side lying biceps curl
instructions: Lie on your side with your legs extended and your head supported by your arm.
Hold your upper arm against your side and bend your elbow to curl your forearm towards your shoulder.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your forearm back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl using your bodyweight as the resistance here.
coaching_cues: Keep upper arm pinned to floor.
Curl your body toward your hand.
Squeeze bicep hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a unique bicep burn from this angle. Control the movement both directions. Great isolation work right there.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling your shoulder forward takes tension off biceps.

name: bodyweight squatting row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding onto a sturdy object or suspension trainer with your arms extended.
Lower your body into a squat position, keeping your back straight and your knees behind your toes.
From the squat position, pull your body up towards the object or suspension trainer, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your chest toward your hands while dropping into the squat.
coaching_cues: Drive elbows back and squeeze shoulder blades.
Sit hips back as you pull up.
Keep your chest proud throughout the movement.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this working between your shoulder blades and upper back. The squat adds a stretch at the bottom. Own that position and pull strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your upper back means you lost the squeeze.

name: bodyweight squatting row (with towel)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a towel in front of you with your palms facing down.
Bend your knees and lower your body into a squat position, keeping your back straight and your chest up.
As you lower into the squat, simultaneously pull the towel towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the squat, then slowly return to the starting position while extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Grip the towel tight and use it to increase your pulling range.
coaching_cues: Pull the towel apart as you row.
Elbows drive back past your ribcage.
Lower slowly to feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel deep engagement in your upper back and lats. The towel grip fires up your forearms too. Squeeze hard at the top every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting the towel go slack kills the tension.

name: bodyweight standing calf raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing forward.
Place your hands on a wall or stable surface for balance.
Slowly raise your heels off the ground, lifting your body weight onto the balls of your feet.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rise up tall on your toes — get as high as possible and squeeze at the top.
coaching_cues: Push through your big toes.
Rise as high as possible.
Squeeze hard at the peak.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this burning deep in your calves, right in the thick muscle belly. If you are not feeling it, focus on getting higher and squeezing harder at the top. Keep pushing through that calf burn.
coach_guide.warning: Not getting full range of motion limits the muscle activation.

name: bodyweight standing close-grip one arm row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a dumbbell in one hand with a neutral grip.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the dumbbell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Single arm work means full focus on one side at a time.
coaching_cues: Pull elbow tight to your side.
Rotate palm inward at the top.
Control the return without twisting your torso.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel one side of your upper back working hard. This close grip hits the inner back muscles. Stay square and feel that isolation.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating your body turns this into a cheat rep.

name: bodyweight standing close-grip row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Extend your arms straight in front of you, gripping the bar or handles with a close grip.
Pull the bar or handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Close grip targets your inner back and rear delts more.
coaching_cues: Keep elbows tucked close to ribs.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at top.
Stand tall and pull to lower chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a tight squeeze between your shoulder blades. The close grip emphasizes that inner back contraction. Make every rep count with a hard squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring elbows wide shifts work away from target.

name: bodyweight standing one arm row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a dumbbell in one hand.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Let the dumbbell hang straight down in front of you, with your arm fully extended.
Pull the dumbbell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Unilateral rows build balanced back strength and stability.
coaching_cues: Drive your elbow straight back powerfully.
Keep hips and shoulders square throughout.
Control the negative for three counts.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel one entire side of your back firing. Great for finding and fixing imbalances. Build that mind muscle connection one side at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your shoulder roll forward loses the contraction.

name: bodyweight standing one arm row (with towel)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a towel with one hand.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the towel towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the towel back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: The towel extends range of motion for deeper back work.
coaching_cues: Grip towel firmly and pull through elbow.
Keep working shoulder packed down tight.
Squeeze hard at peak contraction.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an extended stretch and strong contraction in your upper back. The towel lets you pull further back. Dominate each rep with control.
coach_guide.warning: Loose towel grip means lost tension and wasted reps.

name: bodyweight standing row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Grasp a bar or handles with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Keep your back straight and core engaged.
Pull the bar or handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement.
Slowly release and extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Classic bodyweight row hits your entire upper back.
coaching_cues: Pull chest toward your anchor point.
Elbows at forty five degrees from body.
Squeeze shoulder blades like cracking a walnut.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats and mid back doing the work. Keep your body in a straight line throughout. Strong pulls build a strong back.
coach_guide.warning: Sagging hips turn this into a weak partial rep.

name: bodyweight standing row (with towel)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a towel in front of you with both hands.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Pull the towel towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Towel grip adds forearm work and extended pulling range.
coaching_cues: Pull towel to lower chest level.
Keep tension on towel entire time.
Lower with control for full stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back and grip working together. The towel makes this harder in the best way. Embrace the burn and keep pulling.
coach_guide.warning: Jerky movements mean you need to slow down.

name: bottoms-up
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your arms by your sides.
Bend your knees and bring them towards your chest, keeping your feet off the ground.
Engaging your abs, lift your hips off the ground, bringing your knees towards your head.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lift your hips straight up toward the ceiling using lower abs.
coaching_cues: Press lower back into the floor.
Lift hips up not legs toward you.
Lower with control do not drop.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your lower abs below the belly button. Keep the movement small and controlled. That burn means your core is working.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum by swinging legs cheats your abs.

name: box jump down with one leg stabilization
instructions: Stand in front of a box or platform with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and jump onto the box, landing softly with one foot on the box and the other foot hanging off the edge.
Stabilize yourself on the box with the foot that is on it, while keeping the other foot off the ground.
Hold this position for a few seconds, engaging your calf muscles to maintain balance.
Slowly step down with the foot that is on the box, returning to the starting position.
Repeat the exercise with the other leg.

coach_guide.intro: Land softly on one leg and stick it like a gymnast.
coaching_cues: Step off the box do not jump.
Land quietly with knee slightly bent.
Hold the landing for three full seconds.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your calf and ankle stabilizers working hard. This builds real world balance and control. Stick that landing with confidence every time.
coach_guide.warning: Knee caving inward puts dangerous stress on joints.

name: bridge - mountain climber (cross body)
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line.
Engage your core and lift your right foot off the ground, bringing your right knee towards your left elbow.
Return your right foot to the starting position and repeat the movement with your left foot towards your right elbow.
Continue alternating sides, moving at a controlled pace.
Keep your hips level and avoid lifting your hips too high or sagging them too low.
Maintain a steady breathing pattern throughout the exercise.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your hips stable while driving knees across your body.
coaching_cues: Hips stay level, no rocking side.
Drive knee toward opposite elbow tight.
Keep your core braced the entire time.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your obliques and lower abs. If your lower back aches, slow down. Stay tight and keep moving.
coach_guide.warning: Hips bouncing up and down means you lost core control.

name: burpee
instructions: Start in a standing position with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and placing your hands on the floor in front of you.
Kick your feet back into a push-up position.
Perform a push-up, keeping your body in a straight line.
Jump your feet back into the squat position.
Jump up explosively, reaching your arms overhead.
Land softly and immediately lower back into a squat position to begin the next repetition.

coach_guide.intro: Move fast but stay in control through every single transition.
coaching_cues: Explode up on that jump.
Keep your core tight throughout.
Land soft, move fast.
coach_guide.feel: Your heart should be pounding and you should feel this in your legs, core, and lungs all at once. If you're getting sloppy, slow down just enough to maintain form. Push through that full-body burn.
coach_guide.warning: Sloppy form leads to injury when you're moving this fast.

name: butt-ups
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands by your sides, palms facing down.
Engaging your abs, lift your legs off the ground, bringing your knees towards your chest.
At the top of the movement, squeeze your abs and pause for a moment.
Slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Push your hips straight up by squeezing your abs hard.
coaching_cues: Drive hips up, not back overhead.
Keep legs as straight as possible now.
Lower with control, do not just drop.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in your lower abs at the top. If you feel lower back strain, reduce the range. Push through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging momentum up means your abs are not doing the work.

name: butterfly yoga pose
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended in front of you.
Bend your knees and bring the soles of your feet together, allowing your knees to fall out to the sides.
Hold onto your ankles or feet with your hands.
Sit up tall and lengthen your spine.
Gently press your knees down towards the floor, feeling a stretch in your inner thighs.
Hold this position for a few breaths.
To release, slowly bring your knees back up and extend your legs.

coach_guide.intro: Let gravity gently open your hips while staying tall.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, lengthen through your spine.
Let knees fall naturally toward floor.
Breathe deep and relax into the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a gentle stretch through your inner thighs and groin. If there is sharp pain, ease off the depth. Let your body soften and open.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back forward means you lost the hip stretch.

name: cable alternate shoulder press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and grasp the handles of the cable machine with an overhand grip.
Position your hands at shoulder height, with your palms facing forward.
Keep your core engaged and your back straight.
Press one handle up and forward until your arm is fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handle back to the starting position.
Repeat with the other arm.
Alternate between arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press one arm at a time with full shoulder control.
coaching_cues: Press up and slightly inward overhead.
Keep your core tight, no leaning.
Lower slow, feel the shoulder work.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel each shoulder working independently through the full press. If your back arches, brace harder. Own every single rep.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning to one side means you are compensating with momentum.

name: cable alternate triceps extension
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the cable handle with your right hand and bring your arm up so that your upper arm is parallel to the ground and your elbow is bent at a 90-degree angle.
Keep your upper arm stationary and extend your forearm backward, fully straightening your arm.
Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat with your left arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pin that upper arm parallel to the floor and only move your forearm.
coaching_cues: Upper arm stays completely still.
Extend back, squeeze at the end.
Switch arms, maintain the angle.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel isolated burn on the back of your working arm. If your shoulder is working, your upper arm is moving too much. Lock into that single-arm tricep fire and carry it through.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your upper arm moves, your shoulder takes over the work.

name: cable assisted inverse leg curl
instructions: Adjust the cable machine so that the ankle attachment is at the lowest setting.
Lie face down on the bench with your legs straight and the ankle attachment secured to your ankles.
Hold onto the handles of the bench for stability.
Keeping your upper body stationary, exhale and curl your legs up towards your glutes by flexing your knees.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your hamstrings.
Inhale and slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the cable to assist while you master the leg curl.
coaching_cues: Pull heels toward glutes with control.
Keep hips pressed into the pad firmly.
Resist the weight on the way back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your hamstrings working hard through the entire curl. If you cramp, ease up slightly. Build that hamstring strength now.
coach_guide.warning: Hips lifting off the pad means you are using too much weight.

name: cable bar lateral pulldown
instructions: Adjust the cable pulley to a high position and attach a straight bar.
Sit facing the cable machine with your feet flat on the ground and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lean back slightly and keep your chest up, maintaining a slight arch in your lower back.
Pull the bar down towards your chest, leading with your elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Think about pulling your elbows to your back pockets, not the bar to your chest.
coaching_cues: Elbows drive down and back.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Chest stays proud and open.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong squeeze between your shoulder blades and down the sides of your back. If it's burning in your biceps instead, you're pulling wrong. Lock into that back squeeze and keep it going.
coach_guide.warning: The second you start leaning back too far, you turn this into a rowing movement.

name: cable bench press
instructions: Adjust the cable machine to chest height and attach the handles.
Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and step forward to create tension in the cables.
Position your feet firmly on the ground and engage your core.
Bend your elbows and bring your hands to shoulder level, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Push the handles forward, extending your arms fully in front of you.
Pause for a moment, then slowly reverse the movement, bringing your hands back to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press with constant tension through the entire range of motion.
coaching_cues: Drive handles together at the top.
Keep shoulder blades pinched on bench.
Control the cables back, no snapping.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel constant tension across your chest the whole time. If shoulders take over, adjust your angle. Squeeze hard at the top.
coach_guide.warning: Elbows flaring too wide means shoulders are taking over.

name: cable close grip curl
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a low pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Exhale and curl the bar up towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl with your biceps only — keep those elbows locked tight to your sides.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay pinned to your sides.
Squeeze your biceps at the top.
Slow and controlled on the way down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this burning right in the peak of your biceps with constant tension from the cable. If your shoulders are creeping forward, step back and reset. Keep that bicep burn going strong through every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbows drift forward turns this into a shoulder exercise instead of pure bicep work.

name: cable concentration curl
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the floor and your knees slightly bent.
Hold the cable handle with an underhand grip and rest your elbow against the inside of your thigh.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the cable handle towards your shoulder while contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then inhale and slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Isolate one bicep at a time with zero momentum.
coaching_cues: Pin your elbow against inner thigh.
Curl up and squeeze at the top.
Lower slowly, keep the tension on.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense squeeze in your bicep peak. If your elbow drifts, reset your position. Make every rep count now.
coach_guide.warning: Elbow moving off your thigh means you are cheating the curl.

name: cable concentration extension (on knee)
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Hold the cable handle with your right hand and place your elbow on the inside of your right knee.
Extend your arm fully, keeping your elbow stationary and close to your knee.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arm back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Lock your arm and extend only from the elbow.
coaching_cues: Keep upper arm completely locked still.
Squeeze tricep hard at full extension.
Lower with control, no swinging back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong burn in the back of your arm. If your shoulder moves, you are using too much weight. Isolate and squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Upper arm moving means you lost the tricep isolation.

name: cable cross-over lateral pulldown
instructions: Attach a cable handle to each side of a cable machine at shoulder height.
Stand in the middle of the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and step back to create tension in the cables.
Lean forward slightly from the hips, keeping your back straight and your chest up.
Pull the handles down and across your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull both handles down and across to fully engage your lats.
coaching_cues: Drive elbows down toward your hips.
Squeeze your lats hard at bottom.
Control the return, stay under tension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats working through a wide range of motion. If you feel mostly arms, focus on elbow drive. Crush that contraction.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with just arms means your lats are not engaged.

name: cable cross-over revers fly
instructions: Attach a D-handle to each low pulley cable and stand in the middle of the cable crossover machine.
Grasp the handles with a pronated grip (palms facing down) and take a step forward, positioning your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees slightly and lean forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your abs engaged.
With your arms extended out to the sides and slightly bent at the elbows, exhale and squeeze your shoulder blades together as you pull the cables back and upward in a reverse fly motion.
Pause for a moment at the peak contraction, then inhale and slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Cross the cables behind you to hit rear delts hard.
coaching_cues: Lead with elbows, not your hands.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at back.
Control forward, do not let cables snap.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this burning in your rear delts and upper back. If you feel traps taking over, lower the weight. Keep that squeeze going.
coach_guide.warning: Shrugging up means your traps stole the movement.

name: cable cross-over variation
instructions: Adjust the cable pulleys to chest height.
Stand in the center of the cable machine with one foot in front of the other.
Grasp the handles with your palms facing down and your arms extended out to the sides.
Take a step forward, keeping your arms slightly bent.
With a slight bend in your elbows, bring your hands together in front of your chest.
Pause for a moment, then slowly return your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bring your hands together like you're hugging a tree trunk.
coaching_cues: Keep a slight bend in elbows.
Squeeze your chest at the middle.
Control the stretch on the way out.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch across your entire chest when your arms go wide, then a hard squeeze when they come together. That stretch-squeeze combo is what builds the muscle. Keep going with that chest pump.
coach_guide.warning: Use your arms like they're straight and you'll stress your elbows.

name: cable curl
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the cable attachment with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and curl the cable attachment towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the cable attachment back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep elbows pinned to your sides throughout the curl.
coaching_cues: Squeeze biceps hard at the top.
Lower with control, feel the stretch.
Elbows stay glued to your ribs.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel constant tension in your biceps throughout. The cable keeps muscles working even at bottom. Embrace that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Elbows drifting forward means shoulders are taking over.

name: cable deadlift
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend at the hips and knees, lowering your torso until your back is parallel to the ground.
Grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip, keeping your arms straight and your shoulders back.
Engage your glutes and hamstrings to lift the cable handles, extending your hips and standing up straight.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the cable handles back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge at hips and drive through your heels powerfully.
coaching_cues: Push hips back, chest stays proud.
Squeeze glutes hard at the top.
Keep the cable path straight up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your glutes and hamstrings. Lower back stays neutral not rounded. Drive those hips forward strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts your spine at risk.

name: cable decline fly
instructions: Adjust the cable machine to a decline position.
Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the handles with your palms facing forward and your arms extended straight out in front of you.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, open your arms out to the sides in a controlled motion.
Pause for a moment at the fully extended position, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Arc the cables down and together squeezing your chest.
coaching_cues: Slight bend in elbows stays locked.
Bring handles together below chest level.
Control the stretch on the way back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch across your lower chest. Squeeze hard when hands meet at bottom. Build that chest definition.
coach_guide.warning: Straightening arms turns this into a press movement.

name: cable decline one arm press
instructions: Adjust the cable machine to a decline position.
Stand facing away from the machine and grab the handle with one hand.
Position yourself with your back against the decline bench and your arm extended straight in front of you.
Bend your elbow and lower the handle towards your chest while keeping your upper arm stationary.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the handle back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Press down and across your body with control.
coaching_cues: Drive handle down toward opposite hip.
Keep shoulder blade pinned to bench.
Squeeze chest hard at full extension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower and inner chest working hard. Single arm lets you focus on each side. Push through and stay strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting shoulder lift off bench reduces chest activation.

name: cable decline press
instructions: Adjust the cable machine to a decline position.
Sit on the decline bench facing the cable machine.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position them at chest level.
Keep your feet flat on the ground and your back firmly against the bench.
Exhale and push the handles away from your body, extending your arms fully.
Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, squeezing your chest muscles.
Inhale and slowly return the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press both handles down targeting your lower chest fibers.
coaching_cues: Drive handles down toward your hips.
Keep shoulder blades squeezed into bench.
Control the path back to start.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower chest burning with each rep. Constant cable tension makes this extra effective. Finish every rep strong.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring elbows too wide shifts stress to shoulders.

name: cable decline seated wide-grip row
instructions: Sit on the decline bench facing the cable machine with your feet securely placed on the footrests.
Grasp the cable attachment with a wide overhand grip, palms facing down.
Lean back slightly, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the cable towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the contraction, then slowly release the cable back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull wide to your upper abs squeezing shoulder blades.
coaching_cues: Lead with elbows out to sides.
Squeeze shoulder blades together hard.
Chest stays lifted throughout the row.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back and rear delts working. The decline angle hits different fibers. Keep that back tight and strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding forward means you lost your upper back.

name: cable drag curl
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the cable attachment with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the cable attachment towards your shoulders by contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the cable attachment back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drag the bar up your body keeping elbows behind you.
coaching_cues: Elbows travel straight back behind you.
Bar stays close to your torso.
Squeeze biceps hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense bicep peak contraction. This targets the long head for better peaks. Keep dragging and squeezing.
coach_guide.warning: Elbows coming forward turns this into regular curls.

name: cable floor seated wide-grip row
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended and your back straight.
Attach a cable handle to a low pulley and position the cable machine behind you.
Grasp the handle with a wide overhand grip, palms facing down.
Lean back slightly, keeping your back straight and your chest lifted.
Pull the handle towards your waist, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit tall and pull wide to your midsection powerfully.
coaching_cues: Drive elbows out wide to sides.
Squeeze shoulder blades at peak contraction.
Keep your torso upright and stable.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire upper back lighting up. Wide grip emphasizes rear delts and rhomboids. Pull strong every single rep.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning back too far uses momentum not muscles.

name: cable forward raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Hold the cable handle with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and your arms fully extended in front of you.
Keeping your arms straight, raise the cable handle up to shoulder level.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the cable handle back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise the cable forward with a slight elbow bend.
coaching_cues: Lift to shoulder height no higher.
Keep core tight and body still.
Lower slowly with full control now.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the front of your shoulders burning. Constant tension makes this harder than dumbbells. Stay controlled and keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weight up means you went too heavy.

name: cable front raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and grasp the cable handle with an overhand grip.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Raise the cable handle in front of you, keeping your arms straight and your palms facing down.
Continue lifting until your arms are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive arms forward targeting your front deltoid heads.
coaching_cues: Raise handles to shoulder height only.
Keep slight bend in your elbows.
Control the descent do not drop.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your front delts working continuously. Cable tension hits muscles the entire time. Build those boulder shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Going above shoulder height shifts work to traps.

name: cable front shoulder raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and grasp the cable handle with an overhand grip.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Raise the cable handle in front of you, keeping your arms straight and your palms facing down.
Continue lifting until your arms are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your arm straight and lift from the shoulder.
coaching_cues: Arm stays straight, lead with knuckles.
Stop at shoulder height, no higher.
Control the cable all the way down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this in the front of your shoulders. If your traps burn, lighten up. Stay strong and controlled.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weight means you are using momentum not muscle.

name: cable hammer curl (with rope)
instructions: Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Hold the cable rope attachment with an underhand grip, palms facing each other, and your arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the cable rope attachment until your biceps are fully contracted and the rope is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the cable rope attachment back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your palms facing each other throughout the curl.
coaching_cues: Elbows glued to your sides always.
Squeeze hard at the top position.
Lower slow, fight the cable down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this in your biceps and forearms equally. If elbows drift forward, reset your position. Keep squeezing every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Elbows moving forward takes tension off the biceps.

name: cable high pulley overhead tricep extension
instructions: Attach a rope to a high pulley and stand facing away from the machine.
Grasp the rope with both hands and extend your arms overhead.
Keep your elbows close to your head and your upper arms stationary.
Slowly lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lock your elbows high and extend only at the elbow.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay fixed pointing forward.
Stretch deep then squeeze triceps hard.
Keep your core tight, no arching.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and burn in your triceps. If shoulders ache, check your elbow position. Push through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Elbows dropping down shifts work away from triceps.

name: cable high row (kneeling)
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a cable machine at chest height.
Kneel down in front of the cable machine and grab the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Sit back on your heels, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the bar towards your upper abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the bar back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull high toward your face and squeeze your upper back.
coaching_cues: Drive elbows high and wide back.
Squeeze shoulder blades together hard.
Keep your chest proud, stay tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this between your shoulder blades and rear delts. If your biceps fatigue first, focus on elbow drive. Great work on that squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too low turns this into a regular row.

name: cable hip adduction
instructions: Attach the ankle cuff to your ankle and stand facing the cable machine.
Position yourself far enough away from the machine so that there is tension on the cable.
Place your hands on the machine for support.
Keeping your leg straight, slowly move your leg across your body towards the midline.
Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your leg across your body like you're trying to kick a soccer ball with your inner thigh.
coaching_cues: Leg straight, pull across your body.
Squeeze your inner thigh to move.
Control the return, keep tension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your inner thigh and groin area. It's a weird muscle to isolate, but when you find it, you'll know. Keep that inner thigh burn and push through it.
coach_guide.warning: Using your hip or core to swing the leg misses the inner thigh completely.

name: cable incline bench press
instructions: Adjust the bench to a 45-degree incline.
Attach the cable handles to the high pulleys.
Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and bring them to shoulder height.
Push the handles forward and upward until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press up and squeeze your chest at the top.
coaching_cues: Push handles together at the top.
Keep shoulders pinned to the bench.
Lower with control, stretch the chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel constant tension across your chest. If front shoulders burn more, adjust your angle. Keep that chest working.
coach_guide.warning: Shoulders rolling forward means you lost your back position.

name: cable incline bench row
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle and attach a cable handle to the low pulley.
Sit on the bench facing the cable machine with your feet flat on the floor and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the cable handle with an overhand grip and extend your arms fully in front of you.
Lean forward from your hips while keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the cable handle towards your chest by retracting your shoulder blades and bending your elbows.
Squeeze your back muscles at the top of the movement, then slowly extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Row toward your chest and squeeze your upper back.
coaching_cues: Pull elbows back toward your hips.
Chest stays pressed into the bench.
Squeeze shoulder blades every single rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this across your upper back and lats. If biceps tire first, think elbows not hands. Strong pulling motion.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your chest off the bench means too heavy.

name: cable incline fly
instructions: Adjust the cable machine to a low position and attach the handles.
Sit on an incline bench with your back against the pad and feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, open your arms out to the sides in a controlled motion.
Pause for a moment at the fully extended position, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Wide arc motion focusing on squeezing the chest together.
coaching_cues: Slight bend in elbows stays locked.
Bring handles together over upper chest.
Stretch wide but keep shoulders safe.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and squeeze in your upper chest. If shoulders feel strained, reduce the range. Great chest isolation.
coach_guide.warning: Bending elbows too much turns this into a press.

name: cable incline fly (on stability ball)
instructions: Set up a stability ball at an incline angle.
Attach the cable handles to the high pulleys of a cable machine.
Sit on the stability ball facing away from the machine, with your feet firmly planted on the ground.
Grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip, palms facing forward.
Lean forward slightly, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
With a controlled motion, bring your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Continue the motion until your arms are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stabilize on the ball while flying the cables together.
coaching_cues: Keep hips up and core engaged.
Squeeze handles together over your chest.
Control the stretch, do not collapse.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest and core working together. If you wobble too much, plant feet wider. Stay stable and strong.
coach_guide.warning: Hips dropping means you lost your core engagement.

name: cable incline pushdown
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lean forward slightly and keep your back straight.
Pull the bar down towards your thighs by extending your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive the bar down in an arc to squeeze your lats.
coaching_cues: Keep arms straight throughout movement.
Pull down toward your hips forcefully.
Squeeze lats hard at the bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your lats under your armpits. If triceps burn more, keep arms straighter. Finish strong every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your elbows shifts work to your triceps.

name: cable incline triceps extension
instructions: Adjust the cable machine to a low pulley position.
Attach a straight bar to the cable.
Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight overhead.
Lean forward slightly, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Bend your elbows and lower the bar behind your head, keeping your upper arms close to your ears.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows fixed and extend fully behind you.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay in position, only forearms move.
Squeeze triceps hard at full extension.
Lower slow, get that deep stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the long head of your triceps stretching and burning. If elbows drift, reset your position. Keep pushing through.
coach_guide.warning: Elbows moving means you are losing tricep isolation.

name: cable judo flip
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the cable handle with both hands at chest level, palms facing down.
Engage your core and rotate your torso to the right, pulling the cable across your body.
As you rotate, pivot your back foot and allow your hips to rotate naturally.
Extend your arms fully and finish the movement by flipping the cable handle over your shoulder.
Return to the starting position by reversing the movement, rotating your torso back to the center.
Repeat the movement on the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use your core to rotate and pull the cable down like you are throwing an opponent.
coaching_cues: Brace your abs tight before pulling.
Rotate your torso, not just arms.
Control the return, resist the cable.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn across your entire midsection as you rotate. Your obliques will work hard on the twist. Stay powerful through every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with your arms removes all the core engagement.

name: cable kickback
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the cable handle with your right hand and step back to create tension in the cable.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Keep your upper arm close to your body and your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle.
Extend your forearm backward, straightening your arm fully.
Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your upper arm locked in place and extend only from the elbow.
coaching_cues: Upper arm stays parallel to floor.
Squeeze your tricep hard at lockout.
Slow return, keep constant tension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the back of your arm burning with each extension. If your shoulder moves, you are swinging too much. Isolate that tricep and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your shoulder turns this into a useless movement.

name: cable kneeling crunch
instructions: Attach a rope handle to a high pulley and kneel down facing away from the machine.
Hold the rope handle with both hands and place it behind your head, keeping your elbows out to the sides.
Keeping your hips stationary, flex your waist and crunch your torso down towards your thighs.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your hips still and crunch your ribcage down toward your pelvis.
coaching_cues: Hips stay locked in position.
Crunch ribcage down toward pelvis.
Feel the squeeze at the bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn right in your upper abs now. If you feel it in your hip flexors or lower back, focus on keeping your hips completely still. Lock onto that deep abdominal burn and keep crunching through it.
coach_guide.warning: Any hip movement turns this into a hip flexor exercise instead of abs.

name: cable kneeling rear delt row (with rope) (male)
instructions: Attach a rope handle to a low cable pulley and kneel down facing the machine.
Grasp the rope with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and extend your arms fully in front of you.
Keeping your back straight and core engaged, pull the rope towards your body by retracting your shoulder blades.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement and hold for a brief pause.
Slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the rope toward your face while keeping your elbows high and wide.
coaching_cues: Kneel tall with core braced tight.
Elbows stay high, pull to your ears.
Squeeze your rear delts at peak.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn in the back of your shoulders near your shoulder blades. If your traps take over, lower the weight. Keep targeting those rear delts.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your elbows shifts work to your lats instead.

name: cable kneeling triceps extension
instructions: Attach a rope handle to a high pulley and kneel down facing the cable machine.
Grasp the rope with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and bring your hands to the sides of your head.
Keep your elbows close to your head and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Extend your forearms by contracting your triceps until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position by bending your elbows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your upper arms vertical and extend straight down using only your triceps.
coaching_cues: Upper arms stay beside your ears.
Extend fully and squeeze at bottom.
Lower slowly, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense stretch at the top and a hard squeeze at the bottom. Your triceps will be on fire. Push through and earn that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbows flare forward reduces tricep activation.

name: cable lat pulldown full range of motion
instructions: Sit on the lat pulldown machine with your knees positioned under the pads.
Grasp the cable bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lean back slightly and keep your chest up, maintaining a slight arch in your lower back.
Pull the bar down towards your upper chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the bar back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the bar all the way down while driving your elbows deep behind you.
coaching_cues: Lean back slightly, chest up.
Pull elbows down and back hard.
Squeeze your lats at the bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire lat muscle working from armpit to lower back. The full range gives maximum stretch and contraction. Own every inch of this movement.
coach_guide.warning: Cutting the range short leaves gains on the table.

name: cable lateral pulldown (with rope attachment)
instructions: Attach a rope attachment to the cable machine at a high position.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the rope with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Keep your back straight and lean slightly back.
Pull the rope down towards your sides, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement.
Slowly release the tension and allow the rope to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the rope down and apart to fully engage your lat muscles.
coaching_cues: Spread the rope wide at bottom.
Drive elbows down toward your hips.
Control the rope back up slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong squeeze in your outer lats when you spread the rope. Your back should feel wide and engaged. Keep pulling with purpose.
coach_guide.warning: Keeping hands together limits your lat contraction.

name: cable lateral pulldown with v-bar
instructions: Sit down on the cable pulldown machine and grab the v-bar attachment with an overhand grip.
Adjust the knee pad so that your thighs are secured under it.
Keep your back straight and lean back slightly.
Pull the v-bar down towards your upper chest while keeping your elbows close to your body.
Squeeze your back muscles at the bottom of the movement.
Slowly return the v-bar to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the V-bar to pull with a neutral grip and target your inner lats.
coaching_cues: Grip tight, pull to upper chest.
Squeeze shoulder blades together hard.
Slow controlled return every rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats and middle back working together on each pull. The neutral grip is easier on your shoulders. Build that thick back one rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your shoulders forward limits back engagement.

name: cable lateral raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip.
Keep your arms straight and your core engaged.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull from your side delts, not your hands — keep those shoulders locked down.
coaching_cues: Lead with your elbows, not your hands.
Shoulders stay down and back.
Slow and controlled on the way down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn on the outer sides of your shoulders right now. If it is creeping into your neck or upper traps, drop the weight. Lock in that side-delt burn and keep going.
coach_guide.warning: The second your shoulders start shrugging up, you have lost the target.

name: cable low fly
instructions: Attach the handles to the low pulleys of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
Stand in the middle of the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Maintaining control, slowly bring your arms forward in a sweeping motion, crossing them in front of your body.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, feeling the stretch in your chest muscles.
Reverse the motion and slowly return your arms to the starting position, keeping tension on your chest muscles throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sweep the cables up from low to high like you are hugging a barrel.
coaching_cues: Slight bend in elbows throughout.
Squeeze your chest at the top.
Lower with control, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your chest at the bottom and a strong squeeze at the top. This targets your upper and inner chest beautifully. Embrace that pump.
coach_guide.warning: Straightening your arms turns this into a shoulder exercise.

name: cable low seated row
instructions: Sit on the machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward, maintaining a slight bend in your elbows.
Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit tall and pull the handle to your lower chest while squeezing your back.
coaching_cues: Chest up, do not round forward.
Pull elbows straight back, squeeze.
Control forward, keep tension constant.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your mid and lower back muscles contracting hard on every pull. Your posture should feel stronger with each rep. Build that powerful back.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts your spine at risk.

name: cable lying bicep curl
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a low pulley cable machine.
Lie face up on a flat bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms fully, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the bar up towards your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie flat and curl the cable toward your shoulders keeping upper arms still.
coaching_cues: Pin your elbows to the floor.
Curl up and squeeze your biceps.
Lower slowly, maintain full tension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working through the entire range with constant resistance. The lying position removes all cheating. Focus and feel every contraction.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your elbows off the floor reduces bicep isolation.

name: cable lying close-grip curl
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a low pulley cable machine.
Lie face up on a flat bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms fully, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, curl the bar towards your chest by contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Slowly lower the bar back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your upper arms glued to the floor and curl with pure bicep power.
coaching_cues: Pin elbows down, wrists stay neutral.
Squeeze biceps hard at the top.
Lower slowly, feel the cable tension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your biceps throughout the entire range. If your shoulders lift off the floor, you are using momentum. Stay flat and let your biceps do all the work.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your shoulders off the floor takes tension away from your biceps.

name: cable lying extension pullover (with rope attachment)
instructions: Attach a rope to a cable machine and set the pulley at the highest position.
Lie down on a bench with your head towards the cable machine.
Hold the rope with both hands and extend your arms straight up above your chest.
Keeping your arms straight, slowly lower the rope behind your head while maintaining control.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly raise the rope back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the rope in a wide arc while keeping your arms nearly straight throughout.
coaching_cues: Start with arms overhead, slight bend.
Pull down in an arc toward hips.
Squeeze your lats hard at the bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats stretching overhead and contracting as you pull down. If your elbows bend too much, your triceps take over. Keep that long arc and feel your back work.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your elbows too much turns this into a tricep exercise.

name: cable lying fly
instructions: Attach the handles to the cables and lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Hold the handles with your palms facing each other and your arms extended straight above your chest.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sweep the cables together in a hugging motion while lying flat on the bench.
coaching_cues: Keep a slight bend in your elbows.
Bring handles together over mid chest.
Control the stretch on the way down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch across your chest at the bottom and a strong squeeze at the top. If your shoulders roll forward, reset your position. Keep your chest proud and squeeze those pecs.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your shoulders roll forward puts unnecessary stress on the joint.

name: cable lying triceps extension v. 2
instructions: Attach a rope handle to a low pulley cable machine.
Lie down on a flat bench facing up, with your head towards the cable machine.
Grasp the rope handle with both hands, palms facing each other, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the rope handle towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Extend from your elbows only while keeping your upper arms completely still.
coaching_cues: Lock upper arms in position, elbows up.
Extend arms fully, squeeze triceps hard.
Lower with control, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps burning from elbow to shoulder on every rep. If your elbows drift back, you are losing tension. Keep those upper arms locked and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Moving your elbows shifts the work away from your triceps.

name: cable middle fly
instructions: Attach cables to both sides of a cable machine at chest height.
Stand in the center of the machine with one foot slightly in front of the other.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms out to the sides.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and maintain a slight forward lean.
Engage your chest muscles and bring your arms forward in a sweeping motion.
Pause for a moment at the center, then slowly return your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bring the cables together at chest height with a big hugging motion.
coaching_cues: Stand centered, slight forward lean.
Squeeze handles together, chest tight.
Control the return, keep tension constant.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your inner chest working hard as the handles meet. If your arms feel more tired than your chest, soften your elbows more. Find that squeeze and keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Standing too far forward reduces the resistance on your pecs.

name: cable one arm bent over row
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Grasp the cable handle with one hand, palm facing inward, and extend your arm fully.
Pull the cable handle towards your body, keeping your elbow close to your side, until your hand reaches your lower chest.
Pause for a moment, then slowly extend your arm back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the handle toward your hip while rotating your torso slightly for full contraction.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward, brace your core tight.
Pull elbow back past your ribcage.
Squeeze your shoulder blade at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lat and mid back muscles contracting with each pull. If your lower back rounds, you are pulling too heavy. Stay braced and row with control.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts your spine in a vulnerable position.

name: cable one arm curl
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the cable handle with an underhand grip, palm facing up.
Keep your elbow close to your side and slowly curl your forearm up towards your shoulder.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your forearm back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl one arm at a time while keeping your body completely still.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, elbow pinned at side.
Curl up with a slight wrist turn.
Lower slowly, maintain constant tension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your bicep working from bottom to top without any swinging. If your shoulder dips or your torso twists, go lighter. Isolate that bicep and make it burn.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your body uses momentum instead of muscle.

name: cable one arm decline chest fly
instructions: Attach a D-handle to a low pulley cable machine and set the bench to a decline angle.
Lie down on the bench with your head towards the machine and grab the handle with your right hand.
Extend your arm straight up above your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
With a controlled motion, lower your arm out to the side until your hand is in line with your shoulder.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the motion and bring your arm back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to your left arm and repeat the exercise.

coach_guide.intro: Fly the cable in an arc while lying on a decline to hit your lower chest.
coaching_cues: Set decline bench, brace your legs.
Arc the cable across lower chest.
Squeeze at top, slow on the return.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the lower portion of your chest stretching and squeezing through each rep. If you feel it in your shoulders, raise your arm path slightly. Keep targeting that lower chest.
coach_guide.warning: Going too heavy compromises form and strains your shoulder joint.

name: cable one arm fly on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a cable handle in one hand and extend your arm out to the side, parallel to the ground.
Keep your elbow slightly bent and your palm facing forward.
Slowly bring your arm across your body, squeezing your chest muscles.
Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Balance on the ball while flying one arm to challenge your chest and core together.
coaching_cues: Plant feet wide, squeeze the ball.
Fly arm across chest, stay stable.
Return with control, no wobbling allowed.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working while your core fights to keep you balanced. If you rock side to side, lighten the weight. Stay stable and let your chest do the work.
coach_guide.warning: Losing balance on the ball can cause you to drop the weight.

name: cable one arm incline fly on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back against an incline bench.
Hold a cable handle in one hand with your arm extended and palm facing inward.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbow, slowly lower your arm out to the side until your hand is in line with your shoulder.
Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring your arm back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Position on an incline to target your upper chest while balancing on the ball.
coaching_cues: Walk feet out, create incline angle.
Fly arm up and across upper chest.
Squeeze at top, lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper chest and front shoulder working together on each rep. If your hips sag, reset your position on the ball. Stay tight and power through.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips drop puts strain on your lower back.

name: cable one arm incline press
instructions: Adjust the cable machine to a low pulley position.
Sit on an incline bench facing away from the cable machine.
Grasp the handle with one hand and bring it up to shoulder height.
