Position your feet firmly on the ground and maintain a stable position.
Press the handle forward and upward, extending your arm fully.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Press the cable at an angle to hit your upper chest fibers.
coaching_cues: Keep your shoulder blade pinned back tight.
Drive your arm up and across your body.
Squeeze your chest hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in your upper chest near your collarbone. If your shoulder is taking over, adjust the angle slightly. Keep that mind muscle connection strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your shoulder roll forward puts stress on the joint.

name: cable one arm incline press on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back resting against an incline bench.
Hold a cable handle in one hand and position your arm at a 90-degree angle with your elbow bent.
Press the cable handle forward and upward, extending your arm fully.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: The unstable ball forces your core to work while you press.
coaching_cues: Brace your core and stay balanced throughout.
Press up and inward with controlled power.
Lower slowly and feel the chest stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper chest working along with your core stabilizers. If you are wobbling too much, plant your feet wider. Stay steady and keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement on the ball risks losing balance and form.

name: cable one arm lateral bent-over
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, facing a cable machine.
Grasp the handle with one hand and step back to create tension on the cable.
Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Extend your arm out to the side, parallel to the ground, with a slight bend in your elbow.
Slowly bring your arm back to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: This bent-over cable fly targets your inner chest fibers.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward and keep your back flat.
Sweep the cable across your body smoothly.
Squeeze your chest at the end position.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep squeeze in the center of your chest. If your lower back is straining, bend your knees more. Keep that chest engaged the whole time.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts unnecessary strain on your spine.

name: cable one arm lateral raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, facing the cable machine.
Hold the cable handle with one hand, palm facing down, and stand far enough away from the machine so that there is tension on the cable.
Keep your arm straight and slowly raise it out to the side until it is parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arm back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Raise the cable out to the side to isolate your shoulder cap.
coaching_cues: Lead with your elbow not your hand.
Lift until your arm is parallel to floor.
Lower with control and resist the weight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a burn on the outer part of your shoulder. If your traps are taking over, lower the weight slightly. Keep those delts firing strong.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weight uses momentum instead of your muscles.

name: cable one arm preacher curl
instructions: Adjust the cable machine so that the preacher curl pad is at chest height.
Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the cable handle with an underhand grip and position your upper arm against the preacher curl pad.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Slowly curl the cable handle towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm against the pad.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch arms and repeat the exercise.

coach_guide.intro: The preacher position locks your arm for strict bicep isolation.
coaching_cues: Keep your upper arm pressed against pad.
Curl up and squeeze your bicep hard.
Lower slowly and feel the full stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense pump in your bicep peak. If your elbow is lifting off the pad, go lighter. Stay strict and watch those biceps grow.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your elbow shifts the work away from your bicep.

name: cable one arm press on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a cable handle in one hand and position your arm at chest height, elbow bent.
Place your other hand on your hip for stability.
Press the cable handle forward, extending your arm fully.
Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Press on the ball to challenge your stability and chest.
coaching_cues: Plant your feet wide for solid balance.
Press forward and squeeze your chest tight.
Control the return and stay balanced throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working hard along with your core. If balance is difficult, focus on bracing your midsection. You have got this, stay controlled.
coach_guide.warning: Moving too fast on the ball compromises your stability.

name: cable one arm pulldown
instructions: Attach a single handle to a high pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the handle with an overhand grip and extend your arm fully.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the handle down towards your side while keeping your elbow close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your lat muscle.
Slowly release the handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch sides and repeat the exercise with the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Pull down one arm at a time to focus each lat individually.
coaching_cues: Drive your elbow straight down to hip.
Squeeze your lat hard at the bottom.
Resist the cable slowly on the way up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lat muscle working from armpit to waist. If your bicep is burning first, focus on elbow drive. Build that back one rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with your hand instead of elbow overworks your arm.

name: cable one arm reverse preacher curl
instructions: Adjust the cable machine so that the pulley is at the lowest position.
Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the cable handle with an underhand grip and position your upper arm against the preacher bench pad.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged throughout the exercise.
Slowly curl your forearm towards your bicep, keeping your upper arm stationary against the pad.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your bicep.
Slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch arms and repeat the exercise.

coach_guide.intro: Reverse grip on the preacher targets your brachialis and forearms.
coaching_cues: Keep your palm facing down the whole time.
Curl up smoothly without swinging at all.
Lower with control and stretch fully out.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the work in your outer forearm and lower bicep. If your wrist is uncomfortable, adjust your grip angle. Keep grinding for those detailed arms.
coach_guide.warning: Using too much weight strains your wrist in this position.

name: cable one arm straight back high row (kneeling)
instructions: Attach a handle to a cable machine at waist height.
Kneel down facing the cable machine and grab the handle with one hand.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the handle towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement.
Slowly release the handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch sides and repeat the exercise with the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Kneeling removes momentum so your upper back does all the work.
coaching_cues: Pull your elbow high and back behind you.
Squeeze your shoulder blade toward your spine.
Keep your torso still and core tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction between your shoulder blades. If you are rotating your body, reduce the weight. Stay locked in and build that back.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting your torso takes tension off your upper back muscles.

name: cable one arm tricep pushdown
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with a straight bar attachment at chest height.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip and step back to create tension in the cable.
Position your feet shoulder-width apart and slightly bend your knees.
Keep your back straight and core engaged throughout the exercise.
Start with your arm fully extended and perpendicular to the floor.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and push the bar down until your arm is fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Push down one arm at a time to isolate each tricep fully.
coaching_cues: Keep your elbow pinned tight to your side.
Push down until your arm is fully straight.
Squeeze your tricep hard at the bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong burn in the back of your arm. If your elbow is drifting forward, reset your position. Finish strong and feel that horseshoe pump.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbow drift forward reduces tricep activation.

name: cable overhead curl
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and curl the bar down towards your forehead, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly return the bar to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your upper arms locked in place and curl from the elbows only.
coaching_cues: Arms stay parallel to the floor throughout.
Squeeze biceps hard at the contraction point.
Control the weight back, no arm drift.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense squeeze in the peak of your biceps with each rep. If your shoulders are working too hard, lower the weight and lock those upper arms. Keep that burn going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your elbows forward takes tension off the biceps completely.

name: cable overhead curl on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball and hold the cable handle with an underhand grip.
Extend your arms fully overhead, keeping your elbows close to your ears.
Slowly curl the cable down towards your forehead, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stabilize on the ball while keeping your upper arms perfectly still throughout the curl.
coaching_cues: Core tight, hips level on the ball.
Upper arms fixed, only forearms move up.
Squeeze at the top, slow controlled return.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working overtime while your core fights to keep you stable. If you are rocking around, brace harder through your midsection. Great work adding that extra challenge.
coach_guide.warning: Letting the ball roll shifts focus away from your biceps.

name: cable overhead triceps extension (rope attachment)
instructions: Attach a rope to a cable machine at a high position.
Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the rope with both hands, palms facing each other, and bring your hands above your head.
Keep your upper arms close to your head and your elbows pointing forward.
Slowly lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Face away from the cable and extend your arms overhead keeping elbows pointed forward.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay tight and pointed straight ahead.
Extend fully, squeeze triceps at the top.
Lower with control, feel the deep stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong stretch in your triceps at the bottom and a hard squeeze at lockout. If your elbows are flaring out, bring them back in tight. Push through for those strong arms.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring elbows outward puts unnecessary stress on your shoulders.

name: cable palm rotational row
instructions: Attach a handle to a cable machine at waist height.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the handle with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Step back to create tension on the cable, keeping your back straight and knees slightly bent.
Pull the handle towards your body, rotating your palms to face upwards as you do so.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement.
Slowly release the handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your palm from down to up as you row to engage more of your upper back.
coaching_cues: Start palm down, rotate palm up midway.
Pull elbow back, squeeze shoulder blade in.
Control the rotation on the way back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles and rear shoulder working together through the rotation. If you only feel your arm, focus on that shoulder blade squeeze. Keep rotating and rowing strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the rotation reduces the upper back activation significantly.

name: cable preacher curl
instructions: Adjust the cable machine so that the preacher curl pad is at chest height.
Sit on the preacher curl bench and place your upper arms on the pad, gripping the cable attachment with an underhand grip.
Keep your back straight and your elbows tucked in at your sides.
Slowly curl the cable attachment up towards your shoulders, squeezing your biceps at the top of the movement.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the cable attachment back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lock your upper arms on the pad and curl with strict bicep control.
coaching_cues: Upper arms pressed flat on the pad.
Curl up with control, squeeze the biceps.
Lower slowly, maintain constant tension throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps doing all the work with no momentum helping. If your shoulders are lifting off the pad, lighten the load. Stay strict and feel that pump.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your arms off the pad lets momentum steal your gains.

name: cable press on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees at a 90-degree angle.
Hold the cable handles at chest height with your palms facing down and your elbows bent.
Engage your core and press the cable handles forward until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then slowly release the tension and bring the cable handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press the cables forward while stabilizing your entire body on the ball.
coaching_cues: Core braced, hips stay level and still.
Press handles forward, squeeze your chest hard.
Return with control, keep shoulders stable.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working while your core fights to keep you balanced. If you are wobbling too much, slow down and brace harder. Great job adding that stability challenge.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips drop puts strain on your lower back.

name: cable pull through (with rope)
instructions: Stand facing away from the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grab the rope attachment with both hands and step forward, creating tension in the cable.
Bend at the hips and lower your upper body until it is parallel to the ground, keeping your back straight.
Engage your glutes and hamstrings to pull your body back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge at your hips and drive through your glutes to pull the rope forward.
coaching_cues: Push hips back, feel the hamstring stretch.
Drive hips forward, squeeze glutes hard.
Stand tall at top, core stays braced.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings and a powerful glute squeeze at the top. If your lower back is doing the work, hinge deeper at the hips. Keep driving those hips.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back shifts load away from your glutes.

name: cable pulldown
instructions: Adjust the cable pulldown machine so that the seat is at a comfortable height and the knee pad is secured.
Sit on the seat with your back straight and your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the cable bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lean back slightly and engage your core.
Pull the cable bar down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the bar back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the bar down to your chest by driving your elbows toward your hips.
coaching_cues: Chest up proud, slight lean back only.
Drive elbows down, squeeze lats at bottom.
Control the bar up, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats doing the pulling with each rep. If your biceps are burning out first, focus on the elbow drive. Keep building that strong back.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning too far back turns this into a row movement.

name: cable pulldown (pro lat bar)
instructions: Adjust the seat height so that your thighs are parallel to the ground and your feet are flat on the floor.
Grasp the lat bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Sit down and lean back slightly, keeping your chest up and your back straight.
Pull the bar down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the bar back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the angled grip to target your lats through a full range of motion.
coaching_cues: Grip the angles, chest stays lifted high.
Pull elbows down and back toward hips.
Squeeze lats hard, control the release up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction through the outer edges of your back. If your forearms give out first, loosen your grip slightly. Keep pulling for that wide back.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with straight arms makes your biceps do all the work.

name: cable pulldown bicep curl
instructions: Attach a straight bar to the cable machine at the highest setting.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and slowly curl the bar down towards your thighs, keeping your wrists straight.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly return the bar to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Combine a pulldown with a curl to hit both your lats and biceps together.
coaching_cues: Start with a strong lat pulldown first.
Transition smoothly into the bicep curl.
Control both movements, squeeze each muscle.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats engage on the pulldown and biceps take over on the curl. If one muscle dominates, slow down and focus on the transition. Great combo work here.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the transition reduces muscle activation in both movements.

name: cable pushdown
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and push the bar down until your elbows are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Think about pushing the bar down with just your triceps — elbows stay planted.
coaching_cues: Elbows locked at your sides.
Push down, squeeze at the bottom.
Control the weight back up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel serious tension on the back of your arms as you push down. If your shoulders are working, pin those elbows tighter to your ribs. Feel that tricep squeeze and keep pushing through it.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbows out turns this into a shoulder exercise.

name: cable pushdown (straight arm) v. 2
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, keeping your arms straight and your palms facing down.
Engage your core and keep your back straight as you exhale and push the bar down towards your thighs.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return the bar to the starting position while inhaling.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your arms straight and pull the bar down using only your lats.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, slight lean forward at hips.
Arms locked, pull bar to your thighs.
Squeeze lats hard at the bottom, control up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong stretch in your lats at the top and a deep contraction at the bottom. If your arms are bending, the weight is too heavy. Focus on that lat connection and keep pulling.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your elbows turns this into a tricep exercise.

name: cable pushdown (with rope attachment)
instructions: Attach a rope attachment to a high pulley on a cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and a slight bend in your knees.
Grasp the rope with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Exhale and push the rope downward by extending your elbows until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly return to the starting position by allowing your elbows to flex.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lock your elbows at your sides and squeeze the rope down for maximum tricep burn.
coaching_cues: Elbows pinned tight to your ribs.
Push down and split the rope apart.
Squeeze triceps hard, slow return up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps working through the entire range of motion. If your elbows drift forward, reset your position. Keep that squeeze going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Moving elbows forward takes tension off your triceps completely.

name: cable rear delt row (stirrups)
instructions: Attach a stirrup handle to a low cable pulley and stand facing the machine.
Grasp the handle with your left hand and take a step back with your right foot, positioning your body at a slight angle.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
With your left arm extended and your palm facing down, pull the handle towards your chest by retracting your shoulder blade.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your shoulder blade.
Slowly release the handle back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch sides and repeat the exercise with your right arm.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the handles toward your face while keeping your elbows high and wide.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, arms extended at shoulder height.
Pull handles to face, elbows stay high.
Squeeze rear delts, control the return slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn in the back of your shoulders near your shoulder blades. If you feel it in your traps, lower your elbows slightly. Keep fighting for that rear delt squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your elbows shifts the work to your upper back.

name: cable rear delt row (with rope)
instructions: Attach a rope handle to a low pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the rope handle with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Keep your elbows slightly bent and pull the rope towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the rope apart as you bring it toward your face for rear delt activation.
coaching_cues: Elbows high, pull rope toward your forehead.
Spread the rope wide at the finish.
Squeeze shoulder blades together, slow release back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your rear delts and upper back muscles firing with each rep. If your shoulders roll forward, lighten the weight. Stay proud through your chest and keep going.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling shoulders forward puts unnecessary stress on the joint.

name: cable rear drive
instructions: Attach a handle to a low pulley cable machine and stand facing away from the machine.
Grasp the handle with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Keeping your elbows stationary, pull the handle back towards your body, squeezing your triceps at the end of the movement.
Slowly return the handle to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive the cable back behind you while keeping your arm close to your body.
coaching_cues: Lean slightly forward, core stays tight.
Push arm straight back, elbow locked out.
Squeeze tricep hard at full extension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense contraction in the back of your upper arm. If your shoulder is doing the work, reposition your elbow angle. Push through and feel that tricep fire.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weight uses momentum instead of muscle.

name: cable rear pulldown
instructions: Adjust the cable machine so that the pulley is at the highest position.
Sit facing the machine with your feet flat on the ground and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the cable attachment with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lean back slightly, keeping your back straight and your chest up.
Pull the cable attachment down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the cable back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the bar down behind your head to target your lats from a different angle.
coaching_cues: Grip wide, chest stays lifted and proud.
Pull bar toward upper back, elbows down.
Squeeze lats at bottom, control the release.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong lat contraction and stretch through your back. If your neck strains forward, the weight is too heavy. Keep your chest proud and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too far down can stress your shoulder joints.

name: cable reverse crunch
instructions: Attach a cable to a low pulley and lie down facing up on a mat.
Hold the cable with both hands and extend your arms straight up towards the ceiling.
Bend your knees and lift your legs up, bringing your thighs towards your chest.
While keeping your upper body stable, curl your pelvis up towards your chest, lifting your hips off the mat.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl your hips up off the bench using your lower abs to lift the weight.
coaching_cues: Knees bent, ankles attached to cable.
Curl hips up, lower back lifts off.
Lower with control, do not swing legs.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs burning as you lift your hips. If your hip flexors take over, focus on the curl not the leg pull. Keep those abs engaged throughout.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your legs makes this a hip flexor exercise.

name: cable reverse curl
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a low pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Exhale and curl the bar up towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl the bar up with palms facing down to target your forearms and biceps.
coaching_cues: Elbows locked at sides, palms face down.
Curl bar up, keep wrists straight throughout.
Squeeze at top, lower with full control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your forearms and the outer part of your biceps working hard. If your wrists bend back, lower the weight. Keep grinding through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Bending wrists back can strain your forearm tendons.

name: cable reverse grip triceps pushdown (sz-bar) (with arm blaster)
instructions: Attach a straight bar to the cable machine at the highest setting.
Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Engage your triceps and slowly push the bar down until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the arm blaster to lock your form and isolate your triceps completely.
coaching_cues: Palms up grip, elbows pressed into blaster.
Push bar down until arms fully extend.
Squeeze triceps hard, resist on the way up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps doing all the work with zero cheating. If you feel elbow pain, check your grip width. Stay locked in and keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing past elbow pain can cause serious joint injury.

name: cable reverse one arm curl
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the cable handle with an underhand grip, palm facing down.
Keep your elbow close to your side and slowly curl your forearm up towards your shoulder.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your forearm back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl one arm at a time with palms down to build forearm and bicep strength.
coaching_cues: Palm faces down, elbow stays pinned tight.
Curl handle up, wrist stays neutral throughout.
Squeeze at top, lower slow and controlled.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn across your forearm and into your bicep. If one side is weaker, start with that arm first. Stay focused and keep curling strong.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum cheats your muscles out of the work.

name: cable reverse preacher curl
instructions: Adjust the cable machine so that the preacher curl pad is at chest height.
Sit on the preacher curl bench and place your upper arms on the pad, with your palms facing down and your elbows fully extended.
Grab the cable handles with an underhand grip, shoulder-width apart.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the handles towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the starting position, fully extending your elbows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your palms facing down throughout the curl to target the outer bicep.
coaching_cues: Lock elbows firmly against the preacher pad.
Curl up slow, squeeze hard at top.
Lower with control, do not drop it.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong burn on the outside of your biceps and into your forearms. If your wrists hurt, lighten the weight slightly. Keep that tension and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your elbows off the pad removes tension from the biceps.

name: cable reverse wrist curl
instructions: Attach a cable to a low pulley and sit on a bench facing the cable machine.
Grasp the cable handle with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Rest your forearms on your thighs, with your wrists hanging off the edge.
Keeping your forearms stationary, exhale and curl your wrists upward as far as possible.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower your wrists back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This move builds grip strength and forearm endurance with a reverse grip.
coaching_cues: Rest forearms flat on thighs or bench.
Curl wrists up, knuckles toward ceiling.
Lower slowly, stretch at the bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your forearm extensors burning on top of your forearms. If your grip fails first, try a lighter weight. Push through that burn for stronger forearms.
coach_guide.warning: Jerky wrist movements can strain the small forearm tendons.

name: cable reverse-grip pushdown
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a high pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Using your triceps, push the bar down until your arms are fully extended and your triceps are contracted.
Pause for a moment, then slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Flip your grip to shift the focus onto your triceps long head.
coaching_cues: Keep elbows pinned tight to your sides.
Push down until arms are fully straight.
Squeeze triceps hard at the bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep contraction through the back of your arms. If your wrists feel strained, check your grip is secure. Great work targeting those triceps.
coach_guide.warning: Letting elbows drift forward turns this into a shoulder exercise.

name: cable reverse-grip straight back seated high row
instructions: Sit on the seat facing the cable machine with your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the cable attachment with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward from your hips.
Pull the cable towards your torso by retracting your shoulder blades and squeezing your back muscles.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the contraction, then slowly release the cable back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The reverse grip and upright torso isolate your upper back muscles.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, chest proud, core braced.
Pull elbows straight back toward hips.
Squeeze shoulder blades together, then release.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back and rear delts working hard each rep. If your biceps fatigue first, focus on leading with elbows. Keep that posture tall and strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: cable rope crossover seated row
instructions: Sit on the rowing machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the cable ropes with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Lean back slightly, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the cable ropes towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The rope allows a wider squeeze to hit your upper back from a new angle.
coaching_cues: Sit upright, pull rope toward lower chest.
Spread the rope ends apart at peak.
Control the return, keep tension constant.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your rhomboids and mid traps firing as you pull. If your shoulders hunch up, drop them down and back. You are building a strong powerful back.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning too far back turns this into a momentum swing.

name: cable rope elevated seated row
instructions: Sit on the elevated seat facing the cable machine.
Grab the cable rope handles with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Keep your back straight and lean slightly back, maintaining a slight bend in your knees.
Pull the cable towards your body by retracting your shoulder blades and squeezing your back muscles.
Pause for a moment at the fully contracted position.
Slowly release the tension and extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The elevated angle targets your upper traps and rear delts.
coaching_cues: Pull the rope toward your face level.
Keep elbows high, squeeze shoulder blades.
Slowly return, resist the cable pull.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel tension across your upper back and shoulders. If your neck strains, relax your traps and focus on the squeeze. Keep grinding for that strong back.
coach_guide.warning: Shrugging your shoulders up takes work away from your back.

name: cable rope extension incline bench row
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle and attach a cable machine to the low pulley.
Attach a rope handle to the cable machine and sit on the incline bench facing the machine.
Grab the rope handle with an overhand grip and lean forward, keeping your back straight.
Extend your arms fully, pulling the rope towards your upper chest while keeping your elbows close to your body.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement.
Slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The incline position keeps constant tension on your upper back muscles.
coaching_cues: Lie chest down, grip rope firmly.
Pull elbows wide, row toward your chest.
Squeeze your back, lower under control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats and rhomboids engaged through the full range. If your chest lifts off, you are using momentum. Stay tight and keep those reps clean.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back off the bench risks spinal strain.

name: cable rope hammer preacher curl
instructions: Attach a rope attachment to a low pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the rope with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
Position your upper arms against the preacher bench pad, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the rope towards your shoulders by contracting your biceps.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the rope back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The neutral rope grip emphasizes your brachialis for fuller arm development.
coaching_cues: Lock elbows against the preacher pad.
Curl with palms facing each other.
Squeeze at top, lower slowly down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your outer biceps and forearms working together. If your wrists rotate, reset your neutral grip. Keep those arms growing stronger.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weight steals tension from your biceps.

name: cable rope high pulley overhead tricep extension
instructions: Attach a rope to a high pulley and adjust the weight accordingly.
Stand facing away from the pulley machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the rope with both hands, palms facing down, and bring your hands above your head.
Keep your upper arms close to your head and perpendicular to the floor.
Slowly lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The overhead position stretches your triceps for a deeper contraction.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, elbows pointing straight up.
Extend arms overhead, squeeze triceps hard.
Lower behind head with full control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong stretch then contraction in your triceps. If your lower back arches, brace your core tighter. Finish every rep with power.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring elbows out shifts stress onto your shoulder joints.

name: cable rope incline tricep extension
instructions: Attach a rope to a high pulley and adjust the incline bench to a comfortable angle.
Stand facing away from the pulley with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the rope with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight overhead.
Keep your elbows close to your head and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows until your forearms touch your biceps.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The incline angle increases the stretch on your triceps long head.
coaching_cues: Lie back, keep upper arms vertical.
Extend rope up, lock out completely.
Lower slow, feel the triceps stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch at the bottom and strong squeeze at top. If your shoulders take over, adjust your arm angle. Push through for stronger triceps.
coach_guide.warning: Letting elbows drift forward reduces triceps activation significantly.

name: cable rope lying on floor tricep extension
instructions: Attach a rope to a cable machine and set it to the lowest position.
Lie on the floor facing up, with your head towards the cable machine.
Hold the rope with both hands, palms facing each other, and extend your arms straight up towards the ceiling.
Keep your upper arms stationary and slowly lower the rope towards your forehead, bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your upper arms locked in place and extend through the triceps only.
coaching_cues: Pin your elbows to the floor throughout.
Extend the rope until arms are straight.
Squeeze triceps hard at full extension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in the back of your arms near the elbow. If your shoulders are moving, you are using momentum instead of triceps. Lock those elbows down and feel the squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your elbows off the floor takes tension away from your triceps.

name: cable rope one arm hammer preacher curl
instructions: Attach a rope handle to a low pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the rope handle with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Position your upper arm against the preacher bench pad, keeping your elbow slightly bent.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged throughout the exercise.
Slowly curl the rope handle towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the rope handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Brace your arm against the pad and curl with a neutral hammer grip.
coaching_cues: Keep your wrist neutral, thumb up.
Curl the rope toward your shoulder slowly.
Lower with control, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your bicep and forearm working together through the whole movement. If your elbow lifts off the pad, you are swinging. Stay locked in and build that arm strength.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your elbow off the preacher pad removes the isolation benefit.

name: cable rope seated row
instructions: Sit on the rowing machine with your feet flat on the footrests and knees slightly bent.
Grasp the cable ropes with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward, maintaining a slight bend in your elbows.
Pull the cable ropes towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the rope to your torso by driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades.
coaching_cues: Sit tall with a slight forward lean.
Pull elbows past your ribs, chest proud.
Squeeze your back, then control the return.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles firing between your shoulder blades. If your lower back is rounding, reduce the weight. Keep that chest up and row strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts unnecessary stress on your spine.

name: cable russian twists (on stability ball)
instructions: Sit on a stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Hold the cable handle with both hands and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Lean back slightly while keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Twist your torso to the right, bringing the cable handle towards your right hip.
Pause for a moment, then twist your torso to the left, bringing the cable handle towards your left hip.
Continue alternating twists for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your torso against the cable resistance while keeping your hips stable on the ball.
coaching_cues: Brace your core and stabilize the ball.
Rotate from your ribcage, not your arms.
Move slow and controlled in both directions.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques burning as you twist against the resistance. If you are wobbling too much, engage your core harder. Stay balanced and keep those abs working.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum instead of core strength reduces the effectiveness completely.

name: cable seated chest press
instructions: Adjust the seat height and cable handles to a comfortable position.
Sit on the bench with your back straight and feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip at shoulder height.
Push the handles forward and away from your body, extending your arms fully.
Pause for a moment, then slowly bring the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press the handles forward by squeezing your chest, not pushing with your shoulders.
coaching_cues: Keep your back flat against the seat.
Press handles together in front of you.
Squeeze your chest hard at full extension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your pecs contracting as you press the handles forward. If your shoulders are rolling forward, pull them back. Focus on that chest squeeze every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your shoulders roll forward shifts stress onto the joint.

name: cable seated crunch
instructions: Sit on a cable machine with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent.
Hold the cable handle with both hands and position it behind your head.
Engage your abs and slowly curl your upper body forward, bringing your chest towards your knees.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Crunch your ribcage toward your hips using your abs to pull the weight down.
coaching_cues: Keep your hips locked on the seat.
Curl your spine, not just your head.
Squeeze your abs tight at the bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs burning as you crunch down against the resistance. If your hip flexors take over, focus on curling your spine. Keep squeezing and build that core.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with your arms instead of your abs wastes the movement.

name: cable seated curl
instructions: Sit on a cable machine with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Grasp the cable attachment with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the cable attachment towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the cable attachment back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl the handle up by flexing your biceps while keeping your back against the pad.
coaching_cues: Pin your elbows at your sides throughout.
Curl up smoothly, squeeze at the top.
Lower slowly to full arm extension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working through the entire range of motion. If your back is arching, reduce the weight and stay seated. Keep curling and feel that pump building.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back uses momentum and reduces bicep activation.

name: cable seated high row (v-bar)
instructions: Sit on the cable machine with your feet flat on the floor and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the v-bar attachment with an overhand grip, palms facing each other, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward from the hips.
Pull the v-bar towards your torso by retracting your shoulder blades and squeezing your back muscles.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the contraction, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the v-bar to your upper chest by driving your elbows down and back.
coaching_cues: Sit tall and puff your chest out.
Pull elbows down, squeeze your lats hard.
Control the weight back to full stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats flaring out as you pull the bar down. If your traps are burning more than your lats, focus on elbow drive. Keep pulling and build that back width.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning too far back turns this into a row and reduces lat activation.

name: cable seated one arm alternate row
instructions: Sit on a bench facing a cable machine with your feet flat on the ground and knees slightly bent.
Grasp the handle with one hand and keep your arm fully extended in front of you.
Pull the handle towards your body, retracting your shoulder blade and keeping your elbow close to your side.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your back muscles.
Slowly release the handle back to the starting position.
Repeat with the other arm.
Alternate between arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Row one arm at a time while keeping your torso square and stable.
coaching_cues: Keep your chest up and core braced.
Pull your elbow straight back, squeeze hard.
Alternate arms with control, no twisting.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel each side of your upper back working independently. If you are rotating your torso, reduce the weight and stabilize. Stay square and build balanced back strength.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting your torso takes tension off the working muscles.

name: cable seated one arm concentration curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor and your back straight.
Hold a cable handle with one hand and place your elbow on the inside of your thigh, just above the knee.
Keep your upper arm stationary and curl the cable handle towards your shoulder while exhaling.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position while inhaling.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Brace your elbow against your inner thigh and curl with focused bicep contraction.
coaching_cues: Press your elbow firmly into your thigh.
Curl up slowly, squeeze at the peak.
Lower with control, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense contraction in your bicep on every rep. If your shoulder is creeping up, relax it down and focus. Stay locked in and make every rep count.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbow drift away removes the concentration benefit.

name: cable seated overhead curl
instructions: Sit on a bench facing the cable machine with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the cable attachment with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your upper arms stationary and your elbows close to your sides.
Exhale and curl the cable attachment towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the cable attachment back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your upper arms vertical and curl the cable straight overhead.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, pin elbows pointing at ceiling.
Curl handles toward your head, squeeze hard.
Lower slow, keep tension on the biceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your biceps with each squeeze at the top. If your shoulders are doing the work, reset your elbow position. Keep that focus and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting elbows drift forward turns this into a shoulder exercise.

name: cable seated rear lateral raise
instructions: Sit on a bench facing the cable machine with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms straight in front of you.
Keeping your arms straight, slowly raise them out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise your arms out to the sides while squeezing your rear delts at the top.
coaching_cues: Sit tall with slight forward lean, chest proud.
Lead with elbows, raise arms out and back.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn in the back of your shoulders near your shoulder blades. If you feel it in your traps, lower the weight and focus on the squeeze. You are building boulder shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum robs your rear delts of all the work.

name: cable seated row
instructions: Sit on the cable row machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, keeping your back straight and your shoulders relaxed.
Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Squeeze your shoulder blades together like you're trying to pinch a pencil between them.
coaching_cues: Pull with your elbows, not your hands.
Squeeze shoulder blades together tight.
Slow and controlled on the return.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your upper back between your shoulder blades. If you feel it more in your arms, focus on pulling with your elbows instead. Keep going with that upper back squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Don't lean back too far or you'll turn this into a different exercise.

name: cable seated shoulder internal rotation
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair facing the cable machine with your feet flat on the ground.
Hold the cable handle with your arm extended straight out in front of you, parallel to the ground.
Keep your elbow slightly bent and your shoulder blades pulled back and down.
Slowly rotate your arm inward, bringing the cable handle towards the center of your body.
Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your arm inward against the cable to strengthen your rotator cuff.
coaching_cues: Elbow pinned to your side at 90 degrees.
Pull handle toward your belly button smoothly.
Control the return, do not let cable snap.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep engagement in the front of your shoulder joint. This is a small muscle so keep it light and controlled. Strong rotator cuffs protect you for bigger lifts.
coach_guide.warning: Heavy weight here stresses your shoulder joint not your muscles.

name: cable seated twist
instructions: Sit on a cable machine with your feet flat on the ground and your knees slightly bent.
Hold the cable handle with both hands and extend your arms straight in front of you.
Keeping your core engaged, slowly rotate your torso to one side, pulling the cable across your body.
Pause for a moment at the end of the range of motion, then slowly rotate back to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your torso against the cable resistance while keeping your hips still.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, brace your core tight throughout.
Rotate from your ribcage, not your hips.
Control back to center, pause, then repeat.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques working hard to twist and resist. If your hips are shifting, anchor your lower body better. Keep grinding and build that strong core.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating from your hips takes all tension off your abs.

name: cable seated wide-grip row
instructions: Sit on the cable row machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the handle with a wide overhand grip, palms facing down.
Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward from the hips.
Pull the handle towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the contraction.
Slowly release the handle back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the wide bar to your chest while driving your elbows back and squeezing your back.
coaching_cues: Chest up, sit tall, grip outside shoulders.
Pull elbows back, squeeze shoulder blades together.
Return with control, feel the stretch forward.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back and lats doing the heavy lifting. If your biceps burn out first, focus more on pulling with your elbows. You are building a powerful back.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back forward puts stress on your spine.

name: cable shoulder press
instructions: Adjust the cable machine so that the handles are at shoulder height.
Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and bring them up to shoulder level, with your elbows bent and pointing outwards.
Press the handles upwards until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press the cables overhead while keeping your core braced and ribs down.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, core tight, ribs stacked over hips.
Press handles up and slightly together overhead.
Lower with control, elbows at 45 degrees.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders burning as you press through each rep. If your lower back arches, tighten your core and use less weight. Keep pushing and build those strong shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back puts dangerous pressure on your spine.

name: cable shrug
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip and let your arms hang down in front of you.
Keeping your arms straight, shrug your shoulders up towards your ears.
Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears and squeeze your traps at the top.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, arms straight, shoulders relaxed down.
Shrug straight up, ears to shoulders fast.
Hold the squeeze, then lower slowly with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your traps light up from your neck to mid back. If your arms are bending, you are using biceps instead of traps. Keep squeezing and build that strong yoke.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling your shoulders can irritate your shoulder joints.

name: cable side bend
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and grasp the cable handle with one hand.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Slowly bend sideways at the waist, lowering the cable handle towards your knee.
Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side.
Alternate sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bend sideways against the cable to target your obliques on each side.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, handle at your side, core braced.
Bend away from the cable, squeeze your oblique.
Return slowly, resist the cable pulling you back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in the side of your waist. If you feel it in your lower back, reduce the range of motion. Great work building a rock solid core.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting during the bend puts unnecessary stress on your spine.

name: cable side bend crunch (bosu ball)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a cable handle in one hand.
Place the other hand on your hip.
Engage your core and slowly bend sideways towards the hand holding the cable, keeping your back straight.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Balance on the bosu ball and crunch sideways against the cable for extra core challenge.
coaching_cues: Stabilize on the bosu, brace your whole core.
Crunch sideways, squeeze your oblique hard.
Control back up, stay balanced throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques and deep stabilizers working overtime. If you are wobbling too much, slow down and find your balance. This challenge builds real core strength.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement will cause you to lose balance and form.

name: cable side crunch
instructions: Attach a cable handle to a low pulley and stand sideways to the machine.
Grasp the handle with the hand furthest from the machine and place your other hand on your hip.
Keep your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
With your abs engaged, bend sideways at the waist, bringing your elbow down towards your hip.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Stand sideways to the cable and crunch down to fire up your obliques.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, inside hand behind your head.
Crunch sideways, elbow toward your hip.
Rise with control, keep tension in obliques.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong squeeze in the side of your abs with each crunch. If your hip is shifting, plant your feet wider for stability. Keep crunching and carve those obliques.
coach_guide.warning: Using hip movement takes the work away from your abs.

name: cable squat row (with rope attachment)
instructions: Attach a rope to a cable machine at waist height.
Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and lower your body into a squat position, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Grasp the rope with an overhand grip, with your hands shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and pull the rope towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Keep your elbows close to your body and continue pulling until your hands reach your chest.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the rope and extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Combine a squat with a powerful row to hit your lats and legs together.
coaching_cues: Squat low then pull rope to ribcage.
Squeeze shoulder blades hard at the top.
Keep your chest lifted throughout the move.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats engage as you pull and your legs burn in the squat. If your lower back rounds, sit your hips back more. Great compound movement keep it strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: cable squatting curl
instructions: Attach a cable handle to the lowest setting on a cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the cable handle with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and arms fully extended.
Lower your body into a squat position, keeping your back straight and knees behind your toes.
As you squat down, curl the cable handle towards your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Pause for a moment at the top of the curl, squeezing your biceps.
Slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stay low in your squat while curling to challenge your biceps and legs.
coaching_cues: Hold a deep squat, chest stays up.
Curl with control, squeeze biceps hard.
Keep elbows pinned and completely still.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense bicep burn while your legs work to hold position. If you stand up during curls, go lighter on the weight. Stay low and keep grinding.
coach_guide.warning: Standing up defeats the purpose and removes leg engagement.

name: cable standing back wrist curl
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the cable handle with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Keep your arms straight and your elbows close to your sides.
Slowly curl your wrists upward, bringing the cable handle towards your body.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Strengthen your wrist extensors by curling the cable behind your back.
coaching_cues: Stand tall with cable behind your body.
Curl wrists up using only forearm muscles.
Control the movement slow on the way down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn on top of your forearms near your elbows. If your shoulders roll forward, stand taller and stay proud. Small muscles big results.
coach_guide.warning: Jerky movements can strain the delicate wrist tendons easily.

name: cable standing calf raise
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold onto the cable machine handles or attach a cable ankle strap to your ankles.
Raise your heels off the ground by extending your ankles as high as possible.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use cable resistance to build strong powerful calves with constant tension.
coaching_cues: Rise up high onto your toes.
Pause at the top and squeeze hard.
Lower slowly feeling the stretch below.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your calves burning through the entire range of motion. If your ankles wobble, slow down and find your balance. Push through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing removes tension and increases ankle injury risk.

name: cable standing cross-over high reverse fly
instructions: Attach a D-handle to each side of a cable machine at shoulder height.
Stand in the middle of the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms out to the sides, palms facing forward.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and maintain a straight back throughout the exercise.
Engage your shoulder muscles and squeeze your shoulder blades together as you pull the handles towards the front of your body.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Cross the cables high to target your rear delts and upper back.
coaching_cues: Pull handles back with arms slightly bent.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the end.
Control the return do not let cables snap.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your rear delts and upper back muscles working hard. If your traps take over, lower the weight and focus on squeezing back. Keep those shoulders healthy.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum shifts work away from the target muscles.

name: cable standing crunch
instructions: Attach a cable handle to a high pulley and stand facing away from the machine.
Hold the handle with both hands and place it behind your head, keeping your elbows bent.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Keeping your abs engaged, exhale and crunch your torso down towards your knees, bringing your elbows towards your thighs.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Crunch down against cable resistance to build strong defined abs.
coaching_cues: Tuck chin and curl ribcage toward hips.
Exhale hard and squeeze your abs tight.
Control the way back up stay engaged.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs burning especially in the upper region. If your hip flexors cramp, focus on curling not bending at the hips. Strong core coming your way.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with your arms takes all the work from your abs.

name: cable standing crunch (with rope attachment)
instructions: Attach a rope to a cable machine at chest height.
Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the rope with both hands and bring it behind your head, keeping your elbows bent.
Engage your abs and slowly crunch your torso forward, bringing your elbows towards your knees.
Pause for a moment at the top of the crunch, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The rope lets you pull down harder for a deeper ab contraction.
coaching_cues: Hold rope at your forehead stay locked.
Crunch down bringing elbows toward knees.
Pause at the bottom and squeeze hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core working to pull you down. If your back rounds too much, focus on curling your spine. You are building a solid midsection.
coach_guide.warning: Sitting back into a hip hinge removes the ab focus completely.

name: cable standing fly
instructions: Attach the handles to the cables at chest height.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, facing away from the cable machine.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, palms facing forward.
Step forward slightly to create tension in the cables.
Keep your core engaged and your back straight throughout the exercise.
With a slight bend in your elbows, slowly bring your arms forward and together in front of your chest.
Squeeze your chest muscles at the peak of the movement.
Slowly reverse the movement, returning your arms to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bring the cables together in front to squeeze your chest muscles.
coaching_cues: Slight bend in elbows keep it locked.
Bring handles together with a big squeeze.
Control back slowly feeling the chest stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong squeeze across your entire chest at the front. If your shoulders roll forward, pull them back and open up. Great chest isolation keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Straightening your arms turns this into a pressing movement.

name: cable standing hip extension
instructions: Attach a cable to a low pulley and stand facing away from the machine.
Place the cable around your ankle and stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Keep your core engaged and your back straight throughout the exercise.
Slowly extend your leg straight back, squeezing your glutes at the top of the movement.
Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch sides and repeat with the other leg.

coach_guide.intro: Extend your leg back against the cable to fire up your glutes.
coaching_cues: Keep your standing leg slightly bent.
Drive your heel straight back and squeeze.
Control the return do not swing forward.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glute working hard at the top of each rep. If your lower back arches, tighten your core and reduce range. Strong glutes equal a strong body.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back shifts stress onto your spine.

name: cable standing inner curl
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the cable handle with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and curl the cable handle towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl across your body to hit the inner head of your biceps.
coaching_cues: Start with arm out to the side.
Curl handle toward your opposite shoulder smoothly.
Squeeze at the top then lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your bicep peak working as you curl inward. If your elbow drifts forward, pin it at your side. Great isolation work keep it up.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weight uses momentum instead of muscle.

name: cable standing lift
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the cable handle with both hands and position it at waist height.
Engage your core and maintain a straight back throughout the exercise.
Keeping your arms straight, exhale and lift the cable handle up towards your opposite shoulder, rotating your torso.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the cable handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your torso diagonally while keeping your hips stable and core braced.
coaching_cues: Start low, pull cable up and across.
Rotate through your ribcage not your hips.
Control the return, resist the pull down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques and deep core muscles working through the rotation. If your lower back is doing the work, you are twisting too fast. Stay controlled and feel that core burn.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating from the hips instead of the core removes all ab engagement.

name: cable standing one arm triceps extension
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, facing the cable machine.
Hold the cable handle with your right hand, palm facing down, and position your arm so that it is fully extended and parallel to the ground.
Keep your elbow stationary and close to your body.
Slowly bend your elbow, lowering the cable handle towards the back of your head.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then extend your arm back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and perform the exercise with your left arm.

coach_guide.intro: Extend your arm overhead while keeping your elbow locked in place beside your head.
coaching_cues: Elbow points up, stays completely still.
Extend fully, squeeze your tricep hard.
Lower with control, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the back of your upper arm burning with each extension. If your elbow drifts forward, you are using momentum. Lock that elbow and isolate the tricep.
coach_guide.warning: Moving your elbow turns this into a shoulder exercise.

name: cable standing one leg calf raise
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold onto the cable machine for support.
Lift one leg off the ground and balance on the other leg.
Slowly raise your heel off the ground, lifting your body up onto your toes.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

coach_guide.intro: Rise onto the ball of your foot with full calf contraction while balancing on one leg.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, hold cable for balance.
Push through your big toe, rise high.
Pause at top, lower slow and controlled.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense squeeze at the top and a deep stretch at the bottom. If you feel wobbly, focus your gaze forward. Build that single leg strength.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement prevents full calf muscle activation.

name: cable standing pulldown (with rope)
instructions: Attach a rope to the cable machine at the highest setting.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the rope with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Keep your back straight and core engaged throughout the exercise.
Pull the rope down towards your thighs, squeezing your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly release the rope back up.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the rope down to your thighs while keeping your elbows pinned at your sides.
coaching_cues: Elbows tight to ribs, do not move.
Pull down and spread the rope apart.
Squeeze biceps hard at the bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working through the entire pulling motion. If your shoulders are sore, your elbows are drifting. Pin those elbows and crush it.
coach_guide.warning: Letting elbows move forward shifts work to your front delts.

name: cable standing rear delt row (with rope)
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the cable attachment with both hands, palms facing each other, and step back to create tension in the cable.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the cable towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the cable back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the rope toward your face while driving your elbows high and wide.
coaching_cues: Pull to your face, elbows go high.
Spread the rope, squeeze your rear delts.
Control the return, keep tension constant.
