coach_guide.feel: You should feel the muscles behind your shoulders burning intensely. If your traps are taking over, lower your elbows slightly. Keep chasing that rear delt squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too low turns this into a standard row movement.

name: cable standing reverse grip one arm overhead tricep extension
instructions: Stand facing away from the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the cable handle with an underhand grip and extend your arm overhead, keeping your elbow close to your head.
Keep your upper arm stationary and slowly lower the cable handle behind your head by bending your elbow.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arm back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Extend your arm overhead with an underhand grip for a different tricep stretch and squeeze.
coaching_cues: Palm faces up, elbow points to ceiling.
Extend fully, feel the inner tricep.
Lower slowly, maintain complete control throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a unique stretch and contraction in your tricep with this grip. If your wrist feels strained, loosen your grip slightly. Push through and feel the difference.
coach_guide.warning: Gripping too tight puts unnecessary stress on your wrist joint.

name: cable standing row (v-bar)
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the v-bar attachment with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the v-bar towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the v-bar to your stomach while squeezing your shoulder blades together.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, chest proud, core braced.
Pull to belly button, squeeze your back.
Return with control, arms fully extended.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your mid back muscles contracting with each pull. If your biceps fatigue first, focus on elbow drive. Build that strong upper back.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your shoulders forward reduces back muscle activation.

name: cable standing shoulder external rotation
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Hold the cable handle with your arm extended in front of you, parallel to the ground.
Keep your elbow slightly bent and your shoulder blades pulled back.
Slowly rotate your arm outward, away from your body, while keeping your elbow in the same position.
Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your forearm outward while keeping your elbow pinned at ninety degrees.
coaching_cues: Elbow at side, bent ninety degrees.
Rotate outward, keep elbow completely still.
Control back slowly, feel the rotator.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the small muscles on the back of your shoulder working. If your whole arm moves, reset your elbow position. This builds bulletproof shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Moving your elbow defeats the purpose of this rotator cuff exercise.

name: cable standing twist row (v-bar)
instructions: Attach a v-bar attachment to a cable machine at chest height.
Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the v-bar with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Take a step back to create tension in the cable.
Keep your back straight and core engaged throughout the exercise.
Pull the v-bar towards your torso by retracting your shoulder blades and bending your elbows.
As you pull, twist your torso to one side, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, feeling the contraction in your upper back.
Slowly release the tension and return to the starting position, untwisting your torso.
Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, alternating the twisting direction with each rep.

coach_guide.intro: Row the v-bar while adding a twist to engage your core and back together.
coaching_cues: Pull and rotate your torso slightly.
Squeeze your back, then control the twist.
Alternate sides with smooth steady rhythm.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats and obliques working together on each rep. If you feel off balance, widen your stance. Keep the rhythm and feel the connection.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting too aggressively can strain your lower back.

name: cable standing up straight crossovers
instructions: Stand in the middle of a cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the handles of the cables with your palms facing down and your arms extended straight out to the sides.
Keeping your arms straight, bring your hands together in front of your body, crossing them over each other.
Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position, keeping your arms extended.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bring the cables together at chest height while keeping your arms straight and chest lifted.
coaching_cues: Arms straight, slight bend in elbows.
Squeeze cables together, flex your chest.
Open wide with control, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a powerful squeeze across your entire chest at the center. If your shoulders take over, lower the weight. Chase that perfect chest contraction.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your elbows too much turns this into a pressing movement.

name: cable straight arm pulldown
instructions: Attach a straight bar to the high pulley of a cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, keeping your arms straight and your palms facing down.
Engage your lats and pull the bar down towards your thighs, keeping your arms straight throughout the movement.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return the bar to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep those arms completely straight and think about bringing your lats to your ribs.
coaching_cues: Arms stay locked straight throughout.
Pull from your lats, not arms.
Drive the bar toward your hips.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in the sides of your back, like you're trying to tuck your lats into your ribs. If your arms are bending, you're doing it wrong. Stay with that straight-arm lat burn.
coach_guide.warning: Any bend in your elbows and you've turned this into a pulldown.

name: cable straight arm pulldown (with rope)
instructions: Attach a rope to the cable machine at the highest setting.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the rope with both hands, palms facing down.
Extend your arms fully in front of you, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Engage your lats and slowly pull the rope down towards your thighs, keeping your arms straight.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your arms straight and pull the rope down by squeezing your lats not your hands.
coaching_cues: Slight forward lean, arms extended overhead.
Pull down in an arc, lats leading.
Squeeze at your hips, then control back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction through your entire lat muscle from armpit to lower back. If your biceps burn first, you are bending your elbows too much. Focus on that lat stretch and squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your elbows turns this into a tricep exercise and steals lat activation.

name: cable straight back seated row
instructions: Sit on the cable row machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the cable handles with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward from the hips.
Pull the cable handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit tall with a proud chest and row the handle straight back into your belly button.
coaching_cues: Chest lifted, back stays perfectly vertical.
Drive elbows straight back, squeeze blades together.
Control forward, feel the stretch, repeat.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles pinching together at the top of each row. If your lower back rounds or rocks, reduce the weight and stabilize your torso. Keep that proud posture strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rocking your torso uses momentum and puts unnecessary strain on your spine.

name: cable supine reverse fly
instructions: Attach a D-handle to a low pulley cable machine and lie face down on a flat bench.
Grasp the D-handle with each hand, palms facing down, and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Keeping your arms straight, raise them out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie face up and open your arms wide to target the rear delts with constant tension.
coaching_cues: Arms slightly bent, palms facing each other.
Open wide in an arc, squeeze rear delts.
Control back to start, maintain tension throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a burn deep in the back of your shoulders near your shoulder blades. If your traps take over, lower the weight and focus on the squeeze. Your rear delts will thank you.
coach_guide.warning: Using too much weight causes your traps to dominate the movement.

name: cable thibaudeau kayak row
instructions: Attach a cable handle to a low pulley and sit facing the machine with your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handle with your right hand and extend your arm fully, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
Lean forward from your hips, keeping your back straight and your abs engaged.
Pull the handle towards your torso by retracting your shoulder blade and bending your elbow, keeping your arm close to your body.
Squeeze your back muscles at the top of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and perform with your left arm.

coach_guide.intro: Mimic a kayak paddle motion to hit your lats from a unique rotational angle.
coaching_cues: Stand sideways, grab handle with both hands.
Pull low to high in a diagonal paddle.
Rotate through your core, squeeze the lat.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lat working through a full range with each paddle stroke. If your shoulders feel strained, check your hand position and rotation. This one builds powerful functional lats.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement reduces lat engagement and stresses your rotator cuff.

name: cable triceps pushdown (v-bar)
instructions: Attach a v-bar attachment to the cable machine at the highest setting.
Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the v-bar with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Engage your triceps and exhale as you push the v-bar down until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your triceps.
Inhale as you slowly return the v-bar to the starting position, maintaining control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lock your elbows at your sides and push the bar down using only your triceps.
coaching_cues: Elbows pinned tight to your ribs.
Push down until arms are fully straight.
Squeeze triceps hard, control the return.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense squeeze in the back of your upper arms at full extension. If your elbows drift forward, you are using too much weight. Keep those elbows locked and feel the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Letting elbows flare forward shifts work to your shoulders and chest.

name: cable triceps pushdown (v-bar) (with arm blaster)
instructions: Attach a v-bar attachment to the cable machine at the highest setting.
Stand facing the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the v-bar with an overhand grip, palms facing down, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Engage your triceps and exhale as you push the v-bar down until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, squeezing your triceps.
Inhale as you slowly return the v-bar to the starting position, maintaining control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The arm blaster locks your elbows so your triceps do all the work with zero cheating.
coaching_cues: Arm blaster snug, elbows pressed against pad.
Push down with triceps, full extension.
Slow return, keep elbows absolutely fixed.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps working harder than ever because the blaster eliminates momentum. If the pad digs in uncomfortably, adjust the strap height. Trust the isolation and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Fighting against the arm blaster position can strain your elbow joints.

name: cable tuck reverse crunch
instructions: Attach a cable to a low pulley and lie down on a mat facing up.
Hold the cable with both hands and extend your arms straight up above your chest.
Bend your knees and lift your legs up, bringing your knees towards your chest.
At the same time, curl your pelvis up towards your chest, lifting your hips off the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs and hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the cable resistance to curl your hips toward your ribs with your abs doing all the work.
coaching_cues: Knees tucked, lower back pressed flat down.
Curl hips up, pull knees toward chest.
Lower slow, keep tension in your abs.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs burning as you curl your pelvis up off the bench. If your hip flexors cramp, focus on the pelvic tilt more than knee movement. Stay controlled and feel every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your legs uses momentum and takes tension off your abs.

name: cable twist
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, facing the cable machine.
Hold the cable handle with both hands in front of your chest, keeping your arms slightly bent.
Engage your core and twist your torso to the right, pulling the cable across your body.
Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, feeling the contraction in your abs and obliques.
Slowly return to the starting position, resisting the cable's pull.
Repeat the movement to the left side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate through your core with control to carve your obliques with constant cable tension.
coaching_cues: Arms extended, hands at chest height.
Rotate from your core, not your arms.
Control back to center, repeat each side.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques working hard to control the rotation against the cable pull. If your arms are doing the work, lock them straighter and twist from your waist. Build that rotational power.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting too fast with heavy weight can strain your lower back.

name: cable twist (up-down)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, facing the cable machine.
Hold the cable handle with both hands in front of your chest, keeping your arms slightly bent.
Engage your core and slowly rotate your torso to one side, keeping your hips and legs stable.
Pause for a moment at the end of the rotation, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat the rotation to the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive the cable from high to low in a diagonal chop pattern to hit your obliques completely.
coaching_cues: Start high, arms extended, core braced.
Chop diagonally down across your body.
Control the return, resist the cable pull.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques and deep core muscles firing through the entire diagonal path. If your shoulders fatigue first, you are pulling with your arms too much. Let your core drive the chop.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back during the chop puts pressure on your spinal discs.

name: cable twisting pull
instructions: Attach a cable handle to a low pulley and stand facing the machine.
Grasp the handle with your left hand and step away from the machine, extending your arm fully.
Position your feet shoulder-width apart, with your knees slightly bent.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged throughout the exercise.
Pull the handle towards your body, rotating your torso to the right as you do so.
Squeeze your back muscles at the end of the movement.
Slowly return to the starting position, keeping tension on the cable.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and perform with your right hand.

coach_guide.intro: Combine a twist with a pull to engage your lats through a rotational movement pattern.
coaching_cues: Grab handle, rotate away from the stack.
Pull and twist simultaneously toward your hip.
Squeeze your lat, control back with rotation.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lat contract powerfully as you twist and pull in one coordinated motion. If your lower back twists uncomfortably, reduce the weight and tighten your core. This builds real functional strength.
coach_guide.warning: Over rotating through your lower back instead of your torso risks disc injury.

name: cable two arm curl on incline bench
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with your back against the pad and your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the cable handles with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the handles towards your shoulders while contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The incline angle stretches your biceps at the bottom for a deeper contraction.
coaching_cues: Keep upper arms locked against the bench.
Curl with control, squeeze hard at top.
Lower slowly, feel the stretch at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense stretch in your biceps at the bottom and a strong peak contraction at the top. If your elbows drift forward, reset your arm position. Keep that tension and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbows swing forward removes the stretch and cheats the movement.

name: cable two arm tricep kickback
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Hold the cable handle in each hand with your palms facing inwards and your arms bent at a 90-degree angle.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, extend your forearms backwards until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lock your upper arms parallel to the floor and only move your forearms.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward, keep your back flat.
Extend arms straight back, squeeze triceps hard.
Control the return, do not swing weights.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a burning squeeze in the back of your arms at full extension. If you feel nothing, you are likely swinging. Slow it down and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your upper arms turns this into momentum and wastes your effort.

name: cable underhand pulldown
instructions: Adjust the cable machine so that the pulldown bar is at a height above your head.
Sit down on the seat and grab the pulldown bar with an underhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and lean back slightly.
Pull the bar down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the bar back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The underhand grip shifts focus to your lower lats and biceps as helpers.
coaching_cues: Grip shoulder width, palms facing you.
Pull elbows down and back toward hips.
Squeeze lats at bottom, control the release.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower lats engage as you pull down toward your waist. If your biceps burn out first, focus on driving elbows not hands. Stay with it and feel that back working.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with just your arms will exhaust biceps before your lats get enough work.

name: cable upper chest crossovers
instructions: Attach the handles to the cables at chest height.
Stand in the center of the cable machine with one foot slightly in front of the other.
Grasp the handles with your palms facing down and your arms extended out to the sides.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and engage your core.
Pull the cables together in front of your chest, crossing them over each other.
Squeeze your chest muscles at the peak of the movement.
Slowly release the cables back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The low to high angle targets your upper chest fibers for better definition.
coaching_cues: Set cables low, step forward slightly.
Bring hands up and together at chin height.
Squeeze chest hard at top, lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper chest near the collarbone firing as you bring your hands together. If your front shoulders take over, lower the weight. Keep that upper chest squeeze going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Using too much weight shifts the work to your shoulders and away from chest.

name: cable upper row
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a cable machine at chest height.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the bar towards your upper chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement.
Slowly release the bar back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pulling to your upper chest hits your mid traps and rhomboids for better posture.
coaching_cues: Set cable at chest height or higher.
Pull toward your upper chest, elbows wide.
Squeeze shoulder blades together, pause briefly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the muscles between your shoulder blades working hard on every pull. If your biceps fatigue first, think about pinching your shoulder blades. Build that strong upper back.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your shoulders forward reduces the contraction in your upper back muscles.

name: cable upright row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold the cable attachment with an overhand grip.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged throughout the exercise.
Pull the cable attachment straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Slowly lower the cable attachment back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lead with your elbows to target your side delts and upper traps effectively.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, grip the bar shoulder width.
Pull elbows up high, bar stays close.
Control the descent, do not drop it.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders and upper traps burning as you raise the bar. If your wrists ache, widen your grip slightly. Power through and build those shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with wrists too narrow can pinch and irritate your shoulder joints.

name: cable wide grip rear pulldown behind neck
instructions: Adjust the cable machine so that the pulldown bar is at a height above your head.
Sit down on the seat and grab the pulldown bar with a wide overhand grip.
Keep your back straight and your chest up as you lean back slightly.
Pull the bar down towards your upper chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the bar back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The wide grip behind neck variation emphasizes your outer lats and rear delts.
coaching_cues: Grip extra wide, sit tall with chest up.
Pull bar to base of your neck.
Squeeze lats hard, release with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your outer lats flare as you pull the bar down behind you. If your shoulders feel pinched, bring the bar to your chin instead. Keep working that width.
coach_guide.warning: This movement requires good shoulder mobility or it can strain your rotator cuff.

name: cable wrist curl
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a low pulley cable machine.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Rest your forearms on a bench or pad, with your wrists hanging off the edge.
Keeping your forearms stationary, exhale and curl your wrists upward as far as possible.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Constant cable tension makes this ideal for building forearm size and grip.
coaching_cues: Rest forearms on thighs, palms face up.
Curl wrists up, squeeze forearms at top.
Lower slowly, keep tension throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep pump in the meaty part of your inner forearms. If your grip fails first, lighten the weight slightly. Those forearms are growing with every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum by bouncing the weight reduces time under tension and results.

name: calf push stretch with hands against wall
instructions: Stand facing a wall with your feet hip-width apart.
Place your hands against the wall at shoulder height.
Step back with one foot, keeping your heel on the ground and your leg straight.
Bend your front knee slightly and lean forward, feeling a stretch in your calf.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Switch legs and repeat the stretch.

coach_guide.intro: The bent knee position targets your soleus muscle deep in the calf.
coaching_cues: Hands on wall, back foot flat down.
Bend back knee, push heel into floor.
Hold the stretch, breathe deeply throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch lower in your calf near the Achilles area. If you feel it higher up, straighten your knee more. Breathe into it and let those calves release.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing in this stretch can strain your Achilles tendon over time.

name: calf stretch with hands against wall
instructions: Stand facing a wall with your feet hip-width apart.
Place your hands against the wall at shoulder height.
Step your right foot back, keeping your heel on the ground and your leg straight.
Bend your left knee and lean forward, keeping your back leg straight and your heel on the ground.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Switch legs and repeat the stretch.

coach_guide.intro: The straight leg position targets your gastrocnemius the main calf muscle.
coaching_cues: Hands on wall, back leg straight.
Press back heel firmly into the ground.
Lean forward gently, hold and breathe.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong stretch in the upper part of your calf behind the knee. If it feels too intense, step your back foot closer. Relax into it and let the tension melt away.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing the stretch too aggressively can cause a calf muscle strain.

name: calf stretch with rope
instructions: Stand facing a wall or sturdy object with your feet hip-width apart.
Hold the ends of the rope in each hand and place the middle of the rope around the ball of your right foot.
Step back with your left foot, keeping your heel on the ground and your leg straight.
Lean forward, keeping your back straight, and gently pull on the rope to stretch your calf.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then release.
Repeat on the other leg.

coach_guide.intro: Loop the rope around your foot and gently pull to stretch your calf.
coaching_cues: Keep your knee straight, foot flexed.
Pull the rope slowly, feel the stretch.
Breathe deeply and hold the position.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch along the back of your lower leg from heel to knee. If you feel sharp pain, ease off the tension slightly. Let each breath take you deeper into the stretch.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too hard can strain your Achilles tendon.

name: cambered bar lying row
instructions: Set up a barbell on the floor and lie face down on a bench with your chest just off the edge.
Reach down and grab the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
With your legs straight and feet on the ground, lift the barbell off the floor by extending your arms.
Pull the barbell towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Lower the barbell back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the cambered bar into your lower chest while keeping your back flat on the bench.
coaching_cues: Squeeze shoulder blades together, stay tight.
Pull elbows back past your ribcage.
Lower with control, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles working hard with each pull. If your biceps fatigue first, focus on leading with your elbows. Build that strong back one rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your head or arching excessively strains your lower back.

name: captains chair straight leg raise
instructions: Sit on the captain's chair with your back against the backrest and your forearms resting on the arm pads.
Keep your upper body stable and your back straight.
Engage your abs and lift your legs up in front of you, keeping them straight.
Continue lifting until your legs are parallel to the ground or as high as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise your straight legs by driving your abs, not swinging momentum.
coaching_cues: Press your back firmly into the pad.
Lift legs slow, keep them straight.
Lower with control, do not swing.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in your lower abs throughout each rep. If your hip flexors cramp, bend your knees slightly. Stay controlled and own every inch of movement.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your legs takes tension off your abs completely.

name: chair leg extended stretch
instructions: Sit on the edge of a chair with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Extend one leg straight out in front of you, keeping your heel on the ground.
Lean forward slightly, feeling a stretch in your quadriceps.
Hold this position for 20-30 seconds.
Switch legs and repeat the stretch.

coach_guide.intro: Extend one leg forward on a chair and lean back to stretch your quad.
coaching_cues: Keep your standing leg slightly bent.
Tuck your hips under, stay tall.
Breathe into the stretch, hold steady.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a lengthening sensation down the front of your thigh. If your knee feels pressure, adjust your foot position. Relax into it and let tension melt away.
coach_guide.warning: Hyperextending your knee joint can cause ligament stress.

name: chest and front of shoulder stretch
instructions: Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms straight out in front of you at shoulder height.
Cross your arms in front of your body, with your right arm on top of your left arm.
Interlace your fingers and press your palms together.
Gently squeeze your shoulder blades together and push your hands forward, feeling a stretch in your chest and front of your shoulders.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then release.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Open your arms wide and pull your shoulders back to stretch your chest.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, chin level, core engaged.
Squeeze shoulder blades together firmly.
Hold the stretch, breathe deeply.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest and front shoulders opening up nicely. If you feel pinching in your shoulder, reduce the range slightly. Enjoy this release after all that pressing work.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing your arms too far back strains the shoulder capsule.

name: chest dip
instructions: Position yourself on parallel bars with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your shoulders are below your elbows.
Push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lean slightly forward and let your chest lead the movement down.
coaching_cues: Lean forward, chest leads down.
Go deep but stay in control.
Drive up through your chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your chest and the front of your shoulders. The lower you go, the better the stretch and the harder your chest works. Push through that deep chest burn.
coach_guide.warning: Stay too upright and you'll work triceps instead of chest.

name: chest dip (on dip-pull-up cage)
instructions: Adjust the dip bars to a height that allows you to comfortably grip them.
Stand between the bars and place your hands on each bar, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Jump up and straighten your arms, supporting your body weight on the bars.
Bend your knees and cross your ankles behind you.
Lower your body by bending your elbows, keeping your chest up and your shoulders down.
Continue lowering until your shoulders are below your elbows or until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Push through your palms and extend your elbows to raise your body back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lean your torso forward and dip deep to target your chest muscles.
coaching_cues: Lean forward, keep elbows slightly flared.
Lower until you feel chest stretch.
Press up strong, squeeze your chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest doing most of the work as you push up. If your shoulders take over, lean forward more. You have the strength for this, trust it.
coach_guide.warning: Staying too upright shifts all the work to your triceps.

name: chest dip on straight bar
instructions: Grab the parallel bars with your palms facing down and your arms fully extended.
Bend your knees and cross your ankles.
Lower your body by bending your arms until your shoulders are below your elbows.
Push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Grip a straight bar and dip forward to hit your chest from a new angle.
coaching_cues: Lean your torso forward over the bar.
Bend elbows, lower chest toward bar.
Push up explosively, chest leading.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong stretch and squeeze in your pecs throughout the movement. If your wrists ache, adjust your grip width slightly. This one is tough but you are tougher.
coach_guide.warning: Dipping too deep can overstretch your shoulder joint.

name: chest stretch with exercise ball
instructions: Sit on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold the exercise ball with both hands and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Slowly bring the exercise ball towards your chest, feeling a stretch in your chest muscles.
Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Kneel behind the ball and let your chest sink to feel a deep stretch.
coaching_cues: Place hands on ball, arms extended.
Let your chest drop toward floor.
Breathe and relax into the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest and shoulders opening beautifully. If the stretch is too intense, move the ball closer to you. Let gravity do the work while you simply breathe.
coach_guide.warning: Pushing too hard into the ball can strain your shoulder.

name: chest tap push-up (male)
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your body in a straight line.
Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
As you lower yourself, tap your chest with your right hand.
Push yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat the movement, this time tapping your chest with your left hand.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Perform a push-up and tap your chest at the bottom for full range.
coaching_cues: Lower chest all the way to floor.
Tap your chest, then explode up.
Keep your core tight throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working through a complete range of motion. If you cannot tap without collapsing, elevate your hands first. Every rep is building a stronger you.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag puts dangerous pressure on your spine.

name: chin-up
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your palms facing towards you and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and pull your body up towards the bar, leading with your chest.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lead with your chest and think about driving your elbows down, not up.
coaching_cues: Chest leads up to the bar.
Elbows drive down toward your sides.
Squeeze shoulder blades at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this working your entire back and biceps together. If it's all biceps, focus on that chest-up position. Push through that full-body burn and keep climbing.
coach_guide.warning: Don't let your chin crane forward to reach the bar.

name: chin-ups (narrow parallel grip)
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with a narrow parallel grip, palms facing towards you.
Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bar, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Grip the parallel handles close together and pull your chin above them.
coaching_cues: Grip narrow, palms facing each other.
Drive elbows down toward your hips.
Chin clears the bar, lower controlled.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats and biceps working together on each rep. If you struggle to clear the bar, use a band for assistance. Keep pulling strong, you are earning this.
coach_guide.warning: Kipping or swinging removes the muscle-building tension.

name: circles knee stretch
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your hands on your hips.
Bend your knees slightly and lift your heels off the ground, balancing on the balls of your feet.
Keeping your knees bent, rotate your knees in a circular motion, first clockwise and then counterclockwise.
Perform the movement for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Circle your knee smoothly to warm up and stretch your calf muscles.
coaching_cues: Keep your standing leg slightly bent.
Draw slow controlled circles with your knee.
Feel the stretch through your entire calf.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a gentle stretch and warmth building in your calf and ankle. If you feel unstable, hold onto something for balance. Keep those circles smooth and controlled.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the circles can strain your ankle joint.

name: clap push up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your core engaged.
Push through your palms explosively to propel your body off the ground.
While in mid-air, clap your hands together before landing back in the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode up with enough power to clap your hands before catching yourself.
coaching_cues: Lower with control, chest near the floor.
Push explosively, hands leave the ground.
Land soft with elbows slightly bent.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest and triceps firing hard on every explosive push. Your heart rate will climb fast with this one. Embrace the power and keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Landing with locked elbows can hyperextend your joints.

name: clock push-up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line.
Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
As you lower, rotate your body to the left, extending your left arm straight out to the side.
Push back up to the starting position, while rotating your body to the center.
Repeat the push-up, this time rotating your body to the right and extending your right arm out to the side.
Continue alternating sides with each repetition.

coach_guide.intro: Move your hands around an imaginary clock face between each push up.
coaching_cues: Perform a push up at each position.
Walk hands to the next clock number.
Keep your core tight throughout the movement.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest and shoulders working from multiple angles as you rotate. This builds stability and strength together. Stay focused and own every position.
coach_guide.warning: Sagging hips put excessive pressure on your lower back.

name: close grip chin-up
instructions: Grab the pull-up bar with your palms facing towards you and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Hang from the bar with your arms fully extended and your feet off the ground.
Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bar, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Grip the bar with hands close together and pull with your back.
coaching_cues: Hands four to six inches apart.
Pull elbows down toward your ribs.
Squeeze your lats hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in your lats and biceps at the top. If your forearms burn out first, focus on pulling with elbows. Keep grinding through each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your body steals the work from your back muscles.

name: close-grip push-up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands placed close together, directly under your shoulders.
Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your hands close together to shift the focus onto your triceps.
coaching_cues: Hands directly under your chest.
Elbows stay tight against your sides.
Push up and squeeze your triceps hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps burning more than your chest on this one. If your wrists hurt, try making fists instead. Power through and feel those arms working.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbows outward defeats the tricep focus.

name: close-grip push-up (on knees)
instructions: Start by getting on your hands and knees, with your hands shoulder-width apart and your knees hip-width apart.
Lower your upper body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Pause for a moment when your chest is just above the ground.
Push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This kneeling version lets you build tricep strength with less intensity.
coaching_cues: Hands close together under your chest.
Keep elbows pinned to your sides.
Lower slowly and push up with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps doing most of the work throughout each rep. This is a great way to build strength before full push ups. Keep at it and feel yourself getting stronger.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag forward strains your lower back.

name: cocoons
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl your entire body inward like you are wrapping into a cocoon.
coaching_cues: Start with arms and legs extended.
Draw knees and arms together simultaneously.
Squeeze your abs tight at the center.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core working as everything comes together. The slow controlled motion is what makes this effective. Stay tight and embrace the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum instead of control reduces the ab engagement.

name: crab twist toe touch
instructions: Start by sitting on the ground with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Place your hands behind you, fingers pointing towards your feet, and lift your hips off the ground.
Extend one leg straight out in front of you while simultaneously reaching your opposite hand towards your toes.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: From a crab position twist and reach to touch your opposite toe.
coaching_cues: Lift hips high in crab position.
Rotate and reach hand to opposite foot.
Keep your core engaged throughout the twist.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs and obliques firing with each twist and reach. Your shoulders and hips get a great stretch too. Keep moving with rhythm and control.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your hips too low puts strain on your shoulders.

name: cross body crunch
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, lift your upper body off the ground and twist to bring your right elbow towards your left knee.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, bringing your left elbow towards your right knee.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Crunch diagonally bringing your elbow toward your opposite knee.
coaching_cues: Twist your torso as you crunch.
Elbow drives toward the opposite knee.
Keep your lower back pressed to floor.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques working hard on the twisting motion. Both sides of your core engage with each rep. Stay controlled and feel that diagonal burn.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling on your neck takes work away from your abs.

name: crunch (hands overhead)
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Extend your arms straight above your head.
Engaging your abs, lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward towards your knees.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Extend your arms overhead to increase the challenge on your abs.
coaching_cues: Arms straight back behind your head.
Lift shoulder blades using only your abs.
Keep your arms by your ears throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a stronger contraction in your upper abs than regular crunches. The extended arms add leverage that your core must overcome. Push through and feel that extra challenge.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your arms forward uses momentum not muscle.

name: crunch (on stability ball)
instructions: Sit on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Lie back on the ball until your lower back is supported and your upper body is parallel to the floor.
Place your hands behind your head or across your chest.
Engage your abs and lift your upper body towards your knees, curling your torso forward.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let the ball support your lower back while your abs do all the curling work.
coaching_cues: Feet flat, hips stable on the ball.
Curl shoulders toward hips, exhale fully.
Lower with control, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn through your entire abdominal wall as you crunch. The ball adds instability so your core works harder to stabilize. Embrace that shake and keep crunching.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling too far back on the ball strains your lower back.

name: crunch (on stability ball, arms straight)
instructions: Sit on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Lie back on the ball until your lower back is supported and your upper body is parallel to the floor.
Place your hands behind your head or cross them over your chest.
Engage your abs and lift your upper body off the ball, curling your shoulders towards your hips.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Reaching your arms overhead increases the challenge so keep your core braced throughout.
coaching_cues: Arms straight overhead, biceps by ears.
Curl up leading with your chest.
Keep arms fixed, only your torso moves.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs burning more intensely with arms extended overhead. The longer lever makes every rep count double. Stay focused and power through.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your arms swing forward removes tension from your abs.

name: crunch floor
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engage your abs and lift your shoulders off the ground, curling forward towards your knees.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press your lower back to the floor and curl your shoulders up, not your neck.
coaching_cues: Lower back stays pressed down.
Lift shoulders, not your head.
Squeeze abs hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your upper abs right now. If you feel strain in your neck, you're pulling with your hands instead of lifting with your abs. Focus on that abdominal fire and keep going with that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling on your head and neck can cause serious injury to your cervical spine.

name: curl-up
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This classic move targets your upper abs with a short controlled range of motion.
coaching_cues: Lower back stays glued to the floor.
Curl shoulders up, chin slightly tucked.
Squeeze abs at top, lower slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper abs tighten as you lift your shoulders off the ground. Keep the movement small and controlled for maximum burn. Every rep builds a stronger core.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling on your neck shifts stress away from your abs.

name: curtsey squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Take a step diagonally behind and across your body with your right foot, crossing it behind your left leg.
Bend both knees as if you were curtsying, lowering your body towards the ground.
Keep your torso upright and your weight on your front foot.
Push through your front foot to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, stepping diagonally behind and across your body with your left foot.

coach_guide.intro: Step diagonally behind you to target your glutes from a different angle.
coaching_cues: Step back and across behind front leg.
Keep chest tall, hips facing forward.
Drive through front heel to stand up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and burn in your glutes and outer hip. This move hits muscles regular squats miss entirely. Keep stepping and feel those glutes fire.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your knee cave inward stresses your knee joint.

name: cycle cross trainer
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the cycle cross trainer.
Place your feet on the pedals and grip the handlebars.
Start pedaling in a smooth and controlled motion.
Maintain a steady pace and increase the resistance if desired.
Continue pedaling for the desired duration of your cardio workout.

coach_guide.intro: Keep a steady rhythm and let your whole body work together on this machine.
coaching_cues: Grip handles lightly, push and pull evenly.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Maintain smooth continuous pedal strokes.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart rate climbing as your legs and arms work in sync. This is full body cardio that builds endurance without pounding joints. Stay in rhythm and enjoy the ride.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning heavily on handles reduces your calorie burn significantly.

name: dead bug
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended towards the ceiling.
Bend your knees and lift your legs off the ground, creating a 90-degree angle at your hips and knees.
Engage your core and lower back to press your lower back into the ground.
Slowly lower your right arm and left leg towards the ground, keeping them straight and hovering just above the floor.
Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Repeat the movement with your left arm and right leg.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Move opposite arm and leg while keeping your lower back completely still.
coaching_cues: Press lower back flat into the floor.
Extend opposite arm and leg slowly.
Return with control, then switch sides.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your deep core muscles working hard to keep your back stable. This exercise builds the foundation for every other movement you do. Stay slow and own each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back during the movement causes strain.

name: decline crunch
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Place your hands behind your head or across your chest.
Engage your abs and lift your upper body towards your knees, curling your torso.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The decline angle increases resistance so focus on controlled movement throughout.
coaching_cues: Hook feet securely under the pads.
Curl up squeezing abs, exhale hard.
Lower slowly, keep tension in abs.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel intense engagement through your entire abdominal wall on every rep. The angle makes gravity work against you even more. Push through the burn and get stronger.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum to swing up removes the work from your abs.

name: decline push-up
instructions: Place your hands on the ground slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, with your feet elevated on a stable surface.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to toe, engaging your core muscles.
Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Elevating your feet shifts more weight to your upper chest and shoulders.
coaching_cues: Hands slightly wider than shoulder width.
Lower chest toward floor, elbows back.
Push up explosively, squeeze your chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper chest and front shoulders working harder than regular pushups. This variation builds serious pressing strength with just bodyweight. Keep your core tight and push strong.
coach_guide.warning: Sagging hips put dangerous pressure on your lower back.

name: decline sit-up
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your knees bent.
Place your hands behind your head or across your chest.
Engage your abs and lift your upper body off the bench, curling forward towards your knees.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The decline angle makes your abs work harder through a fuller range of motion.
coaching_cues: Hook feet, cross arms over chest.
Sit all the way up with control.
Lower slowly, feel abs stretch out.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core burning as you fight gravity on the way up. This builds real abdominal strength that transfers to everything. Stay controlled and finish every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Jerking up quickly can strain your hip flexors and back.

name: deep push up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your body in a straight line.
Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Using dumbbells as handles lets you sink deeper for a bigger chest stretch.
coaching_cues: Grip dumbbells firmly, wrists straight.
Lower deeper than normal pushup range.
Drive up strong, squeeze chest hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an incredible stretch in your chest at the bottom of each rep. That extra depth recruits more muscle fibers for better growth. Keep pushing and feel your chest working overtime.
coach_guide.warning: Going too deep too fast can strain your shoulder joints.

name: diamond push-up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands close together, forming a diamond shape with your thumbs and index fingers.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to toe, engaging your core and glutes.
Lower your chest towards the diamond shape formed by your hands, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Form a diamond shape with your hands to shift the focus onto your triceps.
coaching_cues: Hands together, thumbs and fingers touch.
Lower chest to your hands slowly.
Push up and squeeze triceps at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in the back of your arms and a stretch at the bottom. If your wrists hurt, widen the diamond slightly. Keep grinding through that tricep burn.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbows flare wide removes the tricep focus completely.

name: donkey calf raise
instructions: Stand with your toes on an elevated surface, such as a step or block.
Place your hands on a stable support, such as a wall or railing, for balance.
Raise your heels as high as possible, lifting your body weight onto the balls of your feet.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bend at the hips with weight on your back to maximize calf stretch and contraction.
coaching_cues: Balls of feet on edge, heels hanging.
Lower heels deep for a full stretch.
Rise high on toes and squeeze hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your calves at the bottom and a strong contraction at the top. If you feel it in your lower back, brace your core tighter. Push through and build those calves.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing through reps removes tension and risks Achilles strain.

name: drop push up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lower your chest towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Once your chest is just above the ground, quickly drop your knees to the ground.
Push yourself back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drop from an elevated surface and immediately push up explosively to build power.
coaching_cues: Hands on blocks, body in tight plank.
Drop hands to floor, catch yourself softly.
Explode up fast, land back on blocks.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working hard to absorb and reverse the drop quickly. If your shoulders feel jarred, lower the height of your blocks. Stay explosive and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Locking elbows on landing sends shock straight through your joints.

name: dumbbell alternate biceps curl
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, alternating arms.

coach_guide.intro: Alternate arms to focus on each bicep individually while keeping your core stable.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, elbows pinned at sides.
Curl one dumbbell up, rotate palm up.
Lower with control, then switch arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel each bicep fully engage from bottom to top of the curl. If your body is swaying, go lighter and stay strict. Keep alternating and chase that pump.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your body turns this into a momentum exercise not biceps.

name: dumbbell alternate biceps curl (with arm blaster)
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Place the arm blaster on your upper arms, ensuring a secure fit.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl one dumbbell up towards your shoulder while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbell is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat the movement with the opposite arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The arm blaster locks your elbows in place for strict bicep isolation every rep.
coaching_cues: Press elbows firmly into the blaster pad.
Curl up slow, squeeze bicep at top.
Lower under control, full arm extension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps doing all the work with zero help from your shoulders. If the pad digs in uncomfortably, adjust the strap height. Stay locked in and feel every fiber work.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning back defeats the purpose of the arm blaster entirely.

name: dumbbell alternate hammer preacher curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso and arms fully extended.
Keep your upper arms stationary and exhale as you curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Alternate hammer curls on the preacher bench to isolate each arm with a neutral grip.
coaching_cues: Arm flat on pad, palm facing inward.
Curl up keeping wrist neutral throughout.
Lower slowly, switch to other arm.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your brachialis and outer bicep lighting up with each rep. If your elbow lifts off the pad, you are going too heavy. Stay strict and build that arm thickness.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your elbow off the pad lets momentum steal the work.

name: dumbbell alternate preacher curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up.
Rest your upper arms on the pad of the preacher bench, allowing your arms to fully extend.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbell in your right hand as you contract your biceps.
Continue to curl the dumbbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbell is at shoulder level.
Pause for a moment, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat the movement with your left arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The preacher bench locks your arm in place for maximum bicep isolation each rep.
coaching_cues: Upper arm flat on pad, armpit snug.
Curl dumbbell up, squeeze at the top.
Lower slow, feel the stretch, then switch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch at the bottom and a hard contraction at the top. If your shoulder creeps forward, reset your position on the pad. Keep grinding for that peak.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your shoulder roll forward shifts stress away from biceps.

name: dumbbell alternate seated hammer curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso and arms extended down.
Keep your back straight and your elbows close to your torso.
Exhale and curl the dumbbell in your right hand towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Continue to raise the dumbbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbell is at shoulder level.
Pause for a brief moment, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat the movement with your left arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Seated hammer curls keep you stable so your biceps and forearms do all the work.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, palms facing your thighs.
Curl one arm up, thumb stays high.
Lower with control, alternate sides smoothly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your outer bicep and forearm working together on every rep. If you are rocking in the seat, brace your core harder. Stay seated and stay focused.
coach_guide.warning: Rocking your torso backward takes tension off the biceps completely.

name: dumbbell alternate side press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height.
Press one dumbbell overhead while keeping the other dumbbell at shoulder height.
Lower the pressed dumbbell back to shoulder height while pressing the other dumbbell overhead.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press one dumbbell overhead at a time to challenge your shoulder stability and strength.
coaching_cues: Hold both dumbbells at shoulder height.
Press one up, lock out overhead fully.
Lower with control, press the other side.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulder and tricep working to stabilize and press the weight. If your lower back arches, tighten your core and glutes. Keep alternating and build strong shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back puts dangerous pressure on your spine.

name: dumbbell alternating bicep curl with leg raised on exercise ball
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Place an exercise ball behind you and position one foot on top of it, keeping your balance.
With your arms fully extended and elbows close to your sides, curl one dumbbell towards your shoulder while keeping your upper arm stationary.
Lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position and repeat with the other arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Balancing on the ball while curling forces your core to work alongside your biceps.
coaching_cues: Sit on ball, one leg raised off floor.
Curl one dumbbell up, stay balanced.
Lower slowly, alternate arms each rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps burning while your core fights to keep you stable. If you wobble too much, lower your foot closer to the ground. Stay balanced and keep curling strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing reps on the ball risks falling and losing your form.

name: dumbbell alternating seated bicep curl on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing forward and your arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl one dumbbell while rotating your forearm until your palm is facing your shoulder.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat the curl with the other arm.
Continue alternating curls for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit tall on the ball and curl one arm at a time with total control.
coaching_cues: Brace your core, stay balanced on ball.
Curl slow, squeeze bicep hard at top.
Lower with control, then switch arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in the working bicep while your core stays engaged for balance. If you wobble too much, plant your feet wider. Keep alternating and stay focused.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the reps makes you lose balance and cheat the curl.

name: dumbbell arnold press
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support and hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level, palms facing your body and elbows bent.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended and your palms are facing forward.
Rotate your wrists as you lift, so that your palms are facing forward at the top of the movement.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Start with palms facing you, then rotate as you press — smooth transition, full range.
coaching_cues: Rotate your palms as you press up.
Full extension at the top.
Control the weight on the way down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this working all three heads of your shoulders — front, side, and a bit of rear. If your lower back is taking over, sit up straighter. Keep that complete shoulder burn going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the rotation or pressing too heavy will compromise your shoulder safety.

name: dumbbell arnold press v. 2
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support and hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level, palms facing your body and elbows bent.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended and your palms are facing forward.
Rotate your wrists as you lift, so that your palms end up facing forward at the top of the movement.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your palms outward as you press to hit every angle of your shoulders.
coaching_cues: Start with palms facing you, elbows bent.
Rotate wrists outward as you press up.
Lower slow, return palms facing your face.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your front and side delts working through the full rotation. If your lower back arches, tighten your core more. Own every rep and build those shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back turns this into a dangerous spine exercise.

name: dumbbell around pullover
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet firmly on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight above your chest.
Keeping your arms straight, slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head in an arc motion.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sweep the dumbbell in a wide arc around your head to stretch your chest.
coaching_cues: Lie back, hold dumbbell over your chest.
Circle wide around your head, arms extended.
Keep slight elbow bend throughout the arc.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a big stretch through your chest and lats at the bottom of the arc. If your shoulders click or pop, reduce the range. Embrace the stretch and keep flowing.
coach_guide.warning: Going too deep with straight arms strains your shoulder capsule.

name: dumbbell bench press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your palms facing forward and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive through your chest, not your shoulders — keep those elbows at ninety degrees.
coaching_cues: Press up and slightly in toward center.
Elbows stay at ninety degrees throughout.
Control the weight down, power up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your chest, right across the middle and outer edges. If you feel it more in your shoulders, bring those elbows down and focus on squeezing your chest. Keep that chest burn going strong.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your elbows flare too wide, you lose chest activation.

name: dumbbell bench seated press
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Lean back and position the dumbbells to the sides of your chest, palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press the dumbbells straight overhead while seated to isolate your shoulders.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, dumbbells at shoulder height.
Press straight up, bring weights together.
Lower with control to shoulder level.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your deltoids burning as you press through each rep. If your neck tenses up, relax your traps and focus on shoulders. Push strong and finish every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning back shifts the load to your upper chest and strains your spine.

name: dumbbell bench squat
instructions: Place a dumbbell on the ground in front of a bench.
Stand facing away from the bench with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend at the knees and hips to lower yourself down towards the bench, keeping your chest up and back straight.
Once your glutes touch the bench, push through your heels to stand back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold dumbbells at your sides and squat down to the bench then stand back up.
coaching_cues: Feet shoulder width, dumbbells by sides.
Sit back until glutes touch the bench.
Drive through heels, squeeze glutes at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads firing as you stand from the bench. If your knees cave in, push them out over your toes. Trust the bench and power up strong.
coach_guide.warning: Plopping down hard on the bench can jar your lower spine.

name: dumbbell bent over row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Let your arms hang straight down towards the floor, with your elbows slightly bent.
Pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your back flat and drive those elbows back toward your ribs.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay close to your body.
Squeeze shoulder blades at the top.
Keep that back straight and strong.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong pull right between your shoulder blades and into your lats. If your lower back is burning instead, stand up a bit more. Lock in that upper back burn and power through.
coach_guide.warning: Round your back and you're asking for trouble.

name: dumbbell bicep curl lunge with bowling motion
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing forward.
Take a step forward with your right foot, bending your right knee and lowering your body into a lunge position.
As you lunge forward, curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your body.
At the bottom of the lunge, rotate your torso to the right, as if you were bowling a ball.
Reverse the motion, stepping back with your right foot and returning to the starting position while lowering the dumbbells back down.
Repeat the lunge and curl motion, this time rotating your torso to the left.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Step into a lunge while curling with a bowling motion to challenge balance and biceps.
coaching_cues: Lunge forward, curl arm like bowling motion.
Keep torso upright, core braced tight.
Step back, lower weight, then switch sides.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working while your legs stabilize the lunge. If you lose balance, shorten your stride until steady. Stay smooth and keep that rhythm going.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting your torso during the curl puts shear force on your spine.

name: dumbbell bicep curl on exercise ball with leg raised
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing forward and your arms fully extended.
Slowly curl the dumbbells towards your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
While performing the bicep curl, raise one leg off the ground and hold it in the air for the duration of the exercise.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.
