coach_guide.intro: Lie back on the ball with one leg raised and curl to build biceps and core stability.
coaching_cues: Lie back on ball, raise one leg.
Curl both dumbbells, squeeze at top.
Lower slow, keep hips level throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps burn while your core fights to keep you stable. If your hips drop, lower your leg slightly. Stay tight and crush every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag strains your lower back on the ball.

name: dumbbell bicep curl with stork stance
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Extend one leg behind you, balancing on the toes of that foot.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Stand on one leg while curling to build biceps and single leg balance together.
coaching_cues: Stand on one leg, slight knee bend.
Curl both dumbbells with strict form.
Switch standing leg halfway through set.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working hard while your standing leg stabilizes. If you wobble, focus your eyes on one spot. Balance and strength in one move.
coach_guide.warning: Locking your standing knee puts dangerous stress on the joint.

name: dumbbell biceps curl
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lock those elbows at your sides and think about squeezing a tennis ball in your armpits.
coaching_cues: Elbows glued to your ribs.
Squeeze up, control the negative.
Full range, touch your shoulders.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a tight squeeze right in the front of your arms. If your shoulders are doing the work, pin those elbows tighter. Lock into that bicep burn and keep going.
coach_guide.warning: The second your elbows drift forward, your shoulders take over.

name: dumbbell biceps curl (with arm blaster)
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The arm blaster locks your elbows so your biceps do all the work.
coaching_cues: Press elbows firmly into the blaster pad.
Curl up slow, squeeze biceps at top.
Lower with control, no swinging allowed.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense bicep isolation with zero momentum cheating. If your shoulders creep forward, stand taller and reset. Pure bicep work, enjoy the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning back defeats the purpose and strains your lower back.

name: dumbbell biceps curl reverse
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing down and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Flip your grip palms down to target your forearms and outer biceps.
coaching_cues: Hold dumbbells with palms facing down.
Curl up, keep wrists straight and strong.
Lower slow, feel forearms working hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your forearms and brachialis burning more than regular curls. If your wrists bend back, lighten the weight. Build those grip muscles and keep going.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your wrists back under load strains the tendons badly.

name: dumbbell biceps curl squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing forward.
Keeping your back straight and your elbows close to your sides, exhale and curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Combine a squat with a curl to challenge your legs and biceps at the same time.
coaching_cues: Curl as you lower into the squat.
Keep elbows pinned to your sides throughout.
Stand tall and squeeze biceps at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps burning while your quads and glutes work hard in the squat. If your lower back rounds, sit back more into your heels. Keep that core tight and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: dumbbell biceps curl v sit on bosu ball
instructions: Sit on a bosu ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing forward and your arms fully extended down by your sides.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Balance on the Bosu ball while curling to fire up your core and biceps together.
coaching_cues: Brace your core and hold the V.
Curl slow and controlled, no swinging allowed.
Squeeze biceps hard at the top position.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs shaking to keep balance while your biceps work through each curl. If you wobble too much, focus your eyes on one spot ahead. You have got this, stay steady.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the curls will throw off your balance and strain your back.

name: dumbbell burpee
instructions: Start in a standing position with your feet shoulder-width apart and a dumbbell in each hand.
Lower your body into a squat position, placing the dumbbells on the ground in front of you.
Kick your feet back into a push-up position, keeping your body in a straight line.
Perform a push-up, bending your elbows and lowering your chest towards the ground.
Jump your feet back towards your hands, landing in a squat position.
Stand up explosively, lifting the dumbbells off the ground and bringing them to your shoulders.
Press the dumbbells overhead, fully extending your arms.
Lower the dumbbells back to your shoulders and repeat the entire sequence for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold dumbbells through a full burpee to boost intensity and build total body power.
coaching_cues: Grip dumbbells tight, jump feet back fast.
Chest touches floor, then push up strong.
Explode up and jump with control overhead.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart pounding and every muscle working from chest to legs. If your form breaks down, slow the pace and reset each rep. Push through and keep that energy high.
coach_guide.warning: Sloppy landings with weights can wreck your wrists and shoulders.

name: dumbbell clean
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Bend your knees and lower your hips into a squat position, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Explosively extend your hips and knees, driving through your heels to jump off the ground.
As you jump, shrug your shoulders and pull the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, keeping them close to your body.
Catch the dumbbells at shoulder height, with your elbows pointing forward and your palms facing up.
Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position by reversing the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the dumbbells from the floor to your shoulders in one explosive hip driven movement.
coaching_cues: Hinge at hips, dumbbells below knees.
Drive hips forward and shrug hard upward.
Catch dumbbells at shoulders, stand tall now.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and hips powering the lift while your traps finish the pull. If your arms take over, focus on that explosive hip snap. Great power comes from the legs.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with your arms instead of hips strains your lower back.

name: dumbbell close grip press
instructions: Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Using your thighs to help raise the dumbbells, lift the dumbbells one at a time so that you can hold them in front of you at shoulder width.
Once at shoulder width, rotate your wrists forward so that the palms of your hands are facing away from you. This will be your starting position.
As you breathe in, slowly lower the dumbbells to your side until they are about level with your chest.
As you exhale, use your triceps to lift the dumbbells back to the starting position. Make sure to use only your triceps and do not use your forearms or biceps to help lift the dumbbells.
After a second pause at the contracted position, repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press the dumbbells together as you lower and lift — think about crushing them together.
coaching_cues: Squeeze dumbbells together throughout.
Lower with control, chest level.
Press up using only triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel intense tension on the back of your arms and inner chest. The squeeze keeps it different from regular presses. Lock into that combined burn and keep crushing through it.
coach_guide.warning: Letting the dumbbells drift apart makes this just another chest exercise.

name: dumbbell close-grip press
instructions: Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Using your thighs to help raise the dumbbells, lift the dumbbells one at a time so that you can hold them in front of you at shoulder width.
Once at shoulder width, rotate your wrists forward so that the palms of your hands are facing away from you. This will be your starting position.
As you breathe in, slowly lower the dumbbells to your side until they are about level with your chest.
As you exhale, use your triceps to lift the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep the dumbbells close together to shift the work onto your triceps.
coaching_cues: Dumbbells touch, palms face each other here.
Lower slow with elbows tucked to ribs.
Press up and squeeze triceps at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn deep in the back of your arms on every rep. If your elbows flare wide, bring them back in tight. Keep squeezing those triceps and power through.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring elbows shifts stress to shoulders and reduces triceps work.

name: dumbbell concentration curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your legs spread apart and a dumbbell in one hand, resting your elbow on the inside of your thigh.
Fully extend your arm and hold the dumbbell with an underhand grip.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the weight up towards your shoulder while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbell is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Isolate one bicep at a time with your elbow braced against your inner thigh.
coaching_cues: Elbow locked into inner thigh, no moving.
Curl up slow and squeeze at the top.
Lower with control, feel the full stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your bicep with nowhere to hide or cheat. If your shoulder creeps up, relax it down and focus on the arm. This is pure isolation, make it count.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your elbow off your thigh lets momentum steal the work.

name: dumbbell contralateral forward lunge
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Take a step forward with your right foot, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Lower your body by bending both knees until your right thigh is parallel to the ground.
Push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat with your left leg.
Alternate legs for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold the weight on the opposite side of your stepping leg to challenge balance and glutes.
coaching_cues: Step forward, dumbbell on the opposite side.
Lower until back knee nearly touches floor.
Drive through front heel and stand up tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glute on the front leg firing hard to stabilize and push. If you tip sideways, brace your core tighter and slow down. Great work building single leg strength.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your front knee cave inward stresses the joint badly.

name: dumbbell cross body hammer curl
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps, bringing the dumbbells across your body towards your opposite shoulder.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl across your body to hit the outer bicep and forearm together.
coaching_cues: Curl dumbbell toward your opposite shoulder now.
Keep upper arm still, only forearm moves.
Squeeze at top, then lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the outside of your bicep and forearm working with each cross body rep. If you rock your torso, plant your feet and stabilize. Stay strict and feel that muscle connection.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your body takes tension off the biceps completely.

name: dumbbell cross body hammer curl v. 2
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps, bringing the dumbbells as close to your opposite shoulder as possible.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This variation adds extra forearm engagement as you curl across your chest.
coaching_cues: Palms face in, curl toward opposite pec.
Elbows stay pinned, do not drift forward.
Squeeze hard at top, lower nice and slow.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps and forearms lighting up with each controlled rep. If your elbows move forward, reset them at your sides. Keep the tension constant and finish every rep strong.
coach_guide.warning: Moving elbows forward shifts work away from the biceps.

name: dumbbell cuban press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with your palms facing down.
Keeping your core engaged and your elbows slightly bent, press the dumbbells up and overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing forward.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, rotating your wrists back to the starting position as you do so.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate the dumbbells through external rotation then press overhead for healthy shoulders.
coaching_cues: Start with elbows bent at 90 degrees.
Rotate dumbbells up, keeping elbows still here.
Press overhead and lower back with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your rear delts and rotator cuff muscles working through the rotation. If your shoulders pinch, use lighter weight and focus on the movement. This builds strong and healthy shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Using too much weight compromises your rotator cuff health badly.

name: dumbbell cuban press v. 2
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with your palms facing down.
Keeping your core engaged and your back straight, press the dumbbells straight up overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing forward.
Lower the dumbbells back down to shoulder height, rotating your wrists back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your shoulders through the full range while keeping your core tight.
coaching_cues: Elbows at 90 degrees, row up first.
Rotate dumbbells up, palms face forward.
Press overhead, control the way down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your rear and side delts burning through each rotation. This move targets the smaller stabilizers so keep the weight light. Stay smooth and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Using too much weight will strain your rotator cuff muscles.

name: dumbbell deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing forward.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body, arms extended downwards.
Bend at your hips and knees, lowering the dumbbells towards the ground while keeping your back straight.
Push through your heels and extend your hips and knees, lifting the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge at your hips and let your glutes power the lift from the ground.
coaching_cues: Push your hips back, soft knee bend.
Keep dumbbells close to your legs always.
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings on the way down and your glutes firing to stand up. If your lower back is doing the work, hinge deeper at the hips. Drive through those heels and keep building.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: dumbbell decline bench press
instructions: Lie down on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand and extend your arms straight up above your chest, palms facing forward.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press from the decline angle to hit your lower chest hard.
coaching_cues: Plant feet firmly, arch your lower back.
Lower dumbbells to your outer chest slowly.
Press up and squeeze your chest together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower pecs working with each controlled rep. The decline shifts the focus so embrace that deep stretch at the bottom. Keep pushing and feel that chest pump grow.
coach_guide.warning: Letting the dumbbells drift too low strains your shoulder joints.

name: dumbbell decline fly
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing each other and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Open your arms wide and squeeze your chest to bring the weights together.
coaching_cues: Slight bend in elbows throughout the move.
Lower until you feel a chest stretch.
Squeeze your pecs hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch across your lower chest at the bottom and a strong contraction at the top. Keep the movement slow and controlled for maximum muscle tension. You are building that chest definition rep by rep.
coach_guide.warning: Straightening your arms completely puts excess stress on your elbows.

name: dumbbell decline hammer press
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured and your head lower than your hips.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing each other and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Press the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use a neutral grip to press and target your inner chest fibers.
coaching_cues: Palms face each other the whole time.
Lower dumbbells to your outer chest line.
Drive up powerfully and squeeze together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower and inner chest working hard with the neutral grip. This angle hits muscles that flat pressing can miss. Keep that control and finish every rep strong.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing the weights uses momentum and risks shoulder injury.

name: dumbbell decline one arm fly
instructions: Lie down on a decline bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting it on your thigh.
Using your thigh to help raise the dumbbell, lift it up to shoulder width with your palm facing your torso.
Rotate your wrist so that the palm of your hand is facing forward.
As you breathe in, lower the dumbbell slowly to the side until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Exhale and use your chest muscles to bring the dumbbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Work one side at a time to fix imbalances and build focus.
coaching_cues: Brace your core, stay stable on bench.
Lower the dumbbell with a slight elbow bend.
Squeeze your chest to lift it back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your working pec stretch deep and contract hard at the top. Single arm work forces your core to stabilize so stay tight. Build that balance and keep going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating your torso takes tension off your chest completely.

name: dumbbell decline one arm hammer press
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting on your chest.
Extend your arm straight up, keeping your elbow slightly bent.
Lower the dumbbell down towards your shoulder, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Press the dumbbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Isolate one tricep at a time with the hammer grip for full development.
coaching_cues: Keep your upper arm still throughout.
Lower the dumbbell beside your head slowly.
Extend your arm and squeeze your tricep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your tricep on every extension. The decline angle increases the stretch at the bottom of each rep. Stay focused and push through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Moving your elbow shifts the work away from your tricep.

name: dumbbell decline shrug
instructions: Set up a decline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Lie face down on the bench with your chest and stomach resting against it.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your arms fully extended towards the floor.
Keeping your arms straight, raise your shoulders towards your ears as high as possible.
Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together as you shrug your shoulders up.
coaching_cues: Shoulders up, shoulder blades together.
Squeeze at the top position.
Arms stay completely straight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this across your upper back and the tops of your shoulders. The decline position really targets those mid-traps. Lock into that upper back burn and keep squeezing.
coach_guide.warning: Don't let your arms bend or you'll lose the trap focus.

name: dumbbell decline shrug v. 2
instructions: Set up a decline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Lie face down on the bench with your chest and stomach resting on it.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing each other and your arms fully extended.
Keeping your arms straight, raise your shoulders as high as possible while squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Hold the contraction for a brief pause, then lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Shrug from a decline to hit your traps from a unique angle.
coaching_cues: Let your shoulders drop fully down first.
Shrug straight up toward your ears hard.
Hold the squeeze at the top briefly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper traps contracting with each shrug. The decline position changes the resistance curve for new growth. Keep squeezing and build those traps up.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling your shoulders can damage your rotator cuff over time.

name: dumbbell decline triceps extension
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your head lower than your feet and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms fully, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Lower the dumbbells slowly behind your head, bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lower behind your head and extend to fully isolate your triceps.
coaching_cues: Keep your elbows pointed at the ceiling.
Lower the dumbbells slowly beside your head.
Extend fully and squeeze your triceps hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense stretch in your triceps at the bottom and a strong contraction at the top. The decline adds extra range so control every inch. Keep that burn going and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbows out stresses your shoulder joints badly.

name: dumbbell decline twist fly
instructions: Lie down on a decline bench with your head lower than your hips.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing each other and your arms extended straight up over your chest.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
As you lower the dumbbells, twist your wrists so that your palms face forward at the bottom of the movement.
Reverse the motion and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position, squeezing your chest muscles at the top.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Add a twist to your fly to maximize the chest contraction.
coaching_cues: Start with palms facing each other below.
Open arms wide, rotate palms outward slightly.
Twist back to neutral as you squeeze.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest fibers working through the rotation at the top. The twist adds extra contraction that a regular fly cannot match. Stay smooth and let your chest do all the work.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting too aggressively can strain your shoulder joint.

name: dumbbell finger curls
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with an underhand grip, resting your forearm on your thigh, palm facing up.
Allow the dumbbell to roll down to your fingertips, then curl it back up by flexing your fingers.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other hand.

coach_guide.intro: Let the dumbbells roll to your fingertips then curl them back into your palm.
coaching_cues: Open fingers slowly, control the roll down.
Curl fingertips up, squeeze your forearms tight.
Keep wrists neutral, no bending back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your inner forearms near your wrists. If your wrists hurt, you are bending them too much. Embrace that forearm pump and keep squeezing.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your wrists back puts stress on the joint instead of the muscles.

name: dumbbell fly
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep that slight elbow bend locked — this is all about the arc, not pressing.
coaching_cues: Maintain that elbow bend throughout.
Wide arc out, squeeze back together.
Feel the stretch, control the squeeze.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch across your entire chest at the bottom, then a strong squeeze as you bring the weights together. If your shoulders hurt, you are going too low. Embrace that chest stretch and keep flying.
coach_guide.warning: Going too low will shift stress from your chest to your shoulders.

name: dumbbell fly on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Walk your feet forward and roll your body down until your head, neck, and upper back are supported by the ball.
Extend your arms straight up above your chest, palms facing each other.
Bend your elbows slightly and lower your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and squeeze your chest muscles as you bring the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the unstable ball to challenge your chest through a full stretch and squeeze.
coaching_cues: Plant feet wide, hips up, core tight.
Lower arms wide, feel the chest stretch.
Squeeze dumbbells together at the top, exhale.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch across your chest at the bottom and a strong squeeze at the top. If your shoulders take over, lighten the weight. Stay stable and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips drop puts your lower back at risk.

name: dumbbell forward lunge triceps extension
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Take a step forward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position.
Keep your back straight and your chest up.
Extend your arms straight overhead, keeping your elbows close to your ears.
Lower the dumbbells behind your head by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then straighten your arms to return to the starting position.
Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Combine a forward lunge with an overhead triceps extension for total body control.
coaching_cues: Step forward, lower knee near the floor.
Extend dumbbells overhead as you lunge down.
Press up through front heel, squeeze triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps working hard at the bottom of each extension while your legs stabilize. If you wobble, slow down and find your balance. This combo builds strength and coordination.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement causes loss of balance and poor form.

name: dumbbell front raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your thighs.
Keeping your arms straight, exhale and lift the dumbbells in front of you until they are at shoulder level.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise the dumbbells straight in front to shoulder height with control.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, slight bend in your elbows.
Lift to shoulder height, palms facing down.
Lower slowly, resist gravity the whole way.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the front of your shoulders burning as you lift. If your lower back arches, you are using too much weight. Keep it strict and feel those delts fire.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weight uses momentum and strains your lower back.

name: dumbbell front raise v. 2
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your thighs.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Slowly lift the dumbbells in front of you, with your arms straight, until they are at shoulder level.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This variation keeps constant tension on your front delts throughout the lift.
coaching_cues: Thumbs up or neutral grip, elbows soft.
Raise to eye level, pause at top.
Control the descent, do not drop fast.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel intense tension in the front of your shoulders especially at the top. If you need to lean back, go lighter. Stay strict and build those shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning backward shifts the load away from your delts.

name: dumbbell full can lateral raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Raise your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows, until they are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise with thumbs pointing up to safely target your side delts.
coaching_cues: Thumbs up like pouring a drink upward.
Lift to shoulder height, lead with thumbs.
Lower with control, keep shoulders down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the sides of your shoulders working without any pinching. If you feel impingement, check your thumb position. This is shoulder friendly so enjoy the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Turning thumbs down can cause shoulder impingement over time.

name: dumbbell goblet squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell vertically against your chest with both hands.
Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower your body down into a squat position by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold that dumbbell tight to your chest and drive through your heels to stand up.
coaching_cues: Chest up, dumbbell stays close.
Sit back like there's a chair.
Drive through your heels to stand.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your thighs and glutes right now. If your knees cave in or you feel it in your back, focus on pushing your knees out wider. Embrace that quad burn and carry that fire through every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Never let your knees cave inward or you'll stress the wrong joints.

name: dumbbell hammer curl
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and rotate the palms of your hands until they are facing forward.
This will be your starting position.
Now, keeping the upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Then, inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep those palms neutral like you're holding hammers — no twisting at the wrist.
coaching_cues: Thumbs up, neutral grip throughout.
Elbows stay locked to your sides.
Squeeze at the top, slow descent.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your biceps and into your forearms. If it feels like regular curls, check that neutral grip. Feel that different angle working and carry it through every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating your wrists turns this into a regular curl and misses the point.

name: dumbbell hammer curl on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body and your arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbells while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit on the ball and curl with palms facing in to hit your biceps and forearms.
coaching_cues: Sit tall on ball, feet flat, core engaged.
Curl with palms facing each other, squeeze top.
Lower slowly, keep elbows pinned at sides.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps and the top of your forearms working together. If you rock on the ball, stabilize your core more. Great work building arm strength and balance.
coach_guide.warning: Rocking your body takes tension away from your arms.

name: dumbbell hammer curl v. 2
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and rotate the palms of your hands until they are facing forward.
This will be your starting position.
Now, keeping the upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Then, inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your palms facing in throughout the curl to build bicep and forearm strength.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, palms facing your thighs throughout.
Curl up without rotating, squeeze at top.
Lower with control, full arm extension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel both your biceps and forearms engaged on every rep. If your elbows drift forward, reset your position. Keep grinding and build those arms.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your elbows forward reduces bicep activation.

name: dumbbell hammer curls (with arm blaster)
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and rotate the palms of your hands until they are facing forward.
This will be your starting position.
Now, while holding your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Then, inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The arm blaster locks your elbows in place for strict hammer curls.
coaching_cues: Press elbows firmly into the blaster pad.
Curl with palms in, no swinging allowed.
Squeeze hard at top, lower under control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense bicep contraction with zero momentum. If the blaster digs in uncomfortably, adjust the strap. Strict form means serious gains so stay focused.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning back defeats the purpose of the arm blaster.

name: dumbbell high curl
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Keep your upper arms stationary and curl the dumbbells as high as possible while contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl with your elbows raised high to isolate the bicep peak.
coaching_cues: Raise elbows to shoulder height, keep them there.
Curl dumbbells toward your forehead, squeeze hard.
Lower slowly, maintain that high elbow position.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense squeeze at the top of your biceps near the peak. If your elbows drop, the tension shifts away. Keep those elbows high and chase the pump.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your elbows turns this into a standard curl.

name: dumbbell incline alternate press
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Lean back on the bench and use your thighs to help raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Once at shoulder height, rotate your wrists so that the palms of your hands are facing forward.
Push the dumbbells up with your chest and shoulders, extending your arms fully.
Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, alternating arms.

coach_guide.intro: Alternate arms to build balanced strength and keep constant tension on your chest.
coaching_cues: Press one arm while stabilizing the other.
Lower slow, drive up with chest power.
Keep both shoulders pinned to the bench.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel each side of your chest working independently through the full range. If one arm fatigues faster, that is your weaker side catching up. Stay focused and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting your torso shifts the load off your chest and onto your spine.

name: dumbbell incline bench press
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed firmly against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward, and lift them to shoulder height.
Slowly lower the dumbbells to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Think upper chest — press up and slightly back toward your face.
coaching_cues: Press up and back, not just up.
Elbows track at forty-five degrees.
Squeeze your upper chest at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this right in your upper chest, just below your collarbone. If it is all in your front delts, angle the press more toward your face. Lock onto that upper chest burn and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Pressing straight up misses the upper chest completely.

name: dumbbell incline biceps curl
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward, and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The incline angle stretches your biceps for a deeper contraction at the top.
coaching_cues: Let arms hang straight, palms facing forward.
Curl with control, squeeze hard at top.
Lower slowly, feel the stretch at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong stretch in your biceps at the bottom and a tight squeeze at the top. If your elbows drift forward, you are losing tension. Lock those elbows back and keep curling.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weights uses momentum instead of your biceps.

name: dumbbell incline breeding
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with your back against the pad and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing forward.
Start with your arms fully extended, perpendicular to the ground.
Lower the dumbbells slowly to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Open your arms wide to stretch and squeeze your chest through a full range.
coaching_cues: Slight bend in elbows, arc the weights.
Lower until you feel a deep chest stretch.
Squeeze your chest to bring weights together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest fibers stretching wide at the bottom and contracting hard at the top. If your shoulders take over, lighten the weight. Embrace that stretch and keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Going too heavy forces your shoulders to compensate for your chest.

name: dumbbell incline curl
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Rest your upper arms on the incline bench and let your elbows hang down, fully extending your arms.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The incline position targets your biceps with a full stretch at the bottom.
coaching_cues: Pin your back flat against the bench.
Curl both weights up, squeeze at top.
Control the descent, no bouncing at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working from a fully stretched position to a tight peak contraction. If your shoulders roll forward, reset your posture. Keep that form tight and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbows drift forward reduces biceps activation significantly.

name: dumbbell incline curl v. 2
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This variation emphasizes the stretched position for maximum biceps growth.
coaching_cues: Keep upper arms vertical throughout the curl.
Rotate palms up as you curl higher.
Squeeze at top, lower with full control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense stretch at the bottom and a powerful contraction at the top. If you lose the burn, slow down your tempo. That time under tension builds real strength.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement eliminates the muscle-building tension you need.

name: dumbbell incline fly
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Lie back on the bench and press the dumbbells up to the starting position, directly above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Incline flyes target your upper chest with a deep stretch and squeeze.
coaching_cues: Maintain a slight bend in your elbows.
Lower wide until chest stretches fully open.
Drive weights together, squeezing your chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper chest fibers lengthening at the bottom and contracting at the top. If your front shoulders burn more than your chest, reduce the weight. Trust the process and keep going.
coach_guide.warning: Straightening your elbows puts dangerous stress on your elbow joints.

name: dumbbell incline fly on exercise ball
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Sit on an exercise ball and roll forward until your upper back is resting on the incline bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing each other and your arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The unstable ball forces your core to work while you target your chest.
coaching_cues: Press hips up, keep your core braced.
Lower dumbbells with control, feel the stretch.
Squeeze chest hard as arms come together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest and core working together to stabilize and move the weight. If you wobble too much, plant your feet wider. Stay stable and power through.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips drop puts unnecessary strain on your lower back.

name: dumbbell incline hammer curl
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso and arms fully extended.
Keep your back against the bench and your feet flat on the floor.
While keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The hammer grip targets your brachialis and forearms along with biceps.
coaching_cues: Keep palms facing each other throughout curl.
Pin elbows back, curl with arm strength.
Squeeze forearms at top, lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your outer biceps and forearms working hard on every rep. If your wrists rotate, reset your grip and focus. Build that arm thickness one rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Letting elbows swing forward turns this into a front shoulder exercise.

name: dumbbell incline hammer press
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Lie back on the bench and position the dumbbells at shoulder level with your palms facing each other.
Press the dumbbells up and away from your body until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The neutral grip shifts emphasis to your inner chest and triceps.
coaching_cues: Keep palms facing each other, elbows tucked.
Press straight up, squeeze chest at top.
Lower with control, touch outer chest area.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your inner chest and triceps sharing the workload on each press. If your wrists bend back, grip the dumbbells tighter. Stay locked in and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbows wide removes the inner chest emphasis completely.

name: dumbbell incline hammer press on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Walk your feet forward and roll your body down the ball until your head, neck, and upper back are supported on the ball.
Hold the dumbbells at shoulder level, elbows bent and pointing out to the sides.
Press the dumbbells up and slightly inward, keeping your palms facing each other.
Extend your arms fully, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The ball adds instability making your triceps and core work overtime.
coaching_cues: Bridge hips high, squeeze glutes for stability.
Press with neutral grip, lock out strong.
Lower slow, keep elbows tight to body.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps burning as you press while your core fights to keep you balanced. If you rock side to side, slow down your tempo. Embrace the challenge and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your hips turns this into a decline press and strains your back.

name: dumbbell incline inner biceps curl
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Rest your upper arms on the bench, allowing your elbows to hang down and your palms to face forward.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your pinkies outward to isolate the inner biceps on this incline curl.
coaching_cues: Keep elbows pinned back against the bench.
Rotate pinkies out as you curl up.
Squeeze hard at top, lower slowly down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense contraction on the inner part of your biceps near the elbow. If your shoulders are creeping forward, you are swinging the weight. Stay strict and feel that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Letting elbows drift forward removes tension from the biceps.

name: dumbbell incline one arm fly
instructions: Adjust the incline bench to a 30-45 degree angle.
Sit on the bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting it on your thigh.
Lie back on the bench, keeping your feet flat on the ground.
Hold the dumbbell with your arm extended straight up over your chest.
Lower the dumbbell out to the side in a wide arc, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
Pause when your arm is parallel to the ground, then reverse the motion to bring the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Control one dumbbell in a wide arc to stretch and squeeze your chest.
coaching_cues: Keep a slight bend in your elbow.
Lower in a wide arc until chest stretches.
Squeeze your chest to bring it back up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch at the bottom and a strong chest contraction at the top. If your shoulder feels strained, reduce the range slightly. Own each rep with control.
coach_guide.warning: Going too deep with a straight arm stresses the shoulder joint.

name: dumbbell incline one arm fly on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with a dumbbell in one hand.
Walk your feet forward and roll your body down until your head, neck, and upper back are supported on the ball.
Hold the dumbbell with your arm extended straight up over your chest, palm facing inwards.
Slowly lower the dumbbell out to the side, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
Pause for a moment when your arm is parallel to the ground.
Engage your chest muscles to bring the dumbbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: The unstable ball forces your core to stabilize while your chest does the work.
coaching_cues: Press upper back firmly into the ball.
Lower the dumbbell in a wide controlled arc.
Drive back up squeezing your chest hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working while your core fights to keep you balanced. If you wobble too much, brace your abs tighter. Master the stability and build that chest.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement on an unstable surface risks losing control.

name: dumbbell incline one arm hammer press
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting on your thigh.
Lean back on the bench and use your thigh to help raise the dumbbell to shoulder height.
Rotate your wrist so that your palm is facing inward, towards your body.
Press the dumbbell up and away from your body, extending your arm fully.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: The neutral grip hammer press shifts more work onto your triceps and inner chest.
coaching_cues: Keep your palm facing inward throughout.
Lower until upper arm is parallel to floor.
Press straight up and lock out strong.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps firing hard at the top of each press. If your wrist is rotating, reset your grip. Push through and build those arms.
coach_guide.warning: Letting the elbow flare out shifts stress away from triceps.

name: dumbbell incline one arm hammer press on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with a dumbbell in one hand.
Walk your feet forward and roll your body down until your head, neck, and upper back are supported on the ball.
Hold the dumbbell with your palm facing inward and your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle.
Press the dumbbell up towards the ceiling, straightening your arm.
Lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: The ball adds instability making your core and triceps work even harder.
coaching_cues: Drive upper back into the ball firmly.
Lower with control, elbow tracking close in.
Press up powerfully, squeeze triceps at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps burning and your core engaged to stay balanced. If you feel unstable, slow down the tempo. Stay tight and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Losing core tension on the ball can cause you to roll off.

name: dumbbell incline one arm lateral raise
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting it on your thigh.
Lean forward and position your upper arm against the inside of your thigh.
Raise the dumbbell to the side, keeping your arm slightly bent and your palm facing down.
Continue lifting until your arm is parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: The incline angle targets your side delts while reducing momentum cheating.
coaching_cues: Lie chest down on the incline bench.
Raise the dumbbell out to shoulder height.
Lower with control, do not just drop it.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong burn in the side of your shoulder at the top. If your trap is taking over, lower the weight. Isolate that delt and keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Using too much weight causes the upper trap to dominate.

name: dumbbell incline one arm press
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting on your thigh.
Lie back on the bench and position the dumbbell at shoulder level, palm facing forward.
Press the dumbbell upward and slightly inward, extending your arm fully.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Unilateral pressing builds balanced strength and lets you focus on each side.
coaching_cues: Keep your non-working hand on your hip.
Lower until your elbow is below chest level.
Press up and squeeze your chest at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel one side of your chest fully engaged through the entire rep. If you are rotating your torso, brace your core harder. Build that balanced chest.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating your torso turns this into an unstable twisting motion.

name: dumbbell incline one arm press on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with a dumbbell in one hand, feet flat on the ground.
Slowly walk your feet forward, rolling the ball until your head, neck, and upper back are supported on the ball.
Hold the dumbbell at shoulder height with your palm facing forward.
Press the dumbbell upward until your arm is fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: The ball challenges your stability while you press one arm at a time.
coaching_cues: Press upper back firmly into the ball.
Lower the dumbbell with a controlled tempo.
Drive up strong, squeeze chest at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working while your core stabilizes against rotation. If you are tipping sideways, engage your glutes more. Stay balanced and keep pressing.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips drop puts strain on your lower back.

name: dumbbell incline palm-in press
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Plant your feet firmly on the ground and keep your back straight against the bench.
Start with the dumbbells at shoulder level, elbows bent and palms facing each other.
Press the dumbbells up and away from your body, extending your arms fully.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Palms facing in reduces shoulder strain and emphasizes the inner chest.
coaching_cues: Keep palms facing each other throughout.
Lower until dumbbells are at chest level.
Press up and squeeze your chest together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your inner chest fibers working hard at the top of each press. If your shoulders feel off, check that your grip stays neutral. Push through and build that chest.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring elbows wide negates the shoulder-friendly palm-in position.

name: dumbbell incline press on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Slowly walk your feet forward, rolling your body down the ball until your head, neck, and upper back are supported on the ball.
Hold the dumbbells at shoulder level, elbows bent and pointing out to the sides.
Press the dumbbells upward, extending your arms fully.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The exercise ball adds core activation to your incline chest press.
coaching_cues: Drive your upper back into the ball.
Lower dumbbells to chest level with control.
Press straight up and squeeze your chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper chest and core working together to stabilize each rep. If you wobble excessively, slow down and brace harder. Stay strong and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag puts unnecessary stress on your spine.

name: dumbbell incline raise
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Lean back on the bench and raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Keeping your back against the bench, exhale and raise the dumbbells above your head, fully extending your arms.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your chest on the bench and lift the dumbbells forward with control.
coaching_cues: Press chest firmly into the incline bench.
Raise arms forward to shoulder height, pause.
Lower slowly, keep tension in your delts.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the front of your shoulders burning with each lift. If your traps are taking over, lower the weight and focus on the raise. Keep those shoulders working strong.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting too heavy causes your traps to dominate the movement.

name: dumbbell incline rear lateral raise
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with your chest against the backrest and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Extend your arms straight down with your palms facing each other.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let your arms hang then squeeze your rear delts to raise the weights out wide.
coaching_cues: Chest down on bench, arms hanging straight.
Raise dumbbells out wide, lead with elbows.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in the back of your shoulders near your shoulder blades. If you feel it in your neck, relax your traps. Stay focused on those rear delts.
coach_guide.warning: Shrugging your shoulders up puts unnecessary strain on your neck.

name: dumbbell incline row
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Grab a dumbbell in each hand and sit on the bench with your chest against the incline.
Extend your arms fully, allowing the dumbbells to hang straight down from your shoulders.
Pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the dumbbells toward your hips while squeezing your upper back tight.
coaching_cues: Chest pressed into bench, core engaged tight.
Pull elbows back past your ribs, squeeze.
Lower with control, stretch at the bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles working hard on every row. If your biceps fatigue first, focus on pulling with your elbows. Build that strong back.
coach_guide.warning: Jerking the weights up can strain your lower back.

name: dumbbell incline shoulder raise
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing down.
Rest the dumbbells on your thighs and lean back onto the bench.
Use your thighs to help raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, then rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing forward.
Exhale and slowly raise the dumbbells above your head, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Push the dumbbells forward by protracting your shoulder blades off the bench.
coaching_cues: Start with arms extended, shoulders back.
Push dumbbells forward, spread your shoulder blades.
Control the return, feel the stretch back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the muscles along your ribs under your armpits engaging. If your shoulders feel jammed up, focus on reaching forward. Keep driving that serratus.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your elbows turns this into a pressing movement.

name: dumbbell incline shrug
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand.
Place your feet flat on the ground and let your arms hang straight down with your palms facing your body.
Keeping your arms straight, shrug your shoulders up towards your ears as high as possible.
Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears against the incline.
coaching_cues: Let arms hang, relax your traps first.
Shrug straight up, hold the squeeze tight.
Lower slowly, feel the stretch at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your traps burning from the top of your shoulders toward your neck. If your arms are bending, let them stay straight and relaxed. Strong traps are built here.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling your shoulders can irritate your rotator cuff.

name: dumbbell incline t-raise
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing inwards.
Lean forward and let your arms hang straight down, perpendicular to the floor.
Keeping your arms straight, raise them out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, forming a 'T' shape with your body.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise the dumbbells out to form a T shape with your body.
coaching_cues: Chest on bench, arms hanging straight down.
Raise arms out to sides, forming T.
Hold at top, squeeze delts, lower controlled.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the sides and rear of your shoulders lighting up at the top. If your traps cramp, lower the weight and isolate better. Keep building those shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum reduces the work your delts are doing.

name: dumbbell incline triceps extension
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing inwards.
Extend your arms fully overhead, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Lower the dumbbells behind your head by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbells back to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your upper arms still and extend only at the elbows.
coaching_cues: Upper arms locked, elbows pointing up high.
Extend dumbbells up, squeeze your triceps hard.
Lower behind head slowly, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the back of your arms working from elbow to shoulder. If your elbows flare wide, bring them back in tight. Finish strong with every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting elbows drift forward shifts work away from triceps.

name: dumbbell incline twisted flyes
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Lie back on the bench and press the dumbbells up to the starting position, directly above your chest, with your arms extended.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
As you lower the dumbbells, rotate your wrists so that your palms face forward at the bottom of the movement.
Reverse the motion and bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position, squeezing your chest muscles together at the top.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Open your arms wide then twist the dumbbells as you squeeze together.
coaching_cues: Lower wide with palms facing each other.
Squeeze up, rotate pinkies toward each other.
Feel the chest contract hard at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep squeeze across your entire chest at the top of each rep. If your shoulders feel strained, reduce the range slightly. Keep chasing that chest pump.
coach_guide.warning: Going too deep can overstretch your shoulder joints.

name: dumbbell incline two arm extension
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Slowly lie back on the bench, keeping the dumbbells close to your chest.
Once you are fully lying down, extend your arms straight up towards the ceiling, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press both dumbbells up by straightening your arms overhead together.
coaching_cues: Hold dumbbells together, elbows bent behind head.
Extend both arms up, squeeze triceps tight.
Lower together with control, stretch at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel both triceps burning evenly as you press up. If one arm is weaker, focus on pushing evenly with both. Keep those arms strong and balanced.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back excessively puts pressure on your spine.

name: dumbbell incline y-raise
instructions: Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit on it with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing inwards.
Lean forward slightly and let your arms hang straight down, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Raise your arms out to the sides and up in a Y shape until they are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise the dumbbells up and out to form a Y shape overhead.
coaching_cues: Chest down on bench, arms hanging low.
Raise arms up and out forming Y.
Squeeze upper back at top, lower controlled.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back and rear shoulders working together on every rep. If your lower back arches, press your hips into the bench harder. Keep building that upper back strength.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting too heavy compromises your form and shoulder health.

name: dumbbell iron cross
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the ground, forming a T shape with your body.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise the dumbbells out to your sides forming a T shape with your body.
coaching_cues: Slight bend in elbows throughout the lift.
Raise arms to shoulder height, no higher.
Control the descent, do not just drop them.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn across the caps of your shoulders and into your upper back. If your traps are burning more than your delts, lower the weight and focus on leading with your pinkies. Keep that controlled tempo.
coach_guide.warning: Going too heavy forces your traps to take over the movement.

name: dumbbell kickback
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Bring your upper arms close to your sides, with your elbows bent at a 90-degree angle.
Extend your arms straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge forward and extend the dumbbell straight back squeezing your triceps at the top.
coaching_cues: Keep your upper arm glued to your side.
Extend fully until your arm is straight back.
Squeeze hard at the top, then lower slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in the back of your upper arm at full extension. If you feel nothing, you are probably swinging the weight. Lock that elbow position and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the dumbbell uses momentum instead of your triceps.

name: dumbbell kickbacks on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing inwards and your arms bent at a 90-degree angle.
Extend your arms straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Perform triceps kickbacks while kneeling on an exercise ball for added core challenge.
coaching_cues: Brace your core to stay stable on ball.
Upper arm stays parallel to the floor throughout.
Extend fully and squeeze your triceps hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps working while your core fights to keep you balanced. If you are wobbling too much, use a lighter weight until you build stability. Stay focused and keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement will cause you to lose balance and form.

name: dumbbell kneeling bicep curl exercise ball
instructions: Kneel on the floor with an exercise ball in front of you.
Place your elbows on top of the exercise ball, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing up.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbells while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Kneel on an exercise ball and curl the dumbbells while maintaining your balance.
coaching_cues: Engage your core to stabilize on ball.
Keep elbows pinned at your sides throughout.
Curl with control, squeeze biceps at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps burning while your core works overtime to keep you steady. If you are tipping forward, slow down and use lighter weight. Great work challenging your whole body.
coach_guide.warning: Losing core tension will cause you to fall off the ball.

name: dumbbell lateral raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive with your elbows, not your hands — and keep those shoulders down.
coaching_cues: Elbows lead up, shoulders stay down.
Pinky slightly higher than your thumb.
Slow and controlled on the way down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn on the outer sides of your shoulders right now. If it is in your neck or upper traps, go lighter next set. Lock in that side-delt burn and keep going.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your shoulders shrug toward your ears, you have lost the target.

name: dumbbell lateral to front raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the ground, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Next, raise your arms in front of you until they are parallel to the ground, again keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
