Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise the dumbbells out to your sides then bring them to the front in one fluid motion.
coaching_cues: Start with lateral raise to shoulder height.
Sweep arms forward to front raise position.
Lower with control and repeat the pattern.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders burning from multiple angles as you hit both side and front delts. If your lower back is arching, tighten your core and use less weight. Push through that shoulder burn.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum and swinging reduces the work on your delts.

name: dumbbell lunge
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Take a step forward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position.
Keep your back straight and your chest up as you lower your body.
Push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat with your left leg.
Alternate legs for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Control the descent and explode back up through your front heel.
coaching_cues: Step out strong, chest stays up.
Drop down controlled, knee tracks toe.
Explode up through front heel power.
coach_guide.feel: That front glute should be burning deep right now, with your back leg feeling the stretch. If you're wobbling, focus on that front heel connection. Drive through that burn and keep it steady.
coach_guide.warning: Never let your front knee collapse inward during the movement.

name: dumbbell lunge with bicep curl
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing forward.
Take a step forward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position. Your right knee should be bent at a 90-degree angle and your left knee should be hovering just above the ground.
As you lunge forward, simultaneously curl the dumbbells towards your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the lunge, then push through your right heel to return to the starting position, lowering the dumbbells back down to your sides.
Repeat the lunge and bicep curl on the opposite side, stepping forward with your left foot.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Step into a lunge and curl the dumbbells at the bottom for a total body challenge.
coaching_cues: Step forward into a deep controlled lunge.
Curl both dumbbells at the bottom position.
Push back up as you lower the weights.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your legs burning from the lunge while your biceps work during the curl. If you are losing balance, take a shorter step and stabilize first. You are building strength head to toe.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing through combines sloppiness in both movements.

name: dumbbell lying extension (across face)
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up above your chest, palms facing each other.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the dumbbell in an arc behind your head until your forearms are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then contract your triceps to bring the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie back and lower the dumbbell across your face then extend it back up.
coaching_cues: Keep upper arms vertical and stationary throughout.
Lower the weight slowly across your forehead.
Extend back up squeezing triceps at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your triceps as you lower and a strong squeeze as you press up. If your elbows are flaring wide, bring them in closer together. Control the weight and feel every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping the weight too fast puts your face at risk.

name: dumbbell lying alternate extension
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Lower one dumbbell down towards your head, bending at the elbow, while keeping the other arm extended.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then raise the dumbbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat with the other arm, alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie back and alternate extending each dumbbell while keeping the other arm steady.
coaching_cues: One arm extends while other stays bent.
Lower slowly toward your forehead with control.
Alternate sides with a steady smooth rhythm.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel each triceps working independently giving you a chance to fix imbalances. If one arm is weaker, let it set the pace for both. Stay patient and build equal strength.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the alternating pattern leads to sloppy uneven reps.

name: dumbbell lying elbow press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other and arms extended straight up over your chest.
Lower the dumbbells towards your shoulders by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then press the dumbbells back up to the starting position by extending your elbows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie back and press the dumbbells up by straightening your arms from an elbow down position.
coaching_cues: Start with elbows on the floor, dumbbells up.
Press straight up by extending your elbows.
Lower until elbows touch the floor again.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps doing all the work since your elbows reset on each rep. If your shoulders are taking over, keep your upper arms more vertical. Great isolation work for stronger arms.
coach_guide.warning: Letting elbows flare wide shifts stress to your shoulders.

name: dumbbell lying external shoulder rotation
instructions: Lie on your side on a flat bench with your upper arm against your side and your elbow bent 90 degrees.
Hold a dumbbell in your hand with your palm facing down.
Keeping your upper arm against your side, slowly rotate your forearm upward as far as possible.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your forearm back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Lie on your side and rotate the dumbbell upward to strengthen your rotator cuff.
coaching_cues: Keep your elbow bent at 90 degrees.
Rotate your forearm upward toward the ceiling.
Lower slowly with complete control throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the small muscles deep in your shoulder doing the work. If your whole arm is moving, pin your elbow tighter to your side. This builds bulletproof shoulders so stay consistent.
coach_guide.warning: Using too much weight strains the small rotator cuff muscles.

name: dumbbell lying femoral
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and a dumbbell resting on your lower abdomen.
Bend your knees and bring the dumbbell towards your glutes, keeping your feet flat on the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Squeeze that dumbbell between your feet and curl it up with your hamstrings.
coaching_cues: Keep hips pressed flat into the bench.
Curl the weight slowly toward your glutes.
Lower with control, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in the back of your thighs throughout the movement. If your lower back arches, you are using too much weight. Stay focused and squeeze those hamstrings.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your hips shifts the load to your lower back.

name: dumbbell lying hammer press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other and arms extended straight up.
Lower the dumbbells to the sides of your chest, keeping your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Press the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press with a neutral grip to target your chest while being kind to your shoulders.
coaching_cues: Palms face each other, elbows tucked in.
Lower dumbbells to the sides of chest.
Press up and squeeze your chest hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your inner chest working as you press the weights together at the top. If your shoulders take over, tuck your elbows more. Keep that chest engaged and push strong.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbows outward puts unnecessary stress on your shoulders.

name: dumbbell lying on floor rear delt raise
instructions: Lie face down on the floor with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight out in front of you, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Engaging your shoulder muscles, lift your arms up and out to the sides, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lying face down keeps you honest and isolates your rear delts perfectly.
coaching_cues: Keep a slight bend in your elbows.
Raise arms out to sides, pinch shoulder blades.
Lower slowly, maintain constant tension throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn right in the back of your shoulders near your shoulder blades. If you feel it in your traps, lower the weight. Stay patient and build those rear delts.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum steals the work from your rear deltoids.

name: dumbbell lying one arm deltoid rear
instructions: Lie face down on a flat bench with a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing inwards.
Extend your arm straight down towards the floor, keeping it close to your body.
Raise your arm up and back, squeezing your shoulder blade towards your spine.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arm back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: This single arm variation lets you focus completely on each rear delt.
coaching_cues: Lie on your side, arm hanging down.
Raise the dumbbell up and back slightly.
Control the weight down, no swinging allowed.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your rear shoulder doing all the lifting without help from your back. If your body rocks, brace your core harder. One arm at a time builds balanced shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling your body uses momentum instead of muscle.

name: dumbbell lying one arm press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in one hand and your feet flat on the ground.
Hold the dumbbell at shoulder level with your palm facing forward.
Press the dumbbell upward until your arm is fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Single arm pressing challenges your chest and core stability at once.
coaching_cues: Brace your core, keep hips level.
Lower the dumbbell to your chest slowly.
Press straight up, squeeze your chest hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working while your core fights to keep you stable. If you rotate off the bench, lighten the weight. Stay tight and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your torso twist reduces chest activation significantly.

name: dumbbell lying one arm press v. 2
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your back supported and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing towards your feet.
Extend your arm straight up towards the ceiling, keeping your elbow slightly bent.
Slowly lower the dumbbell down towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the dumbbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: This variation shifts the angle to hit your chest in a new way.
coaching_cues: Keep your non-working hand on hip.
Lower with control to outer chest area.
Drive up powerfully, exhale at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel deep chest engagement with extra core stability demands. If one side feels weaker, give it extra focus. Balance and strength come with consistent practice.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement reduces muscle engagement and control.

name: dumbbell lying one arm pronated triceps extension
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your back and head supported, and your feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing down, and extend your arm straight up above your shoulder.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbow.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arm back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: The pronated grip targets the outer head of your triceps effectively.
coaching_cues: Palm faces down, elbow points to ceiling.
Lower the weight toward your opposite shoulder.
Extend fully, squeeze your triceps hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the outside of your triceps burning with each extension. If your elbow flares out, keep it locked in place. Strong triceps are built one focused rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Moving your elbow shifts work away from the triceps.

name: dumbbell lying one arm rear lateral raise
instructions: Lie face down on a flat bench with a dumbbell in one hand, hanging towards the floor.
Keep your arm straight and lift the dumbbell out to the side, away from your body.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Lying on your side isolates each rear delt without momentum cheating.
coaching_cues: Keep slight elbow bend throughout movement.
Raise arm until parallel with the floor.
Lower slowly, resist gravity the whole way.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a focused burn in the back of your shoulder with every rep. If you feel your trap taking over, lower the weight. Isolate and dominate those rear delts.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting too high recruits your traps instead of delts.

name: dumbbell lying one arm supinated triceps extension
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your back and head supported, and your feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with an underhand grip, and extend your arm straight up above your shoulder.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbow.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then extend your arm back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: The supinated grip shifts focus to the inner head of your triceps.
coaching_cues: Palm faces up, keep elbow completely still.
Lower the dumbbell toward your shoulder slowly.
Extend and lock out, squeeze triceps tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the inner part of your triceps working hard on each rep. If your shoulder moves, you are going too heavy. Lock in that elbow and feel the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Allowing elbow drift turns this into a shoulder exercise.

name: dumbbell lying pronation
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your chest facing down and your arms extended straight down, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Rotate your palms so they are facing up.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbells as you rotate your palms to face down.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your forearm to build grip strength and wrist stability.
coaching_cues: Rest forearm on bench, wrist hanging off.
Rotate palm from up to facing down.
Move slowly with control, no jerky motions.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the muscles on top of your forearm working through the rotation. If your elbow lifts, pin it down firmly. Strong forearms support every upper body lift you do.
coach_guide.warning: Fast uncontrolled rotations can strain your wrist joint.

name: dumbbell lying pronation on floor
instructions: Lie flat on the floor with your face down and your arms extended straight out in front of you, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Rotate your palms so they are facing down towards the floor.
Keeping your arms straight, lift the dumbbells off the floor by contracting your forearms.
Continue lifting until your forearms are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your forearm slowly to build grip strength and wrist stability.
coaching_cues: Keep your upper arm glued to floor.
Rotate palm down with smooth control.
Pause at bottom then return slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn through your outer forearm near your elbow. If your shoulder is lifting, you are using too much weight. Stay focused and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Fast jerky rotations can strain your wrist ligaments.

name: dumbbell lying pullover on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball and roll forward until your upper back is resting on the ball.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head while keeping your arms straight.
Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the ball to extend your range and get a deep chest stretch.
coaching_cues: Hips up, core tight on the ball.
Lower the weight behind your head slowly.
Pull with your chest not your arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a powerful stretch across your chest and lats at the bottom. If your lower back arches, engage your core more. Keep that stretch and squeeze going.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping the weight too far back strains your shoulder joints.

name: dumbbell lying rear delt row
instructions: Lie face down on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing inwards.
Extend your arms straight down towards the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Engaging your back muscles, lift the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the dumbbells toward your hips to target your upper back muscles.
coaching_cues: Chest down on bench, stay flat.
Drive elbows up toward the ceiling.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles working hard between your shoulder blades. If your neck is straining, relax your head down. Great work building that back strength.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your chest off the bench reduces upper back activation.

name: dumbbell lying rear lateral raise
instructions: Lie face down on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight down towards the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Engaging your shoulder muscles, lift your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise the weights out to the sides to isolate your rear delts.
coaching_cues: Keep a slight bend in elbows.
Lift wide leading with your pinkies.
Control the weight down slowly always.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn in the back of your shoulders near your shoulder blades. If your traps are burning first, lower the weight. Keep squeezing those rear delts.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum robs your rear delts of the work.

name: dumbbell lying single extension
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in one hand and your arm fully extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbell in a controlled manner towards your forehead, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Pause briefly at the bottom of the movement, then extend your arm back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Extend one arm at a time to build balanced triceps strength.
coaching_cues: Upper arm stays completely vertical still.
Lower the weight beside your ear slowly.
Extend fully and squeeze your triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps working from elbow to shoulder on every rep. If your elbow drifts outward, reset your position. One arm at a time, you have got this.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbow flare puts stress on your shoulder.

name: dumbbell lying supination
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing up and your arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, curl the dumbbells towards your shoulders by contracting your forearms.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your palm upward to strengthen your forearm supinators.
coaching_cues: Rest your forearm flat on bench.
Rotate palm up with full control.
Pause at top then lower slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the inner part of your forearm working with each rotation. If your wrist feels unstable, use a lighter weight. Keep those controlled rotations coming.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement can irritate your wrist tendons.

name: dumbbell lying supination on floor
instructions: Lie flat on your back on the floor with your legs extended and your arms by your sides, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Rotate your palms to face up, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Slowly curl the dumbbells towards your shoulders, squeezing your forearms.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The floor gives you a stable base for focused forearm supination work.
coaching_cues: Pin your elbow firmly to floor.
Turn your palm up toward ceiling.
Reverse the motion with equal control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your inner forearm muscles contracting with each rep. If you feel wrist discomfort, lighten up the weight. Stay patient and keep building that grip.
coach_guide.warning: Gripping too tight can cause unnecessary wrist strain.

name: dumbbell lying supine biceps curl
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your back and head supported, and your feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing up and your arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lying flat lets you isolate your biceps with zero momentum.
coaching_cues: Arms hang straight down toward floor.
Curl up keeping upper arms still.
Squeeze hard at top then lower slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working from insertion to peak on every curl. If your elbows drift forward, you are swinging. Stay strict and feel that bicep burn.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weights takes tension off your biceps.

name: dumbbell lying supine curl
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your back flat and curl with pure bicep strength.
coaching_cues: Palms face up throughout the curl.
Keep your elbows pinned at sides.
Lower with control, no dropping allowed.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel constant tension in your biceps from bottom to top. If your shoulders are rolling forward, reset your position. Great focus, keep those curls clean.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back shifts the load to your spine.

name: dumbbell lying triceps extension
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Lower the dumbbells down towards your forehead, bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lower the weight behind your head to fully stretch and work your triceps.
coaching_cues: Keep both elbows pointing at ceiling.
Lower the weight toward your forehead.
Extend arms fully, squeeze at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your triceps at the bottom and a strong squeeze at the top. If your elbows flare out, bring them back in. Finish those reps strong.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring elbows puts dangerous stress on your shoulders.

name: dumbbell lying wide curl
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let your arms open wide and curl using only your biceps.
coaching_cues: Arms out wide, palms facing the ceiling.
Curl dumbbells up in a wide sweeping arc.
Squeeze biceps hard at the top, lower slow.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch across your inner biceps at the bottom and a strong squeeze at the top. If your shoulders roll forward, lighten the weight. Keep that wide path and feel the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting too heavy causes your shoulders to take over the movement.

name: dumbbell neutral grip bench press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and your arms extended straight up over your chest.
Slowly lower the dumbbells down towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press with palms facing each other to shift the work onto your triceps.
coaching_cues: Palms face each other, elbows tucked close.
Lower dumbbells to your outer chest slowly.
Press straight up, locking out your elbows.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps working hard as you lock out each rep. If your chest takes over, bring your elbows in tighter. Stay strong through every lockout.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbows wide turns this into a chest exercise.

name: dumbbell one arm bench fly
instructions: Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting it on top of your thigh.
Lie back on the bench, keeping the dumbbell pressed against your thigh.
Using your free hand, help lift the dumbbell up to the starting position.
Hold the dumbbell directly above your shoulder with your arm extended and palm facing inward.
Lower the dumbbell out to the side in a wide arc, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
Pause when your arm is parallel to the ground, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Use one arm at a time to isolate each side of your chest.
coaching_cues: Brace your core, free hand holds bench.
Lower the dumbbell out wide with control.
Squeeze your chest to bring it back up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your chest at the bottom and a strong contraction as you bring the weight up. If you feel it in your shoulder, reduce the range. Keep squeezing that chest.
coach_guide.warning: Going too deep puts excessive stress on your shoulder joint.

name: dumbbell one arm bent-over row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing your body.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Let the dumbbell hang straight down towards the floor, with your arm fully extended.
Pull the dumbbell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Drive that elbow back like you're throwing a punch in reverse.
coaching_cues: Elbow stays tight to your body.
Pull to your ribs, not your chest.
Control the weight down slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this working deep in your lat and between your shoulder blades on the working side. If it's all in your arm, think about pulling your elbow back instead of lifting the weight. Keep that lat burn going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Let your elbow flare out and you lose all the good stuff.

name: dumbbell one arm chest fly on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with a dumbbell in one hand and your feet flat on the ground.
Walk your feet forward, rolling the ball until your upper back is supported on the ball and your head, neck, and shoulders are off the ball.
Extend your arm with the dumbbell straight up above your chest, palm facing inward.
Slowly lower the dumbbell out to the side, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
Pause for a moment when your arm is parallel to the ground.
Engage your chest muscles to bring the dumbbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: The unstable ball forces your core to work while you isolate your chest.
coaching_cues: Hips up, shoulders planted on the ball.
Open your arm wide, slight bend in elbow.
Squeeze your chest to bring dumbbell back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest stretching and contracting while your core fights to keep you balanced. If you wobble too much, widen your feet. Embrace the challenge and keep flying.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your hips causes you to lose core stability.

name: dumbbell one arm concentration curl (on stability ball)
instructions: Sit on a stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing up and your arm extended down towards the floor.
Rest your elbow on the inside of your thigh, just above the knee.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder while contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Pin your elbow in place and curl with pure bicep strength.
coaching_cues: Elbow pressed firmly into your inner thigh.
Curl the dumbbell up slow and controlled.
Squeeze hard at top, lower without swinging.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in your bicep with nowhere to hide. If your elbow lifts off your thigh, you are using momentum. Stay locked in and feel every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbow drift removes the isolation from your bicep.

name: dumbbell one arm decline chest press
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with a dumbbell in one hand, resting on your chest.
Place your feet flat on the ground and keep your back pressed against the bench.
Extend your arm and push the dumbbell up towards the ceiling, fully extending your elbow.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: The decline angle targets your lower chest with one arm at a time.
coaching_cues: Grip the bench, keep your core tight.
Lower the dumbbell to your outer chest.
Press up and across toward your centerline.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower chest doing all the pushing work. If one side feels weaker, focus on that connection. Balance out your strength one rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating your torso steals tension from your chest muscle.

name: dumbbell one arm fly on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with a dumbbell in one hand and your feet flat on the ground.
Walk your feet forward and roll your body down until your upper back is resting on the exercise ball.
Extend your arm with the dumbbell straight up above your chest, palm facing inwards.
Slowly lower the dumbbell out to the side, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: The ball adds instability making your chest and core work together.
coaching_cues: Hips high, shoulder blades on the ball.
Open your arm wide in a big arc.
Squeeze chest to bring the dumbbell back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest stretching wide and your core firing to keep balance. If the ball rolls, slow down and stabilize first. You have got this, stay controlled.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement causes you to lose balance and form.

name: dumbbell one arm french press on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing up and your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle.
Extend your arm straight up towards the ceiling, keeping your elbow stationary.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Isolate one tricep at a time while your core stabilizes on the ball.
coaching_cues: Upper arm locked vertical, do not move.
Lower dumbbell behind your head with control.
Extend your arm straight up, squeeze tricep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your tricep at the bottom and a strong squeeze at the top. If your elbow flares out, reset your position. Keep that arm steady and burn it out.
coach_guide.warning: Moving your upper arm turns this into a shoulder exercise.

name: dumbbell one arm hammer preacher curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with a dumbbell in one hand and your upper arm resting on the pad.
Hold the dumbbell with a neutral grip (palms facing your body).
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: The hammer grip and preacher pad lock in pure bicep isolation.
coaching_cues: Arm flat on pad, palm facing inward.
Curl the dumbbell up without lifting elbow.
Squeeze at top, lower slow and controlled.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your bicep and forearm working together through the full range. If your elbow lifts off the pad, go lighter. Stay strict and feel that deep contraction.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your elbow off the pad lets momentum do the work.

name: dumbbell one arm hammer press on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing inwards and your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle.
Place your other hand on your hip for stability.
Press the dumbbell upwards, extending your arm fully.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: The hammer grip on the ball targets your triceps with added core work.
coaching_cues: Palms face each other, elbows tucked in.
Lower dumbbells slowly toward your shoulders.
Press up strong, lock out your elbows.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps burning on every lockout while your core keeps you stable. If you sink into the ball, squeeze your glutes harder. Stay tight and press through.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips drop puts strain on your lower back.

name: dumbbell one arm incline chest press
instructions: Adjust the incline bench to a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with your back against the pad and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with an overhand grip, resting it on your shoulder.
Push the dumbbell up and away from your body, extending your arm fully.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Press one arm at a time to build balanced chest strength on the incline.
coaching_cues: Plant feet firm, brace your core tight.
Lower slow, elbow at 45 degree angle.
Press up and squeeze chest at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in your upper chest on every rep. The single arm work also fires up your core for stability. Stay focused and own each press.
coach_guide.warning: Do not let your torso rotate or twist during the press.

name: dumbbell one arm kickback
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in your right hand.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Bring your right elbow up to your side, keeping it bent at a 90-degree angle.
Extend your right arm straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Isolate your triceps by kicking the weight back with a locked upper arm.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward, keep upper arm glued still.
Extend arm back until fully straight.
Squeeze triceps hard, then lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a burn deep in the back of your arm at full extension. If your elbow drops, you lose the isolation. Keep it strict and feel that triceps fire.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weight uses momentum and removes tension from triceps.

name: dumbbell one arm lateral raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing your body.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged throughout the exercise.
Raise the dumbbell to the side, keeping your arm straight and your palm facing down.
Continue lifting until your arm is parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Lift the dumbbell out to your side to sculpt your shoulder caps.
coaching_cues: Stand tall with a slight bend in elbow.
Raise arm to shoulder height, pinky up.
Lower slow, do not let gravity win.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your outer shoulder doing all the lifting work. If you shrug or swing, the trap takes over. Stay controlled and feel that delt burn.
coach_guide.warning: Raising higher than shoulder level can pinch your rotator cuff.

name: dumbbell one arm lateral raise with support
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand, with your palm facing your body.
Place your other hand on a stable surface, such as a bench or wall, for support.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Raise the dumbbell out to the side, keeping your arm straight and your palm facing down.
Continue lifting until your arm is parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Use the support to eliminate cheating and isolate one shoulder completely.
coaching_cues: Hold something sturdy, lean slightly away.
Raise arm to shoulder height, control it.
Pause at top, then lower with intention.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel pure isolation in your side delt with zero momentum. The support keeps you honest and maximizes the burn. Embrace the shake and keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning too far puts unnecessary strain on your lower back.

name: dumbbell one arm press on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand and position it at shoulder height, with your elbow bent and palm facing forward.
Slowly press the dumbbell upward until your arm is fully extended, while keeping your core engaged and maintaining balance on the exercise ball.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: The unstable ball forces your core to work while you press one arm.
coaching_cues: Plant feet wide, hips up and stable.
Lower dumbbell slow, elbow at 45 degrees.
Press up strong, squeeze chest at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working while your core fights to keep you balanced. This builds real functional strength you can use anywhere. Stay tight and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Do not let your hips sag or drop during the press.

name: dumbbell one arm prone curl
instructions: Lie face down on a flat bench with a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing down.
Extend your arm fully, letting it hang straight down towards the floor.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder by contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Lying face down removes all momentum for a strict biceps curl.
coaching_cues: Let arm hang straight, shoulder locked down.
Curl up slow, squeeze biceps at top.
Lower with control, full stretch at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense squeeze in your biceps with zero help from swinging. The prone position makes every rep honest and effective. Keep grinding through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your shoulder off the bench reduces biceps activation.

name: dumbbell one arm prone hammer curl
instructions: Lie face down on a flat bench with a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing your body and arm fully extended.
Keep your upper arm stationary and curl the dumbbell towards your shoulder, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: The hammer grip hits your biceps and forearms from a strict prone position.
coaching_cues: Keep palm facing in throughout the curl.
Curl up controlled, squeeze at the top.
Lower slow, feel the full arm stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel both your biceps and forearms working together on each rep. The prone position keeps you honest with no cheating allowed. Stay strict and build real strength.
coach_guide.warning: Do not rotate your wrist or you lose the hammer grip benefit.

name: dumbbell one arm pullover on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Hold a dumbbell with one hand and extend your arm straight up above your chest.
Slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head while keeping your arm straight.
Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: The pullover stretches and contracts your chest while challenging core stability.
coaching_cues: Keep hips up, core braced throughout movement.
Lower weight behind head with slight bend.
Pull back over chest, squeeze pecs hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your chest at the bottom and a strong squeeze at the top. The ball adds an extra stability challenge for your whole body. Breathe deep and control every inch.
coach_guide.warning: Going too deep can overstretch and strain your shoulder joint.

name: dumbbell one arm reverse wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with an overhand grip, palm facing down.
Rest your forearm on your thigh, with your wrist hanging off the edge.
Slowly lower the dumbbell towards the ground by flexing your wrist.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly curl your wrist back up towards your body.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Target the top of your forearms by curling with your palm facing down.
coaching_cues: Rest forearm on thigh, wrist over knee.
Curl wrist up, squeeze forearm extensors hard.
Lower slow, get full range of motion.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn on top of your forearm near your elbow. These small muscles fatigue fast but grow stronger quickly. Stay patient and feel every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Using too heavy weight strains the small wrist tendons.

name: dumbbell one arm reverse fly (with support)
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing inwards.
Lean forward and place your free hand on the bench for support.
Keep your arm slightly bent and raise it out to the side until it is parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arm back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Isolate your rear delts by flying the weight back with a supported position.
coaching_cues: Lean into support, let arm hang down.
Raise arm out and back, slight elbow bend.
Squeeze rear delt, then lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the back of your shoulder doing all the work. If you feel your trap shrugging, reset and focus on the rear delt. Build that balanced shoulder strength.
coach_guide.warning: Jerking the weight up shifts stress to your lower back.

name: dumbbell one arm reverse grip press
instructions: Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing towards your body.
Place your feet flat on the ground and keep your back straight.
Raise the dumbbell to shoulder height, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Press the dumbbell upwards until your arm is fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Flip your grip to underhand and press up to hit your chest from a new angle.
coaching_cues: Palm faces your face throughout the press.
Lower slow, elbow stays close to body.
Squeeze chest hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep contraction in your upper and inner chest. This angle targets fibers regular pressing often misses. Embrace the unique burn and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Do not let the dumbbell drift too far over your face.

name: dumbbell one arm reverse preacher curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with your chest against the pad and your arm extended over the edge of the bench, holding a dumbbell with an underhand grip.
Lower the dumbbell slowly until your arm is fully extended.
Curl the dumbbell back up towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Lock your arm over the preacher pad and curl with your palm facing down.
coaching_cues: Upper arm stays glued to the pad.
Curl up slow, squeeze at the top.
Lower with control, no bouncing down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel intense tension in your outer bicep and forearm. The reverse grip makes this harder so go lighter. Stay focused and feel every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping the weight fast can strain your elbow joint.

name: dumbbell one arm reverse spider curl
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing down and arm fully extended.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, curl the dumbbell towards your shoulder by flexing your elbow.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Hang your arm over the bench and curl with an underhand reverse grip.
coaching_cues: Chest stays pressed into the bench.
Curl up without swinging the elbow.
Squeeze hard then lower under control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your brachialis and forearm working overtime here. The spider position removes all momentum so pure muscle does the work. Keep grinding through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Avoid letting your shoulder roll forward off the bench.

name: dumbbell one arm seated bicep curl on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing up and your arm fully extended.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, curl the dumbbell towards your shoulder by contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Sit tall on the ball and curl one arm to challenge your core and biceps together.
coaching_cues: Stay balanced, core tight on the ball.
Curl up slow, palm rotating upward.
Lower with control, do not rush.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your bicep peak contracting while your core fights to keep you stable. This dual challenge builds functional strength. Stay balanced and keep squeezing.
coach_guide.warning: Losing balance can cause you to drop the weight unexpectedly.

name: dumbbell one arm seated hammer curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other).
Rest your elbow on the inside of your thigh, just above the knee.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your palm facing in and curl one arm at a time for focused hammer work.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, elbow pinned at your side.
Curl up with thumb leading the way.
Squeeze forearm and bicep at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel both your bicep and forearm working together on each rep. The neutral grip builds arm thickness from every angle. Stay steady and power through.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weight uses momentum instead of muscle.

name: dumbbell one arm seated neutral wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing up.
Rest your forearm on your thigh, allowing your wrist to hang off the edge.
Keeping your forearm stationary, curl your wrist upward as far as possible.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your wrist back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other hand.

coach_guide.intro: Rest your forearm on your thigh and curl your wrist in a neutral hammer position.
coaching_cues: Forearm stays flat on your thigh.
Curl wrist up, thumb leads the motion.
Lower slow, feel the forearm stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the meaty part of your outer forearm doing all the work. This builds grip strength you will use everywhere. Stay patient and feel the burn build.
coach_guide.warning: Jerky movements can irritate the small wrist tendons.

name: dumbbell one arm shoulder press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand at shoulder level, palm facing forward.
Press the dumbbell upward until your arm is fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Press one dumbbell overhead to build shoulder strength and core stability together.
coaching_cues: Core braced, ribs down, no leaning.
Press straight up beside your ear.
Lower with control to shoulder height.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulder and core working as a team to stabilize. One arm pressing exposes any imbalances so focus on form. Build that balanced strength one rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: dumbbell one arm shoulder press v. 2
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand at shoulder level, palm facing forward.
Engage your core and press the dumbbell straight up overhead, fully extending your arm.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Try this overhead press variation to target your shoulders from a slightly different angle.
coaching_cues: Keep your elbow slightly forward starting.
Drive the weight straight overhead smoothly.
Control the descent back to start.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your front and middle deltoid firing hard on every rep. The single arm challenge forces total focus. Stay tight and keep pressing strong.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning away from the weight stresses your lower back.

name: dumbbell one arm snatch
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand with an overhand grip.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Lower the dumbbell towards the ground, keeping it close to your body.
Explosively extend your hips, knees, and ankles, driving the dumbbell upwards in a straight line.
As the dumbbell reaches shoulder height, quickly rotate your hand and punch it overhead, fully extending your arm.
Catch the dumbbell overhead with a slight bend in your knees and hips.
Lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Explode from the floor and catch the dumbbell overhead in one powerful movement.
coaching_cues: Hinge hips back, weight between feet.
Drive hips forward, pull weight up.
Catch overhead with arm fully locked.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and hips generate all the power for this lift. Your whole body works together in this explosive movement. Channel that energy and stay explosive.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with your arm instead of hips risks shoulder injury.

name: dumbbell one arm standing curl
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing forward and arm fully extended.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the weight upward while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbell is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Stand tall and curl one dumbbell at a time for maximum focus and control.
coaching_cues: Stand straight, elbow pinned at side.
Curl up smoothly, squeeze at top.
Lower slow, full stretch at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your bicep working through a complete range of motion. Single arm work lets you really focus on the squeeze. Build that mind muscle connection and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Swaying your body steals tension from your bicep.

name: dumbbell one arm standing hammer curl
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your wrist neutral and curl with a strong squeeze at the top.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, core tight, elbow still.
Curl up with thumb leading the way.
Squeeze bicep hard, lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn through your bicep and into your forearm. If your elbow drifts forward, you are using momentum. Stay strict and feel that arm working.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weight steals the tension from your bicep.

name: dumbbell one arm triceps extension (on bench)
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand and place your other hand on the bench for support.
Raise the dumbbell overhead, keeping your upper arm close to your head and your elbow pointing forward.
Lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbow, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Extend your arm back up to the starting position, fully straightening your elbow.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Lock your upper arm in place and let your tricep do all the work.
coaching_cues: Upper arm vertical, elbow points straight up.
Lower the weight behind your head slowly.
Extend fully and squeeze your tricep hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong stretch at the bottom and a tight squeeze at the top. If your shoulder hurts, check your elbow position. Keep pushing through each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbow flare out puts unnecessary stress on your shoulder.

name: dumbbell one arm upright row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand with an overhand grip.
Let the dumbbell hang at arm's length in front of your thighs, with your palm facing your body.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, exhale and lift the dumbbell straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbow.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Lead with your elbow and pull the weight up along your body.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, slight forward lean at hips.
Pull elbow high, keep dumbbell close.
Control the weight down, no swinging.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulder working especially at the top of the movement. If you feel neck tension, you are shrugging too much. Let your delt do the lifting.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too high can pinch your shoulder joint.

name: dumbbell one arm wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with an underhand grip, resting your forearm on your thigh.
Allow your wrist to extend, letting the dumbbell roll down towards your fingers.
Slowly curl your wrist back up, bringing the dumbbell towards your forearm.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other hand.

coach_guide.intro: Rest your forearm on your thigh and curl using only your wrist.
coaching_cues: Forearm flat, wrist just past your knee.
Curl the weight up, squeeze your forearm.
Lower slowly, feel the stretch at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a burning pump building in your inner forearm. If your arm lifts off your leg, you are using too much weight. Stay controlled and chase that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum removes the tension from your forearm muscles.

name: dumbbell one arm zottman preacher curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher curl bench and hold a dumbbell in one hand with an underhand grip.
Rest your upper arm on the preacher bench pad, allowing your arm to fully extend.
Curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm stationary.
At the top of the curl, rotate your wrist so that your palm faces up.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position, rotating your wrist back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Curl up with palms up then rotate and lower with palms down.
coaching_cues: Arm braced on pad, start palm up.
Curl up and squeeze, then rotate wrist.
Lower slowly with palm facing down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your bicep working on the way up and your forearm burning on the way down. If the rotation feels awkward, slow it down. This double work builds serious arms.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the rotation reduces the effectiveness of this exercise.

name: dumbbell one leg fly on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Place one foot on the ground and extend the other leg straight out in front of you.
Lean forward slightly and bring your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Slowly lower the dumbbells down and out to the sides, feeling a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Balance on one leg while performing a chest fly for core and chest together.
coaching_cues: One foot on floor, other leg extended.
Lower dumbbells wide, slight elbow bend.
Squeeze chest to bring weights together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest stretching wide and your core fighting to keep you stable. If you wobble too much, lighten the weight first. Great work combining strength and balance.
coach_guide.warning: Going too heavy compromises your balance and chest form.

name: dumbbell over bench neutral wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up.
Rest your forearms on the bench, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let your wrists hang over the bench and curl both dumbbells with neutral grip.
coaching_cues: Forearms flat on bench, wrists hanging.
Curl weights up keeping thumbs on top.
Squeeze at top, lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your brachialis and outer bicep doing the work. If your forearms lift off the bench, reset your position. Keep grinding through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your forearms off the bench reduces muscle activation.

name: dumbbell over bench one arm neutral wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in one hand and your arm extended over the bench, palm facing up.
Keep your upper arm stationary and curl the dumbbell towards your shoulder, keeping your wrist in a neutral position.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Focus one arm at a time for better mind muscle connection with neutral grip.
coaching_cues: Forearm pressed flat, wrist over edge.
Curl up with thumb staying on top.
Lower slowly, feel the full stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel isolated tension through your bicep and into your forearm. If you rush the reps, you lose the benefit. Stay focused and feel every inch of movement.
coach_guide.warning: Jerky movements can strain your wrist joint.

name: dumbbell over bench one arm reverse wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing down.
Rest your forearm on the bench with your wrist hanging off the edge.
Slowly curl your wrist upwards, bringing the dumbbell towards your forearm.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Flip your grip over and curl to target the top of your forearm.
coaching_cues: Palm faces down, forearm flat on bench.
Curl knuckles up toward the ceiling.
Lower with control, keep forearm still.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the muscles on top of your forearm working hard. If your forearm keeps lifting, use a lighter weight. These small muscles build serious grip strength.
coach_guide.warning: Using too much weight strains the small muscles in your wrist.

name: dumbbell over bench one arm wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing down.
Rest your forearm on the bench with your wrist hanging off the edge.
Slowly curl your wrist upwards, bringing the dumbbell towards your forearm.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Isolate one forearm at a time with palm up for a focused wrist curl.
coaching_cues: Palm up, forearm flat on the bench.
Curl the weight up using only wrist.
Lower slowly and stretch at the bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep pump in your inner forearm with every rep. If your whole arm moves, you are cheating the curl. Stay strict and enjoy the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your forearm breaks the isolation and reduces results.

name: dumbbell over bench revers wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing down.
Rest your forearms on the bench, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Slowly curl your wrists upward, bringing the dumbbells towards your body.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let your wrists hang off the bench and curl the backs of your hands upward.
coaching_cues: Forearms flat, wrists hanging over edge.
Curl knuckles up, squeeze at the top.
Lower slowly, feel the stretch below.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn on the top of your forearms near your wrists. This smaller muscle group fatigues fast and that is normal. Keep the reps controlled and build that grip strength.
coach_guide.warning: Jerky movements can strain the small tendons in your wrist.

name: dumbbell over bench wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your forearms resting on the bench and your palms facing up, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Allow your wrists to hang over the edge of the bench.
Slowly curl your wrists upward, squeezing your forearms at the top of the movement.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower your wrists back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rest your forearms on the bench and curl your palms toward you.
coaching_cues: Palms face up, wrists just past edge.
Curl the weight up using only wrists.
Lower with control, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your inner forearms working hard with each curl. A deep pump and mild burning means you are hitting the right muscles. Stick with it and watch your grip get stronger.
coach_guide.warning: Do not let the dumbbells roll to your fingertips or you risk losing control.

name: dumbbell palm rotational bent over row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Let your arms hang straight down, palms facing your body.
Engage your core and pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
As you pull the dumbbells up, rotate your palms so they face away from your body.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position, rotating your palms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Row the dumbbells up while rotating your palms from down to up at the top.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward, back flat, core tight.
Pull elbows high, rotate palms upward.
Squeeze shoulder blades, then lower slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles and rear shoulders working together. The rotation adds extra engagement so expect a strong contraction at the top. Great work building a powerful back.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating too early shifts tension away from your back muscles.

name: dumbbell palms in incline bench press
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with your back against the backrest and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing inwards.
Extend your arms straight up above your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Lower the dumbbells slowly towards your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then press the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press with palms facing each other to shift focus onto your triceps.
coaching_cues: Set the incline, palms face each other.
Lower dumbbells beside your chest slowly.
Press up, squeeze triceps at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps doing most of the pushing work. Your chest assists but the burn should be in the back of your arms. Keep that focus and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting elbows flare wide removes the triceps emphasis.

name: dumbbell peacher hammer curl
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and rotate the palms of your hands until they are facing forward.
This will be your starting position.
Now, keeping the upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Then, inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the preacher pad with a neutral grip to isolate your biceps.
coaching_cues: Arm flat on pad, palm faces inward.
Curl up without lifting your elbow.
Lower slowly, stop just before straight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in your biceps and some forearm activation. The pad prevents cheating so every rep counts. Embrace the burn and keep curling.
coach_guide.warning: Fully straightening your arm at the bottom can stress your elbow joint.

name: dumbbell plyo squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
As you reach the bottom of the squat, explode upward, jumping off the ground.
While in the air, quickly switch the position of your feet, landing with your opposite foot forward.
Immediately lower your body back into a squat position and repeat the jump, switching your feet again.
Continue alternating the position of your feet with each jump for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drop into a squat then explode up into a small jump while holding dumbbells.
coaching_cues: Hold dumbbells at sides, feet shoulder width.
Squat down, then jump up explosively.
Land soft, absorb through your legs.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads firing hard on every jump. Your heart rate will climb and that is the point. Stay light on your landings and keep that power coming.
coach_guide.warning: Landing with stiff legs puts excessive stress on your knees.

name: dumbbell preacher curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher curl bench with your upper arms resting on the pad and your chest against it.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing up and your arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rest your arm on the preacher pad and curl the dumbbell with full control.
coaching_cues: Upper arm pressed flat on the pad.
Curl up, squeeze your bicep hard.
Lower slowly, keep tension the whole way.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense bicep contraction with nowhere to hide. The pad locks you in so every rep is pure isolation. Push through and enjoy the pump.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weight up takes work away from your biceps.

name: dumbbell preacher curl over exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with an underhand grip, resting your elbow on the exercise ball.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Drape your arm over an exercise ball for an unstable preacher curl challenge.
coaching_cues: Kneel behind ball, arm draped over top.
Curl up smoothly, squeeze at peak.
Lower with control, stay balanced throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working hard while your core stabilizes you. The ball adds instability so expect extra focus and engagement. Great job challenging yourself.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement can cause you to lose balance.

name: dumbbell press on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and dumbbells in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Slowly walk your feet forward, rolling the exercise ball until your lower back is supported on the ball and your knees are at a 90-degree angle.
Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press dumbbells while lying on an exercise ball to engage your core and chest.
coaching_cues: Upper back on ball, hips lifted high.
Press dumbbells up, squeeze your chest.
Lower slowly, keep your core tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest muscles working along with your core fighting to keep you stable. It is a full body challenge disguised as a press. Stay steady and keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips drop puts strain on your lower back.

name: dumbbell pronate-grip triceps extension
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands, palms facing down, and extend your arms straight up overhead.
Keeping your upper arms close to your head and elbows pointing forward, slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Extend the dumbbells overhead with palms facing down to target your triceps.
coaching_cues: Palms face down, elbows point forward.
Lower weights behind your head slowly.
Extend up, lock out and squeeze triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and strong contraction in your triceps. The pronated grip shifts the emphasis slightly so stay focused on control. Finish every rep with purpose.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbows out wide stresses your shoulder joints.

name: dumbbell prone incline curl
instructions: Adjust the bench to a 45-degree incline.
Lie face down on the bench with your chest and stomach resting against it.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing down and your arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let your arms hang straight down and curl with zero body movement.
coaching_cues: Chest pressed firmly into the incline bench.
Curl slowly, squeeze hard at the top.
Lower with control, full arm extension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your biceps with no momentum helping. If your shoulders creep forward, reset your chest position. Stay strict and feel every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your chest off the bench turns this into a cheat curl.

name: dumbbell prone incline hammer curl
instructions: Adjust the bench to a 45-degree incline.
Lie face down on the bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Allow your arms to hang straight down towards the floor, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
