Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your thumbs up throughout the entire movement for hammer grip.
coaching_cues: Chest stays glued to the bench.
Thumbs point up, curl straight up.
Squeeze forearms and biceps at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps and forearms working together on each curl. If your wrists rotate, you are losing the hammer position. Keep it neutral and power through.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating your wrists removes the forearm emphasis completely.

name: dumbbell pullover
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight above your chest.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head until you feel a stretch in your chest and shoulders.
Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Reach the dumbbell back behind your head while keeping your core tight.
coaching_cues: Slight bend in elbows throughout movement.
Lower the weight slowly behind head.
Pull back using your chest muscles.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a big stretch in your chest and lats at the bottom. If your lower back arches off the bench, reduce the range. Embrace the stretch and control the weight.
coach_guide.warning: Excessive back arch puts dangerous pressure on your spine.

name: dumbbell pullover hip extension on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and the dumbbell resting on your thighs.
Slowly walk your feet forward, rolling the exercise ball down your back until your head, neck, and upper back are supported on the ball.
Hold the dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your arms straight and maintaining control.
Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
While keeping your arms extended, lift your hips off the ground, squeezing your glutes and engaging your core.
Lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Combine the pullover with a hip drop for a full body challenge.
coaching_cues: Hips drop as dumbbell goes back.
Drive hips up as you pull over.
Keep the ball stable under shoulders.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest, glutes, and core all firing together. If the ball wobbles, slow down and find your balance. Great combination movement, stay focused.
coach_guide.warning: Losing ball stability can cause you to fall or strain muscles.

name: dumbbell pullover on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball and hold a dumbbell with both hands above your chest, arms extended.
Slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head while keeping your arms straight.
Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The unstable ball forces your core to work overtime during pullovers.
coaching_cues: Upper back supported on the ball.
Hips stay lifted throughout the movement.
Control the dumbbell path behind head.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest stretching and your core stabilizing constantly. If your hips sag, squeeze your glutes harder. Balance and strength in one move.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your hips puts excessive strain on your lower back.

name: dumbbell push press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level.
Bend your knees slightly and dip your body down, then explosively extend your legs and press the dumbbells overhead.
Lock out your arms at the top of the movement, then lower the dumbbells back to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use your legs to help drive the dumbbells overhead explosively.
coaching_cues: Dip knees slightly then drive up.
Push dumbbells overhead as legs extend.
Lock out arms at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your legs, core, and shoulders working as one unit. If the weight stalls, use more leg drive. This is power training so be explosive.
coach_guide.warning: Pressing without the leg drive overloads your shoulders dangerously.

name: dumbbell raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lift the dumbbells out to your sides with controlled shoulder power.
coaching_cues: Slight bend in elbows stays locked.
Raise to shoulder height, no higher.
Lower slowly, resist the weight down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn right on top of your shoulders. If you swing the weights, go lighter immediately. Strict form builds real shoulder strength.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging momentum takes tension off delts and risks injury.

name: dumbbell rear delt raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Raise your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows, until they are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bend forward and raise the weights out to hit rear delts directly.
coaching_cues: Hinge at hips, chest near parallel.
Raise dumbbells out to the sides.
Squeeze shoulder blades at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the back of your shoulders burning intensely. If your traps take over, lower the weight slightly. Rear delts are small so stay patient.
coach_guide.warning: Standing too upright shifts all the work to your traps.

name: dumbbell rear delt row_shoulder
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Extend your arms straight down towards the floor, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Raise the dumbbells out to the sides, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Row with elbows high and wide to target your rear shoulders.
coaching_cues: Elbows flare out at ninety degrees.
Pull toward your upper chest area.
Squeeze rear delts hard at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your rear delts and upper back contracting together. If you feel it in your lats, raise your elbow angle. High elbows are the key here.
coach_guide.warning: Elbows too low turns this into a lat exercise instead.

name: dumbbell rear fly
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Extend your arms straight down towards the ground, palms facing each other.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lift your arms out to the sides and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Fly the weights back with straight arms to isolate rear delts.
coaching_cues: Bend over with flat back position.
Arms sweep back in wide arc.
Pinch shoulder blades together at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the squeeze right in your rear shoulders at the top. If your back rounds, reset your hip hinge position. Light weight with perfect form wins here.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back puts your spine at serious risk.

name: dumbbell rear lateral raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Raise your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows, until they are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge forward and pull your elbows back — feel it in your rear delts, not your traps.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward, chest out, core tight.
Pull elbows back, not up.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this burning right in the back of your shoulders and between your shoulder blades. If you feel it in your lower back or upper traps, adjust your form. Keep that rear delt fire burning through every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Standing too upright or pulling too high will hit your traps instead of rear delts.

name: dumbbell rear lateral raise (support head)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Raise your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows, until they are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rest your forehead on an incline bench and let your arms hang to isolate rear delts.
coaching_cues: Forehead on bench, arms hanging straight down.
Raise dumbbells out wide, lead with pinkies.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn across the back of your shoulders. If your traps take over, lower the weight and focus on the squeeze. Keep building those rear delts.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting too heavy will shift the work to your traps and neck.

name: dumbbell rear lunge
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Take a step backward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position.
Bend your left knee and lower your body until your left thigh is parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then push through your left heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, stepping back with your left foot.

coach_guide.intro: Step back into a deep lunge while holding dumbbells at your sides to fire up your glutes.
coaching_cues: Step back, drop knee toward the floor.
Front knee stays over your ankle always.
Drive through front heel to stand tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads working hard on each leg. If your knee caves inward, push it out over your toes. Stay strong and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your front knee shoot forward stresses the joint badly.

name: dumbbell revers grip biceps curl
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing down and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Flip your grip palms down to target the forearms and outer biceps differently.
coaching_cues: Palms face down, grip the dumbbells tight.
Curl up keeping wrists straight and strong.
Lower with control, feel the forearm stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a burn on top of your forearms and outer biceps. If your wrists bend or ache, lighten the load. Keep grinding for stronger arms.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your wrists under load can strain the joint quickly.

name: dumbbell reverse bench press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing towards your feet.
Extend your arms straight up towards the ceiling, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Slowly lower the dumbbells towards your chest, allowing your elbows to flare out to the sides.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use an underhand grip on the dumbbells to shift focus to your inner chest.
coaching_cues: Palms face your head, dumbbells over chest.
Lower slowly, keeping elbows close to body.
Press up and squeeze your chest together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your inner chest and front shoulders working together. If your wrists feel awkward, rotate them slightly for comfort. Push through and build that chest.
coach_guide.warning: Going too heavy with this grip can strain your wrists.

name: dumbbell reverse fly
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Extend your arms straight down in front of you, palms facing each other.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bend forward and fly the dumbbells out wide to carve your rear delts.
coaching_cues: Hinge at hips, flat back, slight bend.
Raise arms out wide like spreading wings.
Pause at top, squeeze those rear delts hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in the back of your shoulders. If you feel it in your lower back, bend your knees more. Keep flying and build those shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back puts unnecessary stress on your spine.

name: dumbbell reverse grip incline bench one arm row
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Place a dumbbell on the floor next to the bench.
Stand facing the bench with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend at the waist and place your left knee and left hand on the bench for support.
Pick up the dumbbell with your right hand using a reverse grip (palm facing down).
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the dumbbell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Squeeze your back muscles at the top of the movement.
Lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position in a controlled manner.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch sides and repeat the exercise with your left arm.

coach_guide.intro: Lie chest down on an incline bench and row with palms up to hit upper back.
coaching_cues: Chest on bench, palm facing up throughout.
Pull elbow back and squeeze shoulder blade.
Lower slowly, get a full arm stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats and mid back squeezing hard at the top. If your bicep takes over, focus on pulling with your elbow. Build that strong back one arm at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Jerking the weight up will involve momentum instead of muscle.

name: dumbbell reverse grip incline bench two arm row
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with your chest against the backrest and your feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an underhand grip.
Lean forward and let your arms hang straight down, fully extended.
Pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Row both dumbbells together with palms up while lying on an incline bench.
coaching_cues: Chest on bench, both palms facing forward.
Pull both elbows back together, squeeze hard.
Lower under control, stretch at the bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire upper back working as a unit. If one side is weaker, focus on even pulling. Keep rowing and strengthen that back.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your chest off the bench reduces back isolation.

name: dumbbell reverse grip row (female)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing your body.
Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Let your arms hang straight down, fully extended, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Pull the dumbbells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stand hinged forward and row with palms facing away to target your upper back.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward, palms face behind you.
Pull elbows straight back past your ribs.
Squeeze shoulder blades, then lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back and lats doing all the work. If your lower back aches, soften your knees and brace your core. You are getting stronger every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Standing too upright turns this into a shrug movement.

name: dumbbell reverse preacher curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with your upper arms resting on the pad and your chest against the support.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbells as you contract your biceps.
Continue to curl the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the preacher bench with palms down to target your brachialis and forearms.
coaching_cues: Arms flat on pad, palms facing down.
Curl up slow, keep your wrists straight.
Lower all the way, feel the deep stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn on top of your forearms and outer arms. If your wrists hurt, try a lighter weight first. Stay focused and build those arms.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping the weight fast can hyperextend your elbows badly.

name: dumbbell reverse spider curl
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hang over a bench with palms down and curl to isolate your forearms and brachialis.
coaching_cues: Chest on bench, arms hanging straight down.
Curl with palms down, squeeze at top.
Lower slowly, feel the stretch in forearms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn on your outer forearms and biceps. If you cannot control the weight down, go lighter. Finish strong and feel the pump.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum removes the isolation this exercise provides.

name: dumbbell reverse wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Slowly curl your wrists upward, bringing the dumbbells towards your body.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Control the movement to build forearm strength and grip endurance.
coaching_cues: Rest forearms on thighs, palms down.
Lower knuckles slowly toward the floor.
Curl wrists up, squeeze at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn across the top of your forearms near your elbows. If your grip fails first, lighten the weight slightly. Keep squeezing through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Jerky movements can strain the small wrist tendons.

name: dumbbell romanian deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbells towards the ground, allowing your knees to bend slightly.
Lower the dumbbells until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, then push through your heels and engage your glutes to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge back with your hips and feel that deep hamstring stretch.
coaching_cues: Hips back, weights stay close.
Feel the stretch, then drive up.
Squeeze glutes tight at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this stretching and burning down the entire back of your legs. If your back is taking over, really focus on sitting those hips back. Channel that hamstring fire into every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Don't round your back trying to reach lower.

name: dumbbell rotation reverse fly
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing inwards.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Raise your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows, until they are parallel to the floor.
Rotate your arms so that your palms are facing downwards.
Slowly lower your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your thumbs up as you lift to fully engage the rear delts.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward with a flat back.
Lift arms wide, rotate thumbs to ceiling.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your rear delts and upper back working together on every rep. If you feel it in your neck, relax your traps. Stay controlled and keep flying.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum steals the tension from your rear delts.

name: dumbbell scott press
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, with your elbows bent and palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press in an arc while rotating to hit all three delt heads.
coaching_cues: Start with palms facing you at shoulders.
Press up and out in a wide arc.
Rotate palms forward as arms extend.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders burning through the entire range of motion. If your lower back arches, brace your core tighter. Push through and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back puts dangerous pressure on your spine.

name: dumbbell seated alternate front raise
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body and arms extended down by your sides.
Keeping your arms straight, raise one dumbbell in front of you until it is parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat with the other arm.
Alternate between arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Alternate arms to build balanced front delt strength and control.
coaching_cues: Sit tall with dumbbells at your sides.
Raise one arm to shoulder height, palm down.
Lower with control, then switch arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the front of your shoulder working as you lift each arm. If your body sways, you are going too heavy. Stay steady and keep alternating.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weights removes tension from your delts.

name: dumbbell seated alternate hammer curl on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body and your arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl one dumbbell up towards your shoulder while keeping your palm facing your body.
Continue to raise the dumbbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbell is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat the movement with the opposite arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The unstable ball forces your core to stabilize while you curl.
coaching_cues: Sit tall on the ball, feet planted.
Curl one dumbbell with a neutral grip.
Lower slowly, then switch arms immediately.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps and forearms working while your core stays engaged. If you wobble too much, widen your foot stance. Stay balanced and keep curling.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the reps can cause you to lose balance.

name: dumbbell seated alternate press
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, with your elbows bent and palms facing forward.
Press one dumbbell up overhead, fully extending your arm.
Lower the dumbbell back down to shoulder height.
Repeat with the other arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pressing one arm at a time builds balanced shoulder strength.
coaching_cues: Hold dumbbells at shoulder height, palms forward.
Press one arm straight up overhead.
Lower with control, then press the other.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel each shoulder working independently through the full press. If one side is weaker, focus on form not speed. Build that balance one rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning to one side puts uneven stress on your spine.

name: dumbbell seated alternate shoulder
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing inwards, and raise them to shoulder height.
Press one dumbbell up overhead while keeping the other dumbbell at shoulder height.
Lower the raised dumbbell back to shoulder height while simultaneously pressing the other dumbbell up overhead.
Continue alternating between arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Alternate presses challenge your core stability and shoulder endurance.
coaching_cues: Sit upright with dumbbells at shoulders.
Press one arm up while stabilizing the other.
Switch arms in a smooth steady rhythm.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders and core working together throughout the set. If your torso twists, slow down the tempo. Stay strong and finish every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting your torso reduces shoulder engagement and risks injury.

name: dumbbell seated bench extension
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up above your head.
Slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your elbows close to your ears.
Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Extend behind your head to fully stretch and work the triceps.
coaching_cues: Hold one dumbbell overhead with both hands.
Lower it slowly behind your head, elbows in.
Extend arms fully, squeeze triceps hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your triceps at the bottom and a strong squeeze at the top. If your elbows flare out, use a lighter weight. Keep pressing for stronger arms.
coach_guide.warning: Letting elbows flare wide puts stress on your shoulder joints.

name: dumbbell seated bent arm lateral raise
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body and your arms bent at a 90-degree angle.
Keeping your elbows bent, raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bent arms shift focus to the side delts for a targeted burn.
coaching_cues: Sit bent forward, arms hanging with elbows bent.
Raise elbows out to shoulder height.
Squeeze delts at top, lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the sides of your shoulders doing all the lifting. If your traps take over, lower the weight and focus on form. Keep lifting and feel that delt burn.
coach_guide.warning: Shrugging up engages traps instead of targeting your delts.

name: dumbbell seated bent over alternate kickback
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing inwards.
Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and parallel to the ground.
Extend one arm straight back, keeping it close to your body, until your arm is fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat with the other arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Kickbacks isolate each tricep for focused strength and definition.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward, upper arms pinned to sides.
Extend one arm back, squeeze the tricep.
Lower slowly, then switch to other arm.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps working hard at full extension on every rep. If your elbow drops, keep it locked in place. Squeeze hard and build those arms.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your elbow turns this into a useless swinging motion.

name: dumbbell seated bent over triceps extension
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your head up.
Extend your arms straight back, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge forward and extend the dumbbells back to isolate your triceps completely.
coaching_cues: Chest on thighs, elbows pinned high.
Extend arms straight back, squeeze hard.
Lower with control, keep elbows still.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong burn in the back of your upper arms near the top of each rep. If your shoulders are working too hard, pin those elbows tighter to your sides. Keep squeezing out those reps.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weights uses momentum and takes tension off your triceps.

name: dumbbell seated bicep curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up.
Keep your back straight and your elbows close to your torso.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit tall and curl the dumbbells with strict form to build your biceps.
coaching_cues: Back flat against the bench, core tight.
Curl up slowly, squeeze at the top.
Lower with control, full arm extension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps burning from elbow to shoulder on every rep. If your back is arching, you are going too heavy. Stay strict and feel that pump grow.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning back turns this into a momentum exercise and strains your lower back.

name: dumbbell seated biceps curl (on stability ball)
instructions: Sit on a stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing forward and your arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbells while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The unstable ball forces your core to work while you curl for bigger biceps.
coaching_cues: Sit tall on the ball, feet wide.
Curl up with control, no swaying.
Squeeze biceps hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working while your core stays engaged for balance. If you are wobbling too much, widen your feet for stability. Stay focused and keep curling strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the reps on an unstable surface can cause you to lose balance.

name: dumbbell seated biceps curl to shoulder press
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your elbows close to your sides, curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders.
Once your forearms are vertical, rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing forward.
Press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position by reversing the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl the dumbbells up then press overhead for a powerful combo movement.
coaching_cues: Curl to shoulders, palms facing you.
Rotate and press dumbbells straight up.
Lower to shoulders, then down slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps fire on the curl and shoulders take over on the press. If you are using leg drive, stay seated and keep it strict. Great work combining two moves into one.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back during the press puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: dumbbell seated calf raise
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground and a dumbbell resting on your thighs.
Place the balls of your feet on a raised surface such as a step or block, with your heels hanging off the edge.
Hold onto the dumbbell for stability.
Raise your heels as high as possible, lifting your body weight onto the balls of your feet.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Place dumbbells on your knees and raise your heels to build stronger calves.
coaching_cues: Balls of feet on edge, heels low.
Push through toes, rise up high.
Squeeze calves at top, lower slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense squeeze in your calf muscles at the top of each rep. If you are bouncing, slow down and control the movement. Keep pushing for that deep calf burn.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom removes tension and can strain your Achilles tendon.

name: dumbbell seated curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Keep your back straight and your elbows close to your torso.
Exhale and curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: A classic seated curl that keeps strict form for maximum bicep growth.
coaching_cues: Elbows pinned at your sides always.
Curl up smoothly, rotate palms up.
Lower with control, stretch at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps doing all the work without any body movement. If your elbows are drifting forward, reset and keep them locked in place. You are building great arms.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the dumbbells shifts the load away from your biceps.

name: dumbbell seated front raise
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Keep your back straight and core engaged.
Raise the dumbbells in front of you, with your palms facing down, until they are at shoulder level.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise the dumbbells in front of you to target your front shoulder muscles.
coaching_cues: Arms straight, slight bend in elbows.
Raise to shoulder height, pause briefly.
Lower with control, core stays tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the front of your shoulders burning as you lift. If your traps are taking over, lower the weight and focus on your delts. Keep those shoulders working strong.
coach_guide.warning: Raising the weight too high shifts stress to your upper traps.

name: dumbbell seated hammer curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso and arms extended straight down.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your palms facing in to hit your biceps and forearms together.
coaching_cues: Palms face each other throughout lift.
Curl up keeping wrists neutral always.
Squeeze at top, lower under control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps and the top of your forearms working together. If your wrists are rotating, lock them in that neutral grip. Great job building arm strength.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating your wrists changes the exercise and reduces forearm engagement.

name: dumbbell seated inner biceps curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up.
Rest your upper arms on your thighs, allowing the dumbbells to hang down.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders by contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Turn your palms out wide to target the inner head of your biceps.
coaching_cues: Rotate palms outward, elbows in tight.
Curl wide dumbbells toward shoulders slowly.
Squeeze inner biceps hard at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a unique squeeze on the inside of your biceps near your chest. If you feel elbow strain, reduce the rotation slightly. Keep chasing that inner bicep pump.
coach_guide.warning: Excessive outward rotation can put strain on your elbow joints.

name: dumbbell seated kickback
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Bend your knees slightly and lean forward from your hips, keeping your back straight.
Bring your upper arms close to your sides and keep your elbows bent at a 90-degree angle.
Extend your arms straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge forward and kick the dumbbells back to isolate your triceps fully.
coaching_cues: Torso hinged, back flat, elbows high.
Extend arms straight back, lock out.
Squeeze triceps, lower slowly each rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps fire hard at the top when your arm is fully straight. If your elbow is dropping, keep it pinned high by your ribs. Finish strong with great tricep work.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your elbows turns this into a swinging motion with no tricep benefit.

name: dumbbell seated lateral raise
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Keep your back straight and core engaged.
Raise the dumbbells to your sides with a slight bend in your elbows, until your arms are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lift the dumbbells out to the sides with control to build your shoulder caps.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, slight bend in your elbows.
Raise arms to shoulder height, palms down.
Lower slowly, do not let them drop.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a burning sensation right on the sides of your shoulders. If your traps are taking over, lower the weight and focus on leading with your elbows. Keep pushing through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weights uses momentum and risks shoulder impingement.

name: dumbbell seated lateral raise v. 2
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Keep your back straight and core engaged.
Raise the dumbbells to your sides with a slight bend in your elbows, until your arms are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This variation targets your side delts with a slightly different arm angle for complete development.
coaching_cues: Keep your torso upright and stable.
Lead the movement with your elbows high.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the outer shoulder muscles working hard at the top of each rep. If you feel neck tension, relax your traps and lighten the load. You are building boulder shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting too heavy causes you to shrug and strain your neck.

name: dumbbell seated neutral wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing the dumbbells to hang down.
Keeping your wrists in a neutral position, curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This wrist curl variation hits your forearms with a neutral hammer grip position.
coaching_cues: Rest forearms flat on your thighs firmly.
Curl the dumbbells up using only wrists.
Lower with control, feel the stretch below.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn along the thumb side of your forearms. If your grip fails before the burn starts, the weight may be too heavy. Stick with it and watch your grip strength grow.
coach_guide.warning: Jerky movements can strain the small wrist tendons and ligaments.

name: dumbbell seated one arm bicep curl on exercise ball with leg raised
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing up and your arm fully extended.
Place your other hand on your hip for stability.
Slowly curl the dumbbell towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your bicep.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch arms and repeat the exercise.

coach_guide.intro: This challenging curl adds core stability work while isolating one bicep at a time.
coaching_cues: Balance on the ball, one leg raised.
Curl the dumbbell slow and controlled up.
Squeeze your bicep hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your bicep working while your core fights to keep you balanced. If you wobble too much, lower your leg slightly until you build stability. Great job challenging yourself.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing this move can cause you to fall off the ball.

name: dumbbell seated one arm kickback
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in one hand.
Bend your torso forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and parallel to the ground.
Extend your arm straight back, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Kickbacks isolate your triceps by extending your arm straight back behind you.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward, upper arm parallel to floor.
Extend your arm back, squeeze the tricep.
Lower slowly, keep your elbow locked still.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in the back of your upper arm at full extension. If your elbow drops, you lose the tension completely. Keep that squeeze and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weight removes all tension from the triceps muscle.

name: dumbbell seated one arm rotate
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and hold a dumbbell in one hand, resting it on your thigh.
Raise the dumbbell up to shoulder height, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Rotate your forearm outward, away from your body, while keeping your upper arm stationary.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly rotate your forearm back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: This rotation move strengthens the small forearm muscles that control wrist turning.
coaching_cues: Rest your forearm flat, grip dumbbell vertically.
Rotate the weight inward then outward slowly.
Keep your elbow and forearm completely still.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the muscles wrapping around your forearm doing all the work. If your whole arm moves, anchor your elbow down firmly. These small muscles will thank you later.
coach_guide.warning: Using too much weight strains the delicate wrist rotator muscles.

name: dumbbell seated one leg calf raise
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground and a dumbbell resting on your right thigh.
Extend your left leg straight out in front of you, keeping your foot flexed.
Place the ball of your right foot on an elevated surface, such as a step or weight plate.
Using your calf muscles, raise your right heel as high as possible.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

coach_guide.intro: Single leg calf raises with a dumbbell build balanced lower leg strength.
coaching_cues: Place the dumbbell on your working thigh.
Press through the ball of your foot.
Lower your heel slowly for a full stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your calf muscle squeeze hard at the top and stretch at the bottom. If one calf is weaker, give it extra attention to build balance. Strong calves are coming your way.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom can strain your Achilles tendon badly.

name: dumbbell seated one leg calf raise - hammer grip
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground and a dumbbell resting on your thighs.
Place one foot on a raised surface, such as a step or block, with your heel hanging off the edge.
Hold the dumbbell with a hammer grip, meaning your palms are facing each other and your fingers are wrapped around the handle.
Keeping your core engaged and your back straight, slowly raise your heel as high as possible by pushing through the ball of your foot.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg.

coach_guide.intro: The hammer grip variation shifts the load slightly for complete calf development.
coaching_cues: Hold dumbbell with neutral hammer grip tight.
Drive up high onto your toes now.
Control the descent, feel the calf stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire calf muscle from knee to heel working hard. If you lose balance, hold onto something stable nearby. Every rep builds more powerful calves.
coach_guide.warning: Locking your knee too hard puts pressure on the joint.

name: dumbbell seated one leg calf raise - palm up
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your back straight and your feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand and place it on top of your thigh, palm facing up.
Lift one leg off the ground and extend it in front of you, keeping your knee slightly bent.
Raise your heel as high as possible by pushing through the ball of your foot.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Palm up grip challenges your grip while still targeting the calf muscle.
coaching_cues: Hold dumbbell palm up on your thigh.
Rise up onto the ball of foot.
Lower with control through full range motion.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel both your calf contracting and your forearm working to hold the weight. If the grip fails first, use a lighter dumbbell for now. You are building strength everywhere.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping the weight can injure your foot or toes.

name: dumbbell seated palms up wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up.
Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Slowly curl your wrists upward, squeezing your forearms at the top of the movement.
Pause for a moment, then lower your wrists back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Palms up wrist curls build the forearm flexors for a crushing grip.
coaching_cues: Rest forearms on thighs, palms facing up.
Curl the dumbbells up toward your body.
Lower slowly and let your wrists extend.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep pump in the meaty inner part of your forearms. If your wrists ache, use less weight and focus on form. Strong forearms make everything else easier.
coach_guide.warning: Going too heavy too fast can aggravate the wrist tendons.

name: dumbbell seated preacher curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher curl bench with your feet flat on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with an underhand grip, resting your upper arm against the preacher pad.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: The preacher bench locks your arm so your bicep does all the work.
coaching_cues: Armpit snug against the pad top.
Lower slowly, feel the full stretch.
Curl up, squeeze hard at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn right in the belly of your bicep. If your shoulder rolls forward, reset your position on the pad. Let that isolation do its job.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping the weight too fast can strain your elbow tendons.

name: dumbbell seated revers grip concentration curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing up.
Rest your elbow on the inside of your thigh, just above the knee.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbell towards your shoulder.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: The reverse grip shifts the focus to your forearms and outer bicep.
coaching_cues: Palm faces down, grip firm throughout.
Keep your elbow pinned to inner thigh.
Curl up controlled, squeeze at peak.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your forearms working hard alongside your biceps. If your wrist bends backward, lighten the weight. Build that grip strength with each rep.
coach_guide.warning: A bent wrist puts dangerous stress on your forearm tendons.

name: dumbbell seated reverse grip one arm overhead tricep extension
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell with an underhand grip and extend your arm straight up overhead.
Lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbow, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arm back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: The reverse grip overhead extension targets your tricep from a unique angle.
coaching_cues: Palm faces forward, elbow points up.
Lower the weight behind your head slowly.
Extend fully and squeeze your tricep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and burn in the back of your arm. If your elbow flares out, reset your position. Keep that arm steady and strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbow drift forward removes tension from the tricep.

name: dumbbell seated shoulder press
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, resting on your thighs.
Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press straight up from your shoulders — keep your core tight and back supported.
coaching_cues: Drive straight up from shoulder level.
Keep your core engaged throughout.
Control the weight on the way down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this working your entire shoulder complex, especially the front and middle delts. If your lower back is arching off the bench, lighter weight next time. Keep that shoulder burn driving through every press.
coach_guide.warning: Pressing behind your head or arching your back excessively puts your shoulders at risk.

name: dumbbell seated shoulder press (parallel grip)
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing inward.
Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, elbows bent and palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The parallel grip press is easier on your shoulders while building strong delts.
coaching_cues: Palms face each other, elbows forward.
Press straight up, keep core tight.
Lower with control to ear level.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders burning with each press overhead. If your lower back arches, engage your core harder. Push through and build those boulder shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back shifts the load onto your spine.

name: dumbbell seated triceps extension
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up overhead.
Bend your elbows and lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your upper arms close to your ears.
Pause for a moment, then straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Seated tricep extensions build serious arm strength with full range of motion.
coaching_cues: Both hands grip the weight overhead.
Lower behind your head, elbows still.
Extend up fully, squeeze the triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch at the bottom and strong contraction at top. If your elbows flare wide, bring them back in. Keep pushing for those defined arms.
coach_guide.warning: Flared elbows put unnecessary stress on your shoulder joints.

name: dumbbell shrug
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Keep your arms straight and let the dumbbells hang by your sides.
Raise your shoulders as high as possible, as if you are trying to touch your ears with your shoulders.
Hold the contraction for a second, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Shrugs build your trap muscles for that powerful upper back look.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, dumbbells at your sides.
Shrug straight up toward your ears.
Hold the squeeze, then lower slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your traps bunching up hard at the top of each rep. If you are rolling your shoulders, stop and just lift straight up. Keep stacking those reps.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling your shoulders adds no benefit and risks joint injury.

name: dumbbell side bend
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand, letting it hang down by your side.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, slowly bend sideways at the waist towards the opposite side of the dumbbell, lowering the weight as far as you comfortably can.
Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Side bends carve out your obliques with controlled lateral movement.
coaching_cues: Hold one dumbbell, stand straight up.
Bend sideways slowly, sliding weight down.
Contract your oblique to pull back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your side abs working hard to bring you upright. If your hips shift, plant your feet and stay stable. Stay focused on that oblique squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting or leaning forward takes tension off your obliques.

name: dumbbell side lying one hand raise
instructions: Lie on your side with your legs extended and your head supported by your arm.
Hold a dumbbell in your top hand with your palm facing down.
Keeping your arm straight, raise the dumbbell up to shoulder height.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: This side lying raise isolates your rear delt with zero momentum.
coaching_cues: Lie on your side, arm in front.
Raise the dumbbell straight toward ceiling.
Lower slowly, keep tension throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the back of your shoulder doing all the lifting. If you are rocking your body, stabilize your core. Let gravity make your delt work harder.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum cheats your rear delt out of the work.

name: dumbbell side plank with rear fly
instructions: Start by lying on your side with your legs extended and stacked on top of each other.
Place your forearm on the ground directly below your shoulder, keeping your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle.
Hold a dumbbell in your top hand, with your arm extended straight down towards the ground.
Engage your core and lift your hips off the ground, creating a straight line from your head to your heels.
While maintaining the side plank position, lift the dumbbell up towards the ceiling, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: This combo move builds core stability while sculpting your upper back.
coaching_cues: Hold side plank, hips stacked high.
Row the weight up toward ceiling.
Lower controlled, keep body still.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques bracing hard while your upper back squeezes. If your hips drop, reset your plank position first. You are building total body strength here.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag puts dangerous stress on your lower back.

name: dumbbell single arm overhead carry
instructions: Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand.
Raise the dumbbell overhead, fully extending your arm.
Engage your core and keep your back straight as you walk forward, maintaining the dumbbell overhead.
Continue walking for the desired distance or time.
Switch hands and repeat the exercise.

coach_guide.intro: This carry builds shoulder stability and core strength with every step.
coaching_cues: Press one dumbbell overhead, lock out.
Walk tall, keep your ribs down.
Breathe steady, do not lean sideways.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulder working to stay stable while your core fights to stay straight. If you are leaning away from the weight, go lighter. Own that overhead position proudly.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning to one side puts uneven stress on your spine.

name: dumbbell single leg calf raise
instructions: Stand on the edge of a step or platform with your heels hanging off and your toes on the step.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand and place your other hand on a wall or railing for support.
Raise your heel as high as possible, lifting your body up onto your toes.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down below the step.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg.

coach_guide.intro: Rise onto your toes slowly and squeeze at the top for maximum calf activation.
coaching_cues: Hold something sturdy for balance support.
Push through the big toe, pause up.
Lower your heel below the step edge.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your calf muscle with each rep. If you feel ankle wobbling, slow down and find your balance. Build that single leg strength one rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing through reps reduces the stretch and limits your gains.

name: dumbbell single leg deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in your right hand.
Shift your weight onto your left leg and lift your right foot slightly off the ground.
Keeping your back straight, hinge forward at the hips and lower the dumbbell towards the ground.
At the same time, extend your right leg straight behind you, maintaining a slight bend in your left knee.
Lower the dumbbell until your torso and right leg are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then engage your glutes and hamstrings to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge at your hips while keeping your back flat and core braced throughout.
coaching_cues: Standing leg has a soft knee bend.
Reach the dumbbell down, hips push back.
Squeeze your glute to stand back up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a stretch in your hamstring and a strong glute squeeze at the top. If your lower back aches, you are rounding your spine. Keep that chest proud and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: dumbbell single leg deadlift with stepbox support
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in your right hand.
Place your left foot on a stepbox or elevated surface behind you.
Keeping your back straight and core engaged, hinge forward at the hips, lowering the dumbbell towards the ground.
As you lower the dumbbell, simultaneously lift your left leg behind you, maintaining a straight line from head to heel.
Lower the dumbbell until you feel a stretch in your right hamstring, then return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Use the step box for balance so you can focus on the hip hinge pattern.
coaching_cues: Light touch on box, do not lean.
Hinge hips back, keep back flat always.
Drive through your heel to stand tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your hamstring stretch and glute fire up as you rise. If balance is still hard, press lighter on the box. You are building stability and strength together.
coach_guide.warning: Relying too much on the box reduces glute and core activation.

name: dumbbell single leg split squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Take a step forward with one foot and position your feet so that your front foot is flat on the ground and your back foot is elevated on a bench or step.
Lower your body by bending your front knee and hip, keeping your back knee slightly bent and your back heel off the ground.
Continue lowering until your front thigh is parallel to the ground, then push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Drop your back knee straight down while keeping your front knee tracking over your toes.
coaching_cues: Torso stays upright, core stays tight.
Front knee stays behind your toe line.
Push through front heel to drive up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your front quad burning and glute working hard. If your knee caves inward, lighten the weight and reset. Keep pushing through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your front knee collapse inward stresses the knee joint.

name: dumbbell single leg squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Extend one leg forward and keep it off the ground throughout the exercise.
Bend your standing leg and lower your body down as if sitting back into a chair.
Keep your chest up and your back straight.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

coach_guide.intro: Sit back on one leg like sitting into a chair behind you.
coaching_cues: Arms forward help with your balance here.
Lower slowly, keep your chest lifted high.
Drive through your whole foot to stand.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads and glutes working overtime on every rep. If you tip forward, extend your arms more for counterbalance. This is elite level work so own it.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your knee shoot forward puts excessive stress on it.

name: dumbbell squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower your body down by bending at the knees and hips, as if sitting back into a chair.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold dumbbells at your sides and sit back into a deep squat position.
coaching_cues: Feet shoulder width, toes slightly out.
Push your knees out as you descend.
Stand tall and squeeze glutes at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads powering you up from the bottom. If your heels rise, work on ankle mobility first. Every squat builds a stronger foundation.
coach_guide.warning: Heels lifting shifts stress to your knees and lower back.

name: dumbbell standing alternate hammer curl and press
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and your back straight.
Exhale and curl the dumbbell in your right hand towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Continue to raise the dumbbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbell is at shoulder level.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat the curl with your left hand.
After completing the curl with your left hand, exhale and press the dumbbell in your right hand overhead.
Extend your arm fully and hold for a moment at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat the press with your left hand.
Continue alternating between curls and presses for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl with a hammer grip then press the dumbbell overhead in one smooth flow.
coaching_cues: Curl with palms facing each other always.
Rotate palm forward as you press up.
Control the weight back down each time.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps fire during the curl and shoulders engage on the press. If you are swinging, the weight is too heavy. This combo builds total arm strength.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum steals the work from your muscles entirely.

name: dumbbell standing alternate overhead press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with your palms facing forward.
Press one dumbbell overhead, fully extending your arm.
Lower the dumbbell back to shoulder level.
Repeat with the other arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press one dumbbell overhead while keeping your core braced and ribs down.
coaching_cues: Start with dumbbells at shoulder height.
Press straight up, bicep by your ear.
Lower with control then alternate sides.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulder working hard and your core stabilizing each press. If your back arches, tighten your abs more. Strong shoulders start with solid form.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back puts dangerous pressure on your spine.

name: dumbbell standing alternate raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Raise one dumbbell to the side, keeping your arm straight and your palm facing down.
Continue lifting until your arm is parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat with the other arm.
Alternate between arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise one arm at a time to shoulder height alternating front and side raises.
coaching_cues: Keep a slight bend in your elbows.
Raise to shoulder height then pause briefly.
Lower slowly and switch to other arm.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn across your shoulder caps with each raise. If you are shrugging up, relax your traps and focus. Sculpted shoulders are built with control.
coach_guide.warning: Raising too high shifts the work to your upper traps.

name: dumbbell standing alternating tricep kickback
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Extend your arms straight back, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat with the other arm, alternating sides with each repetition.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge forward and extend one arm back at a time squeezing your tricep hard.
coaching_cues: Upper arm stays glued to your side.
Extend fully and squeeze at the top.
Lower with control then switch arms immediately.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your tricep burning at full extension every single rep. If your elbow drops, reset your arm position and continue. Strong triceps mean stronger pushing power.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weight uses momentum instead of your triceps.

name: dumbbell standing around world
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Extend your arms straight out to the sides at shoulder height, palms facing down.
Keeping your arms straight, slowly rotate your arms in a circular motion, bringing the dumbbells in front of your body and then overhead.
Continue the circular motion, bringing the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Circle the dumbbells in a wide arc keeping your arms nearly straight throughout.
coaching_cues: Start at thighs, palms facing forward.
Sweep wide arcs until dumbbells meet overhead.
Control the path down, stay smooth throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn across all three heads of your shoulders. If your lower back arches, lighten the weight and brace harder. Keep those circles smooth and own every inch.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging with momentum takes tension off your delts and strains your lower back.

name: dumbbell standing bent over one arm triceps extension
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand.
Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and parallel to the ground.
Extend your arm straight back, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge forward and extend one arm back squeezing your tricep at the top.
coaching_cues: Hinge at hips, flat back, core tight.
Pin your upper arm still beside torso.
Extend fully, squeeze tricep, lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in the back of your arm at full extension. If your elbow drops, reset your position. Isolate that tricep and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbow swing steals the work from your tricep.

name: dumbbell standing bent over two arm triceps extension
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Extend your arms straight back, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bend over with both arms and press the dumbbells straight back together.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward, keep spine long and neutral.
Lock upper arms tight against your sides.
Extend both arms back, pause, then lower.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel both triceps light up at the same time when you lock out. If your torso bobs up and down, stabilize your hinge. Stay disciplined and feel that squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Rising out of the hinge shifts work away from your triceps.

name: dumbbell standing biceps curl
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward and arms fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stand tall and curl both dumbbells up by bending only at the elbows.
coaching_cues: Feet hip width, core braced, chest proud.
Curl with palms up, elbows pinned still.
Squeeze biceps hard at top, lower slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working through the entire range of motion. If your elbows drift forward, you are using momentum. Control the weight and let your biceps do the talking.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weights robs your biceps of the tension they need.

name: dumbbell standing calf raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Raise your heels off the ground as high as possible, using your calves.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive through your toes, not your whole foot — get maximum height on every rep.
coaching_cues: Push through your big toes.
Rise as high as possible.
Control down, explode up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this burning deep in your calf muscles with every single rep. Make sure you are getting full range and really squeezing at the top. Keep that calf fire burning strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling onto your whole foot instead of staying on your toes reduces effectiveness.

name: dumbbell standing concentration curl
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand, with your arm fully extended and palm facing inwards.
Place your opposite hand on your thigh for support.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbell towards your shoulder by contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbell is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Brace your elbow on your inner thigh and curl with total focus.
coaching_cues: Sit or stand, elbow pressed on thigh.
Curl the dumbbell toward your shoulder slowly.
Squeeze at top, lower under full control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense peak contraction at the top of each rep. If your shoulder rounds forward, sit taller. Stay locked in and build that peak.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your torso lean steals the isolation from your bicep.

name: dumbbell standing front raise above head
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Keep your arms straight and lift the dumbbells in front of you, raising them above your head.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise the dumbbells in front of you all the way overhead with straight arms.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, slight bend in elbows fixed.
Lift dumbbells forward and up past face.
Control them back down, no swinging allowed.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the front of your shoulders burning on the way up and down. If your back arches, tighten your core and use less weight. Push through the burn and finish proud.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back can compress your spine under load.

name: dumbbell standing inner biceps curl v. 2
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and rotate the palms of your hands until they are facing forward.
While holding your upper arms stationary, curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Slowly begin to bring the dumbbells back to the starting position as your breathe in.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl with your palms facing out and elbows flared to hit the inner bicep.
coaching_cues: Palms face away, elbows point outward slightly.
Curl both dumbbells up toward shoulders together.
