Squeeze inner biceps hard, lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the contraction deep on the inside of your biceps. If your wrists bend, keep them neutral and strong. Stay focused and feel that inner head fire.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting your wrists adds unnecessary strain and reduces bicep engagement.

name: dumbbell standing kickback
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Extend your arms straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge over and kick both dumbbells straight back squeezing at the top.
coaching_cues: Bend forward, pin elbows at your sides.
Kick weights back until arms are straight.
Pause and squeeze, then lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps contract hard when your arms are fully extended. If you feel it in your shoulders, keep your elbows higher. Stay steady and finish every rep strong.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your elbows shifts the load onto your rear delts.

name: dumbbell standing one arm concentration curl
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand, palm facing up.
Place your free hand on your thigh for support.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbell towards your shoulder by contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbell is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Isolate one arm at a time with your elbow braced on your thigh.
coaching_cues: Press working elbow firmly on inner thigh.
Curl dumbbell up, wrist stays neutral throughout.
Squeeze the peak, lower slowly, stay focused.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a powerful squeeze right at the top of your bicep. If your body sways, plant your feet wider. Own every rep and build that peak.
coach_guide.warning: Rocking your torso uses momentum instead of muscle.

name: dumbbell standing one arm curl (over incline bench)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing forward.
Place your other hand on an incline bench for support.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, exhale and curl the dumbbell towards your shoulder by contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Brace your arm over an incline bench and curl for a deep stretch.
coaching_cues: Drape arm over bench, shoulder stays down.
Curl dumbbell up without lifting your shoulder.
Lower fully to stretch, then curl again.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a big stretch at the bottom and a strong squeeze at the top. If your shoulder creeps up, press it down. Enjoy that range of motion and keep grinding.
coach_guide.warning: Shrugging your shoulder reduces the stretch and limits bicep activation.

name: dumbbell standing one arm curl over incline bench
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand.
Place your other hand on an incline bench for support.
Keeping your upper arm stationary, curl the dumbbell towards your shoulder by contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Let your arm hang over the bench and curl with zero momentum.
coaching_cues: Armpit pressed firmly against the bench pad.
Curl slow, squeeze hard at the top.
Lower with control, full arm extension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense squeeze in your bicep with nowhere to hide. If your shoulder rolls forward, reset your chest on the bench. Keep that strict form and feel the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your arm off the bench turns this into a standard curl.

name: dumbbell standing one arm extension
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand.
Raise the dumbbell overhead, fully extending your arm.
Keep your upper arm close to your head and perpendicular to the ground.
Slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head, bending your elbow.
Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbow locked in place and extend only at the elbow.
coaching_cues: Elbow points straight up, stays still.
Lower the weight behind your head slowly.
Extend fully and squeeze the tricep hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and strong contraction in your tricep. If your elbow drifts forward, you are using momentum. Lock it in and let your tricep do the work.
coach_guide.warning: Moving your elbow turns this into a shoulder exercise.

name: dumbbell standing one arm palm in press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand at shoulder height with your palm facing inwards.
Engage your core and keep your back straight.
Press the dumbbell upwards until your arm is fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Press straight up with your palm facing in to target your shoulder.
coaching_cues: Start with dumbbell at shoulder height.
Press straight up, palm facing your head.
Lower with control back to shoulder level.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your deltoid working through the full range of motion. If your lower back arches, brace your core tighter. Stay strong and keep pressing.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: dumbbell standing one arm reverse curl
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand with an overhand grip.
Keep your arm fully extended and close to your body, with your palm facing down.
Slowly curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Curl with your palm facing down to target your forearms and biceps.
coaching_cues: Palm stays facing down the entire time.
Curl up slow, keep wrists straight.
Lower with control, no swinging allowed.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your forearms burning alongside your biceps. If your wrists bend back, lighten the weight. Embrace the burn and keep going.
coach_guide.warning: Bending wrists back strains the forearm tendons.

name: dumbbell standing overhead press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with your palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press straight overhead while keeping your core locked tight — no back arch.
coaching_cues: Core tight, press straight up overhead.
Don't let your back arch.
Control the descent to shoulder level.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this burning through your entire shoulder girdle and your core working overtime. If your lower back is taking over, engage your abs harder. Keep that full-body tension and shoulder burn going.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back or pressing behind your head puts unnecessary stress on your spine.

name: dumbbell standing palms in press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder level with your palms facing inwards.
Keeping your core engaged and your back straight, press the dumbbells upwards until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press both dumbbells overhead with palms facing each other throughout.
coaching_cues: Palms face in, dumbbells at shoulder height.
Press straight up until arms fully extend.
Lower together with control, stay balanced.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel both shoulders working evenly through the movement. If one side drifts forward, reset your stance and posture. Stay tall and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Uneven pressing can lead to shoulder imbalances over time.

name: dumbbell standing preacher curl
instructions: Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Rest the back of your upper arms against the preacher bench or an incline bench, with your elbows slightly bent.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the dumbbells until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Brace against a bench or pad and curl with strict isolation.
coaching_cues: Upper arm locked against the preacher pad.
Curl up and squeeze at the top.
Lower slowly, feel the stretch at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a powerful contraction with no room for cheating. If your shoulder lifts off, you are going too heavy. Stay honest and let your bicep grow.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping the weight fast can hyperextend your elbow.

name: dumbbell standing reverse curl
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.

Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps. Continue to raise the weights until your biceps are fully contracted and the dumbbells are at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl with both palms facing down to hit forearms and biceps together.
coaching_cues: Grip overhand, palms stay facing down.
Curl up together, keep elbows pinned.
Lower slow, maintain that overhand grip.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your forearms working as hard as your biceps. If your elbows flare out, bring them back to your sides. Keep grinding and feel the pump.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum removes the tension from your muscles.

name: dumbbell standing triceps extension
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand.
Raise the dumbbell overhead, keeping your arm straight.
Bend your elbow and lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your upper arm stationary.
Extend your arm back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise both arms overhead and extend at the elbows only.
coaching_cues: Both elbows point up and stay locked.
Lower the weight behind your head together.
Extend up fully, squeeze triceps hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and powerful contraction in both triceps. If your elbows drift apart, reset and squeeze them in. Stay focused and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring elbows outward stresses your shoulder joints.

name: dumbbell standing zottman preacher curl
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Place your upper arms against the preacher bench and keep your elbows slightly bent.
Curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders while keeping your upper arms stationary.
At the top of the movement, rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing downward.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, rotating your wrists back to the starting position as well.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl palms up then rotate to palms down as you lower.
coaching_cues: Arm braced on pad, curl palms up.
Rotate to palms down at the top.
Lower slowly with that overhand grip.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps and forearms working through the rotation. If the rotation feels awkward, slow it down at the top. Master the twist and build complete arms.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the rotation reduces forearm activation.

name: dumbbell step up single leg balance with bicep curl
instructions: Stand in front of a step or platform with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Place your right foot on the step, ensuring your entire foot is in contact with the surface.
Engage your core and push through your right heel to lift your body up onto the step, bringing your left knee up towards your chest.
At the top of the movement, perform a bicep curl by bending your elbows and bringing the dumbbells towards your shoulders.
Lower the dumbbells back down and simultaneously lower your left foot back to the ground.
Repeat the movement on the opposite side, stepping up with your left foot and curling the dumbbells.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Step up, balance on one leg, then curl while staying stable.
coaching_cues: Step up, drive knee high for balance.
Hold steady, then curl with control.
Step down slow, switch legs each rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your leg stabilizing while your bicep curls. If you wobble, focus your eyes on one point ahead. Stay balanced and build functional strength.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the step down risks ankle and knee injury.

name: dumbbell step-up
instructions: Stand in front of a bench or step with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Place your right foot on the bench or step, ensuring your entire foot is in contact with the surface.
Push through your right heel and lift your body up onto the bench or step, straightening your right leg.
Bring your left foot up onto the bench or step, standing fully upright.
Step back down with your left foot, followed by your right foot, returning to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

coach_guide.intro: Step up by driving through your front heel to fire your glutes.
coaching_cues: Plant full foot on box, heel down.
Drive up through heel, squeeze glute top.
Lower with control, do not drop down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glute burning on the working leg with each step. If your quad takes over, lean your torso slightly forward. Keep pushing through that heel.
coach_guide.warning: Pushing off your back foot turns this into a calf exercise.

name: dumbbell step-up lunge
instructions: Stand in front of a step or platform with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your sides.
Place your right foot on the step, ensuring your entire foot is on the surface.
Push through your right heel and lift your body up onto the step, bringing your left foot up as well.
Once both feet are on the step, lower your left foot back down to the starting position, keeping your right foot on the step.
Repeat the movement, alternating which foot you step up with each time.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Combine the step up with a reverse lunge for continuous quad tension.
coaching_cues: Step up, stabilize, then lunge back.
Front knee tracks over toes throughout.
Keep torso upright the entire movement.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel constant tension in your front quad from step to lunge. If you wobble, slow down and find your balance. Stay strong and controlled.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the transition can cause you to lose balance and strain your knee.

name: dumbbell step-up split squat
instructions: Stand in front of a bench or step with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Place your right foot on the bench or step, ensuring your entire foot is in contact with the surface.
Step up onto the bench or step with your right foot, pushing through your heel to lift your body up.
As you step up, simultaneously lift your left knee towards your chest.
Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your left foot back to the ground while keeping your right foot on the bench or step.
Repeat the movement with your left foot on the bench or step.
Continue alternating between your right and left foot for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stay low in the split squat position to maximize quad engagement.
coaching_cues: Rear foot stays elevated on bench.
Drop straight down, front knee at 90.
Push up through front heel explosively.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your front quad and a stretch in your rear hip. If your knee caves inward, widen your stance slightly. You have got this.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your front knee drift past your toes stresses the knee joint.

name: dumbbell stiff leg deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbells towards the ground, allowing a slight bend in your knees.
Lower the dumbbells until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, then squeeze your glutes and push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge at your hips while keeping legs nearly straight to target glutes.
coaching_cues: Push hips back, slight knee bend only.
Lower until you feel hamstring stretch.
Squeeze glutes hard to stand back up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong stretch in your hamstrings and glutes firing on the way up. If you feel it in your lower back, hinge deeper at hips. Keep that posterior chain working.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: dumbbell straight arm pullover
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet planted firmly on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight above your chest.
Keeping your arms straight, slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head in an arc-like motion.
Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your arms straight and let your chest do all the pulling work.
coaching_cues: Arms straight, slight elbow bend locked.
Lower dumbbell behind head with control.
Pull with chest, not arms, exhale top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your chest at the bottom and a squeeze at the top. If your elbows bend too much, you are using triceps. Focus on that chest stretch.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your elbows shifts the work away from your chest.

name: dumbbell straight leg deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, hinge at the hips and lower the dumbbells towards the ground, allowing your torso to lean forward.
Continue lowering the dumbbells until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, keeping your knees slightly bent.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then engage your glutes and hamstrings to lift your torso back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Push your hips back with completely straight legs to load your glutes.
coaching_cues: Legs locked straight, core braced tight.
Hips push back, dumbbells slide down.
Stand tall, squeeze glutes at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense hamstring stretch and glute activation at lockout. If your lower back rounds, reduce your range. Keep those legs straight and strong.
coach_guide.warning: Locking knees too aggressively can hyperextend and damage them.

name: dumbbell sumo pull through
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointed outwards.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands in front of your body, arms extended.
Bend your knees and lower your hips down into a squat position, keeping your back straight.
Lower the dumbbell down between your legs, keeping your arms straight.
Drive through your heels and extend your hips forward, pulling the dumbbell up and in front of your body.
Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement, then lower the dumbbell back down between your legs.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge at hips in a wide stance and pull the dumbbell through your legs.
coaching_cues: Wide stance, toes pointed outward slightly.
Hinge deep, dumbbell between your legs.
Drive hips forward, squeeze glutes hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and inner thighs working together powerfully. If you feel lower back strain, sit deeper into the hinge. Let those hips do the work.
coach_guide.warning: Using your arms to pull removes glute activation entirely.

name: dumbbell supported squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Keeping your chest up and core engaged, slowly lower your body down by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the dumbbell as a counterbalance to squat deeper with control.
coaching_cues: Hold dumbbell at chest, elbows down.
Sit back and down, knees track toes.
Drive up through heels, chest stays tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads burning and glutes activating at the bottom position. If you tip forward, push your hips back more. Nice deep squats.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your heels rise shifts stress to your knees and lower back.

name: dumbbell tate press
instructions: Sit on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, then rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing away from you.
Press the dumbbells up until your arms are fully extended, then lower them back down to shoulder height.
Rotate your wrists back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows pinned and rotate the dumbbells inward to crush triceps.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay fixed, pointed at ceiling.
Rotate dumbbells inward toward your chest.
Press up, squeeze triceps hard at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense contraction in your inner triceps throughout the movement. If your elbows flare out, reduce the weight. Keep those elbows locked in place.
coach_guide.warning: Moving your elbows turns this into a pressing movement.

name: dumbbell tricep kickback with stork stance
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in your right hand.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Bring your right elbow up to your side, keeping it bent at a 90-degree angle.
Extend your right arm straight back, squeezing your triceps.
Pause for a moment, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Balance on one leg while extending the dumbbell back to challenge stability.
coaching_cues: Stand on one leg, hinge torso forward.
Upper arm locked, extend forearm back.
Squeeze tricep hard, hold one second.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your tricep working while your core and standing leg stabilize you. If you wobble, focus on a fixed point ahead. Great balance work.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the dumbbell uses momentum instead of your tricep.

name: dumbbell twisting bench press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip, palms facing away from you.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Lower the dumbbells down towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
As you lower the dumbbells, twist your wrists so that your palms face towards you at the bottom of the movement.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then reverse the movement by pressing the dumbbells back up to the starting position.
As you press the dumbbells up, twist your wrists back to the starting position with palms facing away from you.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press the dumbbells up while rotating your wrists to fully engage your triceps.
coaching_cues: Start palms in, twist to palms forward.
Lock out arms and squeeze triceps hard.
Lower with control, rotate back to start.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in the back of your upper arms at the top. If your chest takes over, focus more on the twist. Keep those triceps burning.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the rotation puts unnecessary strain on your wrists.

name: dumbbell upright row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Let the dumbbells hang in front of your thighs, with your arms fully extended.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, exhale and lift the dumbbells straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the dumbbells straight up along your body leading with your elbows.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, dumbbells resting on thighs.
Lead with elbows, pull to chest height.
Pause at top, lower slowly with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders working hard as you lift. If your traps take over, lower the weight slightly. Build those boulder shoulders one rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too high forces your shoulders into an impingement position.

name: dumbbell upright row (back pov)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Let the dumbbells hang in front of your thighs, with your arms fully extended and your palms facing your body.
Keeping your back straight and your core engaged, exhale and lift the dumbbells straight up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Continue lifting until the dumbbells are at shoulder height, with your elbows pointing out to the sides.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the dumbbells up focusing on driving your elbows back and out.
coaching_cues: Keep dumbbells close to your body throughout.
Elbows rise higher than your hands always.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your rear delts and upper back firing together. If your neck gets tense, relax your traps. You are building solid shoulders keep it up.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning forward shifts the load onto your lower back.

name: dumbbell upright shoulder external rotation
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the ground, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Rotate your arms externally, bringing the dumbbells up towards your head while keeping your elbows in the same position.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your arm outward keeping your elbow pinned at ninety degrees.
coaching_cues: Elbow stays glued to your side throughout.
Rotate forearm out in a slow arc.
Control the weight back to starting position.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the outer part of your shoulder working steadily. If your elbow drifts away, reset your position. This small move builds big stability.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum defeats the purpose and risks rotator cuff strain.

name: dumbbell waiter biceps curl
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Keep your upper arms close to your body and your elbows tucked in.
Slowly curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, keeping your wrists straight.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl the dumbbell balanced on your palm like serving a platter.
coaching_cues: Palm flat under the dumbbell plate throughout.
Curl up keeping your wrist perfectly level.
Squeeze biceps hard at the top position.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense squeeze through your entire bicep. If the weight tips, go lighter and master the balance. This unique grip delivers serious results.
coach_guide.warning: Letting the dumbbell tilt forward strains your wrist joint.

name: dumbbell w-press
instructions: Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, elbows bent and palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press the dumbbells up in a wide W shape to target all three delt heads.
coaching_cues: Start with elbows bent forming a W.
Press up and out at a diagonal.
Return to W position with full control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire shoulder cap working through the movement. If one side lags, focus on pressing evenly. Those rounded shoulders are coming along nicely.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back puts dangerous pressure on your spine.

name: dumbbell zottman curl
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and rotate your palms to face forward.
Curl the dumbbells up to your shoulders while keeping your upper arms stationary.
At the top of the movement, rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing away from your body.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, rotating your palms back to facing your body.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl up with palms facing you then rotate and lower with palms down.
coaching_cues: Curl up regular, squeeze at the top.
Rotate to palms down at peak contraction.
Lower slowly with overhand grip control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel both your biceps and forearms getting torched. If your forearms give out first, that is normal. This exercise builds complete arm strength.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping the weight fast during rotation strains your forearm tendons.

name: dumbbell zottman preacher curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing up and elbows resting on the pad.
Curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, keeping your upper arms stationary and your palms facing up.
At the top of the curl, rotate your wrists so that your palms are facing down.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position, rotating your wrists back to the starting position as you do so.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Perform the Zottman curl with your arms braced on a preacher bench.
coaching_cues: Upper arms stay pressed against the pad.
Curl up, rotate, then lower with control.
Keep the movement smooth no jerking allowed.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an isolated burn in your biceps and forearms. If you lift off the pad, you are going too heavy. Embrace the strict form and feel the gains.
coach_guide.warning: Hyperextending at the bottom risks straining your elbow joint.

name: dumbbells seated triceps extension
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a dumbbell in both hands with an overhand grip, and raise it above your head.
Bend your elbows and lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your upper arms close to your ears.
Extend your arms and raise the dumbbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Extend the dumbbells overhead keeping your elbows pointed at the ceiling.
coaching_cues: Hold one dumbbell with both hands overhead.
Lower behind head, elbows stay stationary.
Press up and squeeze triceps at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch then powerful contraction in your triceps. If your elbows flare out, reset them. Strong triceps are just a few reps away.
coach_guide.warning: Letting elbows drift forward turns this into a shoulder exercise.

name: dynamic chest stretch (male)
instructions: Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms straight out to the sides, parallel to the ground.
Slowly bring your arms forward, crossing them in front of your body.
Feel the stretch in your chest muscles.
Hold the stretch for 10-30 seconds.
Return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Open your chest dynamically by swinging your arms back with controlled momentum.
coaching_cues: Stand tall and swing arms back smoothly.
Open chest wide, feel the stretch.
Keep movements rhythmic and controlled throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest opening up and shoulders loosening with each rep. If you feel any pinching, reduce the range slightly. This is perfect prep for your workout.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging too aggressively can overstretch your shoulder ligaments.

name: elbow dips
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with your hands gripping the edge next to your hips.
Slide your hips forward off the bench and straighten your legs, keeping your heels on the ground.
Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your hands to straighten your arms and lift your body back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows tucked close to your body and lower with control to maximize tricep engagement.
coaching_cues: Elbows point straight back, stay tight.
Lower until upper arms are parallel.
Press up strong, squeeze triceps at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in the back of your upper arms throughout the movement. If your shoulders are doing the work, lean forward slightly less. Keep pushing through that tricep burn.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning too far forward shifts the work to your chest and strains shoulders.

name: elbow lift - reverse push-up
instructions: Start by lying face down on the ground with your legs extended and your hands placed directly under your shoulders.
Engage your core and press through your palms to lift your upper body off the ground, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Pause at the top for a moment, squeezing your upper back muscles.
Slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press through your elbows to lift your upper body and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
coaching_cues: Elbows drive into the floor firmly.
Lift chest by squeezing shoulder blades.
Lower slowly, keep core engaged throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles working hard between your shoulder blades. If your lower back is straining, engage your core more. This subtle move builds real back strength.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back takes tension away from your upper back.

name: elbow-to-knee
instructions: Start by lying flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engage your abs and lift your upper body off the ground, bringing your right elbow towards your left knee.
At the same time, bring your left knee towards your right elbow, creating a twisting motion.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body and extend your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat the movement, this time bringing your left elbow towards your right knee and your right knee towards your left elbow.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bring opposite elbow to knee with a controlled twist to fire up your obliques and abs.
coaching_cues: Twist from your ribcage, not arms.
Extend opposite leg fully each rep.
Keep lower back pressed into the floor.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core working especially the sides of your waist. If your neck is straining, support your head lightly with your hands. Stay controlled and feel the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling on your neck strains it and reduces core activation.

name: elevator
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Place your hands on your hips or cross them in front of your chest.
Keeping your back straight, slowly bend forward at the waist, lowering your upper body towards the ground.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly raise your upper body back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Glide your shoulder blades up and down your back like an elevator to build upper back control.
coaching_cues: Depress shoulders down away from ears.
Elevate shoulders up toward your ears.
Move slowly, feel each position fully.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back and the muscles around your shoulder blades engaging with each glide. If you feel tension in your neck, relax it and focus lower. Great work on this subtle strengthener.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing through reps prevents proper muscle activation and control.

name: exercise ball alternating arm ups
instructions: Sit on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing inwards and your arms extended down by your sides.
Engage your core and slowly lift one arm up towards your shoulder, keeping your elbow slightly bent.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arm back down to the starting position.
Repeat the movement with the other arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Alternate lifting each arm while staying balanced on the ball to challenge your lats and core.
coaching_cues: Keep hips level, do not rock.
Reach arm forward, thumb pointing up.
Lower with control, switch arms smoothly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lat muscles working under each arm as you lift. If you are wobbling too much, widen your stance on the floor. Stay steady and keep those arms reaching strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rocking side to side means your core is not engaged enough.

name: exercise ball back extension with arms extended
instructions: Place the stability ball on the ground and lie face down on top of it, with your hips resting on the ball and your feet against a wall for stability.
Extend your arms straight out in front of you, with your palms facing down.
Engage your core and slowly lift your upper body off the ball, keeping your back straight and your neck in line with your spine.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Extend your spine over the ball with arms reaching forward to strengthen your entire back.
coaching_cues: Hips stay pressed into the ball.
Lift chest and arms together smoothly.
Lower with control, keep arms extended.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower and mid back muscles working as you lift your torso. If your lower back feels pinched, reduce your range of motion. You are building a stronger spine with every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Hyperextending too far can compress your lower spine.

name: exercise ball back extension with hands behind head
instructions: Place the stability ball on the ground and lie face down on top of it with your hips resting on the ball.
Position your feet against a wall or other stable surface for support.
Cross your arms behind your head, with your hands touching the back of your head.
Engage your core and slowly lift your upper body off the ball, extending your back until your body forms a straight line from your head to your hips.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Place hands behind your head and extend up to work your spine with added upper body engagement.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay wide, chest stays open.
Lift by squeezing your back muscles.
Lower slowly, keep neck neutral throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the muscles along your spine firing to lift your torso. If you feel it all in your neck, focus on lifting from your mid back. Keep going and strengthen that posterior chain.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling on your head strains your neck and reduces back engagement.

name: exercise ball back extension with knees off ground
instructions: Place the stability ball on the ground and lie face down on top of it, with your hips resting on the ball and your feet against a wall for stability.
Position your hands behind your head or crossed over your chest.
Engage your core and slowly lift your upper body off the ball, extending your back until your body forms a straight line from your head to your heels.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lift your knees off the ground to add instability and intensify the back extension challenge.
coaching_cues: Hover knees just off the ground.
Extend spine while keeping knees lifted.
Control the movement, no sudden drops.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire posterior chain from glutes to upper back working hard. If you lose balance, reset and find your center. This advanced move is building serious strength.
coach_guide.warning: Losing control can cause you to roll off the ball.

name: exercise ball back extension with rotation
instructions: Start by lying face down on the stability ball with your hips resting on the ball and your feet firmly planted on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head or cross them over your chest.
Engage your core and slowly lift your upper body off the ball, extending your back until your body forms a straight line from your head to your heels.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly rotate your torso to one side, keeping your hips and legs stable.
Return to the center and repeat the rotation to the other side.
Lower your upper body back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Add a rotation at the top of your back extension to engage your obliques and spinal rotators.
coaching_cues: Extend up first, then rotate smoothly.
Rotate from mid back, not shoulders.
Return to center before lowering down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your back muscles and obliques working together through the twist. If you feel any sharp pain, reduce rotation range. This combo move is building functional core strength.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating too aggressively can strain your lower back.

name: exercise ball dip
instructions: Sit on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Place your hands on the ball beside your hips, fingers pointing forward.
Engage your triceps and push through your hands to lift your body off the ball, straightening your arms.
Lower your body back down by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the stability ball for dips to challenge your triceps while building balance and control.
coaching_cues: Hands grip ball firmly, fingers forward.
Lower until elbows reach ninety degrees.
Press up strong, stabilize the ball.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps burning while your core works to keep you balanced. If the ball moves too much, squeeze it tighter with your hands. Great job taking on this stability challenge.
coach_guide.warning: An unstable ball can slip so keep your grip firm throughout.

name: exercise ball hip flexor stretch
instructions: Place the stability ball on the ground and kneel in front of it.
Place your right foot on top of the stability ball, with your knee bent at a 90-degree angle.
Extend your left leg behind you, keeping it straight.
Lean forward, pushing your hips towards the stability ball, until you feel a stretch in your right hip flexor.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then switch sides and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Let the ball support you as you sink deep into your hip flexors.
coaching_cues: Kneel with back foot on the ball.
Push hips forward, keep torso tall upright.
Breathe deeply and hold the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in the front of your hip and thigh. If you feel lower back tension, tuck your pelvis under slightly. Let the stretch release all that tightness.
coach_guide.warning: Do not arch your lower back excessively during this stretch.

name: exercise ball hug
instructions: Sit on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold the stability ball with both hands, hugging it close to your chest.
Engage your core muscles and slowly lean back, keeping your back straight and your feet planted on the ground.
Continue leaning back until you feel a stretch in your back muscles.
Hold the position for a few seconds, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Wrap yourself around the ball and let your spine decompress fully.
coaching_cues: Drape your body over the ball completely.
Wrap arms around like hugging it tight.
Relax your head and breathe into back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a gentle stretch through your entire spine and back muscles. If you feel unstable, position your feet wider apart. Enjoy this moment of total relaxation.
coach_guide.warning: Move slowly onto the ball to avoid rolling off unexpectedly.

name: exercise ball lat stretch
instructions: Sit on a stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand and extend your arm straight up overhead.
Slowly lean to the opposite side, feeling a stretch in your lat muscle.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side.

coach_guide.intro: Use the ball to anchor yourself and lengthen through your lats.
coaching_cues: Kneel beside the ball, arm extended over.
Sink your hips back away from hand.
Hold and breathe into your side body.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch along the side of your back under your armpit. If you feel shoulder discomfort, lower your arm position slightly. Let each breath deepen the stretch.
coach_guide.warning: Avoid rotating your torso and keep hips squared forward.

name: exercise ball lower back stretch (pyramid)
instructions: Sit on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Slowly walk your feet forward, rolling the ball down your back until your lower back is resting on the ball.
Place your hands behind your head or cross them over your chest.
Engage your core and slowly lower your upper body towards the ground, allowing your lower back to stretch over the ball.
Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stack your body in a pyramid shape to stretch your lats and lower back.
coaching_cues: Place hands on ball, walk feet back.
Push hips up high, drop chest down.
Keep arms straight and head between arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a stretch through your lats and along your lower back. If your hamstrings are tight, bend your knees slightly. Hold steady and let gravity do the work.
coach_guide.warning: Do not let the ball roll forward and lose your stable position.

name: exercise ball lying side lat stretch
instructions: Lie on your side with your legs extended and your head supported by the stability ball.
Place your top arm on the ball for stability.
Reach your top arm overhead and allow your torso to rotate slightly.
Feel the stretch in your lat muscles on the side of your body.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then switch sides and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Lie on your side over the ball to isolate each lat individually.
coaching_cues: Drape your side body over the ball.
Top arm reaches long overhead toward floor.
Anchor with your feet for stability control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong stretch along the entire side of your torso and lat. If you feel unbalanced, stagger your feet wider apart. Breathe deeply and release the tension.
coach_guide.warning: Move slowly and control the ball to prevent rolling.

name: exercise ball on the wall calf raise
instructions: Stand with your back against a wall and place an exercise ball between your lower back and the wall.
Position your feet shoulder-width apart, with your toes pointing forward.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your arms extended by your sides.
Raise your heels off the ground, lifting your body weight onto the balls of your feet.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press the ball against the wall for stability while you strengthen your calves.
coaching_cues: Lean back into ball against the wall.
Rise up onto your toes, squeeze calves.
Lower heels below the step slowly down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong burn in both calf muscles at the top of each rep. If you feel knee strain, keep a soft bend throughout. Push through the burn for stronger calves.
coach_guide.warning: Do not let the ball slip by keeping constant pressure back.

name: exercise ball on the wall calf raise (tennis ball between ankles)
instructions: Stand facing a wall with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Place an exercise ball between the wall and your lower back.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your arms extended by your sides.
Place a tennis ball between your ankles.
Raise your heels off the ground, lifting your body up onto your toes.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The tennis ball between ankles forces your calves to work evenly together.
coaching_cues: Squeeze tennis ball between your ankle bones.
Rise up high onto toes together smoothly.
Lower with control, maintain ankle squeeze throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel both calves working equally with extra inner calf engagement. If the ball keeps dropping, focus on constant light pressure. Keep squeezing and build those balanced calves.
coach_guide.warning: Do not let ankles roll outward or you will drop the ball.

name: exercise ball on the wall calf raise (tennis ball between knees)
instructions: Stand with your back against a wall and place an exercise ball between your lower back and the wall.
Position your feet shoulder-width apart and slightly in front of you.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, with your arms extended by your sides.
Place a tennis ball between your knees.
Raise your heels off the ground, lifting your body up onto your toes.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Squeezing the ball between your knees activates your inner thighs during calf raises.
coaching_cues: Place tennis ball between your inner knees.
Squeeze knees together, rise onto toes high.
Lower slowly while maintaining the knee squeeze.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your calves burning along with inner thigh engagement. If your knees keep separating, reduce your range of motion. This extra squeeze builds lower body stability.
coach_guide.warning: Keep knees aligned over toes throughout the entire movement.

name: exercise ball one leg prone lower body rotation
instructions: Lie face down on the stability ball with your hips resting on the ball and your legs extended straight behind you.
Place your hands on the ground in front of you for support.
Engage your glutes and core muscles to stabilize your body.
Slowly lift one leg off the ground, keeping it straight and parallel to the floor.
Rotate your leg outward, away from your body, while keeping your hips and upper body stable.
Pause for a moment at the end of the rotation, then slowly return your leg to the starting position.
Repeat the rotation with the other leg.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Balance on one leg while rotating to fire up your glutes and core.
coaching_cues: Lie prone with one leg on ball.
Rotate lower body slowly side to side.
Keep hips level and core braced tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and deep core stabilizers working hard. If you feel lower back strain, reduce your rotation range. Stay controlled and build that single leg stability.
coach_guide.warning: Do not rotate so far that your hips lift off the ground.

name: exercise ball one legged diagonal kick hamstring curl
instructions: Start by lying on your back with your legs extended and your heels resting on top of the stability ball.
Place your arms by your sides for stability.
Engage your glutes and core muscles to lift your hips off the ground, creating a straight line from your shoulders to your heels.
Bend your right knee and bring it towards your chest, keeping your left leg extended and your foot flexed.
Kick your right leg diagonally across your body, extending it fully and engaging your hamstrings.
Slowly return your right leg to the starting position, maintaining control and stability.
Repeat the movement with your left leg, alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This dynamic move combines a leg curl with a diagonal kick for complete glute activation.
coaching_cues: Lie back with one heel on ball.
Curl the ball in then kick diagonally.
Control the ball back to start position.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your hamstrings and glutes firing throughout the entire movement. If you feel cramping, slow down and reset your position. Master this and your glutes will thank you.
coach_guide.warning: Keep your hips elevated and do not let them drop down.

name: exercise ball pike push up
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands on the floor and your shins resting on the stability ball.
Engage your core and lift your hips up towards the ceiling, rolling the ball towards your hands.
Keep your legs straight and your body in a pike position, forming an inverted V shape.
Bend your elbows and lower your upper body towards the floor, keeping your head in line with your hands.
Push through your hands and extend your arms to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Walk your hands out and use your core to pike your hips high before each push up.
coaching_cues: Hands shoulder width, fingers spread wide.
Pike hips high, keep legs straight.
Lower chest to floor, push back up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest and shoulders burning while your core works overtime to stabilize. If your lower back sags, reset your pike position. Stay tight and own each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag puts dangerous pressure on your lower back.

name: exercise ball prone leg raise
instructions: Lie face down on a mat with your legs extended and your toes resting on top of the stability ball.
Place your hands on the ground, shoulder-width apart, and engage your core muscles.
Keeping your legs straight, slowly lift them off the ground, using your lower back and glutes to raise them as high as possible.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your core braced and lift your legs using your lower back muscles not momentum.
coaching_cues: Hips anchored firmly on the ball.
Squeeze glutes, lift legs slowly together.
Lower with control, do not drop.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower back and glutes working as you lift and lower. If you feel strain in your neck, relax your head down. Build that back strength one rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your legs uses momentum and can strain your lower back.

name: exercise ball seated hamstring stretch
instructions: Sit on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Slowly roll the ball forward, walking your feet out until your upper back is resting on the ball and your legs are extended straight in front of you.
Place your hands on your hips for support.
Engage your core and slowly lower your upper body towards the ground, keeping your back straight and your chest lifted.
Stop when you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, and hold the position for 20-30 seconds.
Slowly return to the starting position by pushing through your heels and using your hamstrings to pull yourself back up.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit tall on the ball and extend one leg to feel a deep stretch through your hamstring.
coaching_cues: Sit upright, one leg extended straight.
Hinge forward from hips, back flat.
Hold the stretch, breathe deeply now.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a gentle pull along the back of your extended leg from hip to knee. If you feel it behind your knee only, soften the joint slightly. Breathe into the stretch and enjoy it.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back reduces the stretch and strains your spine.

name: exercise ball seated triceps stretch
instructions: Sit on a stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand and extend your arm straight up above your head.
Bend your elbow and lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your upper arm close to your ear.
Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then return to the starting position.
Repeat with the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Sit tall and reach one arm overhead to open up your triceps and shoulder.
coaching_cues: Sit upright, reach arm overhead now.
Bend elbow, drop hand behind head.
Gently press elbow back, hold there.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a nice stretch along the back of your upper arm and into your shoulder. If your back arches, engage your core more. Let the tension melt away with each breath.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing the stretch too deep can strain your shoulder joint.

name: exercise ball supine triceps extension
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Hold a dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms straight up towards the ceiling.
Slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping your elbows close to your ears.
Pause for a moment, then raise the dumbbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie back on the ball and lower the dumbbells behind your head with control.
coaching_cues: Upper back supported, hips lifted high.
Lower dumbbells slowly behind your head.
Extend arms up, squeeze triceps hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps working hard as you extend and a stretch at the bottom. If your elbows flare wide, bring them in closer. Keep that core tight and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping the weight too fast can strain your elbow joints.

name: ez bar french press on exercise ball
instructions: Sit on an exercise ball and hold an EZ barbell with an overhand grip.
Extend your arms straight up, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit stable on the ball and lower the bar behind your head to target your triceps.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, core tight, feet planted.
Lower bar behind head, elbows in.
Press up until arms fully extend.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps burning as you press the bar overhead each rep. If your back arches, lighten the weight and brace harder. You are building strong arms right now.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbows shifts stress onto your shoulder joints.

name: ez bar lying bent arms pullover
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your head at one end and your feet on the floor.
Hold the EZ barbell with a pronated grip (palms facing away from you) and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms straight above your chest, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Lower the barbell in an arc motion behind your head, maintaining the slight bend in your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then return the barbell to the starting position by reversing the arc motion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your arms bent and pull the bar in an arc to stretch and squeeze your lats.
coaching_cues: Lie flat, arms bent holding bar.
Lower bar back in arc, feel stretch.
Pull bar over chest, squeeze lats.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your lats at the bottom and a strong contraction at the top. If your lower back lifts, keep your core braced tighter. Great work opening up those lats.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back excessively can cause spinal strain.

name: ez bar lying close grip triceps extension behind head
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your head at the end of the bench.
Hold the ez barbell with a close grip, palms facing up, and extend your arms straight up over your chest.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows locked in place and lower the bar behind your head for a deep triceps stretch.
coaching_cues: Lie flat, elbows pointing to ceiling.
Lower bar slowly behind your head.
Extend arms up, squeeze triceps tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong stretch in your triceps at the bottom and a hard squeeze at the top. If your elbows drift apart, reset them closer together. Push through and feel those triceps work.
coach_guide.warning: Moving your elbows takes tension off the triceps completely.

name: ez bar reverse grip bent over row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and hands shoulder-width apart.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up, until your torso is almost parallel to the floor.
Pull the ez barbell towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the ez barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use an underhand grip and row the bar into your lower chest squeezing your upper back.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward, back flat, grip underhand.
Pull bar to lower chest, elbows back.
Squeeze shoulder blades, lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back and biceps working together on every pull. If your lower back rounds, reset your hip hinge position. Keep pulling strong and build that back.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: ez bar seated close grip concentration curl
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold an EZ barbell with an underhand grip.
Rest your elbow on the inside of your thigh, just above the knee.
Curl the barbell up towards your shoulder while keeping your upper arm stationary.
Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Brace your elbow on your thigh and curl with total control to isolate your biceps.
coaching_cues: Sit forward, elbow braced on thigh.
Curl bar up, squeeze biceps hard.
Lower slowly, keep tension the whole way.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps doing all the work with no shoulder involvement at all. If your elbow lifts off your thigh, lighten the weight. Focus and feel every inch of that curl.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your elbow allows momentum and reduces biceps activation.

name: ez bar standing french press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ez barbell with an overhand grip.
Raise the barbell above your head, fully extending your arms.
Keeping your upper arms close to your head, slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows locked in place and only move from the forearms down.
coaching_cues: Elbows point forward, stay completely still.
Lower the bar behind your head slowly.
Squeeze triceps hard at the top lockout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your triceps at the bottom and a strong contraction at the top. If your elbows drift forward, you are using momentum instead of muscle. Stay controlled and feel that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Moving elbows turns this into a shoulder press and strains your joints.

name: ez barbell anti gravity press
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding the ez barbell with an overhand grip.
Raise the barbell to shoulder height, keeping your elbows slightly bent and your palms facing forward.
Press the barbell overhead, extending your arms fully.
Lower the barbell back to shoulder height and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press the bar overhead while keeping your core tight and shoulders stable.
coaching_cues: Grip the angled handles, elbows slightly forward.
Press straight up, keep your ribs down.
Lower with control, do not bounce at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulder muscles working through the entire range of motion. If your lower back arches, you are using too much weight. Stay strong and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back puts dangerous pressure on your lower spine.

name: ez barbell close grip preacher curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher curl bench and place your upper arms on the pad, gripping the ez barbell with an underhand grip.
Rest your triceps on the pad and fully extend your arms, keeping your back straight.
Slowly curl the barbell towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Lower the barbell back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let the pad lock your arms in place so only your biceps do the work.
coaching_cues: Chest pressed firmly against the pad.
Curl up slow, squeeze biceps at top.
Lower all the way down, full stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense contraction in your biceps with no help from your body. If your shoulders creep forward, you are cheating the movement. Isolate those arms and crush it.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your elbows off the pad removes the bicep isolation completely.

name: ez barbell close-grip curl
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary throughout the movement.
Curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows glued to your sides for maximum bicep tension.
coaching_cues: Grip the inner angled curves tightly.
Curl up without swinging your body.
Lower slowly, feel the stretch at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps doing all the lifting from start to finish. If your elbows drift forward, you lose the tension. Lock in and feel every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weight uses momentum and risks lower back strain.

name: ez barbell curl
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary throughout the movement.
Exhale as you curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale as you slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The angled grip takes pressure off your wrists while you curl.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, grip the outer curves.
Curl with elbows pinned to your sides.
Squeeze hard at top, lower under control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong pump building in your biceps with each rep. If your wrists feel strained, adjust your grip slightly. Keep the tension and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning back to lift the weight shifts stress to your spine.

name: ez barbell decline close grip face press
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your head lower than your feet.
Grasp the ez barbell with a close grip, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up above your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Lower the barbell towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then press the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lower the bar toward your face then press back up using your triceps.
coaching_cues: Elbows stay tucked, pointed toward ceiling.
Lower bar toward your forehead with control.
Press up and squeeze triceps at lockout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps stretch at the bottom and contract powerfully at the top. If your shoulders take over, bring your elbows in tighter. Stay focused and power through.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring elbows puts excessive strain on your shoulder joints.

name: ez barbell decline triceps extension
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your head lower than your feet and your feet secured.
Hold the ez barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms fully, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Lower the barbell slowly towards your forehead, bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The decline angle increases the stretch on your triceps for better growth.
coaching_cues: Lock elbows in place over your chest.
Lower the bar behind your head slowly.
Extend fully and squeeze triceps hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep tricep stretch at the bottom of every rep. If your elbows move around, lighten the weight and focus on form. Push through and feel it working.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping the bar too fast could cause it to hit your head.

name: ez barbell incline triceps extension
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the bench with your back against the pad and hold the ez barbell with an overhand grip.
Extend your arms fully overhead, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The incline position keeps constant tension on your triceps throughout the movement.
coaching_cues: Arms vertical, elbows locked in position.
Lower behind your head in a slow arc.
Press back up, squeeze at full extension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps working hard from stretch to contraction. If you feel it in your shoulders, your elbows are drifting too far back. Stay locked in and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Using too much weight causes your elbows to flare and lose control.

name: ez barbell jm bench press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the ez barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lower the barbell to your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in close to your body.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This hybrid movement blasts your triceps with heavy weight safely.
coaching_cues: Lower bar to your upper chest or chin.
Elbows tuck tight, point toward your feet.
Press up and back, lock out fully.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps doing the majority of the pressing work. If your chest takes over, you are lowering too far down. Keep it high and feel that tricep power.
coach_guide.warning: Lowering to your lower chest turns this into a chest press instead.

name: ez barbell reverse grip curl
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary throughout the exercise.
Curl the barbell upwards by contracting your biceps, while exhaling.
Continue to raise the barbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the barbell is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Slowly begin to bring the barbell back to the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Palms facing down shifts the focus to your forearms and outer biceps.
coaching_cues: Overhand grip, thumbs wrapped around bar.
Curl up keeping wrists straight and strong.
Lower slowly, do not let the bar drop.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your forearms burning along with your biceps on every rep. If your wrists bend backward, you are gripping too tight. Relax your grip slightly and keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Bending wrists backward puts strain on the tendons and joints.

name: ez barbell reverse grip preacher curl
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the ez barbell with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Rest your upper arms on the pad, allowing your forearms to hang down.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the barbell up towards your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Flip your grip palms down and let your forearms do the heavy lifting.
coaching_cues: Lock your upper arms against the pad.
Curl up slow, squeeze at the top.
Lower with control, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your outer forearms and upper biceps. If your wrists hurt, lighten the weight slightly. Keep grinding through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbows lift off the pad removes tension from the target muscles.

name: ez barbell seated curls
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and your hands shoulder-width apart.
Rest your upper arms on your thighs, allowing the ez barbell to hang down in front of you.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the ez barbell upwards towards your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the ez barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sitting down removes momentum so your biceps work harder every rep.
coaching_cues: Sit tall with back flat on bench.
Curl up keeping elbows pinned at sides.
Squeeze hard at top then lower slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps burning from bottom to top of each curl. If your back arches, the weight is too heavy. Stay strict and earn those gains.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning back turns this into a momentum swing instead of a curl.

name: ez barbell seated triceps extension
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold the ez barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Raise the barbell overhead, fully extending your arms.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then raise the barbell back to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows locked in place and let your triceps do all the work.
coaching_cues: Hold bar overhead with elbows pointing forward.
Lower behind head, keeping upper arms still.
Press up and squeeze your triceps hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your triceps at the bottom and a strong squeeze at the top. If your elbows flare out, reset your position. Push through and feel those triceps work.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbows drift wide puts dangerous stress on your shoulders.

name: ez barbell spider curl
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Rest your upper arms on a preacher bench or stability ball, allowing your elbows to hang down.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the barbell up towards your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lean into the pad and isolate your biceps with zero body movement.
coaching_cues: Chest pressed firmly against the pad.
Let arms hang straight then curl up.
Squeeze biceps at top and lower slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense contraction in your biceps peak with each rep. If you feel it in your shoulders, check your arm position. This strict form builds serious arms.
