coach_guide.warning: Lifting your chest off the pad allows you to cheat the movement.

name: ez barbell spider curl
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Rest your upper arms on a preacher bench or stability ball, allowing your elbows to hang down.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the barbell up towards your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This angled position targets your biceps peak like nothing else.
coaching_cues: Keep chest glued to the bench.
Curl straight up with no swinging.
Control the negative on every single rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working overtime in that stretched position. If your arms drift forward, reset against the pad. Stay focused and watch those peaks grow.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the lowering phase wastes half of each rep.

name: ez-bar biceps curl (with arm blaster)
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Place your upper arms against the arm blaster, keeping them stationary throughout the exercise.
Keeping your elbows close to your body, exhale and curl the barbell up towards your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The arm blaster locks you in for the strictest curls of your life.
coaching_cues: Press upper arms firmly into the blaster.
Curl up without letting elbows move forward.
Lower under control and feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps burning with nowhere to hide from the work. If your shoulders creep up, relax and reset your posture. This is pure biceps building.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbows slide forward defeats the purpose of the blaster.

name: ez-bar close-grip bench press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and your back pressed against the bench.
Grasp the ez barbell with a close grip, hands shoulder-width apart, palms facing forward.
Lift the barbell off the rack and hold it directly above your chest with your arms fully extended.
Slowly lower the barbell towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The close grip puts all the pressing work directly on your triceps.
coaching_cues: Grip the bar with hands shoulder width.
Lower to your lower chest with elbows tucked.
Press up and lock out squeezing triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps doing the heavy lifting on every press. If your wrists ache, adjust your grip angle slightly. Keep pushing for bigger stronger triceps.
coach_guide.warning: Gripping too narrow strains your wrists and limits your power.

name: ez-barbell standing wide grip biceps curl
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the ez barbell with an underhand grip, hands wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and your upper arms stationary throughout the movement.
Curl the barbell up towards your shoulders by contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The wide grip shifts focus to your inner biceps for complete development.
coaching_cues: Stand tall with wide grip on bar.
Curl up keeping elbows locked at sides.
Squeeze hard at top then control down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the inner part of your biceps working hard each rep. If your elbows flare out, narrow your stance slightly. Keep that tension and build those arms.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weight up removes all the muscle building tension.

name: farmers walk
instructions: Stand up straight with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your sides.
Keep your back straight and your shoulders back.
Take small, controlled steps forward, maintaining an upright posture.
Continue walking for the desired distance or time.
To finish, stop walking and carefully lower the dumbbells to your sides.

coach_guide.intro: Grab heavy weights and walk with purpose to build total body strength.
coaching_cues: Grip dumbbells tight at your sides.
Stand tall with shoulders back and down.
Take short quick steps and keep moving.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your grip burning while your core and legs work hard. If your posture starts to crumble, set the weights down briefly. Stay strong and keep walking.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your shoulders round forward puts your lower back at risk.

name: finger curls
instructions: Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the ground and hold a barbell with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing your wrists to hang off the edge.
Slowly curl your fingers towards your palms, squeezing the barbell tightly.
Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly release your fingers back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let the bar roll to your fingertips then crush it back into your palm.
coaching_cues: Rest forearms on thighs palms facing up.
Let bar roll down to your fingertips.
Curl fingers closed and squeeze the bar.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your forearms and finger flexors. If your wrists hurt, try a lighter weight to start. This is how you build a crushing grip.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum instead of finger strength defeats the whole exercise.

name: flag
instructions: Start by gripping a vertical pole with both hands, palms facing each other, and arms fully extended.
Engage your core and lift your legs off the ground, keeping them straight.
Using your core and upper body strength, raise your legs until they are parallel to the ground.
Hold this position for as long as you can, maintaining a straight body line.
Slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Grip the pole tight and use your core to hold your body horizontal.
coaching_cues: Squeeze your abs like bracing for punch.
Keep your body in one straight line.
Breathe steady and hold the tension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel intense tension through your entire core and obliques. Your grip and lats will also be working hard to stabilize. This is elite level so be proud of every second you hold.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag puts dangerous stress on your lower back.

name: flexion leg sit up (bent knee)
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
At the same time, lift your legs off the ground, bending your knees and bringing them towards your chest.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body and legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your knees toward your chest as you sit up for a full ab contraction.
coaching_cues: Start flat with knees bent, feet hovering.
Crunch up while pulling knees to chest.
Lower back down with control, repeat.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire ab wall working as you crunch and pull together. The lower abs engage more when you lift those legs. Keep the pace steady and feel the burn build.
coach_guide.warning: Yanking your neck forward strains it instead of your abs.

name: flexion leg sit up (straight arm)
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and arms straight above your head.
Engaging your abs, lift your upper body off the ground while simultaneously lifting your legs towards your chest.
Reach your hands towards your toes as you lift your upper body and legs.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body and legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Reach your arms overhead as you sit up for added core challenge.
coaching_cues: Arms straight overhead, core braced tight.
Sit up reaching arms toward ceiling.
Lower slowly keeping arms extended throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your abs at the bottom and a strong squeeze at the top. The straight arms make your core work harder for balance. Embrace the challenge and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your arms to build momentum cheats your abs.

name: floor fly (with barbell)
instructions: Lie flat on your back on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a barbell with an overhand grip, arms extended straight up over your chest.
Slowly lower the barbell out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Lower the barbell until your arms are parallel to the floor, feeling a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then squeeze your chest muscles to bring the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lying on the floor gives you a safe stretch while building chest strength.
coaching_cues: Grip barbell wide, arms slightly bent.
Lower out to sides until arms touch floor.
Squeeze chest to bring the bar back up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch across your chest at the bottom position. The squeeze at the top should light up your inner pecs. Control the weight and enjoy that chest pump.
coach_guide.warning: Going too heavy can overstretch and damage your shoulder joints.

name: flutter kicks
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your hands by your sides.
Engage your core and lift your legs off the ground about 6 inches.
Keeping your legs straight, alternate lifting one leg slightly higher than the other.
Continue this fluttering motion for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your legs low and move them in small fast kicks.
coaching_cues: Lower back pressed flat into floor.
Legs straight and hovering just above ground.
Kick in small controlled alternating motions.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and lower abs burning as you kick. The lower you keep your legs the harder it gets. Stay tight and push through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back off the floor strains your spine.

name: forward jump
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and lower your body into a squat position.
Swing your arms back for momentum.
Jump forward explosively, extending your hips, knees, and ankles.
Land softly on the balls of your feet and immediately go into the next jump.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode forward from your legs and land soft with bent knees.
coaching_cues: Load up by bending knees and hips.
Swing arms and jump forward explosively.
Land soft with knees bent, absorb impact.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads and glutes powering each jump. The landing should be quiet and controlled through your legs. Channel that explosive energy and keep jumping strong.
coach_guide.warning: Landing with straight legs sends shock through your knees.

name: forward lunge (male)
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and hands on your hips.
Take a big step forward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position.
Bend your right knee to about 90 degrees, keeping your knee aligned with your ankle.
Push off with your right foot and return to the starting position.
Repeat with your left leg, alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Step forward into a deep lunge keeping your torso tall and upright.
coaching_cues: Big step forward, torso stays tall.
Lower until back knee nearly touches floor.
Push through front heel to stand back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads of the front leg doing the work. A good stretch in the back hip flexor is normal too. Stay balanced and power through each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your front knee cave inward stresses the joint badly.

name: frankenstein squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the barbell in front of your body with straight arms.
Keeping your back straight, lower your body by bending at the knees and hips, as if sitting back into a chair.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold the bar in front rack position with arms straight out to challenge your core.
coaching_cues: Arms parallel to floor, elbows high.
Sit back and down keeping chest up.
Drive through heels and squeeze glutes up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads and glutes working hard plus your core fighting to stay upright. The arm position makes your upper back work overtime. Stay proud in your posture and finish every rep strong.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your elbows lets the bar roll and throws you forward.

name: frog crunch
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Open your knees wide like a frog and crunch your abs hard.
coaching_cues: Knees bent and open, soles touching together.
Crunch up reaching hands toward your feet.
Lower with control keeping abs engaged throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs isolated without hip flexors helping much. The open leg position really targets the core directly. Squeeze hard at the top and enjoy the focused burn.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling on your neck takes work away from your abs.

name: frog planche
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back straight.
As you lower your body, lift your feet off the ground and bring your knees towards your chest.
Hold this position for a few seconds, then extend your legs back out and push yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Tuck into a tight ball and lift your body using pure core and shoulder strength.
coaching_cues: Hands flat on floor, fingers spread wide.
Tuck knees tight into your chest.
Lean forward and lift hips off ground.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs and shoulders working together intensely. Your wrists will feel pressure so warm them up first. Every second you hold builds serious strength so stay focused.
coach_guide.warning: Collapsing forward can hurt your wrists and face.

name: front lever
instructions: Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back.
Bend your knees and tuck them towards your chest.
Simultaneously, lift your legs up and extend them straight out in front of you, keeping your body parallel to the ground.
Hold this position for as long as you can, aiming for a full front lever position.
To release, slowly lower your legs back down and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your entire body in one rigid line from shoulders to toes.
coaching_cues: Depress shoulders hard, lats fully engaged.
Squeeze glutes and point toes forward.
Hold the straight line, breathe steady.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel intense tension through your core and lats working together. Your abs will burn as they fight to keep you horizontal. Stay tight and own this position.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag puts dangerous strain on your lower back.

name: front lever reps
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, palms facing away from you.
Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back.
Keeping your body straight, lift your legs up until they are parallel to the ground.
Hold this position for as long as you can, aiming for 10-20 seconds.
Slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your hips up while maintaining that perfect horizontal line throughout.
coaching_cues: Start from a dead hang, engage lats.
Pull hips up, keep body completely straight.
Lower with control, reset each rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats and upper back doing the heavy pulling work. Your core stays braced hard the entire time. Each rep builds serious strength.
coach_guide.warning: Bending at the hips turns this into a different movement entirely.

name: front plank with twist
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line from head to toe.
Engage your core and glutes to maintain a stable position.
Rotate your torso to the right, lifting your right arm and extending it towards the ceiling.
Keep your hips and legs stable as you twist.
Hold for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Repeat the twist on the left side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate through your mid back while keeping your hips completely still.
coaching_cues: Lock hips square to the ground below.
Reach one arm to ceiling, rotate torso.
Return with control, switch sides smoothly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques fire as you twist and resist rotation. Your shoulders and hips stay stable while your mid spine moves. Great work building rotational strength.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating through your hips removes all the core benefit.

name: full maltese
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended straight out to the sides.
Slowly lean forward, keeping your arms straight, until your upper body is parallel to the ground.
Engage your core and hold this position for a few seconds.
Return to the starting position by pushing through your feet and standing back up.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold your body horizontally with arms out wide in a cross position.
coaching_cues: Press hands hard into ground, lean forward.
Keep body straight, squeeze everything tight.
Breathe shallow, maintain total body tension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest, shoulders, and core working at maximum effort. This is elite level strength so every second counts. You are building incredible control.
coach_guide.warning: Attempting this without proper progression risks serious shoulder injury.

name: full planche
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart and your fingers pointing forward.
Engage your core and slowly shift your weight forward, lifting your feet off the ground.
Continue shifting your weight forward until your body is parallel to the ground, balancing on your hands.
Hold this position for as long as you can, maintaining a straight body line.
Slowly lower your feet back to the ground and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Float your body parallel to the ground with straight arms beneath you.
coaching_cues: Lean shoulders forward over your wrists.
Lock arms straight, protract shoulder blades.
Point toes, squeeze glutes, stay horizontal.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders, chest, and core working together intensely. Your whole body shakes as muscles fight to hold position. This is peak bodyweight strength.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing into this without progressions can damage your shoulders.

name: full planche push-up
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands placed slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and lower your body down towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
As you lower yourself, lean your body forward and lift your feet off the ground, balancing on your hands.
Continue to lower your body until your chest is just above the ground.
Push through your hands and extend your arms to lift your body back up to the starting position.
Maintain a straight line from your head to your heels throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Perform push ups while holding your body completely horizontal in planche.
coaching_cues: Maintain forward lean throughout the movement.
Bend elbows, lower chest toward ground.
Press up powerfully, keep body perfectly straight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest, shoulders, and triceps burn with each rep. Your core never stops working to maintain that horizontal float. This is world class strength.
coach_guide.warning: Losing the planche position during reps strains shoulder joints badly.

name: gironda sternum chin
instructions: Stand facing a high bar with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Reach up and grab the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Hang from the bar with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Engage your lats and biceps to pull your chest up towards the bar, leading with your sternum.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your chest all the way to the bar while arching back.
coaching_cues: Start with a supinated grip, hang fully.
Pull chest to bar, lean back hard.
Touch sternum to bar, squeeze back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats working through a massive range of motion. Your lower chest actually touches the bar at the top. This builds serious pulling power.
coach_guide.warning: Jerking up instead of pulling controlled can strain your biceps.

name: glute bridge march
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Engage your glutes and lift your hips off the ground, forming a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
While keeping your hips lifted, lift one foot off the ground and bring your knee towards your chest.
Lower your foot back to the ground and repeat the movement with the other leg.
Continue alternating legs in a marching motion while maintaining the bridge position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold your hips high while marching your knees up one at a time.
coaching_cues: Bridge up, squeeze glutes at the top.
Lift one knee without dropping your hips.
Alternate legs, keep pelvis completely level.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel one glute firing hard while the other stabilizes. Your hips stay perfectly still as legs march. This builds single leg stability beautifully.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your hips between steps means your glutes stopped working.

name: glute bridge two legs on bench (male)
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench with your back against it and your feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands on the bench beside your hips for support.
Engage your glutes and hamstrings, then lift your hips off the bench until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bridge up with both feet elevated on the bench for extra range.
coaching_cues: Heels on bench, drive through your feet.
Lift hips high, squeeze glutes hard.
Lower slowly, tap down, and repeat.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep glute contraction at the top of each rep. The elevation increases the stretch at the bottom. Keep driving those hips up strong.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back means you have pushed past your glute range.

name: glute-ham raise
instructions: Adjust the glute-ham raise machine to fit your body.
Position yourself face down on the machine with your ankles secured.
Place your hands on your chest or cross them over your chest.
Engage your hamstrings and glutes to lift your upper body up towards the ceiling.
Continue lifting until your body is in a straight line from your head to your heels.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lower your body forward with control then pull back up using your hamstrings.
coaching_cues: Lock ankles in, keep body straight.
Lower slowly, control with your hamstrings.
Pull back up, squeeze glutes at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your hamstrings work hard through the entire lowering phase. The pull back up challenges them even more intensely. This builds bulletproof hamstrings.
coach_guide.warning: Bending at the hips instead of staying straight removes hamstring work.

name: gorilla chin
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Hang from the bar with your arms fully extended and your palms facing away from you.
Engage your core and pull your body up towards the bar, bringing your chin above the bar.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hang from the bar and drive your knees up while pulling your chin over.
coaching_cues: Grip tight, engage your core first.
Knees up as you pull chin over.
Lower with control, stay tight throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs working hard to lift your legs while your arms pull. This combination lights up your entire core. Stay strong and keep grinding.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging momentum takes the work away from your abs.

name: groin crunch
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, lift your legs off the ground, bringing your knees towards your chest.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie back with soles together and crunch your upper body toward your knees.
coaching_cues: Soles of feet pressed together, knees wide.
Curl shoulders up, reach toward your feet.
Squeeze abs at top, lower slowly down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep contraction through your lower and upper abs together. The open hip position adds an inner thigh stretch. Embrace the burn and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling on your neck strains it instead of working your abs.

name: hack calf raise
instructions: Adjust the sled machine to a comfortable weight.
Stand on the sled machine with your toes on the platform and your heels hanging off.
Hold onto the handles for stability.
Raise your heels as high as possible by pushing through the balls of your feet.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Position your shoulders under the pads and push through your calves to rise up.
coaching_cues: Feet hip width, toes pointing forward.
Rise up high onto your toes slowly.
Lower heels below platform, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense contraction at the top and a deep stretch at the bottom. Your calves will be burning. Thats exactly where gains happen.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom risks straining your Achilles tendon.

name: hack one leg calf raise
instructions: Adjust the sled machine to an appropriate weight.
Stand on the sled machine with one foot, keeping the other foot off the ground.
Hold onto the handles for stability.
Raise your heel as high as possible, lifting your body up on the ball of your foot.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
Switch legs and repeat the exercise.

coach_guide.intro: Perform the calf raise on one leg to build balanced strength.
coaching_cues: One foot centered, other leg tucked back.
Press high through your toes with control.
Lower deep, stretching your working calf fully.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel each calf working independently without help from the other side. The burn will be real and concentrated. Push through for balanced legs.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement reduces muscle engagement and control.

name: half knee bends (male)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and lower your body down as if you were sitting back into a chair.
Keep your chest up and your weight in your heels.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stand tall and perform controlled half squats at a steady rhythm.
coaching_cues: Feet shoulder width, chest stays lifted.
Bend knees halfway, weight in your heels.
Stand back up, keep the pace steady.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart rate climbing while your legs stay active. This move builds endurance and keeps blood flowing. Stay light on your feet.
coach_guide.warning: Letting knees cave inward stresses your knee joints.

name: half sit-up (male)
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie back and curl up halfway to keep constant tension on your abs.
coaching_cues: Lower back stays pressed into the floor.
Curl shoulders up, eyes look forward ahead.
Hold the squeeze, lower halfway back down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs engaged the entire time without any rest at the bottom. This constant tension builds serious core strength. Keep breathing and pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Coming up too high shifts the work to your hip flexors.

name: hamstring stretch
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step forward with your right foot and shift your weight onto your right leg.
Keeping your back straight, slowly bend forward at the hips, reaching towards your right foot with both hands.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side.

coach_guide.intro: Extend one leg and hinge forward to lengthen your hamstring.
coaching_cues: Keep your extended leg completely straight.
Hinge from hips, reach toward your toes.
Hold the stretch, breathe into the tension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep pull along the back of your thigh. Relax into it and let your muscles release. Flexibility takes patience so stay with it.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back reduces the hamstring stretch.

name: hands bike
instructions: Adjust the seat height and handlebar position to a comfortable level.
Sit on the ergometer with your back straight and feet on the pedals.
Grasp the handles with your hands and position your arms at a 90-degree angle.
Start pedaling with your hands, pushing and pulling the handles in a controlled motion.
Continue pedaling for the desired duration or number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Grip the handles and pedal with your arms in smooth circular motions.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, core engaged and stable.
Push and pull in smooth controlled circles.
Keep your shoulders relaxed and down throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest and arms working together while your heart rate rises. This low impact movement builds endurance. Keep the rhythm going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Shrugging your shoulders up creates unnecessary neck tension.

name: hands clasped circular toe touch (male)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your hands clasped together in front of your chest.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Lower your hands towards your toes in a circular motion, reaching as far as you can without straining.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Clasp hands behind you and circle down to touch alternating toes.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, clasp hands behind your back.
Rotate and reach down toward opposite toe.
Rise up, rotate to the other side.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and hamstrings stretch as you rotate down. Your core stabilizes each twist. Keep flowing smoothly through each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Locking your knees straight puts pressure on your lower back.

name: hands reversed clasped circular toe touch (male)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended to the sides.
Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Reach down with your hands and clasp them together behind your legs.
Slowly raise your hands up and over your head in a circular motion, keeping your legs straight.
Continue the circular motion until your hands touch your toes.
Reverse the motion and bring your hands back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Clasp hands in front and circle down to touch alternating toes.
coaching_cues: Hands clasped in front, stand up tall.
Rotate down, reaching toward opposite foot.
Rise and repeat to the other side.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a nice stretch through your glutes and the back of your legs. The rotation warms up your entire posterior chain. Keep moving fluidly.
coach_guide.warning: Moving too fast reduces the stretch and control.

name: handstand
instructions: Find an open space with enough room to perform a handstand.
Place your hands on the ground shoulder-width apart, fingers pointing forward.
Kick your legs up towards the wall, using your core and shoulders to maintain balance.
Once in a handstand position, engage your triceps to support your body weight.
Hold the handstand for as long as you can maintain balance.
To come down, slowly lower your legs back to the ground.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stack your body in one straight line and push the floor away through your palms.
coaching_cues: Fingers spread wide, grip the ground firmly.
Squeeze your core and glutes tight together.
Look between your hands, stay balanced.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps, shoulders, and core all working to hold you steady. If you feel wobbly, engage your legs more. Balance takes practice so stay patient with yourself.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your back arch puts dangerous pressure on your lower spine.

name: handstand push-up
instructions: Find a wall and face away from it, standing a few feet away.
Place your hands on the ground shoulder-width apart and kick your feet up against the wall, coming into a handstand position.
Bend your elbows and lower your head towards the ground, keeping your body in a straight line.
Push through your hands and extend your arms to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lower your head toward the ground with control then press back up powerfully.
coaching_cues: Keep elbows at 45 degrees, not flared.
Head touches ground gently each rep.
Drive up strong, lock out at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel intense work in your triceps and shoulders throughout each rep. If your neck strains, check your head position. This is elite level work so own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping too fast can cause serious neck or shoulder injury.

name: hanging leg hip raise
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your arms fully extended and your palms facing away from you.
Engage your core and lift your legs up by flexing your hips and knees until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bring your knees up and curl your hips toward your chest for full ab engagement.
coaching_cues: Grip tight, let shoulders stay relaxed.
Curl hips up, do not just swing.
Control the lowering phase every time.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs burning as you lift your hips. If you are swinging, slow down and use less momentum. Keep grinding through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging puts excessive strain on your shoulder joints.

name: hanging leg raise
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your arms fully extended and your palms facing away from you.
Engage your core and lift your legs up in front of you, keeping them straight.
Continue lifting until your legs are parallel to the ground or as high as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your shoulders engaged and lift with your abs, not momentum.
coaching_cues: Shoulders stay active and engaged.
Lift legs with abs, not swing.
Control the legs back down slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn deep in your lower abs and hip flexors. If you're swinging wildly, slow it down and focus on controlled movement. Embrace that deep core burn and keep fighting through every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your legs with momentum completely defeats the purpose and strains your shoulders.

name: hanging oblique knee raise
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your arms fully extended and your palms facing away from you.
Engage your core and lift your knees towards your chest, twisting your torso to the side as you do so.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, twisting your torso in the opposite direction.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise your knees toward one shoulder then alternate to hit your obliques hard.
coaching_cues: Lift knees high toward your armpit.
Rotate from your core, not hips.
Lower with control, then switch sides.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep squeeze in your side abs with each twist. If your grip fails first, work on dead hangs separately. Your obliques will thank you for this.
coach_guide.warning: Excessive swinging can strain your rotator cuff muscles.

name: hanging pike
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your arms fully extended and your palms facing away from you.
Engage your core and lift your legs up towards the bar, keeping them straight.
Continue lifting until your body forms a 'V' shape, with your legs parallel to the ground.
Hold the position for a moment, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your legs straight and fold your body by bringing your toes toward the bar.
coaching_cues: Start from a dead hang, no swing.
Lift with your abs, not momentum.
Touch toes to bar, lower slowly down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core working hard to lift those straight legs. If your hip flexors cramp, bend knees slightly to start. You are building serious core strength.
coach_guide.warning: Jerky movements can cause lower back strain or injury.

name: hanging straight leg hip raise
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your arms fully extended and your palms facing away from you.
Engage your core and lift your legs up in front of you until they are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise your straight legs and curl your hips up to maximize lower ab activation.
coaching_cues: Keep legs locked straight throughout movement.
Curl your hips up at the top.
Lower slowly, fight gravity the whole way.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs working overtime during the hip curl. If you swing, reset and start fresh each rep. This control builds real core power.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging and momentum can lead to lower back strain.

name: hanging straight leg raise
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your arms fully extended and your palms facing away from you.
Engage your core and lift your legs up in front of you, keeping them straight.
Continue lifting until your legs are parallel to the ground or as high as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lift your straight legs to hip height using pure core strength and control.
coaching_cues: Hang with arms straight, shoulders engaged.
Raise legs to parallel, pause briefly.
Lower with control, no swinging allowed.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs and hip flexors firing together. If you cannot keep legs straight, bend knees to build up. Stay consistent and strength will come.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum instead of control risks straining your back.

name: hanging straight twisting leg hip raise
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your arms fully extended and your legs straight.
Engage your core and lift your legs up towards your chest, keeping them straight.
Once your legs are parallel to the ground, twist your hips to one side, bringing your legs towards that side.
Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Repeat the movement, but this time twist your hips to the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise your straight legs while twisting to target your obliques and lower abs together.
coaching_cues: Lift legs and twist toward one side.
Keep the movement slow and controlled.
Alternate sides with each rep smoothly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques and lower abs burning with the twist. If you lose control, reduce your range of motion first. Every twist is making you stronger.
coach_guide.warning: Fast twisting motions can strain your spine and obliques.

name: high knee against wall
instructions: Stand facing a wall with your feet hip-width apart.
Place your hands on the wall for support.
Engage your core and lift your right knee up towards your chest, while keeping your left foot on the ground.
Quickly switch legs, bringing your left knee up towards your chest and lowering your right foot back down.
Continue alternating legs in a running motion, bringing your knees up as high as possible.
Maintain a fast pace and keep your upper body stable throughout the exercise.
Repeat for the desired duration or number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive your knees up high and fast against the wall for an intense cardio burn.
coaching_cues: Hands press flat against the wall.
Drive knees up to hip height fast.
Stay on your toes, keep rhythm steady.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart rate spike and your legs burning quickly. If you lose form, slow your pace but keep moving. Push through and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning too far forward puts excess pressure on your wrists.

name: hip raise (bent knee)
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands by your sides, palms facing down.
Engage your core and glutes, then lift your hips off the ground until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press through your heels to lift your hips while squeezing your abs at the top.
coaching_cues: Feet flat, knees bent at 90 degrees.
Lift hips until body forms straight line.
Squeeze abs and glutes at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs and glutes working together throughout the lift. If your lower back takes over, focus on the ab squeeze more. Keep pushing for that perfect bridge.
coach_guide.warning: Overarching your lower back can cause lumbar spine strain.

name: hug keens to chest
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and lower your body down into a squat position.
As you squat down, bring your knees up towards your chest and hug them with your arms.
Hold this position for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your knees gently toward your chest to release tension in your lower back and glutes.
coaching_cues: Lie flat and relax your shoulders down.
Hug both knees into your chest slowly.
Breathe deep and hold the stretch steady.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a gentle stretch through your glutes and lower back. If you feel pinching in your hips, ease off slightly. Let your body relax into this release.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too aggressively can strain your lower back muscles.

name: hyght dumbbell fly
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other.
Extend your arms straight up over your chest, with a slight bend in your elbows.
Keeping a slight bend in your elbows, lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement and bring the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Open your arms wide with control to stretch and squeeze your chest muscles.
coaching_cues: Keep a slight bend in your elbows.
Lower dumbbells out wide, feel the stretch.
Squeeze your chest to bring weights together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch across your chest at the bottom and a strong squeeze at the top. If your shoulders feel strained, reduce the range. Own that chest contraction.
coach_guide.warning: Straightening your arms completely puts dangerous stress on your elbows.

name: hyperextension
instructions: Adjust the hyperextension bench so that your upper thighs are resting on the pad and your feet are secured.
Cross your arms over your chest or place your hands behind your head.
Lower your upper body towards the ground while keeping your back straight.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then raise your upper body back up until it is in line with your legs.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your back straight and lift with your glutes and hamstrings, not your spine.
coaching_cues: Squeeze your glutes to come up.
Keep your back perfectly straight.
Controlled movement both directions.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this in your glutes and the back of your legs, not your lower back. If your back is doing all the work, you're hyperextending too much. Keep going with that glute squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Never round your back or hyperextend past neutral spine.

name: hyperextension (on bench)
instructions: Adjust the hyperextension bench so that your hips are resting comfortably on the pad and your feet are secured.
Cross your arms over your chest or place your hands behind your head.
Slowly lower your upper body towards the ground while keeping your back straight.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then raise your upper body back up until it is in line with your legs.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge at your hips and lift your torso to strengthen your lower back.
coaching_cues: Anchor your hips firmly on the pad.
Lower your torso slowly with control throughout.
Lift until your body forms one straight line.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower back and glutes working to raise your torso. If you feel sharp pain, reduce your range of motion. Build that posterior chain strength.
coach_guide.warning: Hyperextending past neutral can compress your spinal discs.

name: impossible dips
instructions: Position yourself between two parallel bars with your arms fully extended and your body suspended in the air.
Bend your knees and cross your ankles.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lean forward aggressively to shift maximum tension onto your triceps.
coaching_cues: Start at the top with arms locked.
Lean forward and lower slow with control.
Press up hard and squeeze your triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in the back of your arms throughout the movement. If your shoulders take over, lean forward more. Push through that tricep fire.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping too fast can damage your shoulder joints.

name: inchworm
instructions: Start in a standing position with your feet hip-width apart.
Bend forward at the waist and place your hands on the ground in front of you.
Walk your hands forward until you are in a high plank position, with your body in a straight line from head to toe.
Pause for a moment, then walk your hands back towards your feet, keeping your legs as straight as possible.
Once your hands reach your feet, stand back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Walk your hands out to plank and back to standing for a full body warmup.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward and touch the ground first.
Walk hands out until you reach plank.
Walk hands back and stand up tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your core engaged in plank and a hamstring stretch when folding. If your lower back rounds, bend your knees slightly. Keep that smooth controlled rhythm.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing through the movement reduces core activation completely.

name: inchworm v. 2
instructions: Start in a standing position with your feet hip-width apart.
Bend forward at the waist and place your hands on the ground in front of you.
Walk your hands forward until you are in a high plank position, with your body in a straight line from head to toe.
Keeping your legs straight, walk your feet towards your hands, bringing your hips up towards the ceiling.
Continue walking your hands forward, repeating the movement for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Walk your hands out and back with extra focus on controlled core engagement.
coaching_cues: Keep your hips steady, no sagging allowed.
Walk out to plank, hold and brace.
Walk back slow with legs as straight possible.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs working hard to stabilize during the walk out phase. If your back dips, shorten your range. Stay tight and keep moving forward.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag puts pressure on your lower back.

name: incline close-grip push-up
instructions: Place your hands on an elevated surface, such as a bench or step, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Extend your legs behind you, resting on the balls of your feet, with your body forming a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest towards the elevated surface by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use an elevated surface to target your triceps with a narrow hand position.
coaching_cues: Hands close together directly under chest.
Keep elbows tucked tight to your body.
Lower chest to hands then push up strong.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn in the back of your arms not your chest. If your elbows flare out, reset your hand position. Let those triceps do all the work.
coach_guide.warning: Wide elbows shift the work away from your triceps completely.

name: incline leg hip raise (leg straight)
instructions: Lie on an incline bench with your back flat against the bench and your legs extended straight out in front of you.
Place your hands on the sides of the bench for support.
Engaging your abs, lift your legs off the bench, raising them as high as you can while keeping them straight.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise your straight legs on an incline to challenge your lower abs intensely.
coaching_cues: Press your lower back into the bench.
Lift legs and hips together as one.
Lower with control, do not let legs drop.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your lower abs throughout the lift. If your back arches, reduce your range of motion. Keep that core firing strong.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back transfers stress to your spine.

name: incline push up depth jump
instructions: Find an elevated surface, such as a bench or step, and place your hands shoulder-width apart on the edge.
Step your feet back, keeping your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest towards the edge of the surface, bending your elbows and keeping your body aligned.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Jump off the edge of the surface, landing softly with your knees slightly bent.
Repeat the push-up and depth jump for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode off the elevated surface and land with control for power development.
coaching_cues: Start in strong incline pushup position ready.
Push off explosively, hands leave the surface.
Land soft with elbows bent to absorb.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest and shoulders working explosively on each rep. If your wrists hurt on landing, focus on softer catches. Bring that power every single rep.
coach_guide.warning: Landing with locked elbows can injure your wrists and shoulders.

name: incline push-up
instructions: Place your hands on an elevated surface, such as a bench or step, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Extend your legs behind you, resting on the balls of your feet, creating a straight line from your head to your heels.
Lower your chest towards the elevated surface by bending your elbows, keeping your body in a straight line.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use an elevated surface to make pushups more accessible while building strength.
coaching_cues: Hands shoulder width on the raised surface.
Lower your chest toward your hands slowly.
Push up strong and squeeze your chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest and triceps working together through the full range. If it feels too easy, use a lower surface. Build that foundation one rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Sagging hips reduce core engagement and stress your back.

name: incline push-up (on box)
instructions: Place your hands on the edge of a box or elevated surface, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Extend your legs behind you, resting on the balls of your feet, creating a straight line from your head to your heels.
Lower your chest towards the box by bending your elbows, keeping your body in a straight line.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the elevated surface to build pushing strength with proper form.
coaching_cues: Hands wide, fingers gripping the box edge.
Lower chest toward the box, elbows back.
Push through your palms, squeeze your chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working hard as you press up from the box. Your core stays tight throughout the movement. Keep building that strength.
coach_guide.warning: Sagging hips put unnecessary strain on your lower back.

name: incline reverse grip push-up
instructions: Place your hands on the edge of a bench or elevated surface, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Extend your legs behind you, resting on the balls of your feet, creating a straight line from your head to your heels.
Lower your chest towards the bench by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The reverse grip shifts emphasis to your upper chest and triceps.
coaching_cues: Fingers point toward your feet on surface.
Lower with control, keep elbows tucked in.
Press up strong, squeeze chest at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deeper contraction in your upper chest area. Your triceps will also work harder than standard push-ups. Great variation to master.
coach_guide.warning: Wrist discomfort means you need to adjust hand position.

name: incline scapula push up
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Place your hands on the bench slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Position your feet on the ground, hip-width apart.
Lower your chest towards the bench, keeping your elbows tucked in.
As you lower, retract your shoulder blades, squeezing your scapulae together.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This move strengthens the muscles that control your shoulder blades.
coaching_cues: Hold plank position on the incline surface.
Let shoulder blades pinch together, then apart.
Push the ground away, rounding upper back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the muscles wrapping around your ribcage working. This burn along the side of your chest is exactly right. Strong scapulas protect your shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your elbows turns this into a push-up instead.

name: incline twisting sit-up
instructions: Set up an incline bench at a 45-degree angle.
Lie down on the bench with your feet secured under the foot pads.
Place your hands behind your head or across your chest.
Engage your abs and lift your upper body off the bench, curling forward.
As you curl up, twist your torso to one side, bringing your elbow towards the opposite knee.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat the movement, this time twisting your torso to the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Adding rotation engages your obliques for a complete core workout.
coaching_cues: Secure your feet on the incline bench.
Curl up and rotate elbow toward opposite knee.
Control the lowering phase, alternate sides evenly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire midsection working especially the sides. The twist creates that deep oblique burn you want. Stay controlled and keep twisting.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling on your neck takes work away from abs.

name: intermediate hip flexor and quad stretch
instructions: Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold onto a stable object for support.
Bend your right knee and bring your right foot towards your glutes, grabbing the rope with your right hand.
Slowly pull your right foot towards your glutes, feeling a stretch in your right quad.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Release the stretch and repeat on the left side.

coach_guide.intro: This deeper stretch releases tight hip flexors and quad muscles.
coaching_cues: Kneel with rope looped around back foot.
Pull heel toward glutes using the rope.
Push hips forward, keep torso tall upright.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense stretch down the front of your thigh. Your hip flexor will also release with the forward hip push. Breathe into the stretch.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back reduces the hip flexor stretch.

name: inverse leg curl (bench support)
instructions: Lie face down on a bench with your hips at the edge and your legs extended straight behind you.
Hold onto the bench for support.
Keeping your upper body still, bend your knees and curl your legs towards your glutes.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly extend your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This challenging move builds serious hamstring strength and control.
coaching_cues: Kneel with ankles secured under the bench.
Lower your body forward slowly with control.
Pull back up using your hamstrings only.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your hamstrings working intensely throughout the movement. A deep burn in the back of your thighs means success. This builds bulletproof hamstrings.
coach_guide.warning: Falling forward uncontrolled can strain your lower back.

name: inverse leg curl (on pull-up cable machine)
instructions: Adjust the cable machine so that the ankle straps are at the lowest setting.
Lie face down on the bench with your legs extended and the ankle straps attached to your feet.
Hold onto the handles of the bench for stability.
Bend your knees and curl your legs towards your glutes, squeezing your hamstrings.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The cable machine provides stable ankle support for this movement.
coaching_cues: Secure ankles under the cable machine pad.
Lower forward with straight hips, stay controlled.
Curl yourself back up using hamstring strength.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your hamstrings doing all the heavy lifting. The back of your thighs will burn intensely and that is perfect. You are building serious strength.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum instead of muscle defeats the purpose.

name: inverted row
instructions: Set up a bar at waist height or use a suspension trainer.
Stand facing the bar or suspension trainer, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grab the bar or handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lean back, keeping your body straight and your heels on the ground.
Pull your chest towards the bar or handles, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower yourself back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your body to the bar to build a stronger back.
coaching_cues: Grip bar, hang with arms fully extended.
Pull chest to bar, squeeze shoulder blades.
Lower with control, keep your core tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles squeezing hard at top. Your biceps assist but the back does main work. Every rep builds pulling strength.
coach_guide.warning: Jerky pulling motions can strain your shoulder joints.

name: inverted row bent knees
instructions: Set up a bar at waist height and lie underneath it.
Grab the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Position your body so that your heels are on the ground and your body is straight.
Pull your chest up towards the bar by squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bent knees make this row more accessible while building strength.
coaching_cues: Grip bar with knees bent, feet flat.
Pull chest toward bar, drive elbows back.
Lower slowly, maintain tension throughout movement.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles engaging with each pull. The bent knee position lets you focus on form. Keep pulling strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting hips sag reduces the work your back does.

name: inverted row on bench
instructions: Set up a bench at a height that allows your body to hang freely underneath it.
Lie face up on the ground with your head towards the bench.
Reach up and grab the bench with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Position your body so that your heels are on the ground and your arms are fully extended.
Engage your core and squeeze your shoulder blades together as you pull your chest up towards the bench.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The bench setup provides a stable platform for rowing practice.
coaching_cues: Grip bar, place feet flat on bench.
Pull your chest up to meet the bar.
Squeeze shoulder blades, then lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction between your shoulder blades. Your lats and rhomboids are doing the work here. Great foundation for harder rows.
coach_guide.warning: Pushing with legs instead of pulling cheats the movement.

name: inverted row v. 2
instructions: Set up a bar at waist height on a Smith machine or use a suspension trainer.
Stand facing the bar or suspension trainer and grab it with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Walk your feet forward, leaning back until your body is at a slight angle.
Keep your body straight and pull your chest up towards the bar or handles, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your chest to the bar by squeezing your shoulder blades together.
coaching_cues: Grip bar, body straight like a plank.
Pull chest to bar, elbows back tight.
Lower with control, fully extend arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back and lats working hard on every rep. Your biceps will help but the squeeze between your shoulder blades is the focus. Keep that tension and stay strong.
coach_guide.warning: Sagging hips turn this into a weak arm pull.

name: inverted row with straps
instructions: Set up a suspension trainer or straps at chest height.
Stand facing the anchor point and grab the handles with an overhand grip.
Walk your feet forward, leaning back until your body is at an angle.
Keep your body straight and engage your core.
Pull your chest towards the handles, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower yourself back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the straps to find your perfect angle and row with your back.
coaching_cues: Grip straps tight, arms fully extended.
Pull chest up, squeeze shoulder blades hard.
Lower slow, keep your core engaged throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction across your entire upper back at the top. The straps let you adjust difficulty so find your challenge. Own every rep and build that back.
coach_guide.warning: Jerky movements with loose straps can strain your shoulders.

name: iron cross stretch
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended out to the sides.
Raise your legs up towards the ceiling, keeping them straight.
Slowly lower your legs to one side, aiming to touch the floor with your feet.
Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then return your legs to the starting position.
Repeat the stretch on the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let your crossed leg pull your hip into a deep relaxing stretch.
coaching_cues: Lie flat, arms out to the sides.
Cross one leg over, knee toward floor.
Keep both shoulders pinned to the ground.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch through your glute and outer hip. Your lower back may also release some tension here. Breathe deep and let gravity do the work.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing the knee down can twist your lower spine.

name: isometric chest squeeze
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Extend your arms straight out in front of you, parallel to the ground, with your palms facing each other.
Squeeze your chest muscles together as hard as you can, while keeping your arms straight.
Hold this position for a few seconds, focusing on contracting your chest muscles.
Release the squeeze and relax your chest muscles.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press your palms together hard and hold to fire up your chest.
coaching_cues: Palms together at chest height, elbows out.
Press hands into each other with max force.
Hold the squeeze, keep breathing throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest muscles burning from the sustained contraction. This is pure tension work with no movement needed. Embrace the burn and hold strong.
coach_guide.warning: Holding your breath during the squeeze can spike blood pressure.

name: isometric wipers
instructions: Start by lying flat on your back on a mat or bench.
Extend your arms straight out to the sides, perpendicular to your body.
Engage your core and lift both legs off the ground, keeping them together and straight.
Slowly lower your legs to one side, aiming to touch the ground with your feet while maintaining control.
Pause for a moment, then use your core to lift your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat the movement, this time lowering your legs to the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold the top of a pushup while rotating your hips side to side.
coaching_cues: Lock out pushup position, arms straight.
Rotate hips side to side, controlled pace.
Keep chest facing down, core stays tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest and shoulders working to stabilize while your core twists. This builds stability and endurance in your upper body. Stay controlled and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag puts stress on your lower back.

name: jack burpee
instructions: Start in a standing position with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower your body into a squat position, placing your hands on the ground in front of you.
Kick your feet back, landing in a push-up position.
Perform a push-up, lowering your chest to the ground and then pushing back up.
Jump your feet forward, landing in a squat position.
Jump up explosively, reaching your arms overhead.
Land softly and immediately lower back into the squat position to begin the next repetition.

coach_guide.intro: Explode from the ground into a jumping jack for maximum cardio burn.
coaching_cues: Drop down, chest touches the floor fast.
Jump up explosively, land with feet wide.
Clap hands overhead, then repeat immediately.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart rate spike and your whole body working. This is full body conditioning at its finest. Push through the burn and keep that energy high.
coach_guide.warning: Landing with locked knees can shock your joints hard.

name: jack jump (male)
instructions: Stand with your feet together and your arms by your sides.
Jump up, spreading your feet apart and raising your arms above your head.
As you land, quickly jump back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Jump your feet wide while raising your arms for classic cardio work.
coaching_cues: Start standing, feet together, arms down.
Jump feet wide, swing arms up overhead.
Land soft, return to start, keep rhythm.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart pumping and legs warming up quickly. This simple move builds endurance and coordination together. Stay light on your feet and keep moving.
coach_guide.warning: Hard landings on heels can jar your knees and ankles.

name: jackknife sit-up
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your arms overhead.
Engage your core and lift your legs and upper body simultaneously, reaching your hands towards your toes.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs and upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Bring your hands and feet together at the top for a full ab crunch.
coaching_cues: Lie flat, arms overhead, legs straight.
Lift arms and legs, meet in middle.
Lower with control, do not slam down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core working especially your lower abs. The reaching motion demands coordination and strength together. Keep it controlled and feel every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum instead of abs can strain your lower back.

name: janda sit-up
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press your heels down to activate your hamstrings and isolate your abs.
coaching_cues: Lie down, knees bent, heels dig in.
Press heels hard, curl your torso up.
Squeeze abs at top, lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs working harder than a regular situp. The heel press stops your hip flexors from taking over. This is pure core work so embrace it.
coach_guide.warning: Relaxing heel pressure lets hip flexors dominate the movement.

name: jump rope
instructions: Hold the handles of the jump rope with your hands, palms facing inward.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Swing the rope over your head and jump over it as it comes towards your feet.
Land softly on the balls of your feet and repeat the jump as the rope comes around again.
Continue jumping for the desired duration or number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stay light on your toes and let your wrists do the spinning work.
coaching_cues: Grip handles light, elbows close to sides.
Jump just high enough to clear rope.
Land on balls of feet, stay bouncy.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your calves working and your heart rate climbing fast. Rhythm is everything so find your flow and stick with it. This is fun cardio so enjoy the bounce.
coach_guide.warning: Jumping too high wastes energy and ruins your timing.

name: jump squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Jump explosively off the ground, extending your hips, knees, and ankles.
While in mid-air, quickly bring your arms forward for balance.
Land softly on the balls of your feet and immediately go into the next repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode up from a deep squat and land softly to protect your knees.
coaching_cues: Sink hips back, chest stays lifted.
Drive through heels, jump straight up.
Land soft, absorb with bent knees.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads burning with each explosive jump. Your heart rate will climb fast and thats the point. Keep pushing through and stay powerful.
coach_guide.warning: Hard landings with locked knees can damage your joints.

name: jump squat v. 2
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Jump explosively, extending your hips and knees fully.
Land softly on the balls of your feet and immediately lower your body back into a squat position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This variation adds extra challenge so focus on smooth controlled landings.
coaching_cues: Drop into squat, weight in heels.
Explode upward, extend hips fully.
Catch yourself soft, reset quickly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel intense work in your glutes and thighs on every rep. If your knees cave inward push them out over your toes. Stay explosive and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting knees collapse inward puts serious strain on ligaments.

name: kettlebell advanced windmill
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Hold a kettlebell in your right hand, with your arm extended overhead and your palm facing forward.
Rotate your left foot slightly to the right, and shift your weight onto your left leg.
Bend your left knee and hinge at the hip, lowering your torso towards the left side.
Keep your right arm extended overhead and your eyes on the kettlebell.
As you lower your torso, allow your right leg to straighten and your right foot to pivot slightly.
Lower your torso until you feel a stretch in your left hamstring and your right arm is pointing towards the ground.
Pause for a moment, then engage your core and push through your left heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Move slowly through this windmill to challenge your core and shoulder stability.
coaching_cues: Lock arm overhead, eyes on kettlebell.
Hinge hip out, slide hand down.
Brace core tight, stand up controlled.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques and deep core muscles working hard to stabilize. Your shoulder should stay packed and secure throughout the movement. Master the control and you will build serious strength.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing this movement can cause shoulder instability or injury.

name: kettlebell alternating hang clean
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Allow the kettlebells to hang in front of your body with your arms fully extended.
In one fluid motion, explosively extend your hips, shrug your shoulders, and pull the kettlebells up towards your shoulders.
As the kettlebells reach shoulder height, rotate your wrists and catch the kettlebells in the rack position, with your palms facing inward and the kettlebells resting on the outside of your forearms.
Lower the kettlebells back down to the starting position and repeat the movement with the opposite arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Alternate arms with a quick clean catching the bell with a strong grip.
coaching_cues: Hinge hips, let kettlebell swing back.
Pop hips forward, pull bell up.
Catch at shoulder, switch hands smoothly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your forearms gripping hard and your hips driving each rep. The catch should feel smooth not jarring on your wrist. Stay rhythmic and keep that grip strong.
