coach_guide.warning: Catching with a loose grip can strain your wrist badly.

name: kettlebell alternating press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height.
Press one kettlebell overhead, fully extending your arm.
Lower the kettlebell back to shoulder height.
Repeat with the other arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press one kettlebell overhead while keeping your core tight and stable.
coaching_cues: Stack wrist over elbow, brace core.
Press straight up, lock out strong.
Lower with control, switch sides.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders doing the pressing while your core prevents tilting. Each rep builds stability and overhead strength. Stay balanced and keep pressing.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back shifts stress onto your spine.

name: kettlebell alternating press on floor
instructions: Start by lying on your back on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a kettlebell in each hand, with your palms facing towards your feet and your arms extended straight up towards the ceiling.
Lower one kettlebell down towards your shoulder while keeping the other kettlebell extended straight up.
Press the lowered kettlebell back up to the starting position while simultaneously lowering the other kettlebell down towards your shoulder.
Continue alternating the press motion with each kettlebell for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press from the floor to isolate your chest with each alternating rep.
coaching_cues: Elbows at 45, back flat down.
Press one bell up, squeeze chest.
Lower slow, press the other side.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel each side of your chest working independently on every press. The floor limits your range so really focus on the squeeze at top. Keep alternating and feel the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring elbows out wide strains your shoulder joints.

name: kettlebell alternating renegade row
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands gripping the kettlebells and your feet hip-width apart.
Engage your core and keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Pull one kettlebell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position and repeat with the other arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold a strong plank while rowing one kettlebell at a time.
coaching_cues: Plank tight, hips square to floor.
Row bell to hip, no rotation.
Lower controlled, switch sides smoothly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back pulling while your core fights to stay still. If your hips twist you are going too heavy or too fast. Stay solid and row with purpose.
coach_guide.warning: Letting hips rotate puts twisting stress on your spine.

name: kettlebell alternating row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a kettlebell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull one kettlebell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position and repeat with the other arm.
Continue alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge forward and row alternating arms to build a strong upper back.
coaching_cues: Hinge hips, flat back position.
Pull elbow back, squeeze shoulder blade.
Lower with control, alternate arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats and upper back muscles doing all the work. Keep your lower back neutral and avoid rounding forward. Stay steady and pull strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back during rows risks lower back strain.

name: kettlebell arnold press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height with your palms facing towards you.
Engage your core and press the kettlebells overhead, rotating your palms to face forward as you extend your arms.
Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the kettlebells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate the kettlebells as you press to hit your shoulders from all angles.
coaching_cues: Start palms in at shoulder height.
Rotate outward as you press up.
Lower with control, palms return in.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel all three heads of your shoulders working through the rotation. This builds complete shoulder development and stability. Keep the motion smooth and controlled.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum instead of control can irritate shoulder joints.

name: kettlebell bent press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in your right hand.
Bend your knees slightly and press the kettlebell overhead with your right arm, keeping your elbow locked.
Rotate your torso to the left, shifting your weight onto your left foot.
Bend your left knee and lower your torso towards the ground, keeping your right arm extended overhead.
As you lower, keep your eyes on the kettlebell and your chest lifted.
Once your left hand touches the ground, push through your left foot and straighten your left leg, driving your hips forward.
As you drive your hips forward, use your core and right arm to press the kettlebell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: This advanced press requires total body coordination and core strength.
coaching_cues: Kettlebell overhead, lean into hip.
Push hips out, lower slowly down.
Drive through legs, press and stand.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques and entire core working to control this movement. Your shoulder must stay locked and stable throughout. Take your time and own every inch.
coach_guide.warning: Losing shoulder position under load can cause serious injury.

name: kettlebell bottoms up clean from the hang position
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand with an overhand grip.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Allow the kettlebell to hang down between your legs, with your arm fully extended.
In one fluid motion, explosively extend your hips and knees while pulling the kettlebell up towards your shoulder.
As the kettlebell reaches shoulder height, rotate your wrist so that the bottom of the kettlebell is facing up.
Catch the kettlebell at shoulder height with your elbow bent and your palm facing up.
Lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position by reversing the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Clean the kettlebell to a bottoms up position using grip strength and smooth hip drive.
coaching_cues: Squeeze the handle hard, wrist straight.
Pop hips forward, guide bell up.
Catch with bell inverted, elbow tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps and forearms working hard to stabilize that inverted bell. If the kettlebell keeps flipping over, slow down and grip tighter. Master this balance and your grip will thank you.
coach_guide.warning: A loose grip means the kettlebell will flip and crash into your wrist.

name: kettlebell double alternating hang clean
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand with an overhand grip.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Allow the kettlebells to hang straight down in front of your body.
In one fluid motion, explosively extend your hips and knees while shrugging your shoulders.
As the kettlebells rise, pull them up towards your shoulders, keeping your elbows high and out to the sides.
Catch the kettlebells at shoulder height, with your palms facing inward and your elbows pointing forward.
Lower the kettlebells back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Alternate cleaning two kettlebells from the hang position with a strong hip hinge.
coaching_cues: Hinge hips back, bells between knees.
Drive hips, clean one bell up.
Alternate smoothly, stay in rhythm.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps catch each bell while your hips power the movement. If your arms are burning out fast, use more hip drive. Keep that rhythm flowing and stay strong.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with your arms instead of your hips will drain your energy fast.

name: kettlebell double jerk
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height.
Bend your knees slightly and engage your core.
Press the kettlebells overhead, fully extending your arms.
Bend your knees and quickly drop into a partial squat.
Explosively extend your hips and knees, driving the kettlebells overhead.
Lock out your arms and catch the kettlebells overhead with your knees slightly bent.
Stand up straight and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive two kettlebells overhead using leg power and a quick dip under the bells.
coaching_cues: Dip knees, then explode upward fast.
Punch bells overhead, lock elbows out.
Catch with soft knees, stand tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders and legs working together as a powerful unit. If your lower back arches, tighten your core before each rep. This is total body power so own it.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: kettlebell double push press
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height.
Bend your knees slightly and engage your core.
Initiate the movement by explosively extending your hips, knees, and ankles, driving the kettlebells overhead.
As the kettlebells reach the top, press them fully overhead, locking out your arms.
Lower the kettlebells back to the starting position by bending your elbows and bringing them back down to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press two kettlebells overhead using leg drive to help power through the sticking point.
coaching_cues: Dip slightly, drive through your heels.
Press bells up as legs extend.
Lock out overhead, squeeze your glutes.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders burning while your legs give that helpful boost. If your wrists bend back, keep them stacked and strong. Push through and finish every rep tall.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your wrists bend backward strains the joint and weakens your press.

name: kettlebell double snatch
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand at arm's length in front of your thighs.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back flat and chest up.
In one explosive motion, extend your hips, knees, and ankles, and simultaneously pull the kettlebells up towards your shoulders.
As the kettlebells reach shoulder level, rotate your wrists and punch the kettlebells overhead, fully extending your arms.
Lower the kettlebells back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Snatch two kettlebells from the floor to overhead in one explosive movement.
coaching_cues: Hinge deep, bells between your legs.
Explode hips, pull bells high fast.
Punch through, lock out overhead strong.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders catch the bells while your hips do the heavy lifting. If the bells crash on your forearms, pull higher before punching through. This is athletic power at its best.
coach_guide.warning: Poor timing causes the kettlebells to slam painfully onto your forearms.

name: kettlebell double windmill
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in your right hand.
Extend your right arm overhead, keeping your eyes on the kettlebell.
Rotate your left foot 45 degrees to the left and your right foot 90 degrees to the right.
Bend at the waist to the left, keeping your right arm extended overhead.
Lower the kettlebell towards the ground, reaching towards your left foot.
Pause for a moment, then engage your core and push through your right foot to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Hold two kettlebells and hinge sideways while keeping your core braced and stable.
coaching_cues: Overhead arm locked, eyes on bell.
Hinge hip out, slide down slowly.
Brace your core, return with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques working hard to control the descent and return. If you feel wobbly, narrow your stance slightly for stability. Stay focused and own that side bend.
coach_guide.warning: Losing focus on the overhead bell can cause it to fall dangerously.

name: kettlebell extended range one arm press on floor
instructions: Start by lying on your back on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a kettlebell in one hand, with your palm facing towards your feet.
Extend your arm straight up towards the ceiling, keeping your elbow locked and your wrist straight.
Slowly lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Press the kettlebell from the floor with extended range to maximize chest activation.
coaching_cues: Lie flat, kettlebell at chest level.
Press up and across your body.
Lower deep, feel that chest stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your chest at the bottom of each rep. If your shoulder pops forward, keep it pinned to the floor. Embrace that extra range and grow stronger.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your shoulder lift off the floor puts your joint at risk.

name: kettlebell figure 8
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Pass the kettlebell between your legs, switching hands as it reaches the back of your legs.
As the kettlebell comes forward, pass it to the other hand between your legs.
Continue passing the kettlebell between your legs in a figure 8 motion.
Maintain a steady pace and engage your core throughout the exercise.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pass the kettlebell around and through your legs in a figure eight pattern.
coaching_cues: Stay low in an athletic stance.
Pass bell smoothly, hand to hand.
Keep your chest up, core tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs working to stabilize as the bell moves around you. If you stand too tall, you lose the core benefit so stay low. Keep it smooth and find your flow.
coach_guide.warning: Standing too upright turns this into an arm exercise and wastes your effort.

name: kettlebell front squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Hold the kettlebell with both hands in front of your chest, close to your body.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your hips down and back, as if sitting into a chair.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Drive through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold kettlebells in the rack position and squat deep with an upright torso.
coaching_cues: Elbows high, bells resting on chest.
Sit straight down, knees track toes.
Drive up through heels, stay tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads burning while your core keeps you upright. If you tip forward, focus on keeping those elbows high. Strong legs start with solid squats like this.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbows drop pulls your torso forward and strains your back.

name: kettlebell goblet squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell close to your chest with both hands.
Keeping your chest up and core engaged, lower your body down into a squat position by bending at the knees and hips.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold a kettlebell at your chest like a goblet and squat deep with control.
coaching_cues: Cup the bell, keep elbows inside.
Sink hips down, chest stays proud.
Push through heels, squeeze glutes up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads working hard through the full range. If your heels lift, sit back more and open your hips. This is your foundation squat so make it count.
coach_guide.warning: Heels lifting off the ground puts excess strain on your knees.

name: kettlebell hang clean
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in front of your thighs.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Lower the kettlebell towards the ground, allowing it to swing between your legs.
Quickly extend your hips and knees, using the momentum to swing the kettlebell up to shoulder height.
As the kettlebell reaches shoulder height, rotate your wrists and catch the kettlebell in the rack position, with your elbow tucked in and the kettlebell resting on your forearm.
Lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position between your legs, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge at your hips and use your legs to power the kettlebell up.
coaching_cues: Push hips back, keep chest tall.
Snap hips forward, pull bell close.
Catch soft at shoulder, absorb smoothly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your hamstrings load up during the hinge and fire as you stand. If your lower back is straining, you are not hinging deep enough. Trust your legs and keep building power.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with your arms instead of your hips causes lower back strain.

name: kettlebell lunge pass through
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in front of your chest with both hands.
Take a step forward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position.
As you lunge forward, pass the kettlebell under your right thigh and transfer it to your left hand.
Push off with your right foot to return to the starting position, while simultaneously passing the kettlebell back to your right hand.
Repeat the lunge on the opposite side, passing the kettlebell under your left thigh.
Continue alternating lunges and passing the kettlebell between hands for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Step into a deep lunge and pass the kettlebell under your front leg with control.
coaching_cues: Drop knee low, stay balanced upright.
Pass bell smoothly under front thigh.
Drive through front heel to stand.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes fire hard as you push out of the lunge. If your knee caves inward, reset your stance wider. Keep that glute burn going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the pass throws off your balance and stresses your knee.

name: kettlebell one arm clean and jerk
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand with an overhand grip.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips, lowering the kettlebell between your legs.
Explosively extend your hips, knees, and ankles, using the momentum to swing the kettlebell up to shoulder height.
As the kettlebell reaches shoulder height, rotate your wrist and punch your hand straight up, locking out your arm overhead.
Lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Clean the bell to your shoulder then drive it overhead in one powerful motion.
coaching_cues: Hinge and snap for the clean.
Dip knees slightly, punch bell up.
Lock out arm, stack over shoulder.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your delts working hard to stabilize overhead at the top. If your wrist bends back, focus on punching through the handle. You are building serious full body power.
coach_guide.warning: Pressing without the leg dip puts excessive stress on your shoulder.

name: kettlebell one arm floor press
instructions: Lie flat on your back on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Hold the kettlebell in one hand with your palm facing towards your feet and your arm extended straight up towards the ceiling.
Slowly lower the kettlebell towards your chest by bending your elbow, keeping your upper arm close to your body.
Pause for a moment when the kettlebell is just above your chest, then push it back up to the starting position by extending your elbow.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Lie flat and press the kettlebell straight up keeping your elbow close to your body.
coaching_cues: Plant feet, squeeze glutes for stability.
Lower bell slow, elbow near ribs.
Press up strong, lock elbow out.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest muscles engage through the entire pressing motion. If your shoulder rolls forward, pull it back into the floor. Own every rep and feel that chest working.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbow wide shifts strain onto your shoulder joint.

name: kettlebell one arm jerk
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand at shoulder height.
Bend your knees slightly and engage your core.
Press the kettlebell overhead in a straight line, fully extending your arm.
As you press the kettlebell overhead, simultaneously dip your knees and quickly straighten them to generate momentum.
As the kettlebell reaches its highest point, quickly drop underneath it by bending your knees and hips.
Catch the kettlebell with a slight bend in your knees and hips, and your arm fully extended overhead.
Stand up straight, fully extending your knees and hips, and stabilize the kettlebell overhead.
Lower the kettlebell back to the starting position by bending your knees and hips, and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use a quick dip and drive to punch the kettlebell overhead explosively.
coaching_cues: Quick shallow dip with knees.
Explode up, punch through the bell.
Lock arm straight, control the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your delts catch fire as you lock out each rep overhead. If the bell drifts forward, keep it close to your body. Stay explosive and keep that momentum rolling.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning back to compensate puts dangerous pressure on your spine.

name: kettlebell one arm military press to the side
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand at shoulder height.
Engage your core and keep your back straight.
Press the kettlebell overhead, extending your arm fully.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the kettlebell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Press the kettlebell out to the side with a straight arm path and full control.
coaching_cues: Start at shoulder, core braced tight.
Press out to side, arm straight.
Lower with control, shoulder stays down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your side delts doing all the lifting work throughout the press. If your trap is hiking up, relax your neck and reset. Keep those delts burning strong.
coach_guide.warning: Shrugging your shoulder up causes neck tension and reduces delt work.

name: kettlebell one arm push press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand at shoulder level.
Bend your knees slightly and engage your core.
Press the kettlebell overhead by extending your arm and fully extending your legs.
Lower the kettlebell back to the starting position by bending your knees and bringing the kettlebell back to your shoulder.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Dip your knees then drive up to help push the kettlebell overhead with leg power.
coaching_cues: Quick dip, knees over toes.
Drive legs and press simultaneously.
Finish tall, arm locked overhead.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your delts working with your legs to move the weight together. If the press feels all arms, use more leg drive. You are getting stronger every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Pressing without the dip overloads your shoulder with unnecessary strain.

name: kettlebell one arm row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand with an overhand grip.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the kettlebell up towards your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and repeat with the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge forward and pull the kettlebell toward your hip squeezing your back at the top.
coaching_cues: Flat back, hinge at the hips.
Pull elbow back, squeeze shoulder blade.
Lower controlled, keep core engaged.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles squeeze tight at the top of every row. If you feel it mostly in your arm, focus on leading with your elbow. Keep pulling strong and build that back.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back shifts the load onto your lower spine dangerously.

name: kettlebell one arm snatch
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand between your legs.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips, lowering the kettlebell towards the ground.
Explosively extend your hips and knees, using the momentum to swing the kettlebell up towards your shoulder.
As the kettlebell reaches shoulder height, rotate your hand and punch it straight up overhead, fully extending your arm.
Lower the kettlebell back down between your legs and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Swing the kettlebell and pull it overhead in one explosive fluid movement.
coaching_cues: Powerful hip snap to start.
Pull bell close, punch straight up.
Catch soft overhead, arm locked out.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your delts stabilize hard as you catch the bell overhead each rep. If your forearm gets banged, keep the bell closer on the pull. You are building explosive total body power.
coach_guide.warning: Letting the bell swing wide strains your shoulder and wrist.

name: kettlebell pirate supper legs
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Raise the kettlebell up to shoulder height, keeping your elbow close to your body.
Extend your arm fully overhead, straightening your elbow.
Lower the kettlebell back down to shoulder height, then return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Hold the kettlebell and lift one leg to challenge your shoulder and balance together.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, grip the bell tight.
Lift one leg, hold position steady.
Keep shoulder packed, core braced hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your delts working overtime to keep the bell stable while balancing. If you wobble, focus your eyes on one spot ahead. Stay strong and keep that balance sharp.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your shoulder rise up causes unnecessary neck and trap tension.

name: kettlebell pistol squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in front of your chest with both hands.
Lift your left foot off the ground and extend it forward, keeping it parallel to the ground.
Slowly lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your right foot flat on the ground and your left leg extended.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the squat, then push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

coach_guide.intro: Hold the kettlebell in front for balance and lower with control on one leg.
coaching_cues: Extend free leg forward, stay tall.
Sit back slowly, keep heel grounded.
Drive through heel, squeeze glute at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glute and quad of the working leg burning intensely. If you fall backward, lean slightly forward with the kettlebell. Stay patient and own each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your knee cave inward puts dangerous stress on the joint.

name: kettlebell plyo push-up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands on the kettlebells, shoulder-width apart.
Lower your chest towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Push through your hands explosively, lifting your hands off the kettlebells and extending your arms fully.
Land softly back on the kettlebells and immediately lower your chest back down for the next repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode off the kettlebells and land softly with your chest in control.
coaching_cues: Grip handles firm, core stays braced.
Push explosively, hands leave the bells.
Land soft, lower chest with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest and triceps firing with each explosive push. If your wrists hurt, make sure handles are stable. Keep that power coming strong.
coach_guide.warning: Landing with locked elbows can cause serious joint injury.

name: kettlebell seated press
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height, palms facing forward.
Press the kettlebells overhead, fully extending your arms.
Lower the kettlebells back to shoulder height.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit tall with your core tight and press the kettlebell straight overhead.
coaching_cues: Sit upright, no leaning back allowed.
Press straight up, bicep by ear.
Lower with control, elbow stays tucked.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulder muscles working hard at the top of each press. If your lower back arches, brace your core tighter. Keep pressing strong.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back transfers load to your spine dangerously.

name: kettlebell seated two arm military press
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder level with your palms facing forward.
Press the kettlebells overhead by extending your arms fully.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the kettlebells back to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold both kettlebells at shoulder height and press them overhead together.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, both bells at shoulders.
Press up together, lock arms overhead.
Lower slowly, keep your core engaged.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel both shoulders burning evenly through the movement. If one side takes over, focus on the weaker arm. Push through and stay balanced.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning back to lift heavier puts your lower back at risk.

name: kettlebell seesaw press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height.
Press one kettlebell overhead while keeping the other kettlebell at shoulder height.
Lower the pressed kettlebell back to shoulder height while simultaneously pressing the other kettlebell overhead.
Continue alternating the pressing motion, creating a seesaw-like movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Alternate pressing each kettlebell while one lowers as the other rises.
coaching_cues: Start both bells at shoulder height.
Press one up while lowering the other.
Keep rhythm smooth, core stays tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel each shoulder working independently through the alternating motion. If you lose balance, slow down the tempo. Stay controlled and keep moving.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement causes you to lose stability and form.

name: kettlebell sumo high pull
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing outwards.
Hold a kettlebell with both hands in front of your body, arms extended downwards.
Bend your knees and lower your hips into a squat position, keeping your back straight.
Drive through your heels and explosively extend your hips and knees, pulling the kettlebell up towards your chin.
As you pull the kettlebell up, keep your elbows high and wide, and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the kettlebell explosively from a wide stance up to chin height.
coaching_cues: Wide stance, toes pointed slightly out.
Hinge and snap hips to drive up.
Pull elbows high, kettlebell reaches chin.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your traps and hips working together explosively. If your lower back hurts, focus on the hip snap. Let that power flow upward.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with just your arms puts excessive strain on shoulders.

name: kettlebell swing
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointed slightly outward.
Hold the kettlebell with both hands in front of your body, arms extended.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips, pushing your butt back.
Swing the kettlebell back between your legs, keeping your arms straight and maintaining a flat back.
Drive your hips forward and swing the kettlebell up to shoulder height, using the momentum generated by your hips.
Allow the kettlebell to swing back down between your legs and repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge at your hips and snap them forward to swing the kettlebell.
coaching_cues: Hinge back, keep spine neutral always.
Snap hips hard, arms stay relaxed.
Float the bell to chest height.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and hamstrings powering every swing. If your back gets sore, hinge deeper at your hips. Let your hips do all the work.
coach_guide.warning: Using your arms to lift turns this into a back injury.

name: kettlebell thruster
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in front of your chest with both hands, palms facing each other.
Lower into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, keeping your chest up and your back straight.
As you reach the bottom of the squat, explosively drive through your heels to stand up, simultaneously pressing the kettlebell overhead.
Lock out your arms at the top of the movement, fully extending your elbows.
Lower the kettlebell back to the starting position by reversing the movement, bending your elbows and lowering the weight back to your chest.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Combine a squat with an overhead press in one powerful fluid movement.
coaching_cues: Squat deep, kettlebells at shoulders ready.
Stand explosively, press straight overhead.
Lower bells as you drop into squat.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your legs, shoulders, and lungs all working hard together. If you lose breath, control your pace better. This is total body power.
coach_guide.warning: Pressing before you fully stand puts your shoulders at risk.

name: kettlebell turkish get up (squat style)
instructions: Start by lying on your back with your legs extended and the kettlebell held in your right hand, arm fully extended above your shoulder.
Bend your right knee and place your right foot flat on the ground, keeping your left leg extended.
Pressing through your right foot, lift your hips off the ground, coming into a bridge position.
Slide your left leg underneath your body, bending your left knee and placing your left foot flat on the ground.
Rotate your torso to the left, bringing your left hand to the ground for support.
Pressing through your right foot and left hand, lift your torso off the ground, coming into a kneeling position.
From the kneeling position, stand up, keeping the kettlebell extended overhead.
Reverse the movement to return to the starting position.
Repeat the exercise on the other side, starting with the kettlebell in your left hand.

coach_guide.intro: Rise from the floor to standing using a squat while holding the kettlebell overhead.
coaching_cues: Keep arm locked, eyes on bell.
Roll to kneel, then squat stance.
Stand tall, reverse the movement down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire body stabilizing and working as one unit. If you lose the bell, keep your eyes locked on it. Trust the process and stay focused.
coach_guide.warning: Taking your eyes off the kettlebell leads to losing control.

name: kettlebell two arm clean
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in front of your thighs with both hands, palms facing towards you.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge at the hips, lowering the kettlebell towards the ground.
Explosively extend your hips and knees, using the momentum to pull the kettlebell up towards your shoulders.
As the kettlebell reaches shoulder height, rotate your wrists and catch the kettlebell in the rack position, with your elbows tucked in and the kettlebell resting on the back of your forearm.
Lower the kettlebell back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Swing both kettlebells between your legs and catch them at shoulder height.
coaching_cues: Hinge back, both bells swing through.
Snap hips, pull elbows back fast.
Catch soft at shoulders, stand tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your hips driving the power while your arms guide the bells. If they crash into your forearms, pull your elbows faster. Smooth power wins here.
coach_guide.warning: Catching with straight arms causes the bells to slam painfully.

name: kettlebell two arm military press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder level with your palms facing forward.
Engage your core and press the kettlebells overhead, fully extending your arms.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the kettlebells back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press both kettlebells overhead by driving through your shoulders and keeping your core tight.
coaching_cues: Rack kettlebells at shoulder height, elbows in.
Brace your core and press straight up.
Lock out arms, biceps near your ears.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders burning as you press and control the weight overhead. Your core should stay engaged throughout to protect your lower back. Own every rep and stay strong.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: kettlebell two arm row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a kettlebell in each hand with your palms facing your body.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the kettlebells up towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the kettlebells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge at your hips and pull both kettlebells toward your ribcage squeezing your back.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward, flat back, soft knees.
Pull kettlebells to your lower ribs tight.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles working hard on every pull. If you feel it mostly in your arms you are not squeezing your back enough. Keep pulling and build that strong back.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back during rows risks straining your lower spine.

name: kettlebell windmill
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in your right hand overhead.
Rotate your left foot outwards about 45 degrees and keep your right foot pointing forward.
Bend your torso to the left side, keeping your right arm extended overhead and your eyes on the kettlebell.
Lower your torso as far as you can while keeping your right arm straight and your left arm extended to the side.
Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position by pushing through your right foot and engaging your obliques.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Hold the kettlebell overhead while hinging sideways to strengthen your core and shoulders.
coaching_cues: Lock arm overhead, eyes on kettlebell.
Push hip out, slide hand down leg.
Keep legs straight, core braced throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your side and hamstrings while your core fires to stabilize. Your shoulder works hard to keep that kettlebell steady overhead. Move slowly and stay in control.
coach_guide.warning: Losing focus on the kettlebell overhead can cause it to fall.

name: kick out sit
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Lean back slightly and place your hands on the edge of the bench or chair for support.
Engaging your hamstrings, lift your feet off the ground and extend your legs straight out in front of you.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly bend your knees and bring your feet back towards your body.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Extend your legs out then pull them back in while sitting to fire up your hamstrings.
coaching_cues: Start seated, hands behind for support.
Kick legs straight out, squeeze hamstrings.
Pull knees back in with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your hamstrings engage as you extend and control your legs. Your core also works to keep you balanced and stable throughout. Keep the rhythm going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning too far back strains your lower back unnecessarily.

name: kipping muscle up
instructions: Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and use a swinging motion to generate momentum.
As you swing forward, pull your chest towards the bar, using your lats and biceps to initiate the movement.
Continue the upward motion until your chest reaches the bar, then transition into a dip position by pushing down on the bar and extending your arms.
Lower yourself back down to the starting position by bending your arms and controlling the descent.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use hip drive and momentum to pull yourself above the bar in one powerful motion.
coaching_cues: Swing hips forward, generate strong momentum.
Pull hard as hips rise up.
Transition fast, press out over bar.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats and entire upper body working explosively to get above the bar. The timing of your hip drive is everything in this movement. Stay aggressive and trust your power.
coach_guide.warning: Attempting without proper strength risks shoulder and elbow injury.

name: knee touch crunch
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, lift your shoulder blades off the ground and reach your right hand towards your left knee.
Return to the starting position and repeat, this time reaching your left hand towards your right knee.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Crunch up and reach your hands to touch your knees squeezing your abs hard.
coaching_cues: Lie back, knees bent, feet flat.
Crunch up and touch your knees.
Lower with control, keep abs engaged.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs working hard as you lift and reach toward your knees. Focus on the squeeze at the top rather than speed. Every rep brings you closer to stronger abs.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling on your neck takes tension away from your abs.

name: kneeling jump squat
instructions: Start by kneeling on the ground with your feet hip-width apart and your toes pointing forward.
Hold a barbell across your upper back, resting it on your shoulders.
Engage your core and glutes, then explosively jump up into the air, extending your hips and knees.
As you jump, push through your toes and fully extend your ankles, knees, and hips.
Land softly back on the ground, bending your knees to absorb the impact.
Immediately go into the next repetition, repeating the jump squat motion.

coach_guide.intro: Explode from a kneeling position into a squat stance using powerful glute drive.
coaching_cues: Start kneeling, barbell on upper back.
Explode hips forward, land in squat.
Absorb landing softly, glutes stay engaged.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes fire explosively as you jump from kneeling to standing. This is a power move so give it everything you have. Land soft and reset with confidence.
coach_guide.warning: Landing with stiff legs puts excessive impact on your knees.

name: kneeling lat stretch
instructions: Kneel on the ground with your knees hip-width apart and your toes pointing back.
Extend your arms overhead and interlace your fingers.
Keeping your back straight, slowly lean to the right side, feeling a stretch in your left lat muscle.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then return to the starting position.
Repeat the stretch on the left side, leaning to the left and feeling a stretch in your right lat muscle.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Kneel and reach forward to stretch your lats and improve upper body mobility.
coaching_cues: Kneel down and extend arms forward.
Sink hips back, press chest down.
Breathe deep and hold the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch running down the sides of your back. Let your breath help you sink deeper into the position. Relax into it and enjoy the release.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing the stretch too aggressively can strain your shoulders.

name: kneeling plank tap shoulder (male)
instructions: Start in a kneeling position with your hands on the ground, shoulder-width apart.
Extend your legs behind you, resting on your toes, and lift your body into a plank position.
Keeping your core engaged and your hips stable, lift one hand off the ground and tap the opposite shoulder.
Return the hand to the ground and repeat with the other hand.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold a kneeling plank while tapping each shoulder to challenge your core stability.
coaching_cues: Knees down, body straight, core tight.
Lift one hand, tap opposite shoulder.
Minimize hip rotation, stay controlled throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs and obliques working hard to keep your body from rotating. The slower you go the more your core has to fight. Stay steady and embrace the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sway side to side reduces core engagement.

name: kneeling push-up (male)
instructions: Start by kneeling on the ground with your hands shoulder-width apart, fingers pointing forward.
Extend your legs behind you, resting on the balls of your feet, so that your body forms a straight line from head to heels.
Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Continue lowering until your chest is just above the ground, then push back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Perform pushups from your knees to build chest strength with proper form.
coaching_cues: Knees down, hands under shoulders wide.
Lower chest toward floor, elbows back.
Push up strong, squeeze your chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest muscles working as you lower and press back up. This is a great way to build strength for full pushups. Keep pushing and you will get stronger every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag puts unnecessary strain on your back.

name: korean dips
instructions: Position yourself between two parallel bars with your arms extended and supporting your body weight.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lean forward and dip deep to maximize chest engagement.
coaching_cues: Grip behind you, lean your torso forward.
Lower until shoulders dip below your elbows.
Press up strong, squeeze your chest hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and burn across your entire chest. If your shoulders are screaming, lean forward more and reduce depth. Keep pushing through that chest burn.
coach_guide.warning: Staying too upright puts dangerous stress on your shoulder joints.

name: l-pull-up
instructions: Grab the pull-up bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Hang with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Engage your lats and biceps to pull your body up towards the bar, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your legs straight out in front while you pull your chin over the bar.
coaching_cues: Legs parallel to floor, core locked tight.
Pull elbows down and back toward hips.
Chin clears bar, control the way down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats working overtime while your abs fight to hold your legs up. If your legs are dropping, focus on that core brace. This is elite level work.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging or kipping removes the strength benefit entirely.

name: l-sit on floor
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended in front of you.
Place your hands on the floor beside your hips, fingers pointing forward.
Engage your core and lift your legs off the ground, keeping them straight.
Try to bring your legs parallel to the floor, forming an 'L' shape with your body.
Hold this position for as long as you can.
Slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press the floor away and lift your legs while keeping them perfectly straight.
coaching_cues: Hands flat, arms locked, shoulders pressed down.
Lift legs until parallel, point your toes.
Breathe steady, hold position with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your hip flexors and abs burning intensely while your triceps work to keep you elevated. Shaking is normal and means you are building strength. Stay with it.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back collapses the position and strains your spine.

name: landmine 180
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the barbell with both hands in front of your chest.
Bend your knees slightly and rotate your torso to the right, swinging the barbell down towards your right hip.
As you reach the bottom of the movement, quickly reverse the motion and rotate your torso to the left, swinging the barbell up and across your body towards your left shoulder.
Continue this twisting motion, alternating sides, for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate the barbell in a wide arc using your core not your arms.
coaching_cues: Feet wide, hips low, arms extended out.
Rotate from hip to hip in controlled arc.
Core drives the movement, arms just guide.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques and entire core working through each rotation. If your lower back is straining, sit deeper into your hips. Control the arc and own each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Using arm momentum cheats your core out of the work.

name: landmine lateral raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, resting it on the front of your shoulders.
Keeping your core engaged and back straight, lift the barbell up and away from your body, raising it to shoulder height.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Grip the barbell end and raise it out to the side with a straight arm.
coaching_cues: Stand at barbell end, slight forward lean.
Raise arm to shoulder height, controlled tempo.
Lower with control, do not let it drop.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn right in the side of your shoulder on every rep. If your traps are taking over, lower the weight and focus on that lateral delt. Keep those shoulders burning.
coach_guide.warning: Jerking the weight up can strain your rotator cuff badly.

name: lean planche
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart and your body straight.
Engage your core and slowly shift your weight forward, bringing your shoulders past your hands.
Keep your elbows slightly bent and your body straight as you lean forward.
Hold this position for a few seconds, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lean forward in a plank with hands by your hips to load your shoulders and core.
coaching_cues: Hands by hips, fingers pointed toward feet.
Lean shoulders far forward past your wrists.
Hold tight, breathe steady, do not collapse.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel intense pressure in your shoulders and a strong core engagement. If your wrists hurt, lean back slightly and build up gradually. You are training serious skill here.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning too far too soon can injure your wrists.

name: left hook. boxing
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Keep your left hand up to protect your face and your right hand by your chin.
Rotate your hips and pivot on your left foot as you extend your left arm forward in a punching motion.
Twist your torso and engage your core muscles to generate power in the punch.
Snap your arm back to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate through your hips and core to throw a powerful left hook.
coaching_cues: Pivot on left foot, rotate hips explosively.
Arm stays bent at ninety degrees throughout.
Snap back to guard, stay light on feet.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the power coming from your hip rotation and core twist not just your arm. If your shoulder is fatiguing fast, you are muscling it. Let your whole body throw that punch.
coach_guide.warning: Locking your elbow turns the hook into a dangerous arm swing.

name: leg pull in flat bench
instructions: Sit on a flat bench with your legs extended straight out in front of you.
Place your hands on the bench beside your hips for support.
Engage your abs and lift your legs off the ground, bringing your knees towards your chest.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie flat and pull your knees in toward your chest then extend them back out.
coaching_cues: Grip bench edges, lower back stays flat.
Pull knees to chest, squeeze your abs.
Extend legs out slow, do not touch down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs working hard through the entire range of motion. If your back is arching, shorten the range and keep that lower back pressed down. Strong finish ahead.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back transfers stress to your spine.

name: leg up hamstring stretch
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended.
Bend one knee and bring it towards your chest, holding onto your thigh or shin.
Straighten your leg as much as possible while keeping it elevated.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Repeat with the other leg.

coach_guide.intro: Raise one leg and gently pull it toward you to stretch your hamstring.
coaching_cues: Lie flat, raise one leg straight up.
Hold calf or thigh, pull leg gently closer.
Keep other leg flat, breathe into stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep but comfortable stretch down the back of your raised leg. If it feels sharp or painful, ease off and let the muscle relax into it. Patience brings flexibility.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too aggressively can strain your hamstring muscle.

name: lever alternate leg press
instructions: Adjust the seat and foot platform of the leverage machine to your desired position.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet on the foot platform.
Place your hands on the handles or sides of the machine for stability.
Push one foot against the foot platform, extending your leg until it is almost fully straight.
Pause for a moment, then slowly bend your leg and return to the starting position.
Repeat with the other leg.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press one leg at a time to build balanced strength in each quad.
coaching_cues: Foot centered on plate, knee tracks over toes.
Press through heel until leg is almost straight.
Lower with control, feel the quad stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel each quad working independently through the full press. If one side feels much weaker, start with that leg each set. Balance your strength one rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Locking your knee at the top stresses the joint.

name: lever alternating narrow grip seated row
instructions: Adjust the seat height and footplate position to ensure proper alignment.
Sit on the machine with your back straight and feet flat on the footplate.
Grasp the handles with a narrow grip, palms facing each other.
Keep your chest up and shoulders back throughout the exercise.
Pull one handle towards your torso while keeping the other handle stationary.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement.
Slowly return the handle to the starting position and repeat with the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Alternate arms to focus on each side of your upper back independently.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, chest proud, core braced tight.
Pull one handle back, squeeze shoulder blade.
Control the return, then switch sides smoothly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel each side of your upper back working separately with every pull. If one side feels weaker, focus on that connection. Even out your strength one rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating your torso steals tension from your back muscles.

name: lever assisted chin-up
instructions: Adjust the leverage machine to your desired resistance level.
Stand on the foot platform and grip the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Hang with your arms fully extended, keeping your body straight.
Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the handles, leading with your chest.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the handles.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the assistance to build your pulling strength until you own the movement.
coaching_cues: Grip firm, shoulders pulled down and back.
Drive elbows down toward your hips now.
Chin clears the bar, lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats doing the heavy lifting from armpit to ribs. If your biceps burn out first, focus on leading with your elbows. Build that back strength rep by rep.
coach_guide.warning: Kipping or swinging removes the muscle building benefit entirely.

name: lever back extension
instructions: Adjust the machine to fit your body size and range of motion.
Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and your feet secured.
Place your hands on the handles or grip bars.
Engage your core and slowly lean forward, allowing your back to round slightly.
Pause for a moment at the bottom position, feeling a stretch in your lower back.
Using your back muscles, slowly raise your torso back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use your back muscles to return to upright, don't just rock back and forth.
coaching_cues: Engage your back to come up.
Control the forward lean completely.
Feel the stretch, then squeeze back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a good stretch in your lower back at the bottom, then your back muscles working to bring you up. If it's all momentum, slow it down. Stay with that controlled back engagement.
coach_guide.warning: Don't use momentum or bouncing to help you up.

name: lever bent over row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Pull the barbell towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge at the hips and row the bar into your belly button.
coaching_cues: Flat back, hinge forward, knees slightly bent.
Pull the bar to your lower stomach area.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire upper back light up with each row. If your lower back aches, raise your torso angle slightly. Stay strong and keep pulling.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: lever bent-over row with v-bar
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself facing the machine.
Grasp the v-bar with an overhand grip, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Pull the v-bar towards your abdomen, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the weight back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull that bar to your belly button and squeeze those shoulder blades hard.
coaching_cues: Pull to your lower ribs.
Squeeze shoulder blades together tight.
Control the negative slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this working your entire upper back, especially between your shoulder blades. If it's all in your arms, focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together first. Keep going with that upper back fire.
coach_guide.warning: Pull too high toward your chest and you're missing the target.

name: lever bicep curl
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
Grasp the handles with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and keep your elbows close to your sides.
Exhale and curl the handles upward, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let the machine guide you — focus on squeezing your biceps at the top.
coaching_cues: Back pressed into the pad.
Curl up, squeeze at the peak.
Slow negative, full extension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel isolated tension right in your biceps with no cheating possible. The machine keeps you honest — embrace that controlled burn and keep pushing through it.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning forward off the pad lets momentum take over.

name: lever calf press
instructions: Adjust the seat of the leverage machine so that your shoulders are aligned with the lever pad.
Place your toes on the lever pad, with your heels hanging off the edge.
Grasp the handles or side supports for stability.
Push the lever pad down by extending your ankles, contracting your calf muscles.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement.
Slowly return to the starting position by allowing your heels to rise back up.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press through the balls of your feet to build powerful calves.
coaching_cues: Position feet hip width on the platform.
Press toes away, extend ankles fully now.
Lower heels slowly for a deep stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong burn through both calf muscles at the peak. If only one calf works harder, even out your foot pressure. Push through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom risks straining your Achilles tendon.

name: lever chest press
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back flat against the pad.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Push the handles forward until your arms are fully extended, exhaling during the movement.
Pause briefly at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position, inhaling as you do so.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press forward with your chest muscles while keeping your back flat.
coaching_cues: Sit back, shoulder blades pinned to pad.
Push handles forward, squeeze chest muscles hard.
Return with control, keep constant tension on.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest doing all the pushing work throughout the movement. If your shoulders take over, lower the seat slightly. Own every rep with your chest.
coach_guide.warning: Letting shoulders roll forward strains the front of the joint.

name: lever chest press
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back flat against the pad.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Push the handles forward until your arms are fully extended, exhaling during the movement.
Pause briefly at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position, inhaling as you do so.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Chest forward, shoulders back — drive through your chest, not your arms.
coaching_cues: Chest up, shoulders pinned back.
Push through your chest muscles.
Control back, explode forward.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this firing across your entire chest with your shoulders staying stable. If your shoulders are doing all the work, reset your posture and focus on chest engagement. Keep that chest connection strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your shoulders roll forward turns this into a shoulder exercise.

name: lever deadlift
instructions: Adjust the seat height and foot platform to your desired position.
Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and your feet flat on the foot platform.
Grasp the handles or the sides of the seat for stability.
Engage your glutes and hamstrings, and push through your heels to lift the weight up.
Keep your back straight and avoid rounding your shoulders.
Extend your hips fully at the top of the movement, squeezing your glutes.
Lower the weight back down in a controlled manner, keeping tension on your glutes and hamstrings.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive through your heels and squeeze those glutes at the top.
coaching_cues: Heels down, core stays braced.
Drive hips forward, full extension.
Squeeze glutes hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: Your glutes and hamstrings should be firing hard right now. If you feel it creeping into your back, really focus on that heel drive. Lock into that deep glute burn and power through.
coach_guide.warning: Keep your back pressed against the pad throughout the movement.

name: lever decline chest press
instructions: Adjust the seat height and backrest of the leverage machine to a comfortable position.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Push the handles forward and away from your body until your arms are fully extended.
Slowly lower the handles back towards your chest, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The decline angle shifts the focus to your lower chest fibers.
coaching_cues: Press back into the pad, grip firm.
Drive handles up and together with chest.
Lower slow, feel the stretch in lower chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the work right along the bottom edge of your pecs. If your triceps fatigue first, focus on squeezing your chest harder. Build that lower chest definition.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back off the pad removes the decline benefit.

name: lever donkey calf raise
instructions: Adjust the leverage machine to the appropriate height for your body.
Position yourself facing the machine, with your toes on the foot platform and your heels hanging off the edge.
Place your hands on the handles or the support bars for stability.
Engage your calves and lift your heels as high as possible, using the balls of your feet.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The donkey position loads your calves with a powerful deep stretch.
coaching_cues: Hips under the pad, back flat, hold.
Rise up high onto the balls of feet.
Lower heels deep, feel the full stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense stretch and contraction through your entire calf. If your lower back rounds, reposition your hips under the pad. Embrace the deep burn.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your knees shifts work away from your calves.

name: lever front pulldown
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your knees under the pads and your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Sit upright with your chest lifted and your shoulders back, maintaining a slight arch in your lower back.
Engage your lats and pull the handles down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the bar down in front to build wide powerful lats.
coaching_cues: Grip wide, lean back slightly, chest up.
Pull elbows down and back toward ribs.
Squeeze lats hard, return bar with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats flare and contract with every single pull. If your forearms give out first, focus on pulling with your elbows. Build that V-taper strong.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling behind your neck puts your shoulders in a risky position.

name: lever gripless shrug
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the leverage machine.
Grasp the handles with your palms facing inwards and your arms fully extended.
Keeping your back straight, exhale and elevate your shoulders as high as possible.
Hold the contraction for a brief moment, then inhale and slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let the pads do the gripping so you focus purely on your traps.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, position shoulders under the pads.
Shrug straight up toward your ears now.
Hold the squeeze, lower shoulders with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your traps bunch up powerfully at the top of each shrug. If your neck strains, keep your head neutral and relaxed. Build those trap peaks.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling your shoulders adds no benefit and risks joint strain.

name: lever gripless shrug v. 2
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
Grasp the handles or bars with an overhand grip, keeping your arms straight.
Keeping your back straight, lift your shoulders up towards your ears as high as possible.
Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This variation lets you isolate your traps without grip fatigue.
coaching_cues: Set shoulders snug under the lever pads.
Drive shoulders straight up, squeeze traps hard.
Lower with control, maintain tall upright posture.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your traps working from your neck down to mid back. If tension fades between reps, shorten your range slightly. Keep those traps firing strong.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your elbows turns this into a different movement entirely.

name: lever gripper hands
instructions: Adjust the seat height and grip the handles of the leverage machine.
Keep your back straight and your feet flat on the ground.
Exhale and squeeze the handles, contracting your forearms.
Hold the contraction for a second, then slowly release and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Squeeze the handles with full grip strength and control the release.
coaching_cues: Wrap all fingers firmly around the handles.
Squeeze hard and hold for one second.
Release slowly, do not let it snap open.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn through your forearms and into your grip. Your fingers and wrists will fatigue quickly and that means it is working. Embrace that burn and build crushing grip strength.
coach_guide.warning: Releasing too fast can strain your finger tendons.

name: lever hammer grip preacher curl
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the leverage machine.
Place your upper arms on the preacher pad, ensuring your chest is pressed against it.
Grasp the handles with a hammer grip (palms facing each other).
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the handles towards your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your palms facing each other and curl with your biceps not your shoulders.
coaching_cues: Lock your upper arms against the pad.
Curl up with palms facing each other throughout.
Lower slowly, keeping constant bicep tension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working hard especially near the peak contraction. The hammer grip also recruits your forearms so expect them to burn too. Keep squeezing at the top for maximum growth.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your elbows off the pad shifts work to your shoulders.

name: lever high row
instructions: Adjust the seat height and foot platform to a comfortable position.
Sit on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the foot platform.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your elbows back and squeeze your shoulder blades together at the bottom.
coaching_cues: Sit tall with chest pressed into pad.
Drive elbows down and back, not out wide.
Squeeze shoulder blades together, hold one second.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles working between your shoulder blades. A strong contraction at the bottom means you are hitting the right muscles. Keep that chest proud and row with power.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your shoulders forward removes tension from your back.

name: lever hip extension v. 2
instructions: Adjust the machine to fit your body and sit on it with your back against the backrest.
Place your feet on the footrests and grip the handles for stability.
Engage your glutes and hamstrings, then push against the footrests to extend your hips and raise your legs backward.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press through your heel and squeeze your glute hard at full extension.
coaching_cues: Keep your core tight and back flat.
Push through your heel, not your toes.
Squeeze your glute hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glute doing all the work on each rep. If you feel it mostly in your lower back, you are overextending. Focus on that glute squeeze and build a stronger backside.
coach_guide.warning: Hyperextending your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: lever horizontal one leg press
instructions: Adjust the seat of the machine so that your knees are at a 90-degree angle when your feet are on the footplate.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet shoulder-width apart on the footplate.
Place your hands on the handles or sides of the machine for stability.
Push the footplate away from your body by extending your leg, keeping your back against the backrest.
Pause for a moment at the fully extended position, then slowly bend your leg to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

coach_guide.intro: Drive through one leg at a time focusing on glute power and control.
coaching_cues: Place one foot centered on the platform.
Press through your heel, extend your hip fully.
Lower with control, keep your knee tracking straight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glute and quad burning on the working leg. The single leg focus reveals any strength imbalances between sides. Push through the challenge and build balanced lower body power.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your knee cave inward stresses your knee joint.

name: lever incline chest press
instructions: Adjust the seat and backrest of the leverage machine to a comfortable position.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Push the handles forward and away from your body until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment, then slowly bend your elbows and lower the handles back towards your chest.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press at an angle to target your upper chest while keeping shoulders down.
coaching_cues: Pin your shoulder blades back against seat.
Press handles up and together, exhale hard.
Lower slowly until you feel a chest stretch.
