coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper chest and front shoulders working together. The incline angle shifts focus to the upper pecs for balanced chest development. Keep pushing and build that upper chest shelf.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your shoulders roll forward strains the front deltoids.

name: lever incline chest press v. 2
instructions: Adjust the seat height and backrest angle on the leverage machine to a comfortable position.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Push the handles forward and away from your body until your arms are fully extended, but without locking your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the fully extended position, then slowly bend your elbows and lower the handles back towards your chest.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Focus on squeezing your upper chest at the top of each press.
coaching_cues: Keep your back flat against the pad.
Drive the handles up with your chest.
Squeeze your pecs together at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction across your upper chest at lockout. Your shoulders assist but should not take over the movement. Stay focused on that chest squeeze every single rep.
coach_guide.warning: Arching excessively off the pad removes upper chest activation.

name: lever kneeling leg curl
instructions: Adjust the machine to fit your body and select the desired weight.
Kneel on the machine facing downwards, with your knees resting on the pad and your feet secured under the footpads.
Grasp the handles or the sides of the machine for stability.
Keeping your upper body stationary, exhale and curl your legs up towards your glutes by flexing your knees.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your hamstrings.
Inhale and slowly lower your legs back to the starting position, fully extending your knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl your heels to your glutes with control and squeeze at the top.
coaching_cues: Curl heels up, squeeze at top.
Lower slow, feel the stretch.
Keep hips down, core tight.
coach_guide.feel: Your hamstrings should be cramping up in the best way right now. If you feel it in your back, press your hips down into the pad more. Squeeze through that hamstring burn with every curl.
coach_guide.warning: Don't let your hips lift off the pad during the curl.

name: lever kneeling twist
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your knees resting on the pads and your upper body facing forward.
Grasp the handles or the sides of the machine for stability.
Keeping your core engaged, twist your torso to one side as far as comfortably possible.
Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat the twist to the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate from your core while keeping your hips completely still.
coaching_cues: Sit tall and brace your core tight.
Rotate using only your obliques, not momentum.
Control the return, do not let it swing.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques burning on both sides of your waist. This rotational work builds a strong and stable core. Stay controlled and let your abs do all the work.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum instead of muscle can strain your lower back.

name: lever lateral raise
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and keep your arms straight.
Exhale and raise your arms out to the sides until they are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise your arms out to the sides leading with your elbows not your hands.
coaching_cues: Keep a slight bend in your elbows.
Lead with your elbows, raise to shoulder height.
Lower slowly, resist the weight down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your side delts burning on every rep. If your traps take over, you are shrugging too much. Keep those shoulders down and build boulder shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Raising above shoulder height can impinge your shoulder joint.

name: lever leg extension
instructions: Adjust the seat height and backrest of the machine to fit your body.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet on the footpad.
Grasp the handles or sidebars for stability.
Extend your legs forward by straightening your knees, lifting the weight.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the weight back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press through your heels and squeeze your quads hard at the top of every rep.
coaching_cues: Press through your heels, not toes.
Squeeze quads hard at the top.
Lower the weight with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn right in the front of your thighs now. If you feel it in your knees instead, make sure you're pressing through your heels. Lock in on that quad fire and keep pushing through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Pressing through your toes puts dangerous stress on your knee joints.

name: lever lying leg curl
instructions: Adjust the machine to fit your body and select the desired weight.
Lie face down on the machine with your legs straight and your heels against the padded lever.
Grasp the handles or the sides of the machine for stability.
Keeping your upper body stationary, exhale and curl your legs up as far as possible without lifting your hips off the pad.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your hamstrings.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your hips glued to the pad and squeeze those hamstrings hard at the top.
coaching_cues: Hips stay down, heels drive up.
Squeeze and pause at the top.
Control the weight back down slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn deep in the back of your thighs right now. If you feel it in your lower back, press your hips down harder into the pad. Lock onto that hamstring burn and keep going with that fire.
coach_guide.warning: The second your hips lift off the pad, you've lost the hamstring focus.

name: lever lying two-one leg curl
instructions: Adjust the machine to fit your body and sit on it with your back against the backrest.
Place your legs on the lever pad, just above your ankles.
Grasp the handles on the sides of the machine for support.
Keeping your upper body still, exhale and curl your legs up towards your glutes.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lift with both legs then lower slowly with just one for extra hamstring work.
coaching_cues: Curl both legs up together with control.
Lower down using only one leg slowly.
Keep your hips pressed flat into the pad.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in the hamstring of the lowering leg. This eccentric focus builds serious strength and muscle. Embrace the slow lowering and grow stronger hamstrings.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your hips shifts stress to your lower back.

name: lever military press
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the backrest.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Push the handles upward until your arms are fully extended, but do not lock your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive straight up from shoulder level — keep your back flat against the pad.
coaching_cues: Press straight up, not forward.
Back stays flat against the pad.
Control the weight on the way down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this working your front and middle delts with solid support from the machine. If you feel it in your neck or upper back, adjust the seat height. Keep that controlled shoulder burn going through every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Pressing forward instead of straight up will stress your shoulder joint unnecessarily.

name: lever narrow grip seated row
instructions: Adjust the seat height and footrests to ensure proper form.
Sit on the machine with your feet flat on the footrests and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the handles with a narrow grip, palms facing each other.
Keep your back straight and lean slightly forward.
Pull the handles towards your torso, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement.
Slowly release the handles and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your grip narrow and pull through your elbows to target your upper back.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, chest lifted, core braced tight.
Pull elbows straight back, squeeze shoulder blades.
Control the return, do not let it snap.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep squeeze between your shoulder blades at the end of each pull. If your biceps are burning more than your back, focus on leading with your elbows. Keep that posture proud and strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: lever one arm bent over row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a barbell with an overhand grip.
Bend forward at the hips, keeping your back straight and your head up.
Let the barbell hang in front of you with your arms fully extended.
Pull the barbell up towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
Lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive your elbow high and squeeze your back at the top of every rep.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward, keep your back flat and strong.
Row the bar to your hip, elbow high.
Lower with control, feel the stretch below.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel one side of your upper back working hard with each pull. If you feel twisting through your torso, brace your core harder. Own each rep and build that strength.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating your torso steals tension from your back muscles.

name: lever one arm lateral high row
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself facing the machine.
Grasp the handle with one hand and keep your back straight.
Pull the handle towards your body, keeping your elbow close to your side.
Squeeze your back muscles at the top of the movement.
Slowly release the handle back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull out and up to hit the outer portions of your upper back.
coaching_cues: Grip the handle, sit tall with chest up.
Pull elbow high and wide, squeeze hard.
Return slowly, maintain constant tension throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the muscles along the outer edge of your upper back firing. If your traps are dominating, lower your shoulder away from your ear. You are building a strong and balanced back.
coach_guide.warning: Shrugging up will shift the work to your traps.

name: lever one arm lateral wide pulldown
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handle with an overhand grip and fully extend your arm, keeping a slight bend in your elbow.
Pull the handle down and across your body towards your opposite hip, squeezing your lat muscle at the bottom of the movement.
Slowly return the handle to the starting position, fully extending your arm.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Pull wide and drive your elbow down to fully engage your lat.
coaching_cues: Sit upright, reach up and grip wide.
Pull elbow down toward your hip firmly.
Squeeze your lat hard, then control up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction down the side of your back under your armpit. If you feel it mostly in your arm, think about pulling through your elbow. Keep building that wide powerful back.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning too far back turns this into a row.

name: lever one arm shoulder press
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
Grasp the lever handle with one hand and position your elbow at a 90-degree angle.
Press the lever upward until your arm is fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the lever back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Press straight up and keep your core tight to protect your shoulder.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, grip the handle at shoulder height.
Press up, fully extend without locking out.
Lower with control, stop at shoulder level.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the front and side of your shoulder doing the work on each press. If your lower back is arching, squeeze your abs tighter. Strong shoulders are on their way.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back puts stress on your lower spine.

name: lever overhand triceps dip
instructions: Adjust the machine to the appropriate height and secure your body in position.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your body so that your arms are fully extended.
Lower your body by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms close to your sides.
Continue lowering until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by extending your elbows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use an overhand grip and keep your elbows tight to isolate your triceps.
coaching_cues: Grip handles overhand, elbows pinned to sides.
Press down, squeeze triceps hard at bottom.
Return slowly, do not let elbows flare.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the back of your upper arms burning with every rep. If your shoulders are working too hard, keep those elbows locked in position. Push through and feel those triceps grow.
coach_guide.warning: Letting elbows drift forward reduces triceps activation significantly.

name: lever preacher curl
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the leverage machine.
Place your upper arms on the pad and grip the handles with an underhand grip.
Keep your back straight and your elbows positioned on the pad.
Exhale and curl your forearms towards your upper arms, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let the pad lock your arms in place so your biceps do all the work.
coaching_cues: Pin upper arms firmly against the pad.
Curl up, squeeze biceps hard at top.
Lower slowly, keep constant tension throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense squeeze in your biceps at the top of each curl. If your shoulders are creeping forward, press your chest into the pad. Those biceps are getting stronger every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your elbows off the pad reduces biceps isolation.

name: lever preacher curl v. 2
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your upper arms resting on the pad and your chest against the support.
Grasp the handles with an underhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your upper arms stationary and exhale as you curl the handles towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale as you slowly lower the handles back to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Focus on a full range of motion and squeeze hard at the peak.
coaching_cues: Arms locked on pad, grip the handles.
Curl up smoothly, pause and squeeze hard.
Lower under control, feel the full stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working from full stretch to peak contraction. If you are swinging or rushing, slow it down and own the movement. You are doing great work.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum cheats your biceps out of growth.

name: lever pullover
instructions: Adjust the seat and handles of the leverage machine to a comfortable position.
Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and grasp the handles with an overhand grip.
Keep your arms slightly bent and your core engaged.
Slowly pull the handles towards your chest, squeezing your lats.
Pause for a moment at the peak contraction, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Reach overhead and pull through your lats to stretch and squeeze.
coaching_cues: Grip handles overhead, keep slight elbow bend.
Pull down in an arc, squeeze your lats.
Return slowly, feel the deep lat stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a big stretch in your lats at the top and a strong squeeze at the bottom. If your chest is taking over, focus on pulling with your back. Enjoy that deep stretch and contraction.
coach_guide.warning: Using too much weight causes you to bend your elbows excessively.

name: lever reverse grip lateral pulldown
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your knees under the pads and your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Sit upright with your chest out and shoulders back, maintaining a slight arch in your lower back.
Pull the handles down towards your chest, leading with your elbows and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The underhand grip lets you pull deeper and hit your lower lats.
coaching_cues: Grip underhand, sit tall with chest proud.
Pull elbows down and back toward hips.
Squeeze lats hard, return with full control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower lats and the area near your armpits working hard. If your biceps fatigue first, focus on driving your elbows down not back. Keep squeezing and building that back.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with your arms first limits lat engagement.

name: lever reverse grip preacher curl
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the leverage machine.
Grasp the handles with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Rest your upper arms on the preacher pad, ensuring your elbows are fully extended.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the handles towards your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the handles back to the starting position, fully extending your elbows.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The reverse grip targets your forearms and brachialis for complete arm development.
coaching_cues: Lock elbows firmly against the preacher pad.
Curl with palms facing down, squeeze hard.
Lower slowly, feel the stretch at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your outer biceps and forearms. If your wrists hurt, lighten the weight and focus on control. Keep grinding through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Jerking the weight puts excessive strain on your wrists and elbows.

name: lever reverse grip vertical row
instructions: Adjust the seat height and footplate position to ensure proper alignment.
Sit on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the footplate.
Grasp the handles with an underhand grip, palms facing up.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Pull the handles towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release and extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This reverse grip row emphasizes your lower lats and biceps together.
coaching_cues: Grip handles with palms facing up firmly.
Pull elbows straight back, squeeze your lats.
Control the return, keep tension throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower lats working hard with each pull. If you feel it mostly in your arms, focus on leading with your elbows. Own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back removes lat engagement and risks injury.

name: lever reverse hyperextension
instructions: Adjust the leverage machine to fit your body and secure your feet in the foot pads.
Lie face down on the machine with your upper body hanging off the edge and your hips resting on the pad.
Cross your arms over your chest or place them behind your head.
Engage your glutes and hamstrings to lift your legs upward until they are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This move isolates your glutes and hamstrings through a full hip extension.
coaching_cues: Keep your core braced against the pad.
Lift legs by squeezing glutes, not swinging.
Pause at top, lower with full control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in your glutes at the top. If your lower back takes over, reduce the range of motion. Build that glute strength.
coach_guide.warning: Hyperextending your lower back shifts stress away from glutes.

name: lever reverse t-bar row
instructions: Adjust the seat height and footplate position on the leverage machine.
Sit on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the footplate.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Pull the handles towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release and extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The reverse grip angle hits your lower lats and adds bicep involvement.
coaching_cues: Chest stays pressed firmly on the pad.
Pull the handles toward your hips, squeeze.
Lower under control, stretch those lats fully.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower lats and mid back firing together. If your biceps fatigue first, focus on pulling through your elbows. Keep that back engaged.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your chest off the pad reduces lat activation significantly.

name: lever rotary calf
instructions: Adjust the seat height so that your knees are slightly bent and your feet are flat on the footplate.
Place your toes on the footplate, with your heels hanging off the edge.
Grasp the handles or the sides of the machine for stability.
Push through the balls of your feet, raising your heels as high as possible.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This rotary machine isolates your calves through a smooth circular motion.
coaching_cues: Push through the balls of your feet.
Squeeze hard at the top of contraction.
Lower slowly, get a full deep stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in both calf muscles. If one side dominates, focus on pressing evenly through both feet. Push through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing through reps removes tension and limits calf growth.

name: lever seated calf press
instructions: Adjust the seat of the machine so that your shoulders are aligned with the lever pad.
Place your toes on the lower portion of the platform and position your knees under the lever pad.
Grasp the handles on the sides of the seat for stability.
Press the lever pad down by extending your ankles, lifting your heels as high as possible.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Seated calf presses build thick calves through a loaded stretch position.
coaching_cues: Press through your toes, not your heels.
Hold the squeeze at full extension briefly.
Lower deep, feel the stretch in calves.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your calves stretching and contracting fully each rep. If you feel knee discomfort, adjust your foot placement on the platform. Keep pressing strong.
coach_guide.warning: Using too much weight limits your range of motion significantly.

name: lever seated calf raise
instructions: Adjust the seat height so that your knees are slightly bent and your feet are flat on the footplate.
Place your toes on the footplate with your heels hanging off the edge.
Grasp the handles or the sides of the seat for stability.
Push through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive through the balls of your feet — get maximum range and squeeze hard at the top.
coaching_cues: Push through balls of your feet.
Full range, heel to toe.
Squeeze hard at the peak.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this burning deep in your calves with a strong stretch at the bottom and powerful squeeze at the top. Focus on that full range of motion and keep that calf burn going.
coach_guide.warning: Not using full range of motion wastes the machine's potential.

name: lever seated crunch
instructions: Sit on the leverage machine with your back against the pad and your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles or place your hands on the side pads for support.
Engage your abs and slowly lean back, allowing the pad to move with you.
Once your upper body is at a 45-degree angle, contract your abs and crunch forward, bringing your chest towards your knees.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly release and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This machine crunch isolates your abs while supporting your lower back.
coaching_cues: Curl your ribs down toward your hips.
Squeeze your abs hard at full contraction.
Return slowly, maintain tension on abs throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire abdominal wall working with each crunch. If your hip flexors burn instead, focus on the curling motion. Keep those abs firing.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with your arms removes all tension from your core.

name: lever seated crunch (chest pad)
instructions: Adjust the seat height and chest pad position according to your comfort.
Sit on the machine with your back against the chest pad and your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles or side bars for stability.
Engage your abs and slowly lean back, allowing the chest pad to move with you.
Pause for a moment at the maximum contraction, feeling the tension in your abs.
Slowly return to the starting position by contracting your abs and pulling yourself back up.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The chest pad variation keeps your upper body stable for pure ab work.
coaching_cues: Press chest into pad, stay anchored.
Crunch down by flexing your spine forward.
Control the return, do not let it drop.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep contraction in your upper and lower abs. If your neck strains, relax your head and focus on your core. Strong core equals strong body.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum instead of muscle makes this exercise ineffective.

name: lever seated crunch v. 2
instructions: Sit on the leverage machine with your back against the pad and your feet secured under the footpads.
Place your hands on the handles or the sides of the seat for support.
Engage your abs and slowly crunch forward, bringing your chest towards your knees.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your abs.
Slowly return to the starting position, allowing your back to round slightly.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This crunch variation adds an extra range of motion for deeper ab work.
coaching_cues: Initiate the movement from your abs only.
Exhale fully as you crunch down hard.
Resist the weight on the way back up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs burning throughout the entire set. If you lose that connection, slow down and reset your focus. Every rep counts.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing through reps significantly reduces core muscle activation.

name: lever seated dip
instructions: Adjust the seat height so that your feet are flat on the ground and your knees are at a 90-degree angle.
Grasp the handles of the leverage machine with your palms facing down and your arms fully extended.
Slowly lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Seated dips isolate your triceps while keeping your shoulders safe.
coaching_cues: Grip handles firmly, elbows stay close in.
Press down until arms are fully extended.
Lower with control, feel the tricep stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps doing all the pushing work. If your shoulders ache, adjust the seat height for better alignment. Finish those arms strong.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbows outward puts unnecessary stress on shoulders.

name: lever seated fly
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
Grasp the handles with a pronated grip and keep your elbows slightly bent.
Exhale and push the handles forward, bringing them together in front of your chest.
Pause for a moment, squeezing your chest muscles.
Inhale and slowly return to the starting position, allowing your chest muscles to stretch.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Squeeze your chest together at the center and control the return.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, back flat against the pad.
Bring handles together, squeeze your chest hard.
Slowly open arms, feel the deep stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction across your entire chest when handles meet. A deep stretch in the outer chest on the way back means you are doing it right. Keep squeezing and stay focused.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your shoulders roll forward puts unnecessary strain on the joint.

name: lever seated good morning
instructions: Adjust the seat height so that your hips are slightly higher than your knees.
Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and your feet flat on the footrests.
Grasp the handles or the sides of the seat for stability.
Keeping your back straight, slowly lean forward from your hips until your upper body is parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then slowly return to the starting position by pushing through your glutes and hamstrings.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge forward from your hips and squeeze your glutes to return upright.
coaching_cues: Brace your core, sit back into hips.
Lower with control, feel the hamstring stretch.
Drive through glutes, squeeze hard at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and hamstrings working together through the entire movement. A stretch in the back of your legs at the bottom is perfect. Push through and build that strength.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back during the hinge can cause serious injury.

name: lever seated hip abduction
instructions: Adjust the seat height so that your knees are at a 90-degree angle.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet on the footrests.
Place your hands on the side handles for stability.
Engage your abductors and slowly push your legs apart, away from the midline of your body.
Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, then slowly bring your legs back together to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Push your knees outward against the pads and squeeze at the widest point.
coaching_cues: Sit upright, grip the handles firmly.
Push knees apart, control the movement out.
Hold the squeeze, then slowly return together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn on the outer hips and glutes as you push out. That squeeze at the end is where the magic happens. Keep pushing through each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum instead of muscle reduces effectiveness and risks strain.

name: lever seated hip adduction
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the backrest.
Place your feet on the footrests and grasp the handles for stability.
Engage your adductor muscles and slowly bring your legs together, squeezing your inner thighs.
Pause for a moment at the peak contraction, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Squeeze your inner thighs together and control the release back out.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, keep your back against pad.
Bring knees together, squeeze your inner thighs.
Slowly release, maintain tension throughout the movement.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your inner thighs firing as you bring your legs together. The slow release keeps muscles under tension longer. Stay with it and feel the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Snapping your legs together too fast can strain your groin muscles.

name: lever seated leg curl
instructions: Adjust the machine to fit your body and sit on it with your back against the backrest.
Place your lower legs under the padded lever, just above your ankles.
Grasp the handles on the sides of the machine for support.
Keeping your upper legs stationary, exhale and curl your legs up as far as possible.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your hamstrings.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl your heels toward your glutes and squeeze your hamstrings hard.
coaching_cues: Adjust pad just above your ankles.
Curl heels back, squeeze hamstrings at bottom.
Lower slowly, keep constant tension on muscles.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your hamstrings contract intensely as you curl down. The squeeze at the bottom is where you build real strength. Keep that mind muscle connection strong.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your hips off the seat takes work away from hamstrings.

name: lever seated leg raise crunch
instructions: Sit on the leverage machine with your back against the backrest and your feet on the footrests.
Grasp the handles or sidebars for stability.
Engage your abs and slowly raise your legs up towards your chest, curling your torso forward at the same time.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs and torso back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lift your knees and crunch your abs together in one powerful movement.
coaching_cues: Grip handles, brace your core tight.
Lift knees and crunch simultaneously together.
Lower with control, do not fully relax.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core working as you crunch and lift. The combination hits upper and lower abs together. Keep breathing and stay tight throughout.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your legs uses momentum instead of your ab muscles.

name: lever seated reverse fly
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and keep your arms slightly bent.
Exhale and squeeze your shoulder blades together as you pull the handles back and outward, away from your body.
Pause for a moment at the peak contraction, then inhale and slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the handles back by squeezing your rear delts together.
coaching_cues: Chest against pad, slight bend in elbows.
Pull handles back, squeeze shoulder blades together.
Control the return, feel the stretch forward.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the back of your shoulders and upper back contracting hard. A good stretch at the front shows full range of motion. Build those rear delts one rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Using too much weight causes your traps to take over the movement.

name: lever seated reverse fly (parallel grip)
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles with a parallel grip, palms facing each other, and keep your arms slightly bent.
Exhale and squeeze your shoulder blades together as you pull the handles back and outward, away from your body.
Pause for a moment at the peak contraction, then inhale and slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: With palms facing each other pull back and squeeze your rear delts.
coaching_cues: Grip handles with palms facing each other.
Pull back wide, squeeze rear delts hard.
Return slowly, maintain control the whole time.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn in the back of your shoulders with each rep. The parallel grip shifts focus slightly for balanced development. Stay focused and keep squeezing.
coach_guide.warning: Shrugging your shoulders up takes tension off the rear delts.

name: lever seated row
instructions: Adjust the seat height and footrests to a comfortable position.
Sit on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet on the footrests.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement.
Slowly release the handles and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the handles to your torso by driving your elbows straight back.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, chest up against the pad.
Pull elbows back, squeeze shoulder blades together.
Control forward, keep your back straight throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats and mid back muscles working hard. A strong squeeze at the back of each rep builds thickness. Keep pulling and building that back.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning too far back turns this into a momentum exercise.

name: lever seated squat calf raise on leg press machine
instructions: Adjust the seat of the leg press machine so that your knees are slightly bent when your feet are on the footplate.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet flat on the footplate, shoulder-width apart.
Place your toes and the balls of your feet on the footplate, keeping your heels off the edge.
Release the safety handles and push the footplate away from you by extending your knees.
Once your knees are fully extended, slowly lower your heels by flexing your calves.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the footplate back up by extending your calves.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Push through the balls of your feet and squeeze your calves at the top.
coaching_cues: Position feet on platform, heels hanging off.
Press up high, squeeze calves at top.
Lower slowly, feel the deep calf stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your calves burning as you press and hold at the top. The full stretch at the bottom maximizes growth potential. Push through the burn and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom puts excessive stress on your Achilles tendon.

name: lever shoulder press
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the backrest.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your hands at shoulder level.
Push the handles upward until your arms are fully extended, but do not lock your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press straight up and control the weight back down slowly.
coaching_cues: Sit tall with core braced tight.
Press up until arms are nearly straight.
Lower with control, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulder muscles working through the entire movement. If your neck tenses up, you are shrugging too much. Keep those traps relaxed and push strong.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: lever shoulder press v. 2
instructions: Adjust the seat height and backrest of the leverage machine to a comfortable position.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles of the machine with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Push the handles upward and forward until your arms are fully extended, but not locked.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your shoulders down and let your delts do all the pushing.
coaching_cues: Plant feet flat and brace your core.
Drive the handles up in a smooth arc.
Squeeze at the top then lower slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a burn across the front and side of your shoulders. If your traps are burning instead, reset your shoulder position. Stay focused and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Shrugging your shoulders up shifts work away from your delts.

name: lever shoulder press v. 3
instructions: Adjust the seat height and backrest of the leverage machine to a comfortable position.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles of the machine with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Push the handles upward and forward until your arms are fully extended, but not locked.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Focus on a full range of motion and controlled tempo throughout.
coaching_cues: Back flat against the pad throughout.
Press until arms extend but dont lock.
Lower until you feel a deep stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your deltoids working from bottom to top of each rep. If your elbows flare out too wide, bring them slightly forward. You have got this.
coach_guide.warning: Locking out your elbows aggressively can stress the joint.

name: lever shrug
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the leverage machine with your back against the pad.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and keep your arms straight.
Keeping your back straight, lift your shoulders up towards your ears as high as possible.
Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lift your shoulders straight up toward your ears and squeeze hard.
coaching_cues: Stand tall with a neutral spine.
Shrug shoulders up high and hold briefly.
Lower slowly and feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your trap muscles bunch up at the top of each rep. If your arms are bending, you are using biceps instead. Keep it simple and squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling your shoulders can strain the rotator cuff.

name: lever standing calf raise
instructions: Adjust the machine to your height and stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Place your shoulders under the pads and hold onto the handles for stability.
Raise your heels as high as possible by extending your ankles.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rise up onto your toes as high as possible and squeeze at the top.
coaching_cues: Stand with balls of feet on platform.
Push up high onto your toes slowly.
Lower heels below the platform edge.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense squeeze in your calves at the top. If your knees are bending, keep your legs straighter. Push through and feel that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom risks snapping your Achilles tendon.

name: lever standing chest press
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your hands at chest level.
Push the handles forward until your arms are fully extended, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Pause for a moment, then slowly bring the handles back towards your chest, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive the handles forward and squeeze your chest muscles hard.
coaching_cues: Sit with chest up and shoulders back.
Press forward until arms nearly straighten.
Return slowly and stretch the chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your pecs contracting as you press forward. If your shoulders roll forward, pull them back and reset. Keep that chest proud and push.
coach_guide.warning: Letting shoulders round forward strains the rotator cuff.

name: lever t bar row
instructions: Adjust the seat height and footplate position to ensure proper alignment.
Sit on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the footplate.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Pull the handles towards your torso, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak contraction, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the bar into your torso and squeeze your back at the top.
coaching_cues: Chest against pad with core tight.
Pull elbows back and squeeze shoulder blades.
Lower with control and feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats and mid back muscles working hard. If you only feel your arms, focus on pulling with elbows. Squeeze that back and keep rowing.
coach_guide.warning: Jerking the weight up can strain your lower back.

name: lever t-bar reverse grip row
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and engage your core.
Pull the handles towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release and extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The underhand grip shifts more focus onto your lower lats.
coaching_cues: Grip with palms facing up securely.
Pull elbows tight to your sides.
Squeeze your lats hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in your lower lat muscles. If your biceps fatigue first, focus on elbow drive instead. Stay locked in and pull strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your upper back reduces lat activation significantly.

name: lever triceps extension
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back against the pad.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and fully extend your arms in front of you.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, slowly lower the handles towards your forehead by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the handles back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows locked in place and extend only at the elbow.
coaching_cues: Pin upper arms firmly in position.
Straighten arms fully and squeeze triceps.
Lower slowly and keep tension constant.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the back of your arms burning with each rep. If your shoulders move, your elbows are drifting too much. Lock them in and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring elbows out shifts stress to your shoulder joints.

name: lever unilateral row
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself facing the machine.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and keep your back straight.
Pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release and extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Work one side at a time to fix any strength imbalances.
coaching_cues: Brace your core and stay square.
Pull elbow back and squeeze the lat.
Lower with control then switch sides.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel each side of your back working independently. If one side feels weaker, give it extra focus and attention. Balance builds a stronger back.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating your torso takes tension off the target muscles.

name: london bridge
instructions: Attach the rope to a high anchor point.
Stand facing away from the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the rope with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Lean forward slightly, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Pull the rope towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the rope toward your face while leaning back to target your upper back.
coaching_cues: Lean back and keep your core braced.
Pull rope to chest, squeeze shoulder blades.
Control the return, do not let it yank.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction between your shoulder blades and across your upper back. If your biceps burn first, focus on pulling with your elbows. Keep that back engaged.
coach_guide.warning: Letting the rope pull you forward stresses your lower back.

name: low glute bridge on floor
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your arms by your sides, palms facing down.
Engage your glutes and core, then lift your hips off the ground until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your glutes.
Slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press through your heels and squeeze your glutes hard at the top.
coaching_cues: Feet flat, knees bent at 90 degrees.
Drive hips up, squeeze glutes at top.
Lower slowly, keep tension in your glutes.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes firing and your hips opening at the top of each rep. If your hamstrings cramp, push more through your heels. Own that squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back takes the work away from your glutes.

name: lower back curl
instructions: Lie flat on your stomach with your legs extended and your arms by your sides.
Engage your glutes and hamstrings, and slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling your back upwards.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lift your chest gently off the floor using only your lower back muscles.
coaching_cues: Keep your hips pressed into the floor.
Lift chest slowly, eyes stay down forward.
Lower with control, no sudden dropping here.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a gentle squeeze in your lower back as you lift. If your neck strains, keep your gaze down. Build that spinal strength slowly.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting too high can compress your lower spine joints.

name: lunge with jump
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Take a step forward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position.
Push off with your right foot and jump into the air, switching the position of your feet mid-air.
Land softly with your left foot forward and immediately lower your body into a lunge position.
Continue alternating between lunges and jumps for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode upward from the lunge and switch legs in mid air.
coaching_cues: Drop into a deep lunge, chest stays tall.
Explode up, switch legs in the air.
Land soft, sink straight into the next lunge.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads burning as you power through each jump. If your knees cave in, focus on pushing them out. Keep that power flowing.
coach_guide.warning: Hard landings put heavy stress on your knee joints.

name: lunge with twist
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Take a step forward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position.
As you lunge, twist your torso to the right, bringing your left elbow towards your right knee.
Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, stepping forward with your left foot and twisting your torso to the left.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Step into a lunge then rotate your torso toward your front leg.
coaching_cues: Step forward into a stable deep lunge.
Rotate torso toward the front leg side.
Return to center, then push back up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs working to control the rotation while your legs hold you steady. If you wobble, slow down the twist. Stay controlled and strong.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting too fast can strain your lower back muscles.

name: lying (side) quads stretch
instructions: Lie on your side with your legs straight.
Bend your top leg and grab your ankle or foot with your hand.
Gently pull your ankle or foot towards your glutes until you feel a stretch in your quads.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Release the stretch and repeat on the other side.

coach_guide.intro: Lie on your side and pull your heel toward your glutes to stretch your quad.
coaching_cues: Lie on your side, keep hips stacked.
Grab ankle and pull heel toward glutes.
Push hips forward to deepen the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch running down the front of your thigh. If your lower back arches, tighten your core. Breathe into that stretch.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too hard can strain your knee ligaments.

name: lying elbow to knee
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engage your abs and lift your upper body off the ground, bringing your right elbow towards your left knee.
At the same time, extend your right leg straight out and lift it off the ground.
Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Repeat the movement, this time bringing your left elbow towards your right knee and extending your left leg.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Crunch up and drive your elbow toward the opposite knee with control.
coaching_cues: Lie back, hands behind your head lightly.
Crunch up, drive elbow to opposite knee.
Lower with control, alternate sides smoothly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques firing as you rotate and crunch. If your neck hurts, keep your chin off your chest. Keep twisting strong.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling on your neck shifts stress away from your abs.

name: lying leg raise flat bench
instructions: Lie flat on a flat bench with your back pressed against it.
Place your hands under your glutes for support.
Keep your legs straight and together, and lift them up towards the ceiling.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your lower back pressed flat and lift your legs with control.
coaching_cues: Grip the bench, press lower back flat.
Lift legs together, keep them straight up.
Lower slowly, stop before your back arches.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs working hard to control the movement. If your back lifts off, shorten your range. Stay flat and keep grinding.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your lower back arch strains your spine.

name: lying leg-hip raise
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your arms by your sides.
Place your hands under your glutes for support.
Engage your core and lift your legs off the ground, raising them towards the ceiling.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lift your legs and roll your hips off the floor to work your lower abs.
coaching_cues: Legs straight up, arms flat for support.
Curl hips up off the floor toward ceiling.
Lower hips with control, keep legs vertical.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs contracting as your hips lift off the ground. If you swing your legs, slow it down. Control is everything here.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum takes the work off your abs completely.

name: machine inner chest press
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your back flat against the pad.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and position your elbows at a 90-degree angle.
Push the handles forward until your arms are fully extended, exhaling during the movement.
Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position, inhaling as you do so.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press the handles together in front of your chest to squeeze your inner pecs.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, chest up, shoulders pressed back.
Press handles together, squeeze your chest hard.
Return slowly, keep tension on your pecs.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep squeeze in the center of your chest at the end of each press. If your shoulders take over, lower the weight. Chase that inner chest burn.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your shoulders forward strains the front deltoids.

name: march sit (wall)
instructions: Stand with your back against a wall and your feet hip-width apart.
Slowly slide your back down the wall until your knees are bent at a 90-degree angle.
Lift your right foot off the ground and bring your knee towards your chest.
Lower your right foot back down and lift your left foot off the ground, bringing your knee towards your chest.
Continue alternating between lifting your right and left foot for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press your back flat against the wall and drive each knee up with control.
coaching_cues: Back and hips glued to the wall.
Drive knee to hip height, pause briefly.
Lower with control, alternate sides smoothly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes firing on the standing leg as you stabilize. Your hip flexors will work on the lifting leg too. Stay tall and keep that wall contact strong.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning away from the wall means your glutes are not engaging properly.

name: medicine ball catch and overhead throw
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a medicine ball in both hands at chest level.
Bend your knees slightly and engage your core.
Lower your body into a squat position, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Explosively extend your hips and legs, while simultaneously throwing the medicine ball overhead.
Release the ball at the top of the movement and catch it on the way down.
Lower your body back into the squat position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Catch the ball with soft hands and immediately throw it overhead using your lats.
coaching_cues: Catch with arms extended, elbows slightly bent.
Pull through your lats as you load.
Explode overhead, fully extend your arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats stretch as you catch and powerfully contract as you throw. Your core stays braced throughout the movement. Channel that power from your back.
coach_guide.warning: Throwing with just your arms removes the lat activation entirely.

name: medicine ball chest pass
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding the medicine ball at chest level.
Extend your arms forward, pushing the medicine ball away from your chest with force.
As you release the ball, follow through with your arms and torso, transferring your weight from your back foot to your front foot.
Catch the ball as it rebounds off the wall or partner, and immediately repeat the movement.
Continue for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Load the ball at your chest and explode it forward using your pecs.
coaching_cues: Ball tight to chest, elbows wide.
Push from your chest, not your shoulders.
Snap your arms straight, release with power.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest muscles contract explosively as you push the ball away. Your triceps assist but your pecs drive the movement. Stay athletic and explosive.
coach_guide.warning: Pushing from your shoulders reduces chest activation significantly.

name: medicine ball chest push from 3 point stance
instructions: Start in a 3 point stance with one hand on the medicine ball and the other hand on the ground.
Extend your legs and position your body in a straight line.
Lower your chest towards the ground while keeping your back straight.
Push the medicine ball away from your body, extending your arm fully.
Return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: From a three point stance explode up and push the ball with your chest.
coaching_cues: Start low in three point athletic stance.
Drive through legs as you stand tall.
Push the ball forward with chest power.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel power transfer from your legs through your core into your chest. The pecs fire hard at the release point. Use that whole body explosion.
coach_guide.warning: Starting the push before you stand removes leg drive power.

name: medicine ball chest push multiple response
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a medicine ball at chest level.
Extend your arms forward, pushing the medicine ball away from your chest.
Pause for a moment, then slowly bring the medicine ball back to your chest.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Catch and immediately return the ball with rapid chest pushes.
coaching_cues: Soft catch, absorb into your chest quickly.
Reset fast, elbows stay wide throughout.
Push explosively, repeat without pausing long.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working continuously with minimal rest between reps. This builds reactive power in your pecs. Stay quick and keep that rhythm going.
coach_guide.warning: Pausing too long between catches defeats the reactive purpose.

name: medicine ball chest push single response
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding the medicine ball at chest level.
Extend your arms forward, pushing the medicine ball away from your chest.
Pause for a moment, then slowly bring the medicine ball back to your chest.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Catch the ball and push it back with one powerful chest contraction.
coaching_cues: Absorb the catch with soft bent arms.
Load the ball tight against your chest.
One explosive push, maximum chest power output.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your pecs generate maximum force in that single push. Quality matters more than speed here. Make every rep count with full power.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the catch compromises your pushing power significantly.

name: medicine ball chest push with run release
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a medicine ball at chest level.
Step forward with your right foot and simultaneously push the medicine ball forward, extending your arms fully.
As you push the ball forward, release it and let it roll forward.
Quickly run forward and catch the ball before it hits the ground.
Once you catch the ball, bring it back to your chest and repeat the movement with your left foot forward.
Continue alternating legs and repeating the exercise for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sprint forward and release a powerful chest pass as you run.
coaching_cues: Accelerate into your sprint, ball at chest.
Push the ball forward mid stride smoothly.
Follow through with arms, keep running forward.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest fire while your body moves forward dynamically. This trains athletic pushing power in motion. Stay explosive and coordinated throughout.
coach_guide.warning: Stopping before the push removes the athletic transfer benefit.

name: medicine ball close grip push up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands on the medicine ball, shoulder-width apart.
Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Push back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Place both hands on the ball and perform close grip push ups for your triceps.
coaching_cues: Hands close together on the ball center.
Lower chest toward ball, elbows tucked tight.
Push up strong, squeeze triceps at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps burning as they do most of the pushing work. Your core works hard to stabilize on the unstable ball. Keep squeezing those arms.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbows shifts the work away from triceps.

name: medicine ball overhead slam
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a medicine ball with both hands above your head.
Engage your core and keep your back straight.
Bend your knees slightly and forcefully slam the medicine ball down to the ground in front of you.
As you slam the ball down, use your entire body to generate power, including your shoulders and core.
Catch the ball on the bounce and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Reach the ball overhead and slam it down using your entire upper back.
coaching_cues: Reach tall, extend arms fully overhead first.
Pull hard through lats, slam with power.
Hinge at hips, follow the ball down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back and lats engage powerfully as you pull the ball down. Your core braces hard at the bottom. Slam it like you mean it.
coach_guide.warning: Using only your arms removes the upper back engagement completely.

name: medicine ball supine chest throw
instructions: Lie flat on your back on a bench with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Hold the medicine ball with both hands, extending your arms straight up above your chest.
Lower the medicine ball towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Explosively push the medicine ball upwards, extending your arms fully and throwing the ball as high as possible.
Catch the medicine ball and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie on your back and throw the ball up using your chest and triceps.
coaching_cues: Ball at chest, elbows flared to sides.
Press up explosively, release at top powerfully.
Catch softly, absorb back to chest position.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps and chest work together to launch the ball upward. Control the catch to protect your face and chest. Stay powerful and controlled.
coach_guide.warning: Throwing without control can result in the ball landing on you.

name: mixed grip chin-up
instructions: Grab the pull-up bar with an underhand grip (palms facing towards you) and your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Hang from the bar with your arms fully extended and your feet off the ground.
Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bar, leading with your chest.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Grip the bar with one palm facing you and one facing away for balanced pulling power.
coaching_cues: Pull elbows down toward your hips hard.
Squeeze your lats at the top position.
Lower with control, full arm extension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and contraction through your entire back. One side may feel stronger so alternate grips between sets. Keep pulling strong.
coach_guide.warning: Avoid twisting your torso as the mixed grip can create rotation.

name: modified hindu push-up (male)
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet hip-width apart.
Lower your body towards the ground, bending your elbows and keeping your core engaged.
As you lower your body, shift your weight back and lift your hips up towards the ceiling, creating an inverted V shape with your body.
Continue to lower your body until your chest is just above the ground, then reverse the movement, pushing your hips back down and extending your arms to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Flow from a pike position down through a swooping arc and press up to cobra.
coaching_cues: Start hips high in a pike position.
Swoop chest forward close to the ground.
Press up, hips down, chest proud.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest shoulders and triceps working through the entire movement. Your core stays engaged throughout the flowing motion. Embrace the burn and keep moving.
coach_guide.warning: Do not let your lower back collapse when you press up.

name: modified push up to lower arms
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line.
Lower your body down towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Once your elbows are at a 90-degree angle, lower your forearms to the ground, keeping your elbows directly under your shoulders.
Pause for a moment, then push through your palms to lift your forearms back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lower from a push-up position down to your forearms then press back up.
coaching_cues: Keep your core tight and hips level.
Lower one arm then the other smoothly.
Press back up with control each side.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your forearms and triceps burning as you transition up and down. Your core works hard to prevent hip rotation. Stay steady and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Do not let your hips sway side to side during transitions.

name: monster walk
instructions: Place a resistance band around your ankles.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and slightly bend your knees.
Take a step to the side with your right foot, maintaining tension on the resistance band.
Follow with your left foot, stepping to the side to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Take wide lateral steps while maintaining tension in a mini squat position throughout.
coaching_cues: Stay low in a quarter squat stance.
Step wide and push through your heel.
Keep constant tension in your glutes always.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a serious burn in your outer glutes and hips. The key is staying low and keeping that band tension constant. Push through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Do not stand up tall between steps or you lose glute activation.

name: mountain climber
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line.
Engage your core and bring your right knee towards your chest, then quickly switch and bring your left knee towards your chest.
Continue alternating legs in a running motion, keeping your hips low and your core engaged.
Maintain a steady pace and breathe evenly throughout the exercise.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive your knees toward your chest in a fast running motion from plank position.
coaching_cues: Hands directly under your shoulders stay stable.
Drive knees high toward your chest fast.
Keep your hips down and core braced.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart rate spike and your core working overtime. Your hip flexors and shoulders will also be challenged. Keep that pace up.
coach_guide.warning: Do not let your hips pike up or sag down during the movement.

name: muscle up
instructions: Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your arms fully extended.
Engage your core and pull your body up towards the bar, leading with your chest.
As you reach the top of the movement, transition your grip so that your palms are facing towards you.
Continue pulling yourself up until your chest is above the bar and your arms are fully flexed.
Reverse the movement by slowly lowering yourself back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull yourself up then transition over the bar and press out to full arm extension.
coaching_cues: Pull explosively with a slight swing momentum.
Transition wrists over the bar quickly now.
Press out strong to full lockout above.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats chest and triceps all firing in sequence. This is an advanced move requiring explosive pulling power. You have got this.
coach_guide.warning: Master strict pull-ups and dips before attempting this advanced movement.

name: muscle-up (on vertical bar)
instructions: Start by hanging from a vertical bar with your palms facing away from you and your arms fully extended.
Engage your core and pull your body up towards the bar, leading with your chest.
As you pull yourself up, lean back slightly and bring your elbows towards your sides.
Continue pulling until your chest reaches the bar and your elbows are fully bent.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Perform the muscle-up on a vertical bar requiring more grip and core strength.
coaching_cues: Grip tight and pull your chest high.
Rotate your body around the bar smoothly.
Press out to full extension at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense challenge in your grip lats and entire upper body. The vertical bar demands more core stability throughout. Stay focused and committed.
coach_guide.warning: This variation is extremely advanced so ensure proper progression first.

name: narrow push-up on exercise ball
instructions: Place the stability ball on the ground and position yourself in a push-up position with your hands on the ball, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and keep your body in a straight line from head to toe.
Lower your chest towards the ball by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep hands close together on the ball and squeeze your triceps on every rep.
coaching_cues: Hands close together on the ball center.
Keep elbows tucked tight to your sides.
Press up and squeeze triceps at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps burning and your core fighting to stay stable. The unstable surface makes this much harder than it looks. Keep pushing through.
coach_guide.warning: Do not let the ball roll forward or you will lose shoulder position.

name: neck side stretch
instructions: Stand or sit up straight with your shoulders relaxed.
Tilt your head to one side, bringing your ear towards your shoulder.
Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
Repeat on the other side.
Perform 2-4 sets on each side.

coach_guide.intro: Gently tilt your ear toward your shoulder to release tension in your neck.
coaching_cues: Drop your ear slowly toward your shoulder.
Keep the opposite shoulder pressed down low.
Hold the stretch and breathe deeply now.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a gentle stretch along the side of your neck and into your upper trap. Never force this stretch just relax into it. Let the tension melt away.
coach_guide.warning: Do not pull your head aggressively as the neck is very sensitive.

name: negative crunch
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Start in a crunched position and slowly lower your torso back with control.
coaching_cues: Start curled up with abs fully engaged.
Lower your back down slowly with control.
Fight gravity the entire way down hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs working intensely as they resist the lowering phase. This eccentric focus builds serious core strength over time. Control the descent.
coach_guide.warning: Do not let your lower back arch off the floor as you lower.

name: oblique crunch v. 2
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head or cross them over your chest.
Engage your abs and lift your shoulder blades off the ground, rotating your torso to one side.
Pause for a moment, then lower your shoulder blades back down to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Twist and crunch to one side, focusing on squeezing your obliques at the top.
coaching_cues: Elbow drives toward opposite hip bone.
Squeeze oblique hard at the top.
Control the descent, no flopping down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction along the side of your waist with each rep. If your neck hurts, you are pulling with your hands. Let your obliques do the lifting and keep grinding.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling on your neck strains those muscles instead of working your core.

name: oblique crunches floor
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Place your hands behind your head or cross them over your chest.
Engage your abs and lift your shoulder blades off the floor, rotating your torso to one side.
Pause for a moment, then lower your shoulder blades back down to the floor.
Repeat on the other side, alternating sides with each repetition.
