coach_guide.intro: Lie on your side and crunch up by contracting your obliques, not yanking your head.
coaching_cues: Stack your hips, stay on your side.
Lift shoulder toward hip using obliques.
Pause at the top, then lower slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your side abs firing on every crunch, creating that squeeze along your waist. If you feel neck strain, lighten hand pressure on your head. Keep pushing through the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Jerking your head up puts dangerous stress on your neck.

name: olympic barbell hammer curl
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold an Olympic barbell with an overhand grip.
Let the barbell hang at arm's length in front of your thighs, with your palms facing your body.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the weights while contracting your biceps.
Continue to raise the barbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the barbell is at shoulder level.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your biceps.
Inhale and slowly begin to lower the barbell back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your upper arms locked at your sides and hammer curl the bar with a neutral grip.
coaching_cues: Upper arms glued to your ribs.
Curl with control, squeeze at top.
Lower slowly, resist the weight down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps and forearms working together throughout the movement. If your elbows drift forward, you are using momentum. Stay strict and feel that arm pump.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the bar uses momentum and robs your biceps of work.

name: olympic barbell triceps extension
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and holding the barbell with an overhand grip.
Raise the barbell above your head, fully extending your arms.
Keeping your upper arms close to your head, slowly lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows.
Pause for a moment, then extend your arms back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lower the bar behind your head with control, then extend your arms using only your triceps.
coaching_cues: Elbows point forward, stay locked in.
Lower bar behind head with control.
Extend arms fully, squeeze triceps hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your triceps at the bottom and a strong squeeze at the top. If your elbows flare out, reset them. Own every rep and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring elbows shifts load to shoulders and stresses the joint.

name: one arm against wall
instructions: Stand facing a wall with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Extend one arm straight out in front of you and place your palm against the wall.
Engage your core and lean your body forward, keeping your arm straight and your back flat.
Slowly push against the wall with your palm, activating your lat muscles.
Hold the position for a few seconds, then release and repeat with the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Press your arm against the wall and lean away to create a deep lat stretch.
coaching_cues: Arm extended, palm flat on wall.
Lean hips away, feel the stretch.
Breathe deep and sink into it.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a long stretch running down the side of your back and into your lat. If you feel shoulder pinching, adjust your arm height. Relax and let your muscles release.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing the stretch too aggressively can strain your shoulder.

name: one arm chin-up
instructions: Stand facing a pull-up bar with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Reach up and grab the bar with an underhand grip, with one hand gripping the bar and the other hand holding your wrist for support.
Hang from the bar with your arm fully extended, keeping your body straight and your core engaged.
Pull yourself up towards the bar by bending your elbow and squeezing your back muscles.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar, then slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: This is an elite move so grip tight and pull with total control using your lat.
coaching_cues: Grip the bar tight, engage lat.
Pull elbow down toward your hip.
Control the descent, no dropping down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lat and bicep working overtime on every inch of this pull. If your shoulder feels unstable, you are not ready yet. Build up and stay patient.
coach_guide.warning: Attempting this without proper strength risks serious shoulder injury.

name: one arm dip
instructions: Stand facing away from a bench or chair, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Place one hand on the bench or chair behind you, fingers pointing towards your body.
Extend your legs out in front of you, keeping your heels on the ground.
Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench or chair.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides and repeat with the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Lower yourself with control using one arm and push back up by driving through your tricep.
coaching_cues: Grip tight, core braced and stable.
Lower slowly, elbow tracks back.
Press up powerfully through your tricep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your tricep and chest working hard to control the movement. If your shoulder rolls forward, stop and reset. This takes time so respect the process.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping too fast puts extreme stress on your shoulder joint.

name: one arm slam (with medicine ball)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding the medicine ball with one hand in front of your waist.
Bend your knees slightly and engage your core.
Raise the medicine ball above your head, fully extending your arm.
Forcefully slam the medicine ball down to the ground, using your core and shoulders to generate power.
Catch the medicine ball on the bounce and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Slam the ball down hard with one arm while bracing your core throughout the movement.
coaching_cues: Reach ball high, core stays tight.
Slam down hard, engage your abs.
Catch the bounce, reset and repeat.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs and obliques fire as you slam the ball into the ground. If your lower back aches, brace harder before each slam. Channel that energy and keep it explosive.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back during the slam puts stress on your spine.

name: one arm towel row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and hold a towel with one hand.
Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Extend your arm fully, allowing the towel to hang in front of you.
Pull the towel towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the towel back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch arms.

coach_guide.intro: Grip a towel over a bar and row your body up by pulling your elbow back.
coaching_cues: Grip the towel tight, stay tall.
Pull elbow back, squeeze shoulder blade.
Lower with control, full arm extension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back and grip working hard on every pull. If your shoulders shrug up, focus on pulling your elbow down and back. Keep that back engaged and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Shrugging your shoulders takes tension away from your back.

name: one leg donkey calf raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing forward.
Place your hands on a stable surface for support, such as a wall or a bar.
Lift one leg off the ground, keeping your knee slightly bent.
Raise your heel as high as possible, using your calf muscles.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

coach_guide.intro: Rise onto one foot by pushing through your toes and squeezing your calf at the top.
coaching_cues: Stand on one leg, hold for balance.
Push through toes, rise up high.
Squeeze calf at top, lower slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in your calf and a deep stretch at the bottom. If you wobble, hold something for support. Build that single leg strength and keep it up.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the reps reduces time under tension and limits gains.

name: one leg floor calf raise
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and place your hands on a wall or sturdy object for balance.
Lift one foot off the ground and balance on the other foot.
Slowly raise your heel off the ground, lifting your body up onto the ball of your foot.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Rise up on one foot and squeeze your calf hard at the top.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, hold something for balance.
Push through the ball of your foot.
Pause at the top, feel the squeeze.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong burn in your calf muscle with each rep. If you feel unstable, lightly touch a wall for support. Keep pushing through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement reduces calf activation and results.

name: one leg squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Extend one leg forward, keeping it off the ground.
Bend your standing leg and lower your body down as if sitting back into a chair.
Keep your chest up and your back straight.
Push through your heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat with the other leg.

coach_guide.intro: Lower down on one leg with control and drive back up through your heel.
coaching_cues: Extend one leg forward, arms out front.
Sit back slowly, keep your chest lifted.
Drive through your heel to stand tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads working intensely on the standing leg. If your knee caves inward, focus on pushing it out. You have got this.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your knee collapse inward puts dangerous stress on your joint.

name: otis up
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit up while pressing the weight overhead in one smooth powerful motion.
coaching_cues: Start lying down, weight at your chest.
Sit up and press the weight overhead.
Lower with control, keep your core tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs working hard throughout the entire movement. If your lower back arches, engage your core more. Strong work, keep it up.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back transfers stress away from your abs.

name: outside leg kick push-up
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
As you push back up, kick one leg out to the side, extending it fully and engaging your glutes.
Return your leg to the starting position and repeat the push-up, alternating legs with each repetition.
Continue alternating leg kicks and push-ups for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Kick your leg out to the side at the top of each push up.
coaching_cues: Perform a push up with solid form.
At the top, kick one leg out wide.
Alternate legs, keep your hips stable.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes fire as you kick out to the side. If your hips rotate too much, slow down the kick. Keep that core locked in.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your hips causes lower back strain during the kick.

name: overhead triceps stretch
instructions: Stand or sit upright with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Extend one arm overhead, bending at the elbow so that your hand reaches towards the opposite shoulder blade.
With your other hand, gently pull the elbow of the extended arm towards the opposite side of your head, feeling a stretch in your triceps.
Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, then release.
Repeat on the other side.

coach_guide.intro: Reach your hand down your back and gently press your elbow for a deep stretch.
coaching_cues: Raise one arm and bend at elbow.
Reach your hand down between shoulder blades.
Gently press elbow with your other hand.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a nice stretch along the back of your upper arm. If you feel pinching, ease off the pressure slightly. Breathe and let the muscle release.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing the stretch too hard can strain your shoulder joint.

name: pelvic tilt
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands by your sides.
Engage your abs and tilt your pelvis upward, pressing your lower back into the ground.
Hold this position for a few seconds, focusing on contracting your abs.
Release the tilt and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Flatten your lower back into the floor by tilting your pelvis upward.
coaching_cues: Lie on your back, knees bent up.
Press your lower back flat into floor.
Hold the tilt, breathe and squeeze abs.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your deep core muscles engage as you hold the tilt. If you feel tension in your neck, relax your upper body. Great foundation work.
coach_guide.warning: Holding your breath reduces core activation and increases tension.

name: pelvic tilt into bridge
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your arms by your sides with your palms facing down.
Engage your glutes and core muscles.
Tilt your pelvis upward, lifting your hips off the ground.
Hold the bridge position for a few seconds.
Slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Tilt your pelvis first then lift your hips into a bridge position.
coaching_cues: Start with a pelvic tilt on floor.
Lift hips up, squeeze your glutes hard.
Lower slowly, maintain the pelvic tilt throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and core working together as you bridge. If you feel your hamstrings cramping, drive more through your heels. Excellent control.
coach_guide.warning: Skipping the pelvic tilt puts unnecessary stress on your spine.

name: peroneals stretch
instructions: Sit on the ground with your legs extended in front of you.
Loop the rope around the ball of your foot and hold the ends of the rope with your hands.
Gently pull the rope towards you, flexing your foot and stretching your calf muscles.
Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
Release the tension on the rope and repeat the stretch on the other leg.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the rope to stretch the outer muscles of your lower leg.
coaching_cues: Loop rope around your foot securely.
Pull foot inward to stretch outer calf.
Hold steady, breathe into the stretch deeply.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a stretch along the outer side of your lower leg. If you feel any sharp pain, reduce the tension immediately. Nice and easy does it.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too aggressively can strain the small muscles of your ankle.

name: pike-to-cobra push-up
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Engage your core and lift your hips up towards the ceiling, forming an inverted V shape with your body.
Lower your upper body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body.
As you lower down, shift your weight forward and transition into a cobra pose by straightening your arms and lifting your chest up.
Reverse the movement by bending your elbows and lowering your chest back down towards the ground.
Push through your hands to return to the inverted V position.
Continue the movement by lowering your hips back down towards the ground, returning to the starting push-up position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Flow from pike position down through a push up into cobra stretch.
coaching_cues: Start in pike, hips high toward ceiling.
Dive down through a smooth push up.
Press hips down, chest up into cobra.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes engage in pike and your chest open in cobra. If your lower back feels strained, keep a slight bend in your elbows. Beautiful flowing movement.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping too fast into cobra can compress your lower spine.

name: plyo push up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your body in a straight line.
Push explosively off the ground, using your chest muscles to propel your upper body off the ground.
Land softly with your hands back in the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode off the ground at the top of your push up with power.
coaching_cues: Lower into a solid push up position.
Explode up, hands leave the ground completely.
Land soft, absorb impact with your chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest and triceps firing explosively with each rep. If your wrists hurt on landing, try landing on your fists. Bring that power every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Landing with locked elbows can cause serious joint injury.

name: posterior step to overhead reach
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and your arms by your sides.
Take a step back with your right foot, landing on the ball of your foot.
Bend your left knee and lower your body into a lunge position.
As you lower into the lunge, simultaneously reach your arms overhead.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the lunge, then return to the starting position by pushing through your left heel and bringing your right foot forward.
Repeat the movement on the other side, stepping back with your left foot and bending your right knee.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Step back and reach high to lengthen your entire core and improve balance.
coaching_cues: Step back slow, stay tall and balanced.
Reach overhead, extend through your fingertips.
Squeeze abs tight as you return forward.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch through your midsection and hip flexors as you reach up. Your balance will be challenged so stay focused. Keep flowing smoothly through each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the step back can cause you to lose balance and strain your lower back.

name: posterior tibialis stretch
instructions: Sit on the ground with your legs extended in front of you.
Loop the rope around the ball of your foot and hold the ends of the rope with your hands.
Gently pull the rope towards you, flexing your foot and stretching your calf muscles.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Release the tension on the rope and relax your foot.
Repeat the stretch on the other leg.

coach_guide.intro: Use the rope to gently stretch the muscle running along your inner shin.
coaching_cues: Loop rope around foot, keep knee straight.
Pull toes toward you, then inward gently.
Hold steady, breathe deep into the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a mild stretch along the inner lower leg near your ankle. This area often gets neglected so give it attention. Stay patient and let the tension release.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too hard or fast can strain the delicate muscles around your ankle.

name: potty squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Lower your body down by bending your knees and pushing your hips back as if you were sitting on a chair.
Keep your chest up and your back straight throughout the movement.
Lower yourself until your thighs are parallel to the ground or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drop into a deep squat position to open your hips and engage your core.
coaching_cues: Feet wide, toes pointed slightly outward.
Sink hips low, keep your chest lifted.
Breathe deep and hold the position steady.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your inner thighs and hips opening while your abs work to keep you upright. This position improves mobility over time. Embrace the challenge and breathe through it.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back in this position puts unnecessary stress on your spine.

name: potty squat with support
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Hold onto a stable support, such as a chair or wall, for balance.
Lower your body down into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
Keep your chest up and your back straight throughout the movement.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use support to hold a deep squat and activate your glutes while building mobility.
coaching_cues: Hold support firmly, sink hips down low.
Press knees out, squeeze glutes at bottom.
Keep chest proud, weight in your heels.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes firing as you press your knees outward in the deep position. Your hips will thank you for this mobility work. Use the support confidently and go deeper each time.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your knees collapse inward puts stress on your knee joints.

name: power clean
instructions: Start with the barbell on the ground in front of you, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend down and grip the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and chest up as you lift the barbell off the ground, extending your hips and knees.
As the barbell reaches your mid-thigh, explosively pull it upwards, shrugging your shoulders and pulling your elbows high and to the sides.
As the barbell reaches its highest point, quickly drop underneath it, rotating your elbows around and catching the barbell on your shoulders in a front squat position.
Stand up with the barbell, fully extending your hips and knees.
Lower the barbell back down to the starting position, keeping control of the weight throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode the bar from floor to shoulders in one powerful athletic movement.
coaching_cues: Start with flat back, bar over midfoot.
Drive hips hard, shrug and pull underneath.
Catch with elbows high, absorb with legs.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your hamstrings and hips generate explosive power as you launch the bar upward. This is full body athleticism at its finest. Stay aggressive and trust your speed.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling with your arms instead of your hips can cause serious lower back injury.

name: power point plank
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line from head to toe.
Engage your core and squeeze your glutes to maintain a stable position.
Lower your body down onto your forearms, one arm at a time, maintaining a straight line from head to toe.
Hold this position for the desired amount of time, keeping your core and glutes engaged.
To return to the starting position, push through your forearms and lift your body back up into a high plank position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold a strong plank while extending opposite arm and leg for extra core challenge.
coaching_cues: Set plank tight, hips square to floor.
Extend opposite arm and leg straight out.
Hold steady, do not let hips rotate.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core fighting to keep your body stable and square. This move demands focus and balance so stay locked in. You are building serious functional strength.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag or twist removes the core benefit and strains your back.

name: prisoner half sit-up (male)
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Crunch up halfway with hands behind your head to isolate your upper abs.
coaching_cues: Hands behind head, elbows stay wide open.
Curl up halfway, lift shoulder blades off.
Lower with control, keep abs engaged throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper abs burning as you hold tension at the top of each rep. Do not pull on your neck to get higher. Focus on the squeeze and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Yanking your head forward with your hands strains your neck badly.

name: prone twist on stability ball
instructions: Start by lying face down on a stability ball with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes touching the ground.
Place your hands behind your head or cross them over your chest.
Engage your core muscles and slowly lift your upper body off the ball, keeping your back straight.
Rotate your torso to one side, bringing your shoulder towards your hip. Keep your hips and legs stable throughout the movement.
Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Repeat the rotation to the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your torso over the ball to challenge your obliques and core stability.
coaching_cues: Drape over ball, feet planted wide apart.
Rotate torso slowly, reach across your body.
Control the twist, return to center smoothly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques working hard to rotate and stabilize on the unstable surface. The ball adds a balance challenge so stay controlled. Keep the movement smooth and deliberate.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting too fast on the ball can cause you to slip and strain your back.

name: pull up (neutral grip)
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) and your arms fully extended.
Engage your core and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Pull your body up towards the bar by bending your elbows and driving your elbows down towards your hips.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull yourself up using a neutral grip to build your lats with less shoulder stress.
coaching_cues: Grip with palms facing each other firmly.
Pull elbows down, drive chest toward bar.
Lower with control, full extension each rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats doing the heavy lifting as you pull your chest toward the bar. The neutral grip is easier on shoulders so use it wisely. Every rep is making you stronger.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging or kipping takes tension off your lats and risks shoulder injury.

name: pull-in (on stability ball)
instructions: Start by sitting on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Place your hands on the sides of the stability ball for support.
Engage your abs and slowly roll your hips forward, bringing your knees towards your chest.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly extend your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Tuck your knees toward your chest on the ball to deeply engage your lower abs.
coaching_cues: Start in plank, shins resting on ball.
Pull knees in tight toward your chest.
Roll back out slow, keep hips controlled.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs working intensely as you pull your knees inward. The ball makes this unstable so stay focused and tight. Control the movement and feel the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag during the movement puts pressure on your lower back.

name: pull-up
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your arms fully extended.
Engage your core and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Pull your body up towards the bar by bending your elbows and bringing your chest towards the bar.
Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your chest to the bar by driving your elbows down and back.
coaching_cues: Chest drives up to the bar.
Elbows pull down and back.
Shoulders stay away from ears.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this across your entire back and into your arms. If your shoulders are creeping up to your ears, reset your position. Stay strong with that full back engagement.
coach_guide.warning: Don't swing or use momentum to cheat your way up.

name: push and pull bodyweight
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your body in a straight line.
Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your body straight.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
From the push-up position, pull your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your body straight.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Alternate between pushing and pulling movements to work your chest and back together.
coaching_cues: Push through your chest, pull with back.
Keep your core tight throughout both moves.
Control the tempo, no rushing the reps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest fire on the push and your back engage on the pull. This combo builds balanced upper body strength. Stay focused and keep alternating strong.
coach_guide.warning: Sagging hips during the push will strain your lower back.

name: push to run
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart and your body in a straight line.
Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your body straight.
Push through your hands to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Quickly bring one knee towards your chest, then quickly switch and bring the other knee towards your chest.
Continue alternating knees as fast as you can while maintaining good form.
Continue for the desired duration or number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode from your push up position straight into a sprint to spike your heart rate.
coaching_cues: Push up fast then jump to your feet.
Sprint hard for the set distance or time.
Land soft and drop back down with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest burn from the push and your lungs working hard on the run. This is cardio and strength combined. Push through and finish every rep strong.
coach_guide.warning: Sloppy transitions can cause you to trip or twist an ankle.

name: push up on bosu ball
instructions: Place the bosu ball on the ground with the flat side facing up.
Position yourself in a push-up position with your hands on the outer edges of the bosu ball.
Engage your core and lower your body down towards the bosu ball by bending your elbows.
Push yourself back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The unstable bosu ball forces your core and chest to work overtime for balance.
coaching_cues: Grip the bosu edges, wrists stay neutral.
Lower your chest toward the center slowly.
Press up strong and stabilize at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working while your core fights to keep you steady. Shaking is normal as your stabilizers kick in. Embrace the challenge and keep pressing.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement can cause the bosu to slip out.

name: push-up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your body in a straight line.
Pause for a moment when your chest is just above the ground, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Perfect plank position — your body is one solid line from head to heels.
coaching_cues: Hold that perfect plank line.
Chest leads down, chest leads up.
Core tight, glutes engaged throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this in your chest, shoulders, and core all working together. If your back starts to sag or your hips pike up, reset that plank position. Keep that full-body tension and power through.
coach_guide.warning: The moment your hips sag or pike up, you lose the movement.

name: push-up (bosu ball)
instructions: Place the bosu ball on the ground with the flat side facing up.
Position yourself in a push-up position with your hands on the outer edges of the bosu ball.
Engage your core and lower your body down towards the bosu ball by bending your elbows.
Push yourself back up to the starting position by extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the bosu ball dome side up to add instability and fire up your stabilizers.
coaching_cues: Hands wide on the dome, fingers spread.
Lower with control, chest nearly touches dome.
Push up explosively and hold the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your pecs engage deeply while your shoulders and core stabilize. If your arms shake, thats your body adapting. Stay steady and own each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Placing hands too far forward puts excess stress on your wrists.

name: push-up (on stability ball)
instructions: Place the stability ball on the ground and position yourself facing down with your hands on the ball, shoulder-width apart.
Extend your legs straight out behind you, balancing on your toes.
Engage your core and lower your chest towards the ball by bending your elbows, keeping your body in a straight line.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hands on the stability ball makes your chest and core work double time.
coaching_cues: Place hands shoulder width on the ball.
Lower slowly, keep the ball from rolling.
Press up and squeeze your chest hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest burning while your abs fight to keep you balanced. The instability is the point so embrace it. You are building real functional strength here.
coach_guide.warning: A loose core will cause the ball to roll and dump you.

name: push-up (on stability ball)
instructions: Place the stability ball on the ground and position yourself facing down with your hands on the ball, shoulder-width apart.
Extend your legs straight out behind you, balancing on your toes.
Engage your core and lower your chest towards the ball by bending your elbows, keeping your body in a straight line.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This stability ball push up builds serious chest strength and total body control.
coaching_cues: Engage your core before you start moving.
Lower until your chest nearly touches the ball.
Drive up and lock out with chest squeezed.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel constant tension in your chest and deep core engagement throughout. Wobbling means your stabilizers are firing hard. Keep breathing and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Looking up strains your neck so keep your gaze down.

name: push-up (wall)
instructions: Stand facing a wall, about arm's length away.
Place your hands on the wall at shoulder height, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step back a few feet, keeping your body straight and your feet hip-width apart.
Bend your elbows and lower your chest towards the wall, keeping your body in a straight line.
Push back up to the starting position, straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Wall push ups are perfect for building strength when floor push ups are too tough.
coaching_cues: Stand arms length away from the wall.
Bend elbows and lower your chest in.
Push back to start and squeeze your chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest muscles working as you press away from the wall. This is a great starting point to build toward floor push ups. Every rep is progress so keep going.
coach_guide.warning: Standing too close reduces the challenge and limits chest engagement.

name: push-up (wall) v. 2
instructions: Stand facing a wall, about arm's length away.
Place your hands on the wall at shoulder height, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step back with your feet, keeping them hip-width apart.
Engage your core and keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Bend your elbows and lower your chest towards the wall, keeping your body straight.
Pause for a moment, then push yourself back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This wall push up variation adjusts your angle to target your chest differently.
coaching_cues: Feet slightly back, lean into the wall.
Lower with control, elbows tracking back.
Press away and feel your chest contract.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a nice squeeze in your pecs with each push. Your shoulders should stay relaxed and down throughout. This builds the foundation for harder variations ahead.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your elbows flare wide will stress your shoulder joints.

name: push-up close-grip off dumbbell
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands placed close together, directly under your shoulders.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand, resting them on the ground.
Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Gripping dumbbells close together shifts the focus onto your triceps.
coaching_cues: Hands close together gripping the dumbbells tight.
Lower slowly keeping elbows tucked to sides.
Push up hard and squeeze your triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn in the back of your arms on every rep. Your chest helps but your triceps do the heavy lifting here. Keep those elbows in and finish every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting elbows flare out takes tension off your triceps.

name: push-up inside leg kick
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
As you push back up, lift one leg off the ground and kick it out to the side, keeping it straight.
Lower your leg back down and repeat the push-up, then switch to the other leg.
Continue alternating leg kicks with each push-up repetition.

coach_guide.intro: Adding a leg kick to your push up fires up your glutes and challenges your balance.
coaching_cues: At the top, kick one leg across underneath.
Keep hips stable as you rotate slightly.
Return leg and repeat on the other side.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes activate as you kick while your core works to keep you stable. This move builds coordination along with strength. Stay controlled and alternate sides smoothly.
coach_guide.warning: Kicking too fast can cause you to lose balance and collapse.

name: push-up medicine ball
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands on the medicine ball, shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your back straight.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Place one hand on the medicine ball to add instability and work your chest harder.
coaching_cues: Keep core tight, body in straight line.
Lower chest toward the ball with control.
Push up explosively, squeeze your chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working overtime to stabilize on the ball. Your core will fire up to keep you balanced. Embrace the wobble and get stronger.
coach_guide.warning: Do not let your hips sag or the ball will roll and strain your wrist.

name: push-up on lower arms
instructions: Start in a plank position with your forearms on the ground and elbows directly below your shoulders.
Engage your core and keep your body in a straight line from head to toe.
Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drop to your forearms and push through your triceps to lift your body.
coaching_cues: Forearms flat, elbows directly under shoulders.
Press through forearms, extend your arms.
Lower with control, keep elbows close.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in the back of your arms. This isolates your triceps in a way regular push-ups cannot. Stay with it and feel those arms work.
coach_guide.warning: Avoid letting your elbows flare out wide to protect your shoulders.

name: push-up plus
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Once your chest is just above the ground, push through your hands to extend your arms and lift your upper body up.
At the top of the movement, protract your shoulder blades by pushing your upper back towards the ceiling.
Pause for a moment, then reverse the movement by retracting your shoulder blades and lowering your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Add a protraction at the top to engage your serratus and build shoulder stability.
coaching_cues: Complete a full push-up with good form.
At top, push shoulders forward, round upper back.
Control back to start, repeat the plus.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest work on the push-up and your serratus fire at the top. That extra push builds bulletproof shoulders. Keep reaching and feel the difference.
coach_guide.warning: Do not over-arch your lower back when protracting at the top.

name: push-up to side plank
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart and your body in a straight line.
Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your core engaged.
Push back up to the starting position.
Shift your weight onto your left hand and rotate your body to the right, lifting your right arm towards the ceiling.
Hold the side plank position for a few seconds, then return to the starting position.
Repeat the push-up and side plank on the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Combine a push-up with a side plank rotation to fire up your core.
coaching_cues: Perform a push-up with solid form first.
Rotate body, raise one arm to ceiling.
Return hand down, push-up, rotate other side.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs working hard to control each rotation. Your obliques will burn as you balance in the side plank. Great work building total core strength.
coach_guide.warning: Rotate with control or you will lose balance and strain your shoulder.

name: quads
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back as if sitting on a chair.
Keep your chest up and your back straight.
Lower yourself until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Focus on activating and stretching your quadriceps with controlled movement.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, engage your core throughout.
Feel the front of your thighs working.
Move with control, no rushing the motion.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the front of your thighs engage and lengthen. This builds awareness in muscles you use every day. Stay focused and appreciate the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Avoid locking your knees or bouncing through the movement.

name: quarter sit-up
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lift just your shoulders off the ground to target your upper abs.
coaching_cues: Lie back, knees bent, feet flat down.
Curl shoulders up, reach hands toward knees.
Lower slowly, keep tension in your abs.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper abs contract with each small lift. The range is short but the burn is real. Keep the reps controlled and feel every squeeze.
coach_guide.warning: Do not pull on your neck or use momentum to lift up.

name: quick feet v. 2
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms by your sides.
Begin by rapidly moving your feet up and down, as if you were running in place.
Keep your movements quick and light, focusing on staying on the balls of your feet.
Continue for the desired duration or number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Move your feet as fast as possible to spike your heart rate and build leg speed.
coaching_cues: Stay light on your toes, knees soft.
Pump your feet rapidly, stay low.
Keep arms moving, chest stays up tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads burning and your heart pounding fast. This is pure speed and conditioning work in one move. Push through and keep those feet flying.
coach_guide.warning: Land softly to protect your knees and ankles from impact.

name: raise single arm push-up
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Extend one arm straight out to the side, parallel to the ground.
Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Push back up to the starting position, using your chest muscles to lift your body.
Repeat with the other arm extended.

coach_guide.intro: Raise one arm forward at the top to challenge your chest and core stability.
coaching_cues: Perform a push-up, body straight and tight.
At top, extend one arm straight forward.
Return hand, push-up, switch arms next rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest work double time to stabilize on one arm. Your core will engage hard to prevent rotation. You are building serious functional strength.
coach_guide.warning: Do not let your hips twist or drop when you raise your arm.

name: rear decline bridge
instructions: Lie on your back with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent.
Place your arms by your sides with your palms facing down.
Engage your glutes and hamstrings, and lift your hips off the ground until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Hold this position for a few seconds, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press through your heels and lift your hips to fire up your glutes.
coaching_cues: Sit with hands behind you, fingers back.
Press up, lift hips toward the ceiling.
Squeeze glutes at top, lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong squeeze in your glutes at the top. Your hamstrings will assist but glutes should dominate. Keep pressing up and feel that backside activate.
coach_guide.warning: Do not hyperextend your lower back when lifting your hips up.

name: rear deltoid stretch
instructions: Stand tall with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Extend your right arm across your chest, placing your left hand on your right elbow.
Gently pull your right arm towards your left shoulder, feeling a stretch in your right shoulder.
Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, then release.
Repeat on the other side.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your arm across your body to release tension in your rear shoulder.
coaching_cues: Bring one arm across your chest horizontally.
Use other hand to gently pull deeper.
Hold steady, breathe and relax into stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a gentle stretch in the back of your shoulder. This releases tightness from pushing and pressing movements. Breathe deep and let the tension melt away.
coach_guide.warning: Do not yank your arm or force the stretch beyond comfortable range.

name: rear pull-up
instructions: Grab the pull-up bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Hang from the bar with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bar, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your chest toward the bar while keeping your core tight throughout.
coaching_cues: Grip wide, pull elbows down and back.
Squeeze your lats hard at the top.
Lower slowly with full control, repeat.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn across your entire back, especially the lats. If your biceps fatigue first, focus on leading with your elbows. Keep fighting for each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging or kipping puts excessive strain on your shoulders.

name: reclining big toe pose with rope
instructions: Lie on your back with your legs extended and your arms by your sides.
Loop the rope around the ball of your right foot and hold the ends of the rope with your hands.
Slowly raise your right leg towards your chest, keeping your knee straight and your foot flexed.
Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then slowly lower your leg back down to the starting position.
Repeat with your left leg.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the rope to gently deepen the stretch without forcing the movement.
coaching_cues: Lie flat, loop rope around your foot.
Keep your leg straight, pull gently closer.
Breathe deeply and relax into the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a long stretch down the back of your thigh and into your calf. If you feel sharp pain behind your knee, ease off slightly. Let each breath take you deeper.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too aggressively can strain your hamstring or lower back.

name: resistance band hip thrusts on knees (female)
instructions: Start by kneeling on the ground with your knees hip-width apart and your feet flexed.
Wrap the resistance band around your thighs, just above your knees.
Place your hands on your hips or extend them out in front of you for balance.
Engage your glutes and core muscles.
Push your hips forward and squeeze your glutes as you lift your knees off the ground, extending your hips until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Hold the position for a moment, then slowly lower your knees back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive through your heels and squeeze your glutes hard at the top of each thrust.
coaching_cues: Knees stay in line, resist the band.
Thrust hips up, squeeze glutes at top.
Lower with control, keep tension constant.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes burning intensely with each squeeze at the top. If your lower back takes over, tuck your pelvis more. You are building strength with every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back removes the work from your glutes.

name: resistance band leg extension
instructions: Attach the resistance band to a sturdy anchor point and secure it around your ankle.
Stand facing the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Keeping your core engaged and your upper body stable, extend your leg straight out in front of you.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly return your leg to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

coach_guide.intro: Extend your leg fully and squeeze your quad at the top of each rep.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, anchor the band under feet.
Extend leg straight, squeeze quad hard.
Lower slowly, maintain resistance throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quadriceps working hard especially just above the knee. If you feel knee discomfort, reduce the range slightly. Keep pushing through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Locking out too aggressively can stress your knee joint.

name: resistance band seated biceps curl
instructions: Sit on a chair or bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold the resistance band with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and arms extended down by your sides.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the resistance band up towards your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the resistance band back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl with control and keep your elbows pinned to your sides throughout.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, palms up, grip the band.
Curl up slow, squeeze biceps at top.
Lower with control, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working from elbow to shoulder on every curl. If your shoulders roll forward, sit taller and reset. Every rep is making you stronger.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your arms uses momentum instead of your biceps.

name: resistance band seated chest press
instructions: Sit on a chair or bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold the resistance band handles in each hand, with your palms facing down and elbows bent at a 90-degree angle.
Extend your arms forward, pushing the resistance band away from your chest.
Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press forward with power and squeeze your chest at full extension.
coaching_cues: Anchor band behind you, grip at chest.
Press straight out, squeeze chest hard.
Return slow, keep your core engaged.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest muscles contracting with each press forward. If your shoulders feel strained, lower your hand position slightly. You are building a stronger chest.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbows too wide stresses your shoulder joints.

name: resistance band seated hip abduction
instructions: Sit on a chair or bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Wrap the resistance band around your thighs, just above your knees.
Place your hands on the sides of the chair or bench for support.
Engage your abductors (outer thigh muscles) and slowly push your knees apart, against the resistance of the band.
Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, then slowly bring your knees back together.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Push your knees apart against the band and control the movement back.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, band looped around both knees.
Push knees out, squeeze outer hips hard.
Return slowly, keep constant band tension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your outer hips and glutes firing with each rep. If you feel nothing, use a stronger band or push harder. Keep that burn going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning back takes tension away from your hip muscles.

name: resistance band seated shoulder press
instructions: Sit on a chair or bench with your back straight and feet flat on the ground.
Hold the resistance band with both hands, palms facing forward, and bring it up to shoulder level.
Press the band overhead, extending your arms fully.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the band back down to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press straight overhead and keep your core tight to protect your spine.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, band under seat, hands ready.
Press up, extend arms fully overhead.
Lower with control, elbows to 90 degrees.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders working hard especially at the top of each press. If your back arches, engage your core more. Strong shoulders are built rep by rep.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your back puts dangerous pressure on your spine.

name: resistance band seated straight back row
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended and loop the resistance band around your feet.
Hold the ends of the resistance band with your hands, palms facing each other.
Keep your back straight and lean slightly back, engaging your core.
Pull the resistance band towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly release the tension and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the band toward your torso and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, legs extended, band around feet.
Pull elbows back, squeeze shoulder blades tight.
Return slow, keep your back straight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles squeezing together on each pull. If your lower back rounds, bend your knees slightly. Great work building a stronger back.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back can lead to strain and injury.

name: reverse crunch
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended along your sides.
Bend your knees and lift your feet off the ground, bringing your thighs perpendicular to the floor.
Contract your abs and curl your hips off the floor, bringing your knees towards your chest.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lift your hips by curling your pelvis up using only your lower abs.
coaching_cues: Lie back, knees bent at 90 degrees.
Curl hips up, lift tailbone off floor.
Lower slowly, do not swing your legs.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs working hard to lift your hips off the ground. If your hip flexors burn instead, focus on the pelvic curl. Keep those abs engaged and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum removes the work from your abdominal muscles.

name: reverse dip
instructions: Position yourself between two parallel bars with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Position your hands behind you and press through your palms to work your triceps.
coaching_cues: Fingers pointing forward, grip the edge firmly.
Lower until elbows hit ninety degrees, pause.
Press up strong, squeeze triceps at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in the back of your upper arms throughout the movement. If your shoulders feel strained, you are dipping too low. Keep the range controlled and feel those triceps fire.
coach_guide.warning: Going too deep puts excessive stress on your shoulder joints.

name: reverse grip machine lat pulldown
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your knees under the pads and your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the handles with an underhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Sit upright with your chest out and shoulders back, maintaining a slight arch in your lower back.
Pull the handles down towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Grip the bar with palms facing you and pull down to target your lats and biceps.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, chest lifted, shoulders down.
Pull the bar to your upper chest.
Squeeze your lats hard, then control up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats stretching and contracting with each rep while your biceps assist. If you feel it mostly in your arms, focus on pulling with your elbows. Let your back do the heavy lifting.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning too far back turns this into a row and stresses your lower back.

name: reverse grip pull-up
instructions: Grab the pull-up bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Hang from the bar with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bar, leading with your chest.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Grab the bar with palms facing you and pull your chin over with lat power.
coaching_cues: Hang fully, engage your lats first.
Drive elbows down toward your hips.
Chin clears the bar, lower with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats working hard with bonus bicep engagement on every rep. If your biceps fatigue first, focus on that elbow drive. You have got this, keep pulling strong.
coach_guide.warning: Kipping or swinging takes tension off your lats and risks shoulder injury.

name: reverse hyper extension (on stability ball)
instructions: Lie face down on a stability ball with your hips resting on the ball and your legs extended straight behind you.
Place your hands on the ground in front of you for stability.
Engaging your glutes and hamstrings, lift your legs up towards the ceiling as high as you can.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie face down on the ball and lift your legs using your glutes and lower back.
coaching_cues: Hips centered on ball, hands on floor.
Lift legs until body forms straight line.
Squeeze glutes at top, lower slowly down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and hamstrings firing as you lift your legs. If your lower back cramps, you are lifting too high. Focus on that glute squeeze and stay controlled.
coach_guide.warning: Hyperextending your lower back can cause strain and discomfort.

name: reverse hyper on flat bench
instructions: Lie face down on a flat bench with your hips at the edge and your legs hanging off the bench.
Hold onto the bench for stability.
Keeping your legs straight, raise them up towards the ceiling as high as you can.
Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement.
Slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie face down on a bench and raise your legs by squeezing your glutes hard.
coaching_cues: Hips at bench edge, grip sides tight.
Lift legs to hip height, squeeze glutes.
Lower with control, do not swing legs.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and hamstrings engage powerfully at the top of each rep. If your lower back takes over, reduce your range of motion. Keep squeezing and stay strong.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting legs too high shifts the load onto your lower back.

name: reverse plank with leg lift
instructions: Sit on the ground with your legs extended in front of you and your hands resting on the ground behind you, fingers pointing towards your feet.
Press through your hands and lift your hips off the ground, coming into a reverse plank position.
Engage your core and lift one leg off the ground, extending it straight up towards the ceiling.
Hold for a moment, then lower your leg back down.
Repeat with the other leg.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold a reverse plank and lift one leg at a time to challenge your core.
coaching_cues: Hips high, body in straight line.
Lift one leg, keep hips level.
Alternate legs, breathe steady throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs, glutes, and triceps working to keep you stable. If your hips sag, reset your position before continuing. Stay lifted and keep pushing through.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your hips puts strain on your lower back and shoulders.

name: ring dips
instructions: Start by hanging from the rings with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your shoulders are below your elbows.
Push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Grip the rings and lower yourself with control to build serious tricep strength.
coaching_cues: Rings close to body, elbows tucked.
Lower until shoulders pass elbows, pause.
Press up explosively, lock out strong.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps and chest working hard to stabilize and push. If your shoulders feel unstable, reduce your depth. Master the control and own those rings.
coach_guide.warning: Unstable rings demand control or your shoulders will pay the price.

name: rocking frog stretch
instructions: Start by kneeling on the ground with your knees hip-width apart.
Place your hands on the ground in front of you for support.
Slowly lean forward, shifting your weight onto your hands and extending your legs behind you.
Keep your back straight and engage your glutes as you push your hips back and up towards the ceiling.
Hold this position for a few seconds, feeling a stretch in your glutes.
Slowly return to the starting position by bending your knees and lowering your hips back down.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Get into a deep frog position and rock forward and back to open your hips.
coaching_cues: Knees wide, feet turned out flat.
Rock forward gently, then back slowly.
Keep your spine neutral, breathe deeply.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your inner thighs and hip flexors as you rock. If you feel knee pain, narrow your stance slightly. Relax into it and let your hips open up.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing the stretch too deep can strain your inner thigh muscles.

name: rocky pull-up pulldown
instructions: Stand in front of a pull-up bar with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Reach up and grab the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Hang from the bar with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bar, leading with your chest.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Alternate pulling in front and behind your head to hit your lats from every angle.
coaching_cues: Start with chin over bar in front.
Lower and pull up behind your head.
Alternate each rep, stay controlled throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats working through a full range with each variation. If behind the head bothers your shoulders, stick to front pulls only. Keep moving and feel those lats burn.
coach_guide.warning: Behind the head pulls can stress shoulders if mobility is limited.

name: roller back stretch
instructions: Start by sitting on the ground with your legs extended in front of you.
Place the roller perpendicular to your body, just below your glutes.
Slowly roll your body backwards, allowing the roller to move up your back.
Continue rolling until the roller reaches your upper back.
Pause for a moment, then slowly roll back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie on the roller and gently move to release tension along your entire spine.
coaching_cues: Roller under mid back, knees bent.
Roll slowly from mid to upper back.
Pause on tight spots, breathe and relax.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel tension releasing as you roll across tight areas of your back. If you feel sharp pain, skip that spot and move on. Take your time and let your spine decompress.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling directly on your lower back can cause discomfort and strain.

name: roller body saw
instructions: Start in a plank position with your forearms on the roller and your body in a straight line.
Engage your core and slowly roll the roller forward, extending your body as far as you can while maintaining control.
Pause for a moment at the furthest point, then slowly roll the roller back towards your starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Start in a plank on the roller and saw your body forward and back using your core.
coaching_cues: Keep your hips level, core braced tight.
Push forward slowly, extend arms overhead.
Pull back using your abs, not arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your entire core as you extend and return. If your lower back starts to sag, shorten the range. Stay tight and keep sawing.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips drop puts dangerous pressure on your lower back.

name: roller hip lat stretch
instructions: Start by kneeling on the ground with the roller positioned under your hips.
Place your hands on the roller for support.
Slowly roll the roller forward, extending your hips and stretching your glutes.
Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then roll back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Position the roller under your hip and gently rock to release tension in your glutes and lats.
coaching_cues: Place roller under hip, lean slightly sideways.
Roll slowly from hip toward your ribcage.
Breathe deep and pause on tender spots.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a releasing sensation through your outer hip and side body. If you hit a tight spot, stay there and breathe through it. Let the tension melt away.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling too fast prevents muscles from actually releasing.

name: roller hip stretch
instructions: Start by sitting on the ground with your legs extended in front of you.
Place the roller under your glutes, just above your knees.
Lean back and place your hands on the ground behind you for support.
Engage your glutes and slowly roll the roller forward, bending your knees and bringing them towards your chest.
Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, feeling a stretch in your glutes.
Slowly roll the roller back to the starting position, extending your legs.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the roller under your hip flexors to open up tight hips and improve mobility.
coaching_cues: Roller under front hip, leg extended back.
Sink your weight slowly into the roller.
Rock gently side to side, breathe deep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in the front of your hip and glute area. If it feels too intense, shift some weight to your arms. Stay relaxed and let it release.
coach_guide.warning: Pressing too hard too fast can bruise the muscle tissue.

name: roller reverse crunch
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your arms extended straight above your head and your legs straight out in front of you.
Place the roller between your feet and grip it with your toes.
Engaging your abs, lift your legs off the ground and curl your knees towards your chest, rolling the roller towards your body.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position, rolling the roller away from your body.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold the roller between your legs and curl your hips up using your lower abs.
coaching_cues: Squeeze the roller tight between your knees.
Curl hips up, lifting tailbone off ground.
Lower with control, keep abs engaged throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs working hard to lift your hips each rep. If your neck strains, keep your head down and relaxed. Great work targeting that lower core.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum instead of abs reduces the effectiveness completely.

name: roller seated shoulder flexor depresor retractor
instructions: Sit on a flat surface with your legs extended in front of you.
Hold the roller with both hands, palms facing down, and place it on your thighs.
Lean forward slightly and roll the roller away from your body, extending your arms straight in front of you.
Pause for a moment, then slowly roll the roller back towards your body, bending your arms and bringing the roller back to your thighs.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit tall on the roller and work your shoulder blades together while pressing down and back.
coaching_cues: Sit upright, arms extended in front.
Squeeze shoulder blades down and together.
Hold the squeeze, then release with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest open and shoulder blades activate in the back. If you feel neck tension, relax your upper traps. Keep that proud posture going.
coach_guide.warning: Shrugging your shoulders up defeats the purpose of this movement.

name: roller seated single leg shoulder flexor depresor retractor
instructions: Sit on a flat surface with your legs extended in front of you.
Hold the roller with both hands, palms facing down, and place it on your thighs.
Lean back slightly and engage your core muscles.
Raise the roller up to shoulder level, keeping your arms straight.
Slowly lower the roller back down to your thighs.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Balance on one leg while working shoulder blade control and chest opening on the roller.
coaching_cues: Lift one leg, stay balanced on roller.
Press arms forward, squeeze shoulder blades back.
Hold steady, breathe and maintain posture.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest opening while your core works to keep you balanced. If you wobble, focus your eyes on one spot ahead. Great stability challenge.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing through reps compromises your balance and form completely.

name: roller side lat stretch
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the roller with both hands in front of your body.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Extend your arms forward and roll the roller down towards your feet, feeling a stretch in your lats.
Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then slowly roll the roller back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie on your side with the roller under your lat and gently roll to release tightness.
coaching_cues: Roller under armpit, arm extended overhead.
Roll slowly from armpit toward your ribs.
Pause on tight spots, breathe and relax.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel tension releasing along the side of your back and ribcage. If a spot is extra tender, stay there and take deep breaths. You are doing great work.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling directly on the armpit can compress sensitive nerves.

name: rope climb
instructions: Stand facing the rope with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grab the rope with both hands, palms facing towards you.
Bend your knees slightly and engage your core.
Begin pulling yourself up the rope by alternating hand-over-hand movements.
Use your legs to assist in the upward movement.
Continue climbing until you reach the desired height or the top of the rope.
To descend, reverse the hand-over-hand movement while controlling your descent.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Grip the rope tight and use your legs and back together to pull yourself upward.
coaching_cues: Grip high, wrap rope around one foot.
Stand on the rope, reach higher grip.
Pull with arms and back, push with legs.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your grip, lats, and legs all working together to climb. If your arms fatigue fast, use more leg drive. Keep climbing strong.
coach_guide.warning: Sliding down without control can cause serious rope burns.

name: run
instructions: Start by standing upright with your feet hip-width apart.
Engage your core and keep your upper body relaxed.
Begin jogging in place, lifting your knees up towards your chest and landing softly on the balls of your feet.
Maintain a steady pace and continue jogging for the desired duration or distance.
Remember to breathe deeply and maintain good posture throughout the exercise.

coach_guide.intro: Land with soft knees and keep a steady rhythm that you can maintain throughout.
coaching_cues: Land softly, midfoot under your hips.
Arms swing relaxed, shoulders stay down.
Breathe rhythmically, find your steady pace.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your legs working while your breathing stays controlled and steady. If you start gasping, slow down slightly and recover. Keep moving forward strong.
coach_guide.warning: Overstriding with a heel strike increases impact on your joints.

name: run (equipment)
instructions: Start by standing upright with your feet hip-width apart.
Engage your core and keep your upper body relaxed.
Begin jogging in place, lifting your knees up towards your chest and landing softly on the balls of your feet.
Maintain a steady pace and continue jogging for the desired duration or distance.
Remember to breathe deeply and maintain good posture throughout the exercise.

coach_guide.intro: Match your pace to the machine and focus on smooth consistent strides throughout.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, eyes forward, core engaged.
Land softly with quick light foot strikes.
Pump arms naturally, keep shoulders relaxed.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your cardiovascular system working while maintaining good running form. If the pace feels too hard, dial it back slightly. You are building great endurance.
coach_guide.warning: Holding the handrails changes your posture and reduces workout effectiveness.

name: runners stretch
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart.
Take a step forward with your right foot, keeping your left foot planted.
Bend your right knee and lower your body down, keeping your left leg straight.
Place your hands on your right thigh for support.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then switch sides and repeat.
