coach_guide.intro: Step one foot forward and sink your hips to lengthen the back of your leg.
coaching_cues: Front knee stacked over ankle, hips square.
Straighten back leg, heel pressing down gently.
Breathe deep and relax into the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch running down the back of your front thigh. If you feel pulling in your lower back, tilt your pelvis forward slightly. Let each breath take you deeper.
coach_guide.warning: Do not bounce or force the stretch as this can strain the hamstring.

name: russian twist
instructions: Sit on the ground with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Lean back slightly while keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Hold your hands together in front of your chest or hold a weight if desired.
Lift your feet off the ground, balancing on your sit bones.
Twist your torso to the right, bringing your hands or weight towards the right side of your body.
Pause for a moment, then twist your torso to the left, bringing your hands or weight towards the left side of your body.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Balance on your sit bones and rotate from your core, not your arms.
coaching_cues: Balance on sit bones, feet up.
Rotate from your core muscles.
Touch the ground beside each hip.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your obliques and a challenge in your balance right now. If you're wobbling too much, slow down the rotation and focus on control. Lock into that core burn and keep twisting with that fire.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating too fast with poor balance turns this into a momentum exercise instead of core work.

name: scapula dips
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended in front of you.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Lower your body by bending your elbows and retracting your shoulder blades, as if you are trying to squeeze a pencil between them.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your hands to extend your elbows and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let your shoulder blades glide down and back to build control in your upper back.
coaching_cues: Arms straight, body tall on the bars.
Shrug shoulders up, then depress them down.
Squeeze your traps hard at the bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your traps working as you push your shoulders down and away from your ears. If your arms are bending, keep them locked out. Own that scapular control.
coach_guide.warning: Bending the elbows turns this into a tricep exercise and removes trap activation.

name: scapula push-up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line.
Lower your chest towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your body.
As you lower, squeeze your shoulder blades together and push your chest forward.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Push your upper back toward the ceiling to fire up the muscles around your shoulder blades.
coaching_cues: Plank position, arms straight and locked.
Let shoulder blades pinch together, then spread.
Push the floor away, rounding upper back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a burn along the sides of your ribcage near your armpits. If your hips are sagging, tighten your core and stay rigid. Keep pushing through.
coach_guide.warning: Sagging hips put unnecessary stress on your lower back.

name: scapular pull-up
instructions: Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your arms fully extended.
Retract your shoulder blades by pulling them down and back.
Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bar, focusing on squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hang from the bar and pull your shoulder blades down without bending your arms.
coaching_cues: Dead hang, arms fully extended overhead.
Pull shoulder blades down and back together.
Hold the squeeze, then release with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your traps and upper back engage as you pull down. If you are bending your elbows, reset and keep arms straight. Build that foundation strength.
coach_guide.warning: Using arm bend defeats the purpose and limits scapular activation.

name: scissor jumps (male)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Jump off the ground and simultaneously cross your right leg in front of your left leg.
As you land, quickly switch legs, crossing your left leg in front of your right leg.
Continue alternating legs and jumping as quickly as possible.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode off the ground switching your legs mid air to spike your heart rate.
coaching_cues: Lunge position, chest up and core tight.
Jump high, switch legs in the air.
Land soft, sink into the next lunge.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart pounding and your legs burning with each jump. If your knees are caving inward, focus on tracking them over your toes. Push the pace.
coach_guide.warning: Hard landings put excess force on your knee joints.

name: seated calf stretch (male)
instructions: Sit on the edge of a chair or bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Extend one leg straight out in front of you, keeping your heel on the ground.
Lean forward slightly, feeling a stretch in your calf muscle.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Switch legs and repeat the stretch.

coach_guide.intro: Sit tall and pull your toes back to stretch through your lower leg.
coaching_cues: Legs extended, sit up straight and tall.
Flex foot, pulling toes toward your shin.
Hold and breathe, feeling the calf lengthen.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a gentle stretch along the back of your lower leg from knee to heel. If you feel cramping, ease off slightly and point your toes briefly. Stay patient with it.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing the stretch too aggressively can trigger a calf cramp.

name: seated glute stretch
instructions: Sit on the ground with your legs extended in front of you.
Bend your right knee and cross your right ankle over your left thigh.
Place your right hand on the ground behind you for support.
With your left hand, gently press down on your right knee to deepen the stretch.
Hold the stretch for 30 seconds to 1 minute.
Switch sides and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Cross one ankle over your knee and lean forward to open up your hips.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, ankle resting on opposite knee.
Keep crossed leg knee pressing outward gently.
Lean forward from hips, back stays straight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your outer hip and glute of the crossed leg. If your lower back is rounding, sit up taller first. Let the tension melt away.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back shifts the stretch away from the glutes.

name: seated leg raise
instructions: Sit on a flat bench with your back straight and your feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands on the sides of the bench for support.
Keeping your legs straight, slowly raise them up in front of you until they are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit on the edge and lift your knees to fire up your lower abs.
coaching_cues: Grip the seat, lean back slightly balanced.
Lift knees toward chest, abs tight.
Lower legs with control, do not drop.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs burning as you lift and control your legs. If your hip flexors are taking over, focus on curling your pelvis upward. Keep that core lit up.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your legs uses momentum and reduces ab engagement.

name: seated lower back stretch
instructions: Sit on the edge of a chair with your feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands on your thighs or on the sides of the chair for support.
Slowly lean forward from your hips, keeping your back straight.
Feel the stretch in your lower back and hold for 20-30 seconds.
Slowly return to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Round forward gently to release tension through your entire back.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, feet flat on the floor.
Tuck chin, roll forward reaching toward toes.
Breathe deeply and relax into the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a gentle lengthening through your lower and mid back. If you feel sharp pain, ease off and stay in a comfortable range. Let the stretch feel restorative.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing the forward fold can aggravate existing back issues.

name: seated piriformis stretch
instructions: Sit on the ground with your legs extended in front of you.
Bend your right knee and place your right foot on the outside of your left knee.
Place your left elbow on the outside of your right knee and gently twist your torso to the right.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then switch sides and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Cross your leg and gently twist to release the deep hip rotator muscle.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, cross one ankle over opposite knee.
Hug the crossed knee toward opposite shoulder.
Sit upright and hold, breathing into the hip.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your outer hip and possibly into your glute. If you feel knee discomfort, flex your foot to protect the joint. Stay with it and breathe.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too hard on the knee can stress the joint.

name: seated side crunch (wall)
instructions: Sit on the floor with your back against a wall and your legs extended in front of you.
Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engage your abs and lean to one side, bringing your elbow towards your hip.
Pause for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Crunch sideways into the wall to fire up your obliques.
coaching_cues: Sit tall with your side against wall.
Crunch ribcage down toward your hip bone.
Squeeze obliques hard then return with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong burn along the side of your waist with each crunch. If your hip flexors take over you are bending forward instead of sideways. Stay lateral and keep squeezing.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning forward turns this into a hip flexor exercise.

name: seated wide angle pose sequence
instructions: Sit on the ground with your legs extended in a wide angle.
Flex your feet and engage your quadriceps.
Place your hands on the ground behind you for support.
Keeping your back straight, lean forward from your hips.
Continue leaning forward until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.
Hold this position for a few breaths.
Slowly release the stretch and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Open your legs wide and fold forward to deeply stretch your inner thighs and hamstrings.
coaching_cues: Sit tall then spread legs as wide as comfortable.
Hinge at hips keeping your spine long.
Breathe deeply and relax into the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch along your inner thighs and behind your knees. If your lower back rounds excessively bend your knees slightly. Let each exhale take you deeper.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts unnecessary stress on your spine.

name: self assisted inverse leg curl
instructions: Lie flat on your back on a mat or bench with your legs extended.
Place your hands by your sides or under your glutes for support.
Bend your knees and lift your feet off the ground, bringing your thighs towards your chest.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lower yourself slowly using your hamstrings while your hands assist the movement.
coaching_cues: Kneel tall with hands ready to catch.
Lower your body forward slow and controlled.
Use hamstrings to resist gravity the whole way.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your hamstrings working intensely to control the descent. If you drop too fast your hamstrings are not engaged enough. Fight the fall and build that strength.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping too fast can strain your hamstrings or knees.

name: self assisted inverse leg curl
instructions: Lie face down on a leg curl machine with your legs extended and your ankles hooked under the padded lever.
Place your hands on the side handles of the machine for support.
Keeping your upper body stationary, exhale and curl your legs upward as far as possible.
Hold the contracted position for a brief pause as you squeeze your hamstrings.
Slowly lower your legs back to the starting position while inhaling.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Control your descent with hamstring power while hands provide minimal support.
coaching_cues: Lock ankles and keep your core tight.
Fall forward slowly resisting with your hamstrings.
Catch yourself gently then push back up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense contraction through the back of your thighs. If your lower back arches you have lost core tension. Stay tight and own every inch of the movement.
coach_guide.warning: Losing core tension shifts dangerous stress to your lower back.

name: self assisted inverse leg curl (on floor)
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your arms by your sides.
Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the ground, hip-width apart.
Lift your hips off the ground, engaging your glutes and hamstrings.
Slowly curl your legs towards your glutes, keeping your hips lifted.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly extend your legs back to the starting position.
Lower your hips back down to the ground.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Fight gravity with your hamstrings as you lower toward the floor.
coaching_cues: Secure your ankles and brace your core.
Lower yourself as slowly as possible forward.
Use hands to catch then hamstrings to return.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your hamstrings burning as they resist the fall. If you collapse quickly you need more hand assistance for now. Build up that eccentric strength.
coach_guide.warning: Collapsing forward can overload your hamstrings or lower back.

name: semi squat jump (male)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and lower your body into a squat position.
Jump explosively, extending your hips and knees while swinging your arms for momentum.
Land softly on the balls of your feet and immediately go into the next repetition.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drop into a partial squat then explode upward for quick powerful jumps.
coaching_cues: Bend knees to quarter squat position quickly.
Explode up driving through your whole foot.
Land soft with bent knees and repeat.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads and glutes firing with each explosive jump. If your knees cave inward push them out over your toes. Stay light on your feet and keep moving.
coach_guide.warning: Landing with locked knees puts high impact on your joints.

name: short stride run
instructions: Find an open space or a treadmill to perform the exercise.
Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart.
Start jogging in place, lifting your knees high and pumping your arms.
After a few seconds, start taking short strides forward, maintaining a quick pace.
Continue running with short strides for the desired duration or distance.

coach_guide.intro: Run with quick short steps to boost turnover and cardiovascular effort.
coaching_cues: Keep your steps quick and light underneath.
Stay tall with arms pumping at your sides.
Land midfoot and push off with each step.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart rate climb as your legs move rapidly. If you feel heavy you are overstriding so shorten up. Stay quick and keep that rhythm going.
coach_guide.warning: Overstriding slows you down and stresses your joints.

name: shoulder grip pull-up
instructions: Grab the pull-up bar with a shoulder-width grip, palms facing away from you.
Hang freely with your arms fully extended.
Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bar until your chin is above the bar.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Grip the bar with hands close together to challenge your lats and grip.
coaching_cues: Hands close together palms facing away from you.
Pull your chest up toward the bar.
Lower with control and full arm extension.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats and biceps working hard on every rep. If your shoulders shrug up you are using traps too much. Depress those shoulders and pull with your back.
coach_guide.warning: Shrugging shoulders reduces lat engagement and strains your neck.

name: shoulder tap
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body in a straight line.
Engage your core and lift your right hand off the ground, reaching across to tap your left shoulder.
Place your right hand back on the ground and repeat with your left hand tapping your right shoulder.
Continue alternating shoulder taps while keeping your hips and torso stable.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold a plank and tap each shoulder to challenge your core stability.
coaching_cues: Set up in a strong high plank.
Tap opposite shoulder without rotating your hips.
Keep your core locked tight the whole time.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core fighting to stay stable. If your hips rock side to side widen your feet. Stay solid like a plank of wood.
coach_guide.warning: Excessive hip rotation means your core is not braced properly.

name: shoulder tap push-up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
As you push back up, lift your right hand off the ground and tap your left shoulder.
Return your right hand to the ground and repeat the push-up, this time lifting your left hand and tapping your right shoulder.
Continue alternating shoulder taps with each push-up repetition.
Maintain a stable core and avoid excessive hip rotation throughout the exercise.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Perform a push up then tap each shoulder at the top for core challenge.
coaching_cues: Lower your chest to the floor with control.
Push up strong then tap opposite shoulder quickly.
Keep hips steady and alternate shoulder taps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working on the push and your core stabilizing on the tap. If you wobble too much slow down and widen your stance. You have got this keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the taps causes hip sway and reduces core benefit.

name: side bridge hip abduction
instructions: Lie on your side with your legs extended and stacked on top of each other.
Prop yourself up on your forearm, keeping your elbow directly below your shoulder.
Engage your core and lift your hips off the ground, creating a straight line from your head to your feet.
While keeping your core engaged, lift your top leg as high as possible without rotating your hips.
Pause for a moment at the top, then lower your leg back down.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Hold a strong side bridge while lifting your top leg to fire up your outer hip.
coaching_cues: Stack hips, keep body in straight line.
Lift top leg slowly, squeeze outer hip.
Lower with control, do not drop it.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a burn in the outer hip and glute of your top leg. Your obliques will also work hard to keep you stable. Stay strong and own that position.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag forward puts unnecessary strain on your lower back.

name: side bridge v. 2
instructions: Lie on your side with your legs extended and stacked on top of each other.
Place your forearm on the ground directly below your shoulder, with your elbow bent at a 90-degree angle.
Engage your core and lift your hips off the ground, creating a straight line from your head to your feet.
Hold this position for the desired amount of time.
Lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side.

coach_guide.intro: This side bridge variation challenges your core stability and shoulder strength together.
coaching_cues: Elbow directly under shoulder, hips stacked.
Push hips high, create a straight line.
Breathe steady, keep core tight throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques and deep core muscles working overtime to hold you steady. Your bottom shoulder will also engage to support you. Embrace the shake and stay lifted.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your hips causes your spine to curve and stresses your lower back.

name: side hip (on parallel bars)
instructions: Stand between two parallel bars with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Place your hands on the bars and lift your body off the ground, supporting your weight on your arms.
Engage your abs and slowly lift your legs to the side, keeping them straight.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the parallel bars to lift your hips sideways and target your obliques intensely.
coaching_cues: Grip bars firmly, arms stay straight.
Lift hips sideways, squeeze your obliques.
Lower slowly, control the entire movement.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep contraction in your side abs as you lift your hips. This is advanced work so respect the movement. Keep grinding and your core will thank you.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging or using momentum removes the work from your abs.

name: side hip abduction
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your hands on your hips.
Shift your weight to one leg and lift the opposite leg out to the side, keeping it straight.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your leg back down to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lift your leg straight out to the side like you're pushing through thick water.
coaching_cues: Leg stays straight and controlled.
Lift to the side, not forward.
Squeeze at the top, slow return.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this working on the outside of your hip and glute. If your back is compensating, you're lifting too high or too fast. Feel that outer hip activation and keep going with it.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning away or lifting too high makes your back do the work instead.

name: side lying floor stretch
instructions: Lie on your side with your legs straight and your bottom arm extended straight overhead.
Bend your top knee and place your foot on the ground in front of your bottom leg.
Reach your top arm over your head and grab onto something stable, like a wall or a piece of furniture.
Slowly lift your bottom leg off the ground, keeping it straight, until you feel a stretch in your side.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds, then slowly lower your leg back down.
Repeat on the other side.

coach_guide.intro: Lie on your side and reach overhead to create a deep stretch through your lats.
coaching_cues: Lie on your side, legs stacked comfortably.
Reach top arm overhead, lengthen your side.
Breathe deeply into the stretch, relax.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a long gentle stretch along the side of your back and ribcage. Let each breath help you sink deeper into the position. This is your time to release tension.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing the stretch too aggressively can strain your shoulder or back muscles.

name: side lying hip adduction (male)
instructions: Lie on your side with your legs straight and stacked on top of each other.
Place your bottom arm under your head for support.
Engage your adductors and lift your top leg as high as possible without rotating your hips or leaning backward.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your leg back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Lie on your side and lift your bottom leg to strengthen the inner thigh muscles.
coaching_cues: Top leg bent, foot flat on floor.
Lift bottom leg up, squeeze inner thigh.
Lower slowly, keep tension the whole time.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your inner thigh working hard to lift and control that leg. It is a small movement with big benefits for hip stability. Stay focused and feel the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Rotating your leg outward shifts the work away from your adductors.

name: side plank hip adduction
instructions: Start by lying on your side with your legs extended and stacked on top of each other.
Prop yourself up on your forearm, keeping your elbow directly below your shoulder.
Engage your core and lift your hips off the ground, creating a straight line from your head to your feet.
While maintaining the side plank position, lift your top leg towards the ceiling, keeping it straight.
Slowly lower your leg back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Hold a side plank while squeezing your top leg down to fire your inner thigh.
coaching_cues: Set up in strong side plank position.
Place top foot on elevated surface or hand.
Press down with top leg, squeeze inner thigh.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your inner thigh engage as you press down while your core fights to stay stable. This combination move builds real functional strength. You have got this.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips drop compromises your spine alignment and reduces effectiveness.

name: side push neck stretch
instructions: Stand or sit up straight with your shoulders relaxed.
Tilt your head to the right, bringing your right ear towards your right shoulder.
Place your right hand on the left side of your head and gently apply pressure to increase the stretch.
Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
Repeat on the other side, tilting your head to the left and applying pressure with your left hand.
Repeat the stretch 2-3 times on each side.

coach_guide.intro: Gently push your head to the side to release tension in your upper neck and shoulder.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, relax your shoulders down.
Tilt head sideways, add gentle hand pressure.
Hold steady, breathe and let muscles release.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a stretch along the side of your neck running toward your shoulder blade. This area holds a lot of stress so be patient. Breathe through it and feel the relief.
coach_guide.warning: Pushing too hard or too fast can strain the delicate muscles of your neck.

name: side push-up
instructions: Start by lying on your side with your legs extended and stacked on top of each other.
Place your bottom hand on the ground directly under your shoulder, fingers pointing forward.
Press through your bottom hand to lift your body off the ground, keeping your legs straight and your core engaged.
Extend your top arm straight up towards the ceiling, creating a straight line from your head to your heels.
Lower your body back down to the starting position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Lie on your side and push up to challenge your triceps from a unique angle.
coaching_cues: Lie on side, bottom arm wraps your waist.
Top hand flat on floor near chest.
Push up using top arm, squeeze triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps doing all the work to lift your upper body off the ground. It is a humbling movement that builds real arm strength. Keep pushing and stay consistent.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum or twisting your body takes the focus off your triceps.

name: side wrist pull stretch
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended in front of you.
Extend your right arm out to the side, parallel to the ground, with your palm facing down.
With your left hand, grab your right hand and gently pull it towards your body, feeling a stretch in your right forearm.
Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, then release.
Repeat on the other side.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your wrist gently to the side to stretch the muscles of your forearm.
coaching_cues: Extend one arm straight out in front.
Grab fingers, pull wrist gently to side.
Hold stretch, breathe and relax forearm muscles.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a stretch running through the outer or inner forearm depending on direction. This helps prevent tightness from gripping during workouts. Take your time and enjoy the release.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too aggressively can strain the small muscles and tendons in your wrist.

name: side-to-side chin
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp a pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Hang from the bar with your arms fully extended and your body relaxed.
Pull yourself up by bending your elbows and bringing your chin towards the bar, while keeping your body straight.
Once your chin is above the bar, lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Move side to side at the top of each chin up to challenge your lats through a greater range.
coaching_cues: Pull up, then shift toward one hand.
Pause briefly, then shift to other side.
Lower with control, repeat the pattern.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel each lat working individually as you shift your weight from side to side. This builds balanced strength and serious back definition. Stay controlled and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement or swinging reduces lat engagement and risks shoulder injury.

name: side-to-side toe touch (male)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended to the sides.
Bend at the waist to the right, reaching your right hand towards your right foot while keeping your left hand extended to the side.
Return to the starting position and then bend at the waist to the left, reaching your left hand towards your left foot while keeping your right hand extended to the side.
Repeat the side-to-side bending motion for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Reach side to side with control to fire up your obliques.
coaching_cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor.
Reach fingers toward your heel, crunch sideways.
Alternate sides with a smooth steady rhythm.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in the sides of your waist with each reach. If your neck strains, keep your chin slightly tucked. Let that oblique fire keep you moving.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling on your neck takes tension away from your abs.

name: single arm push-up
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart and one hand placed slightly wider than the other.
Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping your back straight.
As you lower yourself, shift your weight to one side and lift the opposite arm off the ground, extending it straight out to the side.
Push through your chest and triceps to raise your body back up to the starting position, while simultaneously lowering your extended arm back to the ground.
Repeat the movement, alternating the arm you extend with each repetition.

coach_guide.intro: Shift your weight to one arm and lower with total body tension.
coaching_cues: Spread fingers wide, grip the ground hard.
Keep hips square, do not rotate open.
Press up explosively through your palm.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest and tricep working overtime on the pushing arm. Your core has to stay rock solid to keep balance. Master this challenge one rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips twist puts dangerous strain on your shoulder.

name: single leg bridge with outstretched leg
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Extend one leg straight out in front of you.
Engage your glutes and lift your hips off the ground, forming a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Hold for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

coach_guide.intro: Drive through your grounded heel while keeping your extended leg straight.
coaching_cues: Plant foot flat, squeeze glute at top.
Lift hips until body forms straight line.
Keep extended leg parallel to the floor.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in the glute of your planted leg. The extended leg challenges your stability and hip flexor. Push through and own each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back shifts stress away from your glutes.

name: single leg calf raise (on a dumbbell)
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand.
Lift one foot off the ground and balance on the other foot.
Slowly raise your heel as high as possible, using your calf muscles.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other leg.

coach_guide.intro: Rise up tall on one foot to isolate each calf muscle fully.
coaching_cues: Ball of foot on dumbbell, heel hanging.
Rise up as high as possible slowly.
Lower with control, feel the deep stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in your calf and a stretch at the bottom. Hold something for balance so you can focus on the muscle. Build that single leg strength.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing through reps reduces muscle activation and risks injury.

name: single leg platform slide
instructions: Start by standing with one foot on a platform or slide board.
Bend your knee slightly and slide the foot on the platform backward, extending your leg.
Keep your core engaged and maintain a straight posture throughout the movement.
Slowly return to the starting position by sliding your foot back to the initial position.
Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

coach_guide.intro: Slide one leg back while staying strong through your standing leg.
coaching_cues: Keep standing knee slightly bent always.
Slide back with control, hinge at hips.
Drive through your heel to return up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your hamstring and glute working hard on the standing leg. The sliding motion challenges your balance and coordination. Stay controlled and feel every inch.
coach_guide.warning: Locking your standing knee puts unsafe pressure on the joint.

name: single leg squat (pistol) male
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms extended in front of you.
Lift your right foot off the ground and extend it forward.
Slowly lower your body down by bending your left knee and pushing your hips back.
Keep your chest up and your back straight as you lower yourself down.
Lower until your left thigh is parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your left heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

coach_guide.intro: Lower on one leg with control and drive up without assistance.
coaching_cues: Arms forward for balance, chest stays tall.
Sink hips back and down, knee tracks toe.
Drive through heel, squeeze glute at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire leg burning especially through your glute and quad. This move demands balance, strength, and focus all at once. Every rep is a victory.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your knee cave inward stresses ligaments dangerously.

name: sissy squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing slightly outward.
Hold onto a stable object for balance if needed.
Slowly lower your body by bending your knees and leaning back, keeping your torso upright.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or as far as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lean back and lower your hips to stretch and strengthen your quads.
coaching_cues: Rise onto toes, knees travel forward together.
Lower slowly, keep torso in straight line.
Push through toes to rise back up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense stretch and burn across your entire quad. Keep your core tight to maintain that straight body line. Embrace the burn and keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Going too deep too fast can strain your knee joints.

name: sit-up v. 2
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, slowly lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your torso is at a 45-degree angle.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl your torso up by squeezing your abs not pulling your neck.
coaching_cues: Feet flat on floor, knees bent comfortably.
Curl up leading with your chest forward.
Lower back down with control, no flopping.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper abs contracting hard through the entire movement. Keep space between chin and chest to protect your neck. Strong core, strong finish.
coach_guide.warning: Yanking on your head strains your neck and cheats your abs.

name: sit-up with arms on chest
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Cross your arms over your chest.
Engaging your abs, lift your upper body off the ground towards your knees.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Cross arms on your chest and curl up using pure ab strength.
coaching_cues: Press lower back down at the start.
Curl shoulders off the ground, exhale hard.
Lower with control, inhale on the way.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs doing all the work without arm momentum helping. This version keeps you honest with your core engagement. Stay focused and feel the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum robs your abs of the work they need.

name: skater hops
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees slightly and jump to the right, landing on your right foot.
As you land, swing your left leg behind your right leg and tap the ground with your left toes.
Immediately jump to the left, landing on your left foot.
As you land, swing your right leg behind your left leg and tap the ground with your right toes.
Continue alternating sides, jumping and tapping the ground with each leg.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Leap side to side landing softly to build power and cardio.
coaching_cues: Push off explosively from outside leg.
Land softly on opposite foot, absorb impact.
Swing arms naturally for balance and power.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart rate climbing and legs powering each jump. Land quietly to protect your knees and ankles every time. Keep hopping and enjoy the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Hard landings send shock straight into your knees and ankles.

name: ski ergometer
instructions: Adjust the seat height and footrests to a comfortable position.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
Sit up straight with your feet flat on the footrests.
Extend your arms straight out in front of you, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Engage your triceps and push the handles down towards your thighs.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the handles down explosively using your arms and core together.
coaching_cues: Hinge at hips, arms reach overhead.
Pull handles down past your thighs powerfully.
Snap hips forward as arms drive down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps burning along with your lats and core working as a unit. The movement should feel athletic and powerful. Keep that rhythm going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: ski step
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees slightly and keep your back straight.
Jump to the right, landing on your right foot while swinging your left leg behind your right leg.
Immediately jump to the left, landing on your left foot while swinging your right leg behind your left leg.
Continue alternating jumps from side to side, mimicking a skiing motion.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Step side to side with a low athletic stance while pumping your arms.
coaching_cues: Stay low in a half squat position.
Push off powerfully with each outside leg.
Swing arms opposite to legs rhythmically.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart rate climbing quickly as your legs start to burn. This move challenges your coordination and endurance simultaneously. Embrace the challenge and keep moving.
coach_guide.warning: Standing too tall reduces the cardio benefit significantly.

name: skin the cat
instructions: Start by hanging from a bar with your arms fully extended and your body relaxed.
Engage your core and lift your legs up, bringing your knees towards your chest.
Continue to lift your legs up and over your head, allowing your body to pass through the arms.
Once your legs are fully extended over your head, begin to lower them back down towards the starting position.
As you lower your legs, allow your body to pass back through the arms until you are hanging with your arms fully extended again.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hang from the bar and rotate your entire body backward through your arms.
coaching_cues: Start in a dead hang with tight grip.
Tuck knees and roll hips backward slowly.
Rotate through controlled, then reverse the motion.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense stretch through your shoulders and lats as you rotate. Your upper back and core work hard to control the movement. Trust your grip and enjoy the mobility.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing the rotation with tight shoulders risks serious injury.

name: sled 45 degrees one leg press
instructions: Adjust the sled machine to a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the sled machine with your back against the pad and your feet on the footplate.
Place one foot on the footplate and extend your leg, pushing the sled away from you.
Slowly bend your knee and lower the sled back to the starting position.
Repeat with the other leg.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press through one leg at a time focusing on driving through your heel.
coaching_cues: Position working foot center of platform.
Lower the weight with control, knee tracking toes.
Press up through heel, squeeze glute hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glute on the working side doing most of the effort. Each rep builds single leg strength and balance together. Stay focused and power through each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your knee cave inward strains the ligaments badly.

name: sled 45в° calf press
instructions: Adjust the sled machine to a 45-degree angle.
Place your feet on the sled platform with your toes pointing forward.
Push the sled platform away from you by extending your ankles and calves.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the sled platform back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Position your feet low on the platform and press using only your calves.
coaching_cues: Place balls of feet on platform edge.
Lower heels slowly for a deep calf stretch.
Press up high onto your toes powerfully.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense stretch at the bottom and strong contraction at the top. Your calves will be burning but thats the goal. Push through and build those lower legs.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom can tear your Achilles tendon.

name: sled 45в° leg press
instructions: Adjust the seat and footplate of the sled machine to a comfortable position.
Sit on the sled machine with your back against the backrest and your feet shoulder-width apart on the footplate.
Grip the handles on the sides of the seat for stability.
Push the footplate away from your body by extending your legs, keeping your heels on the footplate.
Continue pushing until your legs are almost fully extended, but without locking your knees.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the footplate back towards your body by bending your knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit back into the pad and press the platform away using your legs.
coaching_cues: Feet shoulder width apart on platform center.
Lower until thighs reach parallel or below.
Drive through heels, extend legs without locking.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads sharing the workload evenly. The supported position lets you push heavy safely. Dig deep and finish every rep strong.
coach_guide.warning: Locking your knees at the top risks hyperextension injury.

name: sled 45в° leg press (back pov)
instructions: Adjust the seat of the sled machine so that your knees are at a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the sled machine with your back against the backrest and your feet shoulder-width apart on the footplate.
Grip the handles on the sides of the seat for stability.
Push the footplate away from your body by extending your legs, keeping your heels on the footplate.
Pause for a moment at the fully extended position.
Slowly bend your knees and lower the footplate back towards your body, controlling the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Focus on pushing your hips into the pad as you drive the weight.
coaching_cues: Keep lower back pressed firmly into seat.
Control the descent, feel glutes stretch deep.
Explode up through heels with glute power.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes firing hard as you press the weight away. Your lower back stays protected when you maintain contact with the pad. Keep that form tight and crush it.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting your hips off the pad compresses your spine dangerously.

name: sled 45° leg press (side pov)
instructions: Adjust the seat of the sled machine so that your knees are at a 90-degree angle when your feet are on the footplate.
Sit on the sled machine with your back flat against the backrest and your feet shoulder-width apart on the footplate.
Grip the handles on the sides of the seat for stability.
Push against the footplate to extend your legs, straightening them completely.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly bend your knees to lower the footplate back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Watch your knee tracking and maintain steady controlled movement throughout.
coaching_cues: Knees track directly over your toes always.
Descend with a three count for control.
Press evenly through both feet, no wobbling.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads working together as you press. The side view helps you monitor your form perfectly. Stay patient and own every single rep.
coach_guide.warning: Knees caving inward puts serious stress on your joints.

name: sled 45в° leg wide press
instructions: Adjust the sled machine to a 45-degree angle.
Sit on the sled machine with your back against the pad and your feet on the foot platform.
Position your feet wider than shoulder-width apart.
Push against the foot platform to extend your legs and straighten your knees.
Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, then slowly bend your knees to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Place your feet wide on the platform to emphasize your inner thighs and glutes.
coaching_cues: Feet wide with toes pointed slightly outward.
Lower deep, feeling inner thighs stretch fully.
Drive through heels, squeeze glutes at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your inner thighs and glutes working more than a standard press. The wide stance targets different muscle fibers effectively. Embrace the deep stretch and push hard.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing too much depth can strain your hip flexors badly.

name: sled calf press on leg press
instructions: Adjust the seat of the leg press machine so that your knees are slightly bent when your feet are on the sled.
Place your feet shoulder-width apart on the sled, with your toes pointing forward.
Release the safety handles and push the sled away from you by extending your knees and ankles.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the sled back down by bending your knees and ankles.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the leg press to isolate your calves with heavy controlled resistance.
coaching_cues: Position only balls of feet on platform.
Drop heels low for maximum calf stretch.
Rise onto toes, hold the squeeze briefly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your calves working through a full range of motion. The machine allows you to load heavier than standing raises safely. Keep the tempo slow and feel every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum instead of muscle reduces effectiveness dramatically.

name: sled closer hack squat
instructions: Adjust the sled machine to a comfortable weight and position yourself with your back against the pad.
Place your feet shoulder-width apart on the platform, toes slightly pointed outwards.
Grip the handles on the sides of the machine for stability.
Engage your core and slowly lower your body by bending your knees and hips, keeping your back straight.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to extend your legs and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Place feet close together and sit deep to maximize glute activation.
coaching_cues: Feet narrow, toes pointed slightly out.
Lower until thighs pass parallel, control it.
Drive through heels, squeeze glutes at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your glutes and outer hips throughout the movement. If your knees cave inward, push them out over your toes. Keep that glute connection strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting knees collapse inward puts dangerous stress on your knee joints.

name: sled forward angled calf raise
instructions: Adjust the sled machine to a comfortable weight and position yourself on the machine with your toes on the platform and your heels hanging off.
Place your hands on the handles or the sides of the machine for support.
Engage your calves and slowly raise your heels as high as possible, pushing against the resistance of the sled.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Position your feet low on the platform with heels hanging off the edge.
coaching_cues: Balls of feet on platform, heels free.
Lower heels deep for a full stretch.
Push up high, pause and squeeze calves.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense stretch at the bottom and a strong contraction at the top of each rep. If you feel ankle discomfort, reduce the range slightly. Push through for powerful calves.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom can strain your Achilles tendon.

name: sled hack squat
instructions: Adjust the sled machine to a comfortable position for your height.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart on the platform, toes slightly pointed outwards.
Hold onto the handles or bars for stability.
Lower your body by bending your knees and hips, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to raise your body back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your back flat against the pad and lower with control.
coaching_cues: Feet shoulder width, mid platform position.
Sink hips deep, keep chest lifted high.
Press through heels, extend hips fully.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads working together through the full range. If lower back lifts off the pad, you are going too deep. Stay tight and power through.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back under load risks serious spinal injury.

name: sled lying calf press
instructions: Adjust the sled machine to a comfortable weight.
Sit on the sled machine with your back against the pad and your feet on the platform.
Place your toes and the balls of your feet on the edge of the platform, with your heels hanging off.
Push the platform away from you by extending your ankles, keeping your knees slightly bent.
Continue pushing until your calves are fully contracted.
Hold the contraction for a moment, then slowly lower the platform back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie back and position just the balls of your feet on the platform.
coaching_cues: Legs extended, only forefeet on platform.
Point toes away, push sled with calves.
Return slow, feel that deep calf stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your calves burning from the constant tension in this lying position. If cramping occurs, pause and flex your foot back. Finish strong with controlled reps.
coach_guide.warning: Locking knees completely can hyperextend and damage the joint.

name: sled lying squat
instructions: Adjust the sled machine to a comfortable weight.
Lie on your back on the sled machine with your feet on the footplate.
Position your feet shoulder-width apart and slightly angled outwards.
Grip the handles of the sled machine for stability.
Engage your glutes and core muscles.
Push through your heels and extend your legs to lift the sled.
Lower the sled back down by bending your knees and hips.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie flat with shoulders secure and focus on driving through your glutes.
coaching_cues: Feet flat, hip width on the platform.
Lower sled until hips nearly touch heels.
Explode up, squeeze glutes at lockout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes doing the heavy lifting with quad support throughout. If you feel lower back strain, check your foot position. Keep pushing and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting hips off the pad shifts load dangerously to your spine.

name: sled one leg calf press on leg press
instructions: Adjust the seat of the leg press machine so that your knees are slightly bent when your feet are on the sled.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet on the sled, shoulder-width apart.
Place your toes and the balls of your feet on the sled, keeping your heels off.
Push the sled forward by extending your ankles, keeping your knees slightly bent.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the sled back down by flexing your ankles.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Work one leg at a time to build balanced calf strength and stability.
coaching_cues: Single foot centered, ball on platform.
Lower heel slow for maximum stretch.
Press up powerfully, hold the squeeze.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel each calf working independently with nowhere to hide weakness. If balance is shaky, use lighter weight until stable. Build that single leg power.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing reps with momentum increases risk of calf and ankle strain.

name: sledge hammer
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the sledge hammer with both hands.
Engage your core and keep your back straight.
Swing the sledge hammer down towards the ground, using your core and upper body strength.
As you swing down, pivot your hips and transfer the force to the hammer.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Swing with your whole body and slam the hammer down with core power.
coaching_cues: Grip firm, feet wider than shoulder width.
Rotate hips and core into every slam.
Exhale hard on impact, reset quickly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core firing along with your shoulders and back. If your lower back aches, focus more on hip rotation. Channel that power and keep swinging.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging with arms only strains shoulders and misses the core benefit.

name: smith back shrug
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your arms straight and allow the barbell to hang in front of your thighs.
Lift your shoulders straight up towards your ears, squeezing your traps at the top.
Hold for a moment, then lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stand tall and shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears.
coaching_cues: Grip bar just outside your hips.
Shrug up and back, squeeze traps hard.
Lower with control, no rolling shoulders.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your traps contracting intensely at the top of each shrug. If neck feels strained, relax your head position. Build those powerful traps rep by rep.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling shoulders in circles puts unnecessary stress on the joint.

name: smith behind neck press
instructions: Adjust the seat height of the smith machine so that the bar is at shoulder level.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the bar off the rack and step back, maintaining a stable stance.
Lower the bar down to the back of your neck, keeping your elbows pointing forward.
Press the bar up overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the bar back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press from behind your neck with lighter weight and careful control.
coaching_cues: Grip wide, bar rests on upper traps.
Press straight up, keep core tight.
Lower to base of neck, elbows back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel rear and middle delts working hard through the press. If any sharp shoulder pain occurs, stop immediately and switch exercises. Stay controlled and feel the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Going too heavy or too deep severely strains the shoulder capsule.

name: smith bench press
instructions: Adjust the height of the smith machine bar to chest level.
Lie flat on the bench with your feet firmly planted on the ground.
Grip the bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the bar and lower it towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment when the bar touches your chest.
Push the bar back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the fixed bar path to focus purely on chest contraction.
coaching_cues: Grip slightly wider than shoulder width.
Lower bar to mid chest, elbows angled.
Press up strong, squeeze chest at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your pecs working without worrying about balancing the bar. If shoulders take over, widen your grip slightly. Trust the machine and push hard.
coach_guide.warning: Unracking or racking carelessly can cause wrist and shoulder injuries.

name: smith bent knee good morning
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing forward.
Place the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Lower your torso until it is parallel to the ground, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings.
Engage your glutes and hamstrings to raise your torso back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hinge at the hips while keeping your knees slightly bent to load your glutes.
coaching_cues: Push hips back, chest stays proud.
Feel the stretch deep in your hamstrings.
Squeeze glutes hard to stand tall.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings and a strong contraction in your glutes at the top. If your lower back is doing all the work, hinge more from the hips. Keep that glute connection strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: smith bent over row
instructions: Set up the smith machine with the bar at hip height.
Stand facing the bar with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Pull the bar towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the bar back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the bar toward your belly button while keeping your back flat and core tight.
coaching_cues: Hinge forward, back flat, core braced.
Drive elbows back, squeeze shoulder blades together.
Lower with control, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles working hard as you pull and squeeze. If your biceps are burning out first, focus on leading with your elbows. Build that strong back.
coach_guide.warning: Jerking the weight up strains your lower back and limits gains.

name: smith chair squat
instructions: Adjust the height of the smith machine bar to a comfortable position.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Place the barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you slowly lower your body by bending your knees and hips.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit back onto an imaginary chair behind you to target your quads safely.
coaching_cues: Feet slightly forward, shoulder width apart.
Lower until thighs are parallel, pause.
Drive through heels, squeeze quads at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads burning as you push out of the bottom position. If your knees cave inward, push them out over your toes. You have got this.
coach_guide.warning: Letting knees collapse inward puts harmful stress on your joints.

name: smith close-grip bench press
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the barbell with a close grip, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Lower the barbell towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows tucked close to target the triceps on every rep.
coaching_cues: Grip narrow, hands about shoulder width.
Lower bar to lower chest, elbows tight.
Press up, lock out, squeeze triceps hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps doing most of the pushing work throughout the movement. If your shoulders take over, bring your grip slightly narrower. Finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring your elbows shifts stress away from triceps to shoulders.

name: smith deadlift
instructions: Set up the smith machine with the bar at hip height.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Bend at the hips and knees, keeping your back straight and chest up, and grip the bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and lift the bar by extending your hips and knees, keeping the bar close to your body.
Stand up straight, fully extending your hips and knees.
Lower the bar back down by bending at the hips and knees, maintaining control and keeping your back straight.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive through your heels and squeeze your glutes hard at the top of every rep.
coaching_cues: Stand close to bar, feet hip width.
Push hips back, grab bar, chest up.
Drive hips forward, stand tall, squeeze glutes.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and hamstrings powering you up to standing. If your lower back feels strained, reset your hip hinge. Strong glutes make everything better.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back during the lift risks serious spinal injury.

name: smith decline bench press
instructions: Adjust the decline bench to the desired angle.
Lie down on the bench with your feet secured under the foot pads.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The decline angle shifts more work to your lower chest so focus there.
coaching_cues: Secure legs, unrack with control, breathe in.
Lower to lower chest, elbows at 45.
Press up explosively, squeeze your chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower chest muscles firing hard on every press. If your shoulders are taking over, tuck your elbows a bit more. Keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing the bar off your chest risks rib and sternum injury.

name: smith decline reverse-grip press
instructions: Adjust the smith machine to a decline position.
Lie down on the bench with your feet secured under the foot pads.
Grasp the barbell with a reverse grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The reverse grip shifts focus to your upper chest and front delts.
coaching_cues: Grip underhand, wrists straight and strong.
Lower slowly to your lower chest area.
Press up, squeeze upper chest at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper chest and front shoulders working together on this movement. If your wrists feel strained, adjust your grip width slightly. You are building a complete chest.
coach_guide.warning: Weak wrist position can cause the bar to slip dangerously.

name: smith front squat (clean grip)
instructions: Set up the smith machine with the barbell at shoulder height.
Stand facing the barbell with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step back and position the barbell on your front shoulders, resting it on your collarbone and deltoids.
Keep your chest up, back straight, and core engaged.
Lower your body by bending at the knees and hips, as if sitting back into a chair.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows high and core tight to stay upright throughout the squat.
coaching_cues: Elbows up high, bar rests on shoulders.
Sit straight down, keep torso vertical.
Drive up through heels, squeeze glutes.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads and glutes working hard while your core keeps you stable. If you lean forward, raise those elbows higher. Great work staying tall.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping your elbows causes you to fold forward dangerously.

name: smith full squat
instructions: Set up the smith machine with the barbell at shoulder height.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Step under the bar and position it across your upper back, resting on your traps.
Grip the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the bar and take a step back, maintaining a stable stance.
Keeping your chest up and core engaged, initiate the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Lower your body until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as your flexibility allows.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then drive through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Go deep to maximize glute activation while keeping your core braced throughout.
coaching_cues: Feet shoulder width, toes slightly out.
Descend below parallel with control.
Drive up powerfully, squeeze glutes at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your glutes at the bottom and a strong squeeze at the top. If your heels rise, work on ankle mobility. Depth builds strength.
coach_guide.warning: Rising on your toes shifts stress to your knees dangerously.

name: smith hack squat
instructions: Adjust the barbell on the smith machine to an appropriate height for your body.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly pointed outwards.
Position yourself under the barbell, resting it on your upper traps and shoulders.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you unrack the barbell.
Take a step back and position your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and lower your body down, keeping your chest up and back straight.
Continue descending until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Position your feet forward to shift the work onto your glutes and hamstrings.
coaching_cues: Feet forward, lean back into the bar.
Lower until thighs are parallel, pause.
Drive through heels, squeeze glutes hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and hamstrings doing the heavy lifting on every rep. If your quads dominate, step your feet further forward. Strong glutes coming your way.
coach_guide.warning: Placing feet too far back shifts all stress onto your knees.

name: smith hip raise
instructions: Position yourself on the smith machine with your back against the pad and your feet shoulder-width apart.
Place your hands on the barbell, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and glutes, then push through your heels to raise your hips off the ground.
Continue lifting until your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Hold for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Position your hips under the bar and use your abs to lift.
coaching_cues: Grip bar, feet flat, hips underneath.
Curl hips up, pressing lower back down.
Lower with control, keep abs tight throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your lower abs as you lift your hips. If your lower back is doing the work, reset your position. Keep that core fired up.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your legs uses momentum instead of your abs.

name: smith incline bench press
instructions: Adjust the bench to a 30-45 degree incline.
Sit on the bench with your back flat against the pad and feet firmly on the ground.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your upper chest, keeping your elbows slightly tucked in.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The incline angle shifts the focus to your upper chest so drive through that area.
coaching_cues: Set bench at 30 to 45 degrees.
Lower bar to upper chest, elbows tucked.
Press straight up, squeezing chest at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper chest and front shoulders working hard on each rep. If your shoulders take over, lower the angle slightly. Push strong through your chest.
coach_guide.warning: Going too heavy causes your back to arch excessively.

name: smith incline reverse-grip press
instructions: Adjust the seat of the smith machine to a comfortable incline angle.
Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip that is slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the bar and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment when the bar is just above your chest.
Push the bar back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The reverse grip targets your upper chest differently so keep your wrists strong.
coaching_cues: Grip bar underhand, hands shoulder width apart.
Lower slowly to your upper chest line.
Press up while keeping elbows close in.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a unique squeeze in your upper pecs and some bicep engagement. If your wrists hurt, adjust your grip width. This variation builds serious upper chest.
coach_guide.warning: Weak wrist position can cause the bar to slip dangerously.

name: smith incline shoulder raises
instructions: Adjust the smith machine to an incline position.
Stand facing the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and core engaged.
Raise the barbell up towards the ceiling, leading with your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This isolation move targets the serratus so focus on reaching forward at the top.
coaching_cues: Lie on incline, arms extended holding bar.
Push shoulders forward, reaching toward ceiling.
Control the return, feel the stretch back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the muscles along your ribcage activating with each reach. If you feel it only in your shoulders, reach further forward. Build that athletic frame.
coach_guide.warning: Using too much weight turns this into a pressing movement.

name: smith leg press
instructions: Adjust the seat and footplate of the smith machine to a comfortable position.
Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your feet shoulder-width apart on the footplate.
Grasp the handles or sides of the machine for stability.
Push the footplate away from you by extending your legs, keeping your back against the backrest.
Pause for a moment at the fully extended position.
Slowly bend your knees and lower the footplate back towards you, returning to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lying under the smith bar lets you really load your glutes and legs.
coaching_cues: Feet high on platform, shoulder width apart.
Lower knees toward chest with control slowly.
Drive through heels, squeeze glutes at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads burning with each rep. If your lower back lifts off, bring your feet higher. Power through those legs.
coach_guide.warning: Placing feet too low puts excessive stress on your knees.

name: smith low bar squat
instructions: Set up the smith machine with the barbell at a height that allows you to comfortably rest it on your upper back.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned outwards.
Step under the bar and position it across your upper back, resting it on your traps.
Grip the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the bar by straightening your legs and stepping back from the rack.
Take a deep breath and brace your core.
Initiate the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Lower your body until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Keep your chest up and your back straight throughout the movement.
Drive through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The low bar position lets you hinge more and target your glutes.
coaching_cues: Bar sits low across rear delts securely.
Hinge hips back, keep your chest lifted.
Drive up through heels, squeeze glutes hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and hamstrings working as you stand up. If your back rounds, reduce the weight and reset. Own every single rep.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back under load risks serious injury.

name: smith machine bicep curl
instructions: Adjust the height of the smith machine bar to be at waist level.
Stand facing the smith machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and your upper arms stationary.
Exhale and curl the bar up towards your shoulders, contracting your biceps.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The fixed bar path lets you focus purely on squeezing your biceps.
coaching_cues: Stand close, underhand grip shoulder width apart.
Curl bar up, keeping elbows pinned back.
Lower slowly, resist the weight going down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps working through the entire range of motion. If your shoulders roll forward, stand taller and reset. Get that peak contraction.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning back turns this into a momentum cheat curl.

name: smith machine decline close grip bench press
instructions: Adjust the bench on the smith machine to a decline position.
Lie down on the bench with your feet firmly planted on the ground.
Grasp the barbell with a close grip, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Pause for a moment when the barbell is just above your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
