Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The decline close grip hammers your triceps so keep elbows tight throughout.
coaching_cues: Hands close together, about six inches apart.
Lower bar to lower chest, elbows tucked.
Press up explosively, lockout and squeeze triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps burning especially at lockout on each rep. If your elbows flare, bring your hands slightly wider. Finish every rep strong.
coach_guide.warning: Grip too narrow puts dangerous stress on your wrists.

name: smith machine incline tricep extension
instructions: Adjust the seat of the smith machine so that the bar is at shoulder height.
Sit on the bench with your back against the pad and your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms fully, lifting the bar off the rack and holding it directly above your chest.
Lower the bar slowly towards your forehead, keeping your elbows close to your head.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the bar back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The incline position stretches your triceps deeply for maximum growth.
coaching_cues: Sit back on incline, arms extended above.
Bend elbows, lowering bar behind your head.
Extend arms fully, squeeze triceps at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your triceps at the bottom of each rep. If your shoulders ache, adjust the bench angle slightly. Push through for strong arms.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping the bar too fast strains your elbow joints.

name: smith machine reverse decline close grip bench press
instructions: Adjust the smith machine to a decline position.
Lie down on the bench with your feet secured under the foot pads.
Grasp the barbell with a close grip, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The reverse grip on decline really isolates your upper and inner chest.
coaching_cues: Grip bar underhand, secure your legs tightly.
Lower bar with control toward lower chest.
Press up, focusing on squeezing chest together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your inner and upper chest working hard on each rep. If your wrists feel strained, widen your grip slightly. Keep pushing for a complete chest.
coach_guide.warning: Unsecured legs on the decline can cause you to slide.

name: smith narrow row
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly narrower than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and your chest up as you pull the handles towards your body, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull with a narrow grip to target your inner upper back muscles.
coaching_cues: Grip narrow, chest pressed against pad.
Pull elbows straight back, squeeze hard.
Lower with control, feel the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep squeeze between your shoulder blades at the top. If your biceps burn out first, focus on leading with your elbows. Keep pulling strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back removes tension from the target muscles.

name: smith one arm row
instructions: Adjust the height of the smith machine bar to waist level.
Stand facing the smith machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Grasp the bar with one hand using an overhand grip, with your palm facing down.
Keep your elbow close to your body and pull the bar towards your waist, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Single arm rows let you focus on one side at a time for balanced strength.
coaching_cues: Brace core, keep hips square throughout.
Pull elbow high and back toward hip.
Squeeze lat hard, then lower slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lat working hard on each pull with a strong contraction at the top. If you are rotating your torso, go lighter and stay stable. Great work isolating each side.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting your body turns this into a momentum cheat.

name: smith one leg floor calf raise
instructions: Position yourself on the floor under the smith machine bar, facing away from the machine.
Place the balls of your feet on a raised surface, such as a weight plate or block.
Position the smith machine bar across your lower legs, just above your ankles.
Hold onto the bar with your hands for stability.
Raise your heels off the ground by extending your ankles, lifting your body up.
Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Standing on one leg challenges your calf and builds single leg balance.
coaching_cues: Ball of foot on edge, heel hanging.
Rise up high, squeeze calf at top.
Lower heel below platform, feel stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn through your entire calf muscle. If you wobble, lightly touch the machine for balance only. Push through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Going too fast robs your calf of the tension it needs.

name: smith rear delt row
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the machine with your chest against the pad and your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged as you pull the handles towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, then slowly release the handles back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This row variation isolates your rear delts for stronger shoulders.
coaching_cues: Elbows flared high, grip wider than normal.
Pull toward upper chest, squeeze rear delts.
Control the bar down, stay tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn right in the back of your shoulders near your shoulder blades. If your traps take over, lower the weight and focus on the squeeze. Keep targeting those rear delts.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too low shifts the work away from rear delts.

name: smith reverse calf raises
instructions: Adjust the smith machine bar to a height just below your shoulders.
Stand facing the bar with your feet hip-width apart and toes pointing forward.
Place the balls of your feet on the edge of a step or platform, with your heels hanging off.
Hold onto the bar for support, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Raise your heels as high as possible, lifting your body weight onto the balls of your feet.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Reverse calf raises strengthen the front of your lower leg.
coaching_cues: Heels on edge, toes hanging below.
Lift toes up high, squeeze shin muscles.
Lower slowly, get a full stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the muscles on the front of your shins working hard. This is an unfamiliar burn but it builds ankle stability. Stay with it.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing through reps reduces effectiveness and risks strain.

name: smith reverse calf raises
instructions: Adjust the smith machine bar to a height just below your shoulders.
Stand facing the bar with your feet hip-width apart and toes pointing forward.
Place the balls of your feet on the edge of a step or platform, with your heels hanging off.
Hold onto the bar for support.
Raise your heels as high as possible, lifting your body up onto your toes.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Target the front of your shins with controlled toe raises.
coaching_cues: Stand with heels on platform edge.
Pull toes up toward shins, pause.
Lower toes down slowly, repeat smoothly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a burning sensation along the front of your lower legs. This exercise helps prevent shin splints and improves balance. Keep going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum makes this exercise far less effective.

name: smith reverse grip bent over row
instructions: Set up the smith machine with the bar at hip height.
Stand facing the bar with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Pull the bar towards your lower chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the bar back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The underhand grip shifts focus to your lower lats and biceps.
coaching_cues: Grip underhand, hands shoulder width apart.
Pull bar to lower stomach, squeeze lats.
Lower with control, keep back flat.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong contraction in your lower lats and a good bicep pump. If your lower back rounds, lighten the load. Build that V-taper.
coach_guide.warning: Excessive torso swing stresses your lower back.

name: smith reverse-grip press
instructions: Adjust the height of the smith machine bar to chest level.
Stand facing the bar with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Step back and position yourself with a slight bend in your knees.
Keep your chest up and core engaged throughout the exercise.
Lower the bar towards your chest, keeping your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push the bar back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Reverse grip pressing shifts emphasis to your upper chest and triceps.
coaching_cues: Grip underhand, wrists stacked over elbows.
Lower bar to upper chest, elbows tucked.
Press up powerfully, squeeze chest hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper chest and triceps doing most of the work. If your wrists feel strained, adjust grip width slightly. Keep pressing strong.
coach_guide.warning: Going too heavy with this grip risks wrist injury.

name: smith seated one leg calf raise
instructions: Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and your feet on the footrest.
Place one leg on the footrest and keep the other leg off the footrest.
Using your calf muscles, raise your heel as high as possible.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heel back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Seated single leg calf raises build symmetrical lower leg strength.
coaching_cues: Pad on knee, ball of foot planted.
Press up through big toe, squeeze hard.
Lower heel fully, stretch the calf.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an isolated burn deep in your calf muscle with each rep. If one side is weaker, give it extra attention. Build balanced calves.
coach_guide.warning: Cutting range of motion limits your muscle development.

name: smith seated shoulder press
instructions: Adjust the seat height so that the handles are at shoulder level.
Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and your feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and lift them off the supports, extending your arms fully.
Lower the handles down to shoulder level, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Press the handles up overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the handles back down to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The smith machine lets you press heavy with added stability overhead.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, core braced, back supported.
Press straight up, do not lock elbows.
Lower to chin level, control the descent.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders working hard especially the front and middle heads. If your lower back arches, tighten your core more. Strong shoulders start here.
coach_guide.warning: Pressing behind your neck puts your shoulder joints at risk.

name: smith seated wrist curl
instructions: Sit on a bench in front of a Smith machine with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the barbell with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Rest your forearms on your thighs, allowing your wrists to hang off.
Slowly curl your wrists upward, bringing the barbell towards your forearms.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Let your wrists curl down fully then squeeze your forearms to lift.
coaching_cues: Palms up, forearms resting on your thighs.
Lower the bar slowly, feel the stretch.
Curl wrists up, squeeze forearms at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your inner forearms with every rep. If your biceps are taking over, lighten the weight and focus on wrist movement only. Keep chasing that forearm pump.
coach_guide.warning: Jerky movements can strain your wrist joints quickly.

name: smith shoulder press
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself on the smith machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the bar off the rack and position it at shoulder level, with your elbows bent and palms facing forward.
Press the bar upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the bar back down to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press straight up while keeping your core tight and back flat on the pad.
coaching_cues: Grip just outside shoulder width, elbows forward.
Lower bar to chin level, stay controlled.
Press up powerfully, do not lock elbows.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders burning especially at the front and sides. If your lower back arches, brace your core harder. Push through and build those strong shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Pressing behind your neck puts dangerous stress on shoulder joints.

name: smith shrug
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your arms straight and your shoulders relaxed.
Lift your shoulders up towards your ears, squeezing your traps at the top.
Hold for a moment, then slowly lower your shoulders back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears and squeeze at the top.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, arms straight, grip shoulder width.
Shrug up high, hold the squeeze briefly.
Lower slowly, feel the stretch in traps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your traps working hard from your neck to mid back. If your arms are bending, you are using too much weight. Keep those traps burning strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rolling your shoulders can damage the rotator cuff over time.

name: smith single leg split squat
instructions: Stand in front of the smith machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Place one foot behind you on a bench or step, with your toes pointing forward.
Hold onto the smith machine bar for stability.
Bend your front knee and lower your body down into a lunge position, keeping your back straight.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

coach_guide.intro: Drop your back knee down while keeping your front knee stacked over your ankle.
coaching_cues: Front foot forward, back foot on bench.
Lower straight down, chest stays tall throughout.
Drive through front heel, squeeze your glute.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your front quad and glute doing all the work. If your knee caves inward, push it out over your toes. Stay balanced and finish every rep strong.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning forward puts excessive pressure on your front knee.

name: smith sprint lunge
instructions: Set up the smith machine with the barbell at hip height.
Stand facing away from the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Step back with your right foot and place it on the barbell, resting the top of your foot on the bar.
Bend your left knee and lower your body into a lunge position, keeping your back straight.
Push through your left heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, stepping back with your left foot.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode into alternating lunges while keeping the bar stable across your back.
coaching_cues: Start standing tall, core braced and tight.
Lunge forward explosively, switch legs fast.
Land soft, stay controlled through each switch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart rate spike while your glutes and quads burn. If you feel unstable, slow down the tempo. Keep that explosive energy going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing without control can cause you to twist your knee.

name: smith squat
instructions: Set up the smith machine with the barbell at an appropriate height for your squat.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Position yourself under the barbell, resting it on your upper traps and shoulders.
Grip the barbell with a wide grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and unrack the barbell, stepping back to clear the rack.
Keeping your chest up and back straight, initiate the squat by bending at the hips and knees.
Lower your body until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly below.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then drive through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit back and down like sitting into a chair then drive up through your heels.
coaching_cues: Feet shoulder width, toes slightly turned out.
Sit back deep, keep your chest lifted.
Drive up through heels, squeeze glutes hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads working together throughout. If your heels lift, sit back more into your hips. Own every rep and build that strength.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your knees cave inward stresses your knee ligaments badly.

name: smith standing back wrist curl
instructions: Stand facing the smith machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and your elbows close to your body.
Slowly curl your wrists upwards, bringing the barbell towards your body.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl the bar up by bending your wrists back and squeezing your outer forearms.
coaching_cues: Stand with bar behind you, palms backward.
Curl wrists up, squeeze the top hard.
Lower with control, feel the forearm stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your outer forearms and grip working intensely. If your shoulders shrug up, relax them down. Keep squeezing for those strong forearms.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum takes tension off the forearms completely.

name: smith standing behind head military press
instructions: Adjust the seat height of the smith machine so that the bar is at shoulder level.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the bar off the rack and step back, maintaining a stable stance.
Position the bar behind your head, resting on your upper traps.
Keep your core engaged and your chest lifted throughout the exercise.
Press the bar overhead by extending your arms, fully straightening them.
Pause briefly at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the bar back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press from behind your head only if you have excellent shoulder mobility.
coaching_cues: Lower bar to base of skull, controlled.
Keep elbows pointing outward the whole time.
Press up strong, stop just before lockout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel intense work in your rear and side delts. If any sharp pain occurs, switch to front press immediately. Push through with good form and stay safe.
coach_guide.warning: This exercise is risky for those with limited shoulder mobility.

name: smith standing leg calf raise
instructions: Adjust the smith machine bar to a height that allows you to stand with your feet flat on the ground and your shoulders under the bar.
Position yourself under the bar with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing forward.
Place your hands on the bar for stability.
Engage your calves and slowly raise your heels off the ground, lifting your body up onto your toes.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rise up onto your toes as high as possible and squeeze your calves hard.
coaching_cues: Shoulders under pads, stand on balls of feet.
Rise up high, pause and squeeze at top.
Lower heels below the platform, feel stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your calves burning from ankle to knee. If you bounce at the bottom, slow down for better results. Keep pushing for those strong calves.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom can strain your Achilles tendon.

name: smith standing military press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lift the barbell off the rack and bring it down to shoulder level, with your palms facing forward.
Press the barbell upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to shoulder level.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press the bar straight up in front of your face with a strong core brace.
coaching_cues: Grip just outside shoulders, elbows forward slightly.
Lower to chin level, keep core super tight.
Press straight up, exhale as you push.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your front and side delts working with every press. If your back arches, squeeze your abs and glutes harder. Finish strong and build those boulder shoulders.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: smith sumo squat
instructions: Set up the smith machine with the barbell at hip height.
Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing outwards.
Position yourself under the barbell, resting it on your upper back and shoulders.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your body down into a squat position, pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Lower yourself until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Set a wide stance under the bar and drive through your heels to target your glutes.
coaching_cues: Toes pointed out, knees track over toes.
Sink hips back and down below parallel.
Squeeze glutes hard at the top lockout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your inner thighs and a strong squeeze in your glutes at the top. If your knees cave inward, widen your stance slightly. Keep pushing through those heels.
coach_guide.warning: Knees collapsing inward puts dangerous stress on your knee joints.

name: smith toe raise
instructions: Adjust the smith machine bar to a height that allows you to comfortably stand with your feet flat on the ground.
Position yourself under the bar with your shoulders directly below it and your feet shoulder-width apart.
Place the balls of your feet on a raised platform or weight plates, with your heels hanging off the edge.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and keep your back straight throughout the exercise.
Slowly raise your heels as high as possible, lifting your body weight onto the balls of your feet.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Position the bar across your shoulders and rise onto your toes for a full calf contraction.
coaching_cues: Stand tall with core braced and engaged.
Rise up slowly onto the balls of feet.
Pause at top, lower with slow control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in both calf muscles at the peak contraction. If you feel strain in your Achilles, reduce the range of motion. Keep squeezing for strong calves.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom can strain your Achilles tendon badly.

name: smith upright row
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, facing the smith machine.
Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
Pull the barbell up towards your chin, leading with your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the bar straight up along your body leading with your elbows to hit your shoulders.
coaching_cues: Grip slightly narrower than shoulder width apart.
Lead with elbows, pull bar to chest.
Control the bar down, do not drop.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your side delts working hard as your elbows rise high. If you feel pinching in your shoulders, use a wider grip. Keep those elbows driving up.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too high with a narrow grip can impinge your shoulder.

name: smith wide grip bench press
instructions: Adjust the bench to a comfortable position on the smith machine.
Lie down on the bench with your feet flat on the ground.
Grasp the barbell with a wide grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows out to the sides.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the wide grip to stretch your chest and drive the bar up with power.
coaching_cues: Grip wide, wrists stacked over elbows always.
Lower bar to mid chest with control.
Press up explosively, squeeze your chest hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a big stretch across your entire chest at the bottom position. If your shoulders take over, tuck your elbows slightly more. Own that wide grip stretch.
coach_guide.warning: Going too heavy with a wide grip strains your shoulder joints.

name: smith wide grip decline bench press
instructions: Adjust the decline bench to the desired angle.
Lie down on the bench with your feet secured under the foot pads.
Grasp the barbell with a wide grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the barbell and lower it slowly towards your chest, keeping your elbows pointed outwards.
Pause for a moment when the barbell touches your chest.
Push the barbell back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The decline angle with wide grip hits your lower chest with an intense stretch.
coaching_cues: Lock legs in tight, stay firmly planted.
Lower bar to lower chest line slowly.
Drive up with chest, keep shoulders back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower pecs working through the entire range of motion. If you feel unstable, squeeze your legs into the pads harder. Finish every rep strong.
coach_guide.warning: Racking the bar carelessly on decline is very dangerous.

name: snatch pull
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell on the ground in front of you.
Bend your knees and hinge at the hips to lower into a squat position, gripping the barbell with an overhand grip.
Keep your back straight and chest up as you drive through your heels to lift the barbell off the ground, extending your hips and knees.
As the barbell reaches hip level, explosively pull it upwards, shrugging your shoulders and pulling your elbows high and to the sides.
As the barbell reaches its highest point, quickly drop underneath it, pulling yourself into a deep squat position.
Catch the barbell overhead with your arms fully extended and your knees bent.
Stand up by extending your hips and knees, keeping the barbell overhead.
Lower the barbell back down to the starting position by reversing the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive explosively through your legs and hips to pull the bar high with power.
coaching_cues: Start with hips low, chest up proud.
Explode hips forward, shrug bar up high.
Keep bar close to body entire pull.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads and hips driving the bar upward with serious power. If the bar drifts forward, focus on keeping elbows high. Channel that explosive energy.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back under load risks serious disc injury.

name: spell caster
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the waist, keeping your back straight.
Extend your arm with the dumbbell towards the opposite foot, rotating your torso as you do so.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Swing the dumbbell across your body in a controlled arc to fire up your core.
coaching_cues: Grip dumbbell with both hands firmly locked.
Rotate through core, not just your arms.
Control the swing at each side fully.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques and entire core working hard through the rotation. If your lower back aches, reduce the weight and brace harder. Keep that core tight and controlled.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum instead of core control strains your lower back.

name: sphinx
instructions: Lie face down on the ground with your forearms flat on the floor, elbows directly under your shoulders.
Engage your core and lift your chest off the ground, keeping your forearms and toes on the floor.
Hold this position for a few seconds, making sure to keep your neck in a neutral position.
Slowly lower your chest back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lie on your stomach and gently lift your chest to open up your spine.
coaching_cues: Forearms flat, elbows directly under shoulders.
Lift chest gently, keep hips grounded down.
Breathe deep and relax into the stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a gentle extension through your entire lower and mid back. If you feel pinching, lower your chest closer to the ground. Let your spine open up naturally.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing the extension too far can compress your lower spine.

name: spider crawl push up
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Bring your right knee towards your right elbow, keeping it off the ground.
As you bring your right knee back, simultaneously lower your body towards the ground by bending your elbows.
Push back up to the starting position and repeat with your left knee towards your left elbow.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Crawl your leg forward with each push up to fire your glutes and core together.
coaching_cues: Lower into push up with solid form.
Drive knee toward elbow, squeeze your glute.
Alternate sides with each rep you do.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes activate as your knee drives forward each rep. If your hips sag, engage your core more aggressively. Keep crawling and stay powerful.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips drop puts excessive strain on your lower back.

name: spine stretch
instructions: Sit on the ground with your legs extended in front of you.
Place your hands on the ground behind you, fingers pointing towards your body.
Engage your core and slowly lean back, keeping your back straight.
Continue leaning back until you feel a stretch in your spine.
Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit tall and reach forward to lengthen your entire spine with control.
coaching_cues: Sit with legs extended straight out front.
Round forward slowly, reaching past your toes.
Breathe out as you stretch deeper forward.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a gentle stretch along your entire back and hamstrings. If you feel sharp pain, bend your knees slightly to reduce tension. Relax into it and breathe.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing the stretch with a jerky motion can strain your back.

name: spine twist
instructions: Sit on the ground with your legs extended in front of you.
Bend your knees and place your feet flat on the ground, hip-width apart.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engage your abs and slowly twist your torso to the right, bringing your right elbow towards your left knee.
Pause for a moment at the end of the twist, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat the twist to the left side, bringing your left elbow towards your right knee.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate through your spine while keeping your hips completely still and stable.
coaching_cues: Sit tall, lengthen your spine upward.
Rotate from ribcage, hips stay anchored.
Twist further on exhale, return slow.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep rotation through your midsection and obliques. Your lower back should stay neutral without any compression. Embrace the stretch and control.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting from your lower back instead of mid spine causes strain.

name: split squats
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Take a step forward with one foot and place it about two feet in front of the other foot.
Lower your body by bending your knees and hips, keeping your back straight.
Continue lowering until your front thigh is parallel to the ground, and your back knee is hovering just above the ground.
Pause for a moment, then push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Drop straight down between your legs keeping your front knee stacked over your ankle.
coaching_cues: Front shin stays vertical throughout movement.
Lower until back knee nearly touches ground.
Push through front heel to stand up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your front quad and glute doing most of the work. Your back leg provides balance not power. Keep grinding through each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your front knee drift forward stresses the knee joint.

name: squat jerk
instructions: Start with the barbell resting on your shoulders, with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower your body into a squat position, keeping your chest up and your knees tracking over your toes.
As you reach the bottom of the squat, explosively drive through your legs and push the barbell overhead.
As the barbell reaches its peak, quickly drop into a split position, with one foot forward and one foot back.
Catch the barbell overhead with your arms fully extended and your back knee slightly touching the ground.
Stand up from the split position, bringing your feet back together.
Lower the barbell back to your shoulders and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Catch the bar overhead in a full squat position with arms locked out completely.
coaching_cues: Dip and drive the bar explosively.
Drop under fast, catch in deep squat.
Stand up with bar locked overhead.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel explosive power from your legs and stability through your shoulders. This is a full body challenge requiring speed and timing. Trust your power.
coach_guide.warning: Catching the bar with soft elbows puts dangerous stress on shoulders.

name: squat on bosu ball
instructions: Place the bosu ball on the ground with the flat side up.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and position yourself on top of the bosu ball.
Lower your body down by bending your knees and hips, as if sitting back into a chair.
Keep your chest up and your weight on your heels.
Lower yourself until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The unstable surface forces your core and stabilizers to work overtime.
coaching_cues: Step on center, find your balance.
Squat slow, control the wobble throughout.
Drive up evenly through both feet.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your ankles, core, and quads working hard to stabilize. The instability makes this much harder than it looks. Stay focused and own it.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing the movement can cause you to fall off the ball.

name: squat to overhead reach
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Lower your body down into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
As you come up from the squat, extend your arms overhead, reaching towards the ceiling.
Return to the starting position by lowering your arms and bending your knees to squat down again.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Combine a squat with an overhead reach to mobilize your whole body.
coaching_cues: Squat deep with arms reaching forward.
Stand and reach both arms overhead.
Extend fully, squeeze glutes at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads power the squat while your shoulders open at the top. This move wakes up your entire body beautifully. Let the energy flow through.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back at the top strains your spine.

name: squat to overhead reach with twist
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
As you come up from the squat, raise your arms overhead and twist your torso to one side.
Return to the starting position and repeat the squat, this time twisting your torso to the opposite side.
Continue alternating sides with each squat repetition.

coach_guide.intro: Add rotation at the top to challenge your core and improve thoracic mobility.
coaching_cues: Squat down with arms out front.
Stand and twist, reaching one arm back.
Alternate sides with each rep smoothly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your legs drive the squat and obliques control the twist. The rotation should come from your upper back not lower. Keep moving with purpose.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting too fast with poor control can strain your back.

name: stability ball crunch (full range hands behind head)
instructions: Sit on the stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, slowly curl your upper body forward, bringing your chest towards your knees.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The ball extends your range of motion for a deeper abdominal contraction.
coaching_cues: Drape back fully over the ball.
Crunch up leading with your chest.
Lower with control, stretch abs completely.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs working through a much bigger range than floor crunches. The stretch at the bottom intensifies every rep. Embrace the burn and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling on your neck takes tension off your abs.

name: stalder press
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended overhead.
Bend your knees slightly and engage your core.
Lower your body down into a squat position while keeping your arms extended overhead.
As you squat down, press your arms down towards the ground, engaging your triceps.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the squat, then push through your heels to stand back up while simultaneously raising your arms back overhead.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press from a straddle position to handstand using pure pressing strength.
coaching_cues: Start in straddle, hands flat down.
Press hips up, lean shoulders forward.
Stack hips over shoulders, legs together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel intense work in your triceps, shoulders, and core throughout. This is an advanced skill requiring serious strength. Celebrate every inch of progress.
coach_guide.warning: Kicking up instead of pressing defeats the purpose entirely.

name: standing archer
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Extend your arms straight out in front of you at shoulder height, parallel to the ground.
Rotate your torso to the right, keeping your arms extended and your back straight.
As you rotate, extend your right arm forward and your left arm back, mimicking the motion of drawing a bowstring.
Hold the position for a moment, then return to the starting position.
Repeat the motion, this time rotating your torso to the left and extending your left arm forward and your right arm back.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull one arm back like drawing a bow to strengthen your upper back.
coaching_cues: Extend both arms forward at shoulder height.
Pull one elbow back, squeeze shoulder blade.
Hold the squeeze, then switch sides.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back and rear delts firing hard on the pulling side. The extended arm provides great counterbalance and stretch. Keep that tension strong.
coach_guide.warning: Shrugging your shoulders up reduces upper back activation.

name: standing behind neck press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold the barbell behind your neck with an overhand grip.
Keep your back straight and core engaged.
Press the barbell overhead by extending your arms, fully extending your elbows.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press the barbell from behind your neck to target your shoulders differently.
coaching_cues: Grip wide, bar rests on upper traps.
Press straight up, keep core tight.
Lower with control to starting position.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your deltoids working hard especially the side and rear portions. Your core must stay braced throughout the lift. Push through with confidence.
coach_guide.warning: Going too heavy or too low puts your shoulder joints at risk.

name: standing calf raise (on a staircase)
instructions: Stand on the edge of a step or a sturdy platform with your heels hanging off and your toes on the step.
Hold onto a railing or wall for balance if needed.
Slowly raise your heels as high as possible, lifting your body weight onto the balls of your feet.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the stair edge to get a full stretch and squeeze through your calves.
coaching_cues: Heels drop below the step edge.
Rise up high onto your toes.
Pause at the top, squeeze hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch at the bottom and a strong contraction at the top of each rep. Your calves will burn as you go. Embrace that burn and keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing through reps reduces the stretch and limits your calf development.

name: standing calves
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing forward.
Raise your heels off the ground as high as possible, standing on your toes.
Hold the position for a moment, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stand tall and drive through your toes to build strong defined calves.
coaching_cues: Feet hip width apart, stand tall.
Push up high onto your toes.
Lower slowly with full control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your calf muscles working hard with every lift. The burn builds quickly so stay focused. Keep the pace steady and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom puts unnecessary stress on your Achilles tendon.

name: standing calves calf stretch
instructions: Stand facing a wall or sturdy object, about an arm's length away.
Place your hands on the wall or object at shoulder height.
Step back with one foot, keeping your heel flat on the ground.
Bend your front knee slightly and lean forward, keeping your back leg straight.
You should feel a stretch in your calf muscle.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Repeat on the other leg.

coach_guide.intro: Hold a deep calf stretch to improve flexibility and reduce tightness.
coaching_cues: Keep your back heel pressed down.
Lean forward until you feel stretch.
Breathe deep and hold the position.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a long pull through your entire calf muscle. Let the tension release slowly as you breathe. This is recovery time so enjoy it.
coach_guide.warning: Bending your back knee shifts the stretch away from your calf.

name: standing hamstring and calf stretch with strap
instructions: Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the strap with both hands and place it around the ball of your foot.
Keep your leg straight and slowly lean forward from your hips, keeping your back straight.
Feel the stretch in your hamstring and calf muscles.
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds.
Release the stretch and repeat with the other leg.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the strap to deepen your stretch and open up tight hamstrings and calves.
coaching_cues: Keep your standing leg slightly bent.
Pull the strap gently toward you.
Flex your foot to stretch the calf.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch running from your heel up through your hamstring. Breathe into the tightness and let it release. You are building flexibility that pays off.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back reduces the effectiveness of this stretch.

name: standing lateral stretch
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Extend your arms straight out to the sides, parallel to the ground.
Slowly lean your upper body to one side, feeling a stretch in your side and lats.
Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds.
Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Reach and lean to open up your lats and improve side body mobility.
coaching_cues: Reach your arm high overhead.
Lean slowly to the opposite side.
Keep your hips squared and stable.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a long stretch along your side from hip to fingertips. Let each breath take you a little deeper. This feels amazing so enjoy every second.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting your torso turns this into a different stretch entirely.

name: standing pelvic tilt
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Place your hands on your hips or let them hang by your sides.
Engage your core muscles and tilt your pelvis forward, pushing your lower back towards the wall behind you.
Hold the position for a few seconds, then release and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Tilt your pelvis to engage your core and support healthy spine alignment.
coaching_cues: Stand tall with knees slightly soft.
Tuck your tailbone gently under.
Draw your belly button toward spine.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs engage and your lower back flatten slightly. This subtle movement builds core awareness. Master this and your posture will thank you.
coach_guide.warning: Over-tucking can create tension in your hip flexors.

name: standing single leg curl
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and your hands on your hips.
Shift your weight onto your left leg and lift your right foot off the ground, bending your knee.
Slowly curl your right heel towards your glutes, squeezing your hamstring.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your right foot back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

coach_guide.intro: Curl your heel toward your glutes to isolate and strengthen your hamstrings.
coaching_cues: Stand tall on one leg.
Curl your heel up toward your glutes.
Squeeze at the top then lower slowly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your hamstring contract with each curl. Balance challenges make this even more effective. Stay focused and feel that muscle working.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your leg uses momentum instead of muscle strength.

name: standing wheel rollerout
instructions: Start by standing tall with your feet shoulder-width apart and the wheel roller in front of you.
Bend at your waist and slowly roll the wheel forward, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Continue rolling forward until you feel a stretch in your abs and your body is extended as far as possible.
Pause for a moment, then slowly roll the wheel back towards your body, returning to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Roll out with control to build incredible core strength and stability.
coaching_cues: Start standing, grip the wheel tight.
Roll forward keeping your core braced.
Pull back using your abs not arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core working hard to control the movement. This is advanced so respect the challenge. You are building serious strength with every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your lower back sag puts dangerous pressure on your spine.

name: star jump (male)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms by your sides.
Bend your knees slightly and jump up explosively.
As you jump, spread your legs and extend your arms out to the sides, forming a star shape with your body.
Land softly on the balls of your feet with your knees slightly bent.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode off the ground with energy to get your heart pumping fast.
coaching_cues: Jump up spreading arms and legs.
Land softly with knees slightly bent.
Keep your chest up and stay light.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart rate climb and your whole body working together. This is pure energy so bring the intensity. You have got this so keep jumping.
coach_guide.warning: Landing with locked knees sends shock straight into your joints.

name: stationary bike run v. 3
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position to ensure proper alignment.
Place your feet on the pedals and secure them with the straps if available.
Start pedaling at a comfortable pace.
Maintain a steady rhythm and increase the resistance as desired.
Engage your core muscles to maintain stability and proper posture.
Continue pedaling for the desired duration of your workout.
Gradually decrease the resistance and slow down before coming to a complete stop.
Stretch your legs and cool down after the workout.

coach_guide.intro: Pedal with power and keep your intensity high for maximum cardio benefit.
coaching_cues: Sit tall with core engaged throughout.
Push and pull through each pedal stroke.
Keep a steady powerful rhythm going.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your legs burning and your lungs working hard. This is where endurance is built so stay committed. Push through and finish stronger than you started.
coach_guide.warning: Hunching over the handlebars restricts your breathing capacity.

name: stationary bike walk
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position on the stationary bike to ensure proper alignment.
Place your feet on the pedals and secure them with the straps if available.
Start pedaling at a comfortable pace, keeping your back straight and core engaged.
Maintain a steady rhythm and increase the resistance level if desired.
Continue pedaling for the desired duration of your cardio workout.
Cool down by gradually reducing your pace and resistance level.
Stretch your leg muscles after the workout to prevent tightness and promote recovery.

coach_guide.intro: Keep a steady rhythm and let your legs warm up gradually.
coaching_cues: Sit tall with relaxed shoulders and grip.
Push through your whole foot each pedal stroke.
Maintain a smooth and controlled walking pace.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a gentle warmth building in your legs and your heart rate slowly climbing. This is easy effort so your breathing stays comfortable. Use this time to settle in.
coach_guide.warning: Gripping the handlebars too tight causes unnecessary shoulder and neck tension.

name: straddle maltese
instructions: Start by hanging from a pair of rings with your arms fully extended and your body in a straight line.
Spread your legs wide apart, forming a straddle position.
Engage your core and slowly lower your body until your arms are parallel to the ground.
Hold this position for a few seconds, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This elite hold demands total body tension with legs spread wide.
coaching_cues: Lock your arms straight and protract shoulders.
Squeeze your glutes and point your toes out.
Breathe shallow and hold your core tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel intense pressure through your entire core and shoulders working overtime to stabilize. Your abs will burn deeply as they fight gravity. You have earned this strength.
coach_guide.warning: Attempting this without proper progression can strain shoulders and wrists badly.

name: straddle planche
instructions: Start in a push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart and your feet spread wide apart.
Engage your core and slowly shift your weight forward, bringing your shoulders over your hands.
Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your hands to straighten your arms and lift your body back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lean forward with straight arms and lift your straddled legs off the ground.
coaching_cues: Push the floor away and round shoulders forward.
Keep arms locked and wrists stacked over hands.
Squeeze inner thighs and hold legs parallel.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest and front shoulders burning while your core fights to keep you level. This is extreme difficulty so any hold time is impressive. You are building elite strength.
coach_guide.warning: Wrist and shoulder injuries happen fast if you collapse out of position.

name: straight leg outer hip abductor
instructions: Lie on your side with your legs straight and stacked on top of each other.
Place your bottom arm under your head for support.
Engage your core and lift your top leg as high as possible without rotating your hips or leaning backward.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your leg back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: Lift your straight leg out to the side targeting the outer hip.
coaching_cues: Stand tall and brace your core for balance.
Lead with your heel keeping leg fully straight.
Lift only as high as your hip allows.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a burn on the outside of your hip and glute working hard. If you feel lower back strain you are lifting too high. Control the movement and feel stronger each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the leg uses momentum and removes the work from your abductors.

name: superman push-up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Engage your core and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
As you lower your body, simultaneously lift your right arm and left leg off the ground, extending them straight out.
Pause for a moment at the top, then lower your arm and leg back down while pushing yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat the movement, this time lifting your left arm and right leg.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode up from a push-up and extend arms overhead like superman flying.
coaching_cues: Start in a tight plank with core braced.
Push explosively so hands leave the ground completely.
Reach arms forward and land soft with control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest and shoulders firing hard on the explosive push. Your core works overtime to keep you stable in the air. This is power training so give each rep everything.
coach_guide.warning: Landing with locked elbows can hyperextend and injure your joints.

name: suspended abdominal fallout
instructions: Attach a suspension trainer to a high anchor point and adjust the straps to waist height.
Stand facing away from the anchor point and hold the handles with your arms extended in front of you.
Lean forward at the waist, keeping your body straight and your core engaged.
Lower your body as far as you can while maintaining control and tension in your abs.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly raise your body back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Roll forward on the straps letting your body extend then pull back with your abs.
coaching_cues: Grip handles tight and start standing tall.
Fall forward slowly with arms reaching overhead.
Pull back by crunching your abs not your arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch through your entire core as you extend out. The pull back will light up your abs from top to bottom. Stay controlled and own every inch.
coach_guide.warning: Going too far forward without core control strains your lower back.

name: suspended push-up
instructions: Find a suspension trainer and adjust it to an appropriate height.
Stand facing away from the anchor point and hold the handles with an overhand grip.
Walk your feet forward, leaning your body forward until your weight is supported by the suspension trainer.
Keep your body straight from head to heels, engage your core, and lower your chest towards the handles.
Push through your chest and arms to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Perform push-ups with your feet suspended to challenge stability and chest.
coaching_cues: Lock feet in straps and set tight plank.
Lower chest toward floor with elbows at 45.
Press up strong and do not let hips sag.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working hard while your core fights to stop swinging. The instability makes every rep more demanding than regular push-ups. Embrace the shake and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips drop puts dangerous stress on your lower back.

name: suspended reverse crunch
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your arms fully extended and your palms facing away from you.
Engage your core and lift your knees up towards your chest, curling your pelvis towards your ribcage.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your knees back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hang from straps and curl your knees toward your chest using your abs.
coaching_cues: Start in a plank with feet secured tightly.
Tuck knees toward chest and round lower back.
Control the return and do not swing wildly.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs contracting hard as your knees pull in. The suspension adds instability so your whole core must engage. Fight the swing and feel the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum to swing your legs removes all the ab work.

name: suspended row
instructions: Set up a suspension trainer at an appropriate height.
Stand facing the anchor point with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the handles with an overhand grip, palms facing each other.
Lean back, keeping your body straight and your heels on the ground.
Pull your chest towards the handles, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower yourself back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull your chest toward the handles while keeping your body in a straight line.
coaching_cues: Grip handles and lean back with arms extended.
Pull elbows back and squeeze shoulder blades together.
Lower with control and keep your core tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your upper back muscles squeezing hard at the top of each pull. Your core stays engaged to keep your body rigid throughout. Build that back strength rep by rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag turns this into a poor exercise and strains your back.

name: suspended split squat
instructions: Stand facing away from a suspension trainer with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Extend one leg forward and place the top of your foot in the foot cradle of the suspension trainer.
Bend your standing leg and lower your body down into a lunge position, keeping your chest up and your knee in line with your toes.
Push through your heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

coach_guide.intro: Drop into a lunge with your rear foot suspended for balance and quad work.
coaching_cues: Place rear foot in strap and hop forward.
Lower until front thigh is parallel to floor.
Drive through front heel and stand tall again.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your front quad and glute burning while your balance is constantly challenged. The suspended leg forces extra stability work through your hips. Stay focused and keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your front knee cave inward puts dangerous stress on the joint.

name: swimmer kicks v. 2 (male)
instructions: Lie face down on a mat with your arms extended overhead.
Engage your core and lift your chest and legs off the ground simultaneously.
Kick your legs up and down in a fluttering motion, as if you were swimming.
Continue kicking for the desired number of repetitions.
Lower your chest and legs back down to the starting position.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your legs straight and kick from the glutes not the lower back.
coaching_cues: Squeeze glutes before each kick up.
Keep legs long and toes pointed.
Alternate smoothly without arching back.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your glutes and hamstrings with each flutter. If your lower back is straining, engage your core tighter. Keep those kicks going strong.
coach_guide.warning: Arching your lower back takes tension off glutes and strains your spine.

name: swing 360
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Hold your arms straight out in front of you, parallel to the ground.
Engage your core and swing your arms in a circular motion, rotating your torso as you do so.
Continue the circular motion, swinging your arms and rotating your torso for the desired number of repetitions.
Remember to breathe throughout the exercise.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your entire body with control using your arms to guide momentum.
coaching_cues: Swing arms wide and stay light.
Rotate hips and shoulders together smoothly.
Land soft and keep your core braced.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart rate climbing as you move through full rotations. If you feel dizzy slow down and find your center. Stay light on your feet and enjoy the movement.
coach_guide.warning: Spinning too fast without control can cause you to lose balance.

name: three bench dip
instructions: Sit on a bench with your hands gripping the edge, fingers pointing forward.
Slide your butt off the bench, supporting your weight with your hands.
Bend your elbows and lower your body until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Push yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use three benches to increase range of motion and really challenge your triceps.
coaching_cues: Hands firm on bench behind you.
Lower until elbows hit ninety degrees.
Press up strong and squeeze triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in the back of your arms throughout the movement. If your shoulders roll forward you are going too deep. Push through and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping too low puts excessive stress on your shoulder joints.

name: tire flip
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, facing the tire.
Bend your knees and hinge at the hips, lowering into a squat position.
Reach down and grab the bottom edge of the tire with both hands, fingers facing towards you.
Engage your glutes and leg muscles, and explosively drive through your legs to lift the tire off the ground.
As the tire flips over, use your upper body strength to guide it and maintain control.
Once the tire is fully flipped, quickly step back and reset your stance.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive through your legs and hips to flip the tire not your lower back.
coaching_cues: Get low and grip under the tire.
Explode up with legs and hips first.
Push tire over and reset your stance.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes hamstrings and entire body working together powerfully. If your back rounds you need a lighter tire. Channel that explosive energy every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back under heavy load risks serious spinal injury.

name: trap bar deadlift
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and the trap bar on the ground in front of you.
Bend at the hips and knees to lower yourself down and grip the handles of the trap bar with an overhand grip.
Keep your back straight and chest up as you begin to lift the trap bar off the ground by extending your hips and knees.
As you lift, focus on driving through your heels and squeezing your glutes at the top of the movement.
Lower the trap bar back down to the ground by bending at the hips and knees, keeping your back straight throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stand inside the bar and lift by driving your hips forward.
coaching_cues: Grip handles and stand tall first.
Push floor away and extend hips.
Squeeze glutes hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and hamstrings powering the lift from start to finish. If your back takes over reset your hip position. Stay strong and own every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your back round puts dangerous stress on your spine.

name: triceps dip
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with your hands gripping the edge, fingers pointing forward.
Slide your butt off the bench, supporting your weight with your hands.
Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows tight and lower with control to maximize triceps engagement.
coaching_cues: Grip bars and lock shoulders down.
Lower until elbows reach ninety degrees.
Press up explosively and lock out.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps burning intensely as you press up each time. If your shoulders creep toward your ears reset your form. You have got this keep pushing.
coach_guide.warning: Dipping too deep can strain your shoulders and rotator cuffs.

name: triceps dip (bench leg)
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench with your hands gripping the edge, fingers pointing forward.
Walk your feet forward, sliding your butt off the bench, and straighten your arms.
Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench.
Push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep one leg extended to add core challenge while working your triceps.
coaching_cues: Hands on bench and extend one leg.
Lower with control elbows back not out.
Press up and switch legs next set.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps working hard while your core stabilizes your body. If you wobble too much lower your leg slightly. Stay focused and keep that burn going.
coach_guide.warning: Flaring elbows outward shifts stress to your shoulders.

name: triceps dip (between benches)
instructions: Sit on a bench with your hands gripping the edge of the bench, fingers pointing forward.
Slide your butt off the bench, supporting your weight with your hands.
Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your back close to the bench.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use two benches to increase your range of motion and triceps stretch.
coaching_cues: Hands back and heels on front bench.
Lower deep keeping elbows pointed back.
Drive up hard and squeeze triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and burn in your triceps at the bottom of each rep. If your shoulders protest you are dipping too low. Push through and finish every rep strong.
coach_guide.warning: Excessive depth overstretches and strains shoulder joints.

name: triceps dips floor
instructions: Sit on the edge of a chair or bench with your hands next to your hips, fingers pointing forward.
Slide your butt off the front of the chair with your legs extended in front of you.
Straighten your arms, keeping a little bend in your elbows to keep tension on your triceps and off your elbow joints.
Slowly bend your elbows to lower your body toward the floor until your elbows are at about a 90-degree angle.
Once you reach the bottom of the movement, press down into the chair to straighten your elbows, returning to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your hips close to your hands and press straight up through your palms.
coaching_cues: Hands flat and fingers forward.
Bend elbows straight back not out.
Push floor away and lock arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the back of your arms working hard even from the floor. If your hips drift forward scoot them back closer. Great work keep those reps clean.
coach_guide.warning: Letting elbows flare puts unnecessary strain on your wrists.

name: triceps press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your knees slightly bent.
Extend your arms straight out in front of you, parallel to the ground.
Bend your elbows and lower your body towards the ground, keeping your upper arms close to your sides.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press your body up using only your triceps while keeping your core tight.
coaching_cues: Hands under shoulders fingers forward.
Lower chest toward floor elbows back.
Press up until arms fully extend.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps doing all the pushing work throughout the movement. If your lower back sags tighten your core immediately. Strong arms start here keep it up.
coach_guide.warning: Sagging hips strain your lower back and reduce triceps work.

name: triceps stretch
instructions: Stand or sit upright with your back straight.
Extend one arm overhead, bending it at the elbow.
Place your opposite hand on the bent elbow and gently pull it towards your head.
Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, feeling a gentle stretch in your triceps.
Release the stretch and repeat on the other arm.

coach_guide.intro: Reach your hand down your back to lengthen the triceps muscle.
coaching_cues: Elbow points straight up to ceiling.
Gently press elbow with opposite hand.
Keep your spine tall, ribs down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch along the back of your upper arm from elbow to shoulder. If you feel pinching in your shoulder, ease off the pressure slightly. Breathe into the stretch and let the muscle release.
coach_guide.warning: Forcing the stretch can strain your shoulder joint.

name: tuck crunch
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing outwards.
Engaging your abs, lift your shoulder blades off the ground and bring your knees towards your chest, simultaneously curling your upper body towards your knees.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your shoulder blades and extend your legs back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Curl your knees and chest together to crunch your abs tight.
coaching_cues: Lift shoulders and knees at same time.
Keep lower back pressed into the floor.
Squeeze abs hard at the top position.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire abdominal wall working as you bring your body together. If your neck strains, focus eyes forward not up. Keep that core firing strong.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling on your neck takes tension off your abs.

name: twin handle parallel grip lat pulldown
instructions: Adjust the seat height and position yourself facing the cable machine.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Sit down and position your thighs under the thigh pads, keeping your feet flat on the floor.
Lean back slightly and keep your chest up, maintaining a neutral spine.
Pull the handles down towards your upper chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause for a moment at the bottom of the movement, feeling the contraction in your lats.
Slowly release the handles and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull the handles down and back to squeeze your lats hard.
coaching_cues: Grip handles with palms facing each other.
Drive elbows down toward your hips.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at bottom.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats engage from armpit to lower back on every rep. If your biceps fatigue first, focus on leading with your elbows. Build that back strength one rep at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning too far back turns this into a row.

name: twist hip lift
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Place your hands by your sides for support.
Engage your glutes and lift your hips off the ground, forming a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
While keeping your hips lifted, twist your lower body to the right side, bringing your knees towards the ground.
Return to the starting position and repeat the twist to the left side.
Continue alternating twists for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lift your hips with a twist to target your glutes from a new angle.
coaching_cues: Press through your grounded foot firmly.
Rotate hips as you lift them up.
Squeeze your glute hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel one glute working intensely as you twist and lift. If your lower back takes over, reduce your range of motion. Keep firing those glutes with control.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting too fast can strain your lower back.

name: twisted leg raise
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your arms by your sides.
Place your hands under your glutes for support.
Engage your abs and lift both legs off the ground, keeping them straight.
As you lift your legs, twist your hips to one side, bringing your legs towards your opposite shoulder.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat the movement, this time twisting your hips to the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise your legs while twisting to hit your obliques and lower abs.
coaching_cues: Keep your lower back flat throughout.
Lift legs and twist hips to side.
Control the lowering phase, no swinging.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques and lower abs burning as you twist and lift. If your hip flexors cramp, bend your knees slightly. Stay focused and keep those abs engaged.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your legs removes tension from your core.

name: twisted leg raise (female)
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your legs extended and your arms by your sides.
Bend your knees and lift your legs off the ground, bringing them towards your chest.
As you lift your legs, twist your hips to one side, bringing your knees towards your opposite shoulder.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat the movement, this time twisting your hips to the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise your legs while twisting to hit your obliques and lower abs.
coaching_cues: Keep your lower back flat throughout.
Lift legs and twist hips to side.
Control the lowering phase, no swinging.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques and lower abs burning as you twist and lift. If your hip flexors cramp, bend your knees slightly. Stay focused and keep those abs engaged.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your legs removes tension from your core.

name: two toe touch (male)
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms extended out to the sides.
Bend forward at the waist, keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
Reach down towards your toes with both hands, keeping your legs straight.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Reach for your toes alternating sides to mobilize your spine.
coaching_cues: Stand tall then reach to opposite foot.
Keep both legs as straight as possible.
Alternate sides with a smooth rhythm.
