coach_guide.feel: You should feel a stretch through your hamstrings and a gentle twist through your spine. If your back feels tight, soften your knees a bit. Keep moving with control and enjoy the mobility work.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back excessively can cause strain.

name: upper back stretch
instructions: Stand up straight with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Extend your arms straight in front of you, parallel to the ground.
Interlace your fingers and rotate your palms away from your body.
Slowly raise your arms overhead, keeping them straight and parallel to each other.
As you raise your arms, squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, then release and repeat.

coach_guide.intro: Round your upper back forward to stretch the muscles between your shoulder blades.
coaching_cues: Clasp hands and push them forward.
Round your upper back like a cat.
Drop your chin toward your chest gently.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch across your upper back and between your shoulder blades. If you feel neck tension, relax your shoulders down away from ears. Let that upper back open up and release.
coach_guide.warning: Holding your breath limits how deeply you can stretch.

name: upward facing dog
instructions: Lie face down on the floor with your legs extended behind you.
Place your hands on the floor next to your lower ribs, fingers pointing forward.
Press your hands firmly into the floor and straighten your arms, lifting your torso and thighs off the ground.
Roll your shoulders back and down, opening your chest and lifting your gaze towards the ceiling.
Hold this position for a few breaths, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press your chest forward and up to extend your spine beautifully.
coaching_cues: Hands under shoulders, arms straight.
Roll shoulders back and lift chest.
Keep hips low and legs engaged.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a gentle stretch through your abs and hip flexors while your back extends. If your lower back pinches, press less and engage your glutes. Open your heart and breathe deeply.
coach_guide.warning: Collapsing into your lower back causes compression pain.

name: v-sit on floor
instructions: Sit on the floor with your legs extended in front of you.
Lean back slightly and lift your legs off the ground, keeping them straight.
Simultaneously, lift your upper body off the ground and reach your arms towards your legs.
Hold this position for a few seconds, then slowly lower your upper body and legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Balance on your sit bones with legs and torso lifted in a V shape.
coaching_cues: Lift chest proud, shoulders back.
Extend legs straight, toes pointed forward.
Reach arms parallel to the ground.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core shaking to hold this position steady. If you cannot straighten your legs, keep them bent at first. Hold strong and breathe through the challenge.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your back shifts stress away from your abs.

name: vertical leg raise (on parallel bars)
instructions: Hang from the parallel bars with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Engage your core and lift your legs up in front of you, keeping them straight.
Continue lifting until your legs are parallel to the ground or slightly higher.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your core tight and lift your legs using your abs not momentum.
coaching_cues: Grip bars firmly, shoulders pressed down.
Raise legs slowly to parallel or higher.
Lower with control, no swinging allowed.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in your lower abs throughout each rep. If your hip flexors take over, focus on curling your pelvis up. Stay controlled and keep those abs firing.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging your legs turns this into a hip flexor exercise.

name: walk elliptical cross trainer
instructions: Adjust the resistance level and incline of the elliptical machine to your desired settings.
Step onto the pedals of the machine and grip the handles lightly.
Begin by pushing down with your feet and pulling the handles towards your body.
Continue this motion, alternating between pushing and pulling, to simulate a walking or running motion.
Maintain a steady pace and keep your core engaged throughout the exercise.
Continue for the desired duration of your cardio workout.
Gradually decrease the intensity and speed of the machine before stepping off.

coach_guide.intro: Push and pull with both your arms and legs for a full body cardio workout.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, core engaged throughout movement.
Push down through heels, pull handles actively.
Keep a smooth and steady rhythm going.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart rate climbing while your whole body works together. If it feels too easy, increase resistance or speed. Keep that rhythm and enjoy the burn.
coach_guide.warning: Slouching forward puts unnecessary strain on your lower back.

name: walking high knees lunge
instructions: Stand with your feet hip-width apart.
Lift your right knee up towards your chest as high as you can while balancing on your left leg.
Step forward with your right foot and lower your body into a lunge position, bending both knees to a 90-degree angle.
Push off with your right foot and bring your left knee up towards your chest.
Step forward with your left foot and lower your body into a lunge position.
Continue alternating legs and lunging forward, keeping your core engaged and maintaining a steady pace.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive your knees high with each lunge step to maximize the cardio challenge.
coaching_cues: Step forward into a deep lunge position.
Drive back knee up toward chest explosively.
Keep your chest lifted and core tight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart pumping and your legs burning with each step. If you lose balance, slow down and focus on control first. Push through and keep those knees driving.
coach_guide.warning: Rushing through reps leads to sloppy form and potential falls.

name: walking lunge
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Take a step forward with your right leg, lowering your body into a lunge position.
Keep your torso upright and your front knee aligned with your ankle.
Push off with your right foot and bring your left foot forward, stepping into a lunge position with your left leg.
Continue alternating legs and walking forward, maintaining a controlled and steady pace.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stay tall and drive forward off your front heel with each step.
coaching_cues: Step out long, stay upright.
Drive front heel, step through clean.
Keep rhythm steady, core engaged.
coach_guide.feel: Each leg should be burning as you step through, especially in those glutes. If you're feeling off balance, slow down and take bigger steps. Keep moving with that burn building in every step.
coach_guide.warning: Don't let your torso lean forward as you fatigue.

name: walking on incline treadmill
instructions: Adjust the incline level on the treadmill to your desired intensity.
Stand on the treadmill with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Start walking at a comfortable pace, ensuring that you maintain proper form.
Engage your core muscles and keep your back straight throughout the exercise.
Continue walking on the incline treadmill for the desired duration of your cardio workout.
Gradually decrease the incline and speed of the treadmill to cool down before stopping.

coach_guide.intro: Walk with purpose and use the incline to challenge your legs and lungs.
coaching_cues: Stand upright, avoid leaning on handrails.
Take full strides, pushing through your heels.
Keep a steady pace you can maintain.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and calves working hard against the incline. If you need the rails for balance, lower the speed first. Stay tall and keep climbing strong.
coach_guide.warning: Holding the handrails reduces calorie burn and cheats your legs.

name: walking on stepmill
instructions: Adjust the stepmill machine to a comfortable level.
Step onto the machine and place your hands on the handrails for support.
Start walking by placing one foot on a step and then the other, alternating between legs.
Maintain an upright posture and engage your core muscles.
Continue walking for the desired duration or distance.
Gradually increase the intensity or speed as you become more comfortable with the exercise.
Remember to cool down and stretch after completing the exercise.

coach_guide.intro: Step with intention and let your legs do all the work climbing.
coaching_cues: Stand tall, hands off rails if possible.
Drive through each step with full foot.
Maintain a consistent stepping rhythm throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your quads and glutes burning as you climb continuously. If your calves cramp, push more through your whole foot. Keep stepping and embrace the challenge.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning heavily on rails takes the work away from your legs.

name: weighted bench dip
instructions: Sit on a bench with your hands gripping the edge, fingers pointing forward.
Slide your butt off the bench, supporting your weight with your hands.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Add weight to increase tricep activation while maintaining strict controlled form.
coaching_cues: Secure weight on lap, grip bench firmly.
Lower until elbows reach ninety degrees max.
Press up powerfully, squeezing triceps hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in the back of your arms on every rep. If your shoulders hurt, reduce the depth of your dip. Push through and feel those triceps working.
coach_guide.warning: Going too deep puts excessive stress on your shoulder joints.

name: weighted close grip chin-up on dip cage
instructions: Stand in front of the dip cage and grab the parallel bars with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Hang from the bars with your arms fully extended, feet off the ground, and body straight.
Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bars, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bars, then pause for a moment.
Slowly lower your body back down to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull with your back muscles and let the weight add serious strength challenge.
coaching_cues: Grip close, palms facing you securely.
Pull elbows down toward your hip bones.
Lower slowly, feel the stretch in lats.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats doing the heavy lifting on each rep. If your biceps fatigue first, focus on pulling with your elbows. Keep pulling strong and build that back.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum robs your lats of the strength building tension.

name: weighted cossack squats (male)
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and toes pointing slightly outward.
Hold a weight in front of your chest with both hands.
Shift your weight to one side and lower your body by bending the knee of the side you shifted towards, while keeping the other leg straight.
Go as low as you can while maintaining balance and keeping your chest up.
Push through the heel of the bent leg to return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side, alternating between legs.

coach_guide.intro: Shift side to side with control to deeply challenge your glutes and mobility.
coaching_cues: Hold weight at chest, stand wide stance.
Shift into one leg, straightening the other.
Push through heel to return to center.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and burn in your glutes and inner thighs. If balance is tough, reduce the weight until you own the movement. Stay low and keep shifting strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your knee cave inward stresses your knee joint badly.

name: weighted crunch
instructions: Lie flat on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the ground.
Hold a weight plate or dumbbell on your chest.
Engage your abs and lift your upper body off the ground, curling forward until your shoulder blades are off the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold the weight close and focus on curling your abs not pulling with arms.
coaching_cues: Hold weight against chest or behind head.
Curl up slowly, squeezing abs at top.
Lower with control, keep tension throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your abs working hard against the added resistance every rep. If your neck strains, keep your chin slightly tucked. Embrace the burn and keep crunching.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling on your neck to lift up can cause serious strain.

name: weighted decline sit-up
instructions: Lie flat on a decline bench with your feet secured under the foot pads.
Place your hands behind your head or across your chest.
Engage your abs and slowly lift your upper body off the bench, curling forward until your torso is perpendicular to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the decline angle and weight to maximize tension on your abs throughout.
coaching_cues: Secure feet, hold weight at your chest.
Sit up by curling spine, not hinging.
Lower slowly, feeling abs stretch under control.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core working hard against gravity and the weight. If your hip flexors burn more, focus on the curling motion. Stay strong and finish each rep with purpose.
coach_guide.warning: Using momentum to swing up defeats the purpose of added weight.

name: weighted donkey calf raise
instructions: Stand on a raised platform with your toes on the edge and your heels hanging off.
Hold onto a stable object for support.
Raise your heels as high as possible by extending your ankles.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your heels back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hang your heels off the edge and stretch deep before pushing up through your toes.
coaching_cues: Let heels drop low, feel the stretch.
Push through big toe, squeeze at top.
Control the descent, no bouncing here.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn in both calf muscles from the deep stretch to full contraction. If your ankles wobble, slow down and stabilize. Keep pushing through that burn.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing at the bottom puts dangerous stress on your Achilles tendon.

name: weighted drop push up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and your feet together.
Lower your chest towards the ground, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Once your chest is just above the ground, explosively push yourself up, lifting your hands off the ground.
As you push up, quickly move your hands out to the sides and slightly forward, allowing your body to drop down towards the ground.
Catch yourself with your hands in the wider position and immediately lower your chest towards the ground again.
Repeat the push-up motion, dropping down and catching yourself with your hands in the narrower position.
Continue alternating between the wider and narrower hand positions for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Start elevated, drop down and explode up to the higher surface with chest power.
coaching_cues: Hands wide, chest leads the descent down.
Explode up fast, land soft on platforms.
Reset your position before the next rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest working explosively on each push upward. If your lower back sags, tighten your core. You are building serious power here.
coach_guide.warning: Landing with locked elbows can hyperextend and damage your joints.

name: weighted front plank
instructions: Start by lying face down on the floor.
Place your forearms on the ground, with your elbows directly under your shoulders.
Extend your legs straight out behind you, with your toes on the ground.
Engage your core and lift your body off the ground, balancing on your forearms and toes.
Keep your body in a straight line from your head to your heels.
Hold this position for the desired amount of time.
Lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hold a perfect plank position while the added weight challenges your core stability.
coaching_cues: Squeeze glutes, keep hips completely level.
Breathe steady, do not hold your breath.
Eyes down, neck stays neutral and long.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core working overtime to maintain that straight line. If your hips drop or pike up, reduce the weight. Stay strong and solid like steel.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your lower back sag puts compressive force on spinal discs.

name: weighted front raise
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your palms facing your thighs.
Keeping your arms straight, exhale and lift the dumbbells in front of you until they are at shoulder level.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Raise the weight straight in front with control using only your shoulder muscles.
coaching_cues: Arms straight, slight bend in elbows only.
Lift to shoulder height, pause briefly there.
Lower with control, resist the weight down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the front of your shoulders burning as you lift. If you are swinging or leaning back, the weight is too heavy. Own every inch of the movement.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging momentum takes work from shoulders and strains your lower back.

name: weighted hanging leg-hip raise
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your arms fully extended and your palms facing away from you.
Engage your core and lift your legs up in front of you, keeping them straight.
Continue lifting until your legs are parallel to the ground or slightly higher.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Hang and lift your legs all the way up while the weight adds serious resistance.
coaching_cues: Grip tight, shoulders packed and stable.
Curl hips up, bring legs to bar.
Lower with control, no swinging allowed.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower abs and hip flexors working intensely on each lift. If you are swinging wildly, reset and control the motion. This is elite core work.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging puts excessive rotational stress on your lower spine.

name: weighted hyperextension (on stability ball)
instructions: Position yourself face down on a stability ball with your hips resting on the ball and your feet against a wall for stability.
Place your hands behind your head or cross them over your chest.
Engage your core and slowly lift your upper body off the ball, extending your back until your body forms a straight line.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your upper body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Extend your spine over the ball while holding weight to strengthen your lower back.
coaching_cues: Hinge at hips, lower chest toward floor.
Squeeze glutes and back, lift torso up.
Pause at the top, control going down.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lower back and glutes working together as you rise. If you feel sharp pain, remove the weight immediately. Build that posterior chain strength.
coach_guide.warning: Hyperextending too far backward compresses the spinal joints dangerously.

name: weighted kneeling step with swing
instructions: Start in a kneeling position with your knees hip-width apart and your back straight.
Hold a weight in each hand, with your arms extended straight down in front of you.
Engage your core and swing the weights up and overhead, keeping your arms straight.
Lower the weights back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Step up from kneeling while swinging the weight overhead in one powerful motion.
coaching_cues: Drive through front foot, stand up strong.
Swing weight overhead as you rise up.
Control descent back to kneeling position.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your shoulders and legs working together in this dynamic movement. If you lose balance, use a lighter weight. Great work building total body coordination.
coach_guide.warning: Losing control of the swing can pull your shoulder out of position.

name: weighted lunge with swing
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a weight in each hand.
Take a step forward with your right foot, lowering your body into a lunge position.
As you lunge forward, swing the weights forward and upward, keeping your arms straight.
Push off with your right foot and return to the starting position, swinging the weights back down.
Repeat with your left foot and continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lunge forward while swinging the weight to challenge your legs and shoulders together.
coaching_cues: Step deep, front knee tracks over toes.
Swing weight with control, not momentum.
Push back to start, stay tall throughout.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and quads burning while your shoulders stabilize. If your knee caves inward, focus on pushing it out. You are building functional total body strength.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your front knee collapse inward strains the ligaments badly.

name: weighted muscle up
instructions: Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and pull your body up towards the bar, leading with your chest.
As you reach the top of the movement, transition your grip so that your palms are facing towards you.
Continue pulling yourself up until your chest reaches the bar, then pause for a moment.
Slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull yourself up and over the bar with added weight for ultimate upper body strength.
coaching_cues: Explosive pull, get chest above bar height.
Transition fast, push down to lock out.
Lower with control, reset grip each rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats, chest and triceps all working through this demanding movement. If your form breaks down, drop the weight. This is elite level strength work.
coach_guide.warning: Kipping with weight puts extreme stress on shoulder rotator cuffs.

name: weighted muscle up (on bar)
instructions: Start by hanging from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back.
Bend your elbows and pull your chest towards the bar, keeping your body straight.
Once your chest reaches the bar, push down with your hands and drive your elbows back, lifting your body above the bar.
Pause at the top of the movement, then slowly lower yourself back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Perform a strict muscle up on the bar while weighted for maximum pulling power.
coaching_cues: Pull hard, aim to bring chest to bar.
Quick transition, drive hands down and press.
Control the descent, protect your shoulders always.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel every muscle in your upper body working during this movement. If you cannot complete clean reps, reduce the weight. You are among the strongest now.
coach_guide.warning: Jerky transitions with weight can tear shoulder and elbow connective tissue.

name: weighted one hand pull up
instructions: Grab the pull-up bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Hang from the bar with your arm fully extended and your body straight.
Engage your core and pull your body up towards the bar by bending your elbow and squeezing your back muscles.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Lower your body back down to the starting position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: This is an elite move so control every inch of the pull.
coaching_cues: Grip tight, one arm fully extended overhead.
Pull elbow down hard, chin clears the bar.
Lower slowly, feel the lat stretch completely.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn through your entire lat on the working side. If your bicep gives out first, focus on initiating with your back. You have got the strength for this.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging or kipping puts dangerous stress on your shoulder joint.

name: weighted overhead crunch (on stability ball)
instructions: Sit on a stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Hold a weight plate or dumbbell with both hands and extend your arms overhead.
Engage your abs and slowly curl your torso forward, bringing your chest towards your knees.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your torso back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your hips stable on the ball and let your abs do all the curling.
coaching_cues: Hips pressed into ball, lower back supported.
Weight overhead, crunch ribs toward hips hard.
Lower with control, feel the abs stretch.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn right through your upper abs with each crunch. If your lower back aches, you are arching too much on the way down. Stay focused and keep those reps strong.
coach_guide.warning: Letting the weight pull you into hyperextension strains your lower back.

name: weighted pull-up
instructions: Grab the pull-up bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Hang from the bar with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bar, keeping your elbows close to your body.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Add weight only when bodyweight pull ups feel too easy with perfect form.
coaching_cues: Dead hang, shoulders packed down and back.
Drive elbows to your pockets, chest to bar.
Lower under control, full arm extension each rep.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your lats working hard from stretch to squeeze on every rep. If your shoulders roll forward at the top, you are cutting range short. Finish strong with great form.
coach_guide.warning: Kipping with added weight creates excessive strain on your shoulders.

name: weighted round arm
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand.
Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back straight.
Raise your arms out to the sides, keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Continue lifting your arms until they are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your arms back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep a slight bend in your elbows and lift in a wide arc.
coaching_cues: Arms slightly bent, palms facing down throughout.
Raise in wide arc until arms reach shoulder height.
Lower slowly, maintain tension in your delts.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the burn across the caps of your shoulders throughout the movement. If you feel it in your traps, you are shrugging too much. Keep those shoulders down and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Lifting too heavy causes momentum and strains your rotator cuff.

name: weighted russian twist
instructions: Sit on the ground with your knees bent and your feet flat on the floor.
Hold a weight or medicine ball with both hands in front of your chest.
Lean back slightly, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
Slowly twist your torso to the right, bringing the weight or medicine ball towards the floor on your right side.
Pause for a moment, then twist your torso to the left, bringing the weight or medicine ball towards the floor on your left side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate from your ribcage while keeping your hips as still as possible.
coaching_cues: Sit back slightly, chest proud and tall.
Rotate weight side to side, controlled tempo.
Exhale on each twist, feel obliques engage.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques working hard on each rotation with a deep burn. If your lower back is sore, sit up taller and reduce range. Keep twisting strong.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back while twisting compresses spinal discs.

name: weighted russian twist (legs up)
instructions: Sit on the ground with your knees bent and feet lifted off the ground, keeping your legs together.
Hold the weight with both hands in front of your chest, keeping your elbows slightly bent.
Lean back slightly to engage your core muscles.
Twist your torso to the right, bringing the weight towards the ground on your right side.
Pause for a moment, then twist your torso to the left, bringing the weight towards the ground on your left side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keeping legs elevated forces your core to work double time for stability.
coaching_cues: Legs raised, knees bent at 90 degrees.
Twist side to side, weight touches near hips.
Keep feet still, only your torso rotates.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your entire core firing to keep legs stable while you twist. If your hip flexors cramp, lower your legs slightly between sets. You are doing great.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your legs swing side to side takes tension off your abs.

name: weighted russian twist v. 2
instructions: Sit on the ground with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Hold the weight with both hands in front of your chest.
Lean back slightly to engage your core muscles.
Twist your torso to the right, bringing the weight towards the right side of your body.
Pause for a moment, then twist your torso to the left, bringing the weight towards the left side of your body.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Focus on rotating through your thoracic spine not just moving your arms.
coaching_cues: Lean back slightly, engage your core fully.
Rotate ribcage, arms follow the movement naturally.
Touch weight to floor each side if possible.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong squeeze in your obliques at the end of each rotation. If you feel this in your arms, slow down and twist from your core. Keep that burn going.
coach_guide.warning: Using arm momentum instead of core rotation reduces effectiveness completely.

name: weighted seated bicep curl (on stability ball)
instructions: Sit on a stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your back straight.
Hold a medicine ball with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and let your arms hang down by your sides.
Keeping your upper arms stationary, exhale and curl the medicine ball up towards your shoulders.
Pause for a moment at the top, squeezing your biceps.
Inhale and slowly lower the medicine ball back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: The unstable ball forces your core to work while you curl.
coaching_cues: Sit tall on ball, feet flat and wide.
Curl weight up, squeeze biceps at the top.
Lower slowly, keep upper arms completely still.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps burn while your core works to keep you balanced. If you are rocking back and forth, widen your stance for stability. Finish these reps strong.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning back to lift heavier strains your lower back unnecessarily.

name: weighted seated twist (on stability ball)
instructions: Sit on a stability ball with your feet flat on the ground and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
Hold a weight plate or dumbbell with both hands close to your chest.
Engage your core and slowly rotate your torso to one side, keeping your hips stable.
Pause for a moment at the end of the rotation, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat the rotation to the other side.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Stay balanced on the ball and twist from your midsection only.
coaching_cues: Sit upright, core braced tight throughout movement.
Rotate torso side to side, hips stay square.
Control the weight, no swinging or bouncing.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your obliques and deep core muscles working to rotate and stabilize. If you keep losing balance, try a slightly deflated ball. Stay with it.
coach_guide.warning: Twisting too fast on an unstable surface risks falling and injury.

name: weighted side bend (on stability ball)
instructions: Sit on a stability ball with your feet shoulder-width apart and flat on the ground.
Hold a weight in one hand and place your other hand on your hip.
Engage your core and slowly bend sideways towards the weighted side, keeping your back straight.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch sides.

coach_guide.intro: This isolates your obliques while challenging your balance on the ball.
coaching_cues: Sit sideways on ball, feet anchored securely.
Lower torso toward floor, feel oblique stretch.
Pull up through your side, squeeze at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a strong stretch and contraction along the side of your waist. If your hip flexors take over, reposition yourself further onto the ball. Keep pushing through.
coach_guide.warning: Going too deep into the stretch can strain your lower back muscles.

name: weighted sissy squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing slightly outward.
Hold a weight in front of your chest with both hands, or place a barbell across your upper back.
Keeping your chest up and your core engaged, slowly lower your body down by bending at the knees and hips.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lean back and bend your knees to target your quads with intense focus.
coaching_cues: Stay on your toes, lean torso back.
Lower slowly, knees traveling far forward.
Squeeze quads hard as you stand up.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn in the front of your thighs throughout the movement. If your knees hurt, reduce the range of motion slightly. Embrace that quad burn and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Going too deep too fast can strain your knee joints.

name: weighted squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Hold a weight in front of your chest or on your shoulders.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your hips down and back, as if sitting into a chair.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Push through your heels to stand back up, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Sit back into your hips and drive through your heels for powerful glutes.
coaching_cues: Chest proud, core braced and tight.
Hips back first, then bend knees.
Drive up through heels, squeeze glutes top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your glutes and legs working together as one powerful unit. If your lower back rounds, reduce depth until mobility improves. Keep that chest up and dominate every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Rounding your lower back under load risks serious spinal injury.

name: weighted standing curl
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and exhale as you curl the weights up to shoulder level.
Pause for a moment at the top, then inhale as you slowly lower the weights back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your elbows locked at your sides and curl with strict control.
coaching_cues: Elbows pinned tight against your ribs.
Curl up slow, squeeze biceps at top.
Lower with control, no swinging allowed.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your biceps burning from shoulder to elbow on every rep. If your body sways, the weight is too heavy for strict form. Stay disciplined and feel those arms grow.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging the weight uses momentum and strains your lower back.

name: weighted standing hand squeeze
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a weight in each hand.
Extend your arms straight in front of you, palms facing each other.
Squeeze your hands together as hard as you can, engaging your forearms.
Hold the squeeze for a few seconds, then release.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Squeeze the weight as hard as possible to build crushing grip strength.
coaching_cues: Wrap all fingers firmly around weight.
Squeeze maximally, hold for two seconds.
Release slowly, maintain wrist alignment straight.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your forearms burning and pumping with every squeeze. If your wrists bend awkwardly, adjust your grip position. Build that iron grip one squeeze at a time.
coach_guide.warning: Bending wrists under load can cause strain or injury.

name: weighted straight bar dip
instructions: Position yourself between parallel bars with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lean forward slightly and dip deep to maximize chest engagement.
coaching_cues: Lean torso forward, elbows flare out.
Lower until upper arms go parallel.
Press up strong, squeeze chest at top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest stretching at the bottom and contracting powerfully at the top. If your shoulders protest, reduce your depth slightly. Push through and build that powerful chest.
coach_guide.warning: Dipping too deep can overstretch and damage shoulder joints.

name: weighted stretch lunge
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Take a step forward with your right foot, keeping your back straight.
Lower your body by bending your knees until your right thigh is parallel to the ground.
Push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
Repeat with your left leg.
Continue alternating legs for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Step forward into a deep lunge and feel your glutes stretch and work.
coaching_cues: Big step forward, torso stays upright.
Drop back knee low toward the floor.
Drive through front heel, squeeze glute hard.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch in your hip flexors and a strong contraction in your glutes. If your front knee caves inward, push it out over your toes. Stay strong and own every lunge.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your front knee collapse inward stresses the joint badly.

name: weighted svend press
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and hold a weight plate in front of your chest with both hands.
Keep your elbows slightly bent and your palms facing each other.
Press the weight plate straight out in front of you, fully extending your arms.
Pause for a moment at the end of the movement, then slowly bring the weight plate back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Press the weight out in front of you while squeezing your chest together.
coaching_cues: Squeeze plates together with palms pressing.
Extend arms straight out from chest.
Pull back slow, maintain constant squeeze pressure.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your inner chest working hard throughout the entire movement. If your shoulders take over, focus harder on the squeezing action. Keep that chest engaged and finish strong.
coach_guide.warning: Losing the squeeze shifts work away from your chest completely.

name: weighted three bench dips
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench with your hands gripping the edge, fingers pointing forward.
Walk your feet forward, sliding your butt off the bench and supporting your weight with your arms.
Lower your body by bending your elbows, keeping your back close to the bench.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push yourself back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Elevate your feet on a third bench to increase triceps intensity.
coaching_cues: Hands on bench, fingers forward grip.
Lower body until elbows reach ninety degrees.
Press up explosively, lock out triceps.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps burning intensely on every rep with this elevated position. If your shoulders feel strained, reduce your depth slightly. Push hard and build horseshoe triceps.
coach_guide.warning: Dropping too low puts excessive stress on shoulder joints.

name: weighted tricep dips
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with your hands gripping the edge next to your hips.
Slide your butt off the front of the bench with your legs extended in front of you.
Keep your back close to the bench and your elbows slightly bent.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Keep your body vertical and elbows tight to isolate your triceps.
coaching_cues: Torso upright, do not lean forward.
Elbows stay close, pointing straight back.
Lower controlled, press up and lock out.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your triceps doing all the work from stretch to squeeze. If your chest takes over, you are leaning too far forward. Stay vertical and crush those triceps.
coach_guide.warning: Leaning forward turns this into a chest exercise instead.

name: weighted triceps dip on high parallel bars
instructions: Position yourself between two parallel bars with your hands gripping the bars and your arms fully extended.
Bend your elbows and lower your body until your upper arms are parallel to the ground.
Pause for a moment, then push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Use the high bars to get full range of motion for maximum triceps growth.
coaching_cues: Grip bars firm, body stays vertical.
Lower deep until elbows pass ninety degrees.
Drive up hard, fully lock out arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an incredible stretch and contraction in your triceps with each rep. If your shoulders ache at the bottom, shorten your range slightly. Stay focused and build those arms.
coach_guide.warning: Excessive depth without control can damage your shoulder joints.

name: wheel rollerout
instructions: Kneel on the floor and place the wheel roller in front of you.
Place your hands on the handles of the wheel roller and extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Engage your core muscles and slowly roll the wheel forward, keeping your back straight and your abs tight.
Continue rolling forward until your body is fully extended and your arms are overhead.
Pause for a moment, then slowly roll the wheel back towards your knees, maintaining control and keeping your abs engaged.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Roll out slowly and use your abs to pull yourself back in.
coaching_cues: Brace your core tight before rolling out.
Extend only as far as you control.
Pull back using your abs not arms.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep burn across your entire midsection as you extend and return. If your lower back sags or aches, you are going too far out. Shorten the range and build from there.
coach_guide.warning: Letting your hips sag puts dangerous stress on your lower back.

name: wheel run
instructions: Start in a plank position with your hands on the wheel and your body straight.
Engage your core and start rolling the wheel forward by extending your arms.
Continue rolling until your body is fully extended and your arms are overhead.
Reverse the movement by pulling the wheel back towards your body, using your core and arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Drive your knees high and fast while pumping your arms in rhythm.
coaching_cues: Stay light on your feet, quick turnover.
Pump arms hard to match your legs.
Keep your chest lifted and breathing steady.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart rate climbing and your whole body working together. If you are hunching forward or shuffling, reset your posture. Keep that energy high and push through.
coach_guide.warning: Hunching forward restricts your breathing and slows you down.

name: wide grip pull-up
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with your palms facing away from you and your hands wider than shoulder-width apart.
Engage your core and squeeze your shoulder blades together.
Pull your body up towards the bar until your chin is above the bar.
Lower your body back down to the starting position with control.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Grip wide and pull your chest toward the bar using your back muscles.
coaching_cues: Hands wider than shoulder width apart.
Drive elbows down toward your hips.
Squeeze your lats hard at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your outer back muscles doing most of the lifting work. If your biceps burn out first, focus more on pulling with your elbows. Own each rep and keep climbing.
coach_guide.warning: Swinging or kipping steals the work from your lats.

name: wide grip rear pull-up
instructions: Grab the pull-up bar with a wide overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Hang from the bar with your arms fully extended and your body straight.
Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bar, leading with your chest.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bar.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower your body back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Pull yourself up behind the bar focusing on squeezing your shoulder blades together.
coaching_cues: Grip wide and lean slightly forward.
Pull elbows back and down together.
Squeeze your upper back at the top.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel this deep in your upper back between your shoulder blades. If your neck strains or shoulders pinch, widen your grip slightly. Keep grinding through each rep.
coach_guide.warning: Pulling too high behind your head stresses the shoulder joints.

name: wide hand push up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with your hands wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to toe.
Lower your chest towards the ground by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides.
Push through your palms to extend your arms and return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Place your hands wider than normal to shift the focus onto your chest.
coaching_cues: Hands placed well outside shoulder width.
Lower your chest all the way down.
Push up strong and squeeze your chest.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch and contraction across your pectoral muscles. If your shoulders complain, bring your hands in slightly. Stay strong and finish every rep.
coach_guide.warning: Going too wide puts excessive strain on your shoulder joints.

name: wide-grip chest dip on high parallel bars
instructions: Position yourself on the parallel bars with your arms fully extended and your body suspended in the air.
Lean forward slightly and lower your body by bending your elbows until your chest is just above the bars.
Pause for a moment, then push yourself back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Lean forward with a wide grip to target your chest on every dip.
coaching_cues: Grip wide and lean your torso forward.
Lower until you feel a chest stretch.
Press up squeezing your pecs together.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your chest muscles stretching at the bottom and contracting hard at the top. If your shoulders ache, do not dip as deep. Keep that forward lean and push strong.
coach_guide.warning: Dipping too deep can overstretch and damage your shoulders.

name: wind sprints
instructions: Find an open space or a track to perform the exercise.
Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Begin running as fast as you can, pumping your arms and driving your knees up.
Continue sprinting for a specific distance or time period.
Rest and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Explode through short bursts then recover before going again.
coaching_cues: Sprint all out for the set distance.
Pump your arms and drive your knees.
Recover with controlled breathing between sets.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel your heart pounding and your core working to stabilize each sprint. If you are gasping uncontrollably, extend your rest periods slightly. Push hard and earn each recovery.
coach_guide.warning: Sprinting without warming up risks muscle pulls and strains.

name: world greatest stretch
instructions: Start in a lunge position with your right foot forward and your left foot back.
Place your hands on the ground on either side of your right foot.
Lower your left knee to the ground and extend your right leg, keeping your right foot flat on the ground.
Rotate your torso to the right, reaching your right arm up towards the ceiling.
Hold this position for a few seconds, then return to the starting position.
Switch sides and repeat the stretch with your left foot forward.

coach_guide.intro: Flow through this stretch to open your hips and hamstrings together.
coaching_cues: Lunge deep and twist toward front leg.
Straighten front leg and fold forward slowly.
Breathe deeply into each position held.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel a deep stretch through your hip flexors, hamstrings, and thoracic spine. If anything feels sharp or pinching, back off the range. Let your body open up gradually.
coach_guide.warning: Bouncing through the stretch can cause muscle strains.

name: wrist circles
instructions: Extend your arms straight out in front of you.
Make a fist with both hands.
Rotate your wrists in a circular motion, keeping your arms still.
Continue the wrist circles for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Rotate your wrists slowly to warm up and mobilize the joints.
coaching_cues: Make slow controlled circles with fists.
Rotate both clockwise and counterclockwise directions.
Keep your forearms still, only wrists move.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel the small muscles and tendons around your wrists loosening up. If you hear clicking without pain, that is usually normal. Keep the circles smooth and controlled.
coach_guide.warning: Fast jerky movements can aggravate sensitive wrist tendons.

name: wrist rollerer
instructions: Attach a weight to one end of a rope or bar.
Hold the other end of the rope or bar with both hands, palms facing down.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms fully extended in front of you.
Slowly roll the weight up towards your hands by flexing your wrists.
Pause for a moment at the top, then slowly lower the weight back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

coach_guide.intro: Roll the weight up by curling your wrists then control it back down.
coaching_cues: Grip the handle with arms extended forward.
Curl wrists to roll the weight up.
Lower slowly with control, no dropping allowed.
coach_guide.feel: You should feel an intense burn building through your entire forearm. If your grip fails before the burn, use a lighter weight. Keep rolling and build that grip strength.
coach_guide.warning: Letting the weight drop uncontrolled can injure your wrists.
